Title: Daler Mehndi
Content: Daler Singh, known by his stage name, "Daler Mehndi" (born 18 August 1967) is an Indian recording artist, musician, songwriter, author, record producer, performer and environmentalist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Daler Mehndi
Content:  He is credited with making Bhangra popular worldwide, and setting up a parallel non-film music industry to the pre-Daler era’s then existent Bollywood music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Daler Mehndi
Content:  He is best known for his energetic dance songs, his distinctive voice, turban and long flowing robes.

Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content: “This Ain’t a Love Song” is the lead single from Bon Jovi’s 1995 album "These Days".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  It reached #14 on the "Billboard" Hot 100, #11 on the Mainstream Top 40 and it reached #6 on the UK Singles Chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  The song is an example of the strong rhythm and blues influence that Jon Bon Jovi and Richie Sambora wanted the "These Days" album to have, and the final result by the Q is alternative rock according to the critics of the magazine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  The official music video was shot at Wat Ratchaburana in Ayuthaya, Thailand; and directed by Andy Morahan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  A Spanish version, "Como Yo Nadie Te Ha Amado," was also recorded, however the Spanish version is different from the original because the lyrics are different and it talks about how nobody really love the two people that are in a relationship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  Mexican singer Yuridia covered the Spanish version on her second studio album "Habla El Corazón" which peaked at #16 on the "Billboard" Hot Latin Songs chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  Yuridia's cover received was nominated at the 2008 Latin Billboard Awards for Latin Pop Airplay of the Year by a Female Artist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: This Ain't a Love Song
Content:  Her cover also led to Jon Bon Jovi winning an ASCAP Latin Award in the Pop/Ballad field.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crazy in Love
Content:  Its lyrics describe a romantic obsession that causes the protagonist to act out of character.

Content:  It was released on April 25, 2012 by Flaming June, an independent record label established by Maeda.

Content:  Two different editions of the album were released: a regular CD version and a CD+DVD limited edition.

Title: The Day Before I Met You
Content: "The Day Before I Met You" is a song by Australian recording artist Jessica Mauboy from the platinum edition of her third studio album, "Beautiful".

session_0.text:

Content:  It was released on 13 February 2015 as the album's sixth overall single and the second single from the platinum edition.

Content:  "The Day Before I Met You" was written by Antonio Dixon, Kenneth "Babyface" Edmonds, Khristopher Riddick-Tynes, Helen Jane Culver and Mauboy.

Title: The Day Before I Met You
Content:  Dixon and Edmonds also produced the song with The Rascals.

Content:  "The Day Before I Met You" is a pop love song which features an acoustic guitar riff and "very low pitched" vocals in its verses.

Content:  Lyrically, Mauboy sings about "not wanting to go back to a time before that special someone was in her life".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content: The Holland Society of New York was founded in New York City in 1885 to collect information respecting the settlement and history of New Netherland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content:  Its main objective is to find and preserve documentation about the inhabitants' lives and times so as to elucidate the political, social, and religious patterns in the Dutch colony.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content:  The society sponsors historical publications, and provides resources for family studies and genealogy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content:  Many of its members are especially active in genealogical research and publication.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content:  The Holland Society originated the New Netherland Project, which is translating and publishing the 17th-century records held by the New York State Archives.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holland Society of New York
Content:  Among other current sponsorships are The Papers of Jacob Leisler Project and Records of the Translations of the Reformed Protestant Dutch Church of Flatbush, Kings County, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joris Jansen Rapelje
Content: Joris Jansen Rapelje (28 April 1604 – 21 February 1662/63) was a member of the Council of Twelve Men in the Dutch West India Company colony of New Netherland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joris Jansen Rapelje
Content:  He and his wife Catalina (Catalyntje) Trico (1605–1689) were among the earliest settlers in New Netherland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content: Kieft's War, also known as the Wappinger War, was a conflict (1643–1645) between settlers of the nascent colony of New Netherland and the native Lenape population in what would later become the New York metropolitan area of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  It is named for Director-General of New Netherland Willem Kieft, who had ordered an attack without approval of his advisory council and against the wishes of the colonists.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  Dutch soldiers attacked Lenape camps and massacred the native inhabitants, which encouraged unification among the regional Algonquian tribes against the Dutch, and precipitated waves of attacks on both sides.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  This was one of the earliest conflicts between Native Americans and European settlers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  Displeased with Kieft, the Dutch West India Company recalled him and he died while returning to the Netherlands.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  Peter Stuyvesant succeeded him in New Netherland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kieft's War
Content:  Because of the continuing threat by the Algonquians, numerous Dutch settlers returned to the Netherlands, and growth of the colony slowed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: English Neighborhood
Content: The English Neighborhood was the colonial-era name for the towns in eastern Bergen County, New Jersey, along the Hudson Palisades between the North River (Hudson River) and the Hackensack River, particularly around its main tributary, Overpeck Creek.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: English Neighborhood
Content:  The region had been part of the Dutch New Netherland colony of Bergen, whose main town was located at Bergen Square in today's Jersey City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: English Neighborhood
Content:  The name speaks to the geography of the region, "bergen" being the Dutch word for "hills."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: English Neighborhood
Content:  Earlier attempts at settlement at Achter Col ("behind the ridge") and Vriessendael had been compromised in conflicts with the precolonial population, phratries of the Lenape known by their exonyms, the Hackensack and the Tappan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pound Ridge massacre
Content: The Pound Ridge massacre was a battle of Kieft's War that took place in March 1644 between the forces of New Netherland and members of the Wappinger Confederacy at a Wappinger Confederacy village in the present-day town of Pound Ridge, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pound Ridge massacre
Content:  A mixed force of 130 Dutch and English soldiers led by Captain John Underhill launched a night attack on the village and destroyed it with fire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pound Ridge massacre
Content:  500 to 700 members of the Wappinger Confederacy were killed while the New Netherland force lost one man killed and fifteen wounded.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pound Ridge massacre
Content:  More casualties were suffered in this attack than in any other single incident in the war.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pound Ridge massacre
Content:  Shortly after the battle several local Wappinger Confederacy sachems sued for peace.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of colonial governors of New York
Content: The territory which would later become the state of New York was settled by European colonists as part of the New Netherland colony (parts of present-day New York, New Jersey, Connecticut and Delaware) under the command of the Dutch West India Company in the Seventeenth Century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of colonial governors of New York
Content:  These colonists were largely of Dutch, Flemish, Walloon, and German stock, but the colony soon became a "melting pot."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of colonial governors of New York
Content:  In 1664, at the onset of the Second Anglo-Dutch War, English forces under Richard Nicolls ousted the Dutch from control of New Netherland, and the territory became part of several different English colonies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of colonial governors of New York
Content:  Despite one brief year when the Dutch retook the colony (1673–1674), New York would remain an English possession until the American colonies declared independence in 1776.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nine Men
Content: The council of Nine Men was a citizens board and a form of representational democracy in New Netherland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nine Men
Content:  It replaced the previous councils, the Twelve Men and the Eight Men.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nine Men
Content:  Members of the council were elected in 1647, 1649, 1650 and 1652.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nine Men
Content:  On July 26, 1649, eleven current and former members of the board signed the "Petition of the Commonality of New Netherland", which requested that the States General take action to encourage economic freedom and force local government like that in the Netherlands, removing the colony from the control of the Dutch West India Company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nine Men
Content:  It became the basis for the municipal government when the city of New Amsterdam received its charter in 1653.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tomys Swartwout
Content: 'Thomas or Tomys Swartwout (June 1, 1607, Groningen – 1660, Beverwijck) was one of the earliest importers of tobacco from New Netherland to Western and northern Europe, one of earliest settlers of New Netherland (present day United States), and a founder of Midwood (originally Midwout), Brooklyn, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Twelve Men
Content: The Council of Twelve Men was a group of 12 men, chosen on 29 August 1641 by the residents of New Netherland to advise the Director of New Netherland, Willem Kieft, on relations with the Native Americans due to the murder of Claes Swits.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Twelve Men
Content:  Although the council was not permanent, it was the first representational form of democracy in the Dutch colony.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Twelve Men
Content:  The next two councils created were known as the Eight Men and the Nine Men

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rumachenanck
Content: The Rumanchenank were a Lenape people who inhabited the region radiating from the Palisades in New York and New Jersey at the time of European colonialization in the 17th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rumachenanck
Content:  Settlers to the provincial colony of New Netherland called them the "Haverstroo" meaning "oat straw", which became "Haverstraw" in English, and still used to describe part of their territory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rated X (film)
Content: Rated X is a 2000 American television film starring brothers Charlie Sheen and Emilio Estevez, with the latter also directing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rated X (film)
Content:  Based on the nonfiction book "X-Rated" by David McCumber, the film chronicles the story of the Mitchell brothers, Jim and Artie Mitchell, who were pioneers in the pornography and strip club businesses in San Francisco in the 1970s and 1980s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rated X (film)
Content:  The film focuses on the making of their most profitable film, "Behind the Green Door".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rated X (film)
Content:  It also portrays Artie's descent into drug addiction and increasingly erratic behavior, culminating in his murder at Jim's hands.

Title: The Way (2010 film)
Content: The Way is a 2010 American drama film directed, produced and written by Emilio Estevez, starring his father Martin Sheen, Deborah Kara Unger, James Nesbitt, Yorick van Wageningen, and Renée Estevez.

Content:  The end credits song is "Home Again" by Oingo Boingo and the score by Danny Elfman.

session_0.text:
Title: Culture Clash in AmeriCCa
Content: Culture Clash in AmeriCCa is a 2005 documentary film directed by Emilio Estevez.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Culture Clash in AmeriCCa
Content:  It is an anthology of fun and thought-provoking skits and monologues portraying diverse American immigrants.

session_0.text:
Title: Culture Clash in AmeriCCa
Content:  Emlio Estevez doesn't appear in this documentary film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Culture Clash in AmeriCCa
Content:  Inspiration came from thousands of interviews conducted nationwide during a period of 20 years, by Culture Clash.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Emilio Estevez
Content: Emilio Estevez ( ; born May 12, 1962) is an American actor, director, and writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Emilio Estevez
Content:  He started his career as an actor and is well known for being a member of the acting Brat Pack of the 1980s, starring in "The Breakfast Club", "St. Elmo's Fire", and also acting in the 1983 hit movie "The Outsiders".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Emilio Estevez

Title: Nightmares (1983 film)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commissioner v. Flowers
Content: Commissioner v. Flowers, 326 U.S. 465 (1946), was a Federal income tax case before the Supreme Court of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commissioner v. Flowers
Content:  The Court held that in order to deduct the expense of traveling under §162, the expense must be incurred while away from home, and must be a reasonable expense necessary or appropriate to the development and pursuit of a trade or business.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commissioner v. Flowers
Content:  In this case, the attorney in question could only deduct traveling expenses from her gross income when the railroad's business forced attorney to travel and live temporarily at some place other than the railroad's principal place of business.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commissioner v. Flowers
Content:  Where attorney preferred for personal reasons to live in a different state from the location of his employer's principal office, and his duties required frequent trips to that office, the evidence sustained Tax Court's finding that the necessary relation between expenses of such trips and the railroad's business was lacking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circuit court
Content: Circuit court is the name of court systems in several common law jurisdictions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circuit court
Content:  The core concept of circuit courts requires judges to travel to different locales in order to ensure wide visibility and understanding of cases in a region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circuit court
Content:  More generally, some modern circuit courts may also refer to a court which merely holds trials for cases of multiple locations in some rotation.

Title: Eliot Spitzer
Content: Eliot Laurence Spitzer (born June 10, 1959) is a former American Democratic politician and attorney who served as the 54th governor of New York, from January 1, 2007, until his resignation in disgrace fourteen months later on March 17, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eliot Spitzer
Content:  Prior to being elected governor of New York, he was elected to two four-year terms as the Attorney General of New York, from 1999 to 2006.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eliot Spitzer
Content:  Prior to becoming attorney general, Spitzer worked for six years as a prosecutor with the office of the Manhattan district attorney and also worked as an attorney in private practice with several New York law firms.

Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content: The Attorney General of North Carolina is the elected head of the state's Department of Justice.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  The North Carolina constitution, in Article III Section 7, provides for the election of the Attorney General.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  http://www.ncga.state.nc.us/Legislation/constitution/article3.html By statute, Attorney General's duties include providing legal representation and advice to all state agencies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  The parameters of that duty have been the subject of some debate, when, for example, United States Attorney General Eric Holder suggested that state Attorneys General should not squander their state's resources in defense of laws they know to be unconstitutional.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  By statute, in defense of the public interest, the Attorney General may initiate legal action or intervene in proceedings before any courts, regulatory officers, agencies or bodies — either state or federal — on behalf of the state's agencies and citizens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  http://www.ncga.state.nc.us/EnactedLegislation/Statutes/PDF/ByChapter/Chapter_114.pdf The Attorney General also renders legal opinions, either formally or informally, upon all questions of law submitted by the General Assembly, the Governor or any other state officer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  Attorney General opinions may be viewed online.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Carolina Attorney General
Content:  http://www.ncdoj.gov/About-DOJ/Legal-Services/Legal-Opinions.aspx

Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} (5 October 1732 – 4 April 1802) was a British politician and barrister, who served as Attorney General, Master of the Rolls and Lord Chief Justice.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content:  Born to a country gentleman, he was initially educated in Hanmer before moving to Ruthin School aged 12.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content:  Rather than going to university he instead worked as a clerk to an attorney, joining the Middle Temple in 1750 and being called to the Bar in 1756.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content:  Initially almost unemployed due to the lack of education and contacts which a university education would have provided, his business increased thanks to his friendships with John Dunning, who, overwhelmed with cases, allowed Kenyon to work many, and Lord Thurlow who secured for him the Chief Justiceship of Chester in 1780.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content:  He was returned as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Hindon the same year, serving repeatedly as Attorney General under William Pitt the Younger.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Kenyon, 1st Baron Kenyon
Content:  He effectively sacrificed his political career in 1784 to challenge the ballot of Charles James Fox, and was rewarded with a baronetcy; from then on he did not speak in the House of Commons, despite remaining an MP.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Taipei
Content:  Formerly known as Taipeh-fu during the Qing era and Taihoku under Japanese rule, Taipei became the capital of Taiwan Province as part of the Republic of China in 1945 and has been the capital of the ROC since 1949, when the Kuomintang lost the mainland to the Communists in the Chinese Civil War.

Content:  It aims to promote opportunities for interfaith dialogue through community service projects to “[foster] genuine dialogue that is built around the shared values of service” and to “[inspire] project participants to build cross-identity friendships and strengthen their understanding of other religious traditions.”

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World Faith
Content:  World Faith’s activities pivot around the idea of revising the system of global peacebuilding, with a special focus on the role of faith identity in conflict.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octobass
Content: The octobass is an extremely large and rare (only 7 in existence) bowed string instrument that was first built around 1850 in Paris by the French luthier Jean-Baptiste Vuillaume (1798–1875).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octobass
Content:  It has three strings and is essentially a larger version of the double bass (the specimen in the collection of the Musée de la Musique in Paris measures in length, whereas a full-size double bass is generally approximately in length).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octobass
Content:  Because of the extreme fingerboard length and string thickness, the musician plays it using a system of levers and pedals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octobass
Content:  It has never been produced on a large scale or used much by composers (though Hector Berlioz wrote favorably about the instrument and proposed its widespread adoption).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octobass
Content:  In addition to the Paris instrument, octobasses exist in the collections of the Musical Instrument Museum in Phoenix, Arizona and the Kunsthistorisches Museum in Vienna.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content: "Talk Dirty" is a song by American singer Jason Derulo, released as the second single from his third studio album, "Tattoos" (2013), in Europe and Oceania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  Derulo's third studio album was retitled "Talk Dirty" for its US release, featuring an alternative track listing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  It was released as the third single in the US on January 7, 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  Additional vocals are provided by American rapper 2 Chainz, making it Derulo's first single to feature a guest performer, excluding the French version of "Whatcha Say".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  The artists co-wrote the song with Jason Evigan, Sean Douglas, Ori Kaplan, Tamir Muskat, Tomer Yosef, and Wallpaper frontman Ricky Reed; the latter is also the song's producer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  The song is built around sampled instrumentals from "Hermetico" by the Israeli band Balkan Beat Box from their 2007 studio album "Nu Med".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Talk Dirty (Jason Derulo song)
Content:  The main saxophone lick is based on characteristic Turkish traditional ornamentations, particularly rendering the Turkish wind instrument Zurna.

Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content: Quogue was a station stop along the Montauk Branch of the Long Island Rail Road and the station was built around June, 1875.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content:  During construction the station was moved by the village "on a Sunday morning" from its original and current location to a location on Old Depot Road.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content:  The second depot was built around 1882 and later was moved to a private location around 1905.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content:  The third depot was built around 1905 and at some point was elevated for the bridge over the former New York State Route 113.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content:  The station house was razed around April, 1964 but the station stop itself continued to operate until March 16, 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Quogue (LIRR station)
Content:  This station, along with nine others around that time were closed due to low ridership, which did not make it very cost-effective to build high-level platforms to support the new C3 railcars the LIRR was procuring at the time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content: Xylobands are wristbands that contain light-emitting diodes and radio frequency receivers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  The lights inside the wristband can be controlled by a software program, which sends signals to the wristband, instructing it to light up or blink, for example.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  The RGB version has a white strap and can emit any colour on the spectrum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  The single colour version is available in green, blue, yellow, red, pink and white.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  The first use of Xylobands on a large scale was on Coldplay's 2012 Mylo Xyloto tour.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  A Xyloband was given to each member of the audience, and as the concert played, the flashing of the bands was synchronized to the music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Xyloband
Content:  The inventor of the wristbands, Jason Regler, stated that he had the idea for the flashing wristbands while at a Coldplay concert, during the song "Fix You".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ask (The Smiths song)
Content:  It was released as a single on 20 October 1986 through Rough Trade Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ask (The Smiths song)
Content:  Credited to vocalist Morrissey and guitarist Johnny Marr, "Ask" is an upbeat, positive pop song built around major chords.

session_0.text:
Title: Ask (The Smiths song)
Content:  Its lyrics touch on shyness and encourages the listener to release their inhibitions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ask (The Smiths song)
Content:  Its multiple guitar parts and complex production led to disagreements regarding its final mix.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ask (The Smiths song)
Content:  In addition, Craig Gannon, one-time rhythm guitarist for the group, has claimed he wrote and was denied credit for the song's chord structure.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Fix You
Content:  The track is built around an organ accompanied by slow tempo drums and vocals.

session_0.text:

Content:  It stars Pola Negri as a French farmgirl and Clive Brook as the German prisoner of war she falls in love with.

session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Fort Lee, New Jersey
Content: Fort Lee is a borough at the eastern border of Bergen County, New Jersey, United States, in the New York City Metropolitan Area, situated atop the Hudson Palisades.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Lee, New Jersey
Content:  As of the 2010 United States Census, the borough's population was 35,345, reflecting a decline of 116 (−0.3%) from the 35,461 counted in the 2000 Census, which had in turn increased by 3,464 (+10.8%) from the 31,997 counted in the 1990 Census.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Lee, New Jersey
Content:  The borough is the western terminus of the George Washington Bridge and is located across the Hudson River from the Manhattan borough of New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Lee, New Jersey
Content:  Named for the site of an early American Revolutionary War military encampment, it later became the birthplace of the American film industry.

Title: Mexican Federal Highway 40
Content: Mexican Federal Highway 40, also called the "Carretera Interoceánica" (Interoceanic Highway), is a road beginning at Reynosa, Tamaulipas, just west of the Port of Brownsville, Texas, and ending at Mexican Federal Highway 15 in Villa Unión, Sinaloa, near Mazatlán and the Pacific coast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Federal Highway 40
Content:  It is called Interoceanic as, once finished, the cities of Matamoros, Tamaulipas, on the Gulf of Mexico and Mazatlán on the Pacific Ocean will be linked.

Title: Villa Unión
Content: Villa Unión is a city in northwestern Argentina and the main settlement of Departamento Coronel Felipe Varela with a population of 12,263.

Title: Estado de Occidente
Content: Estado de Occidente (also known as Sonora y Sinaloa) was a Mexican state established in 1824.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Estado de Occidente
Content:  The constitution was drafted in that year and the government was initially established with its capital at El Fuerte, Sinaloa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Estado de Occidente
Content:  The first governor was Juan Miguel Riesgo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Estado de Occidente
Content:  The state consisted of modern Sonora and Sinaloa, and also modern Arizona more or less south of the Gila River (although in much of this area the Yaqui, Pima, Apaches, and other native inhabitants did not recognize the authority of the state),

session_0.text:
Title: Operation Sinaloa
Content: Operation Sinaloa or Operation Culiacan - Navolato (Spanish: Operacion Sinaloa/Operacion Conjunto Sinaloa) is an ongoing Anti-drug trafficking operation in the Mexican state of Sinaloa by the Federal Police and the Mexican Armed Forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Operation Sinaloa
Content:  Its main objective is to cripple all cartel organizations such as the Sinaloa Cartel, Beltrán-Leyva Cartel and Los Zetas that operate in that state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Operation Sinaloa
Content:  The Military was deployed in response to the murder of Mexico's Federal Police commissioner Édgar Eusebio Millán Gómez.

session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content: Berber orthography is the writing system(s) used to transcribe the Berber languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content:  In antiquity, the Libyco-Berber script (Tifinagh) was utilized to write Berber.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content:  Early uses of the script have been found on rock art and in various sepulchres.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content:  Following the spread of Islam, some Berber scholars also utilized the Arabic script.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content:  There are now three writing systems in use for Berber languages: Tifinagh (Libyco-Berber), the Arabic script, and the Berber Latin alphabet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber orthography
Content:  Different groups in North Africa have different preferences of writing system, often motivated by ideology and politics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Madurese language
Content: Madurese is a language of the Madurese people of Madura Island and eastern Java, Indonesia; it is also spoken on the neighbouring small Kangean Islands and Sapudi Islands, as well as from migrants to other parts of Indonesia, namely the eastern salient of Java (comprising Pasuruan, Surabaya, Malang to Banyuwangi), the Masalembu Islands, and even some on Kalimantan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Madurese language
Content:  The Kangean dialect may be a separate language.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Madurese language
Content:  It was traditionally written in the Javanese script, but the Latin script and the Pegon script (based on Arabic script) is now more commonly used.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Madurese language
Content:  The number of speakers, though shrinking, is estimated to be 8–13 million, making it one of the most widely spoken language in the country.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Madurese language
Content:  A variant of Madurese that is Bawean is also spoken by Baweanese (or Boyan) descendants in Malaysia and Singapore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content: The Takri script (sometimes called "Tankri") is an abugida writing system of the Brahmic family of scripts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  It is closely related to, and derived from, the Sharada script employed by Kashmiri.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  It is also related to the Gurmukhī script used to write Punjabi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  Until the late 1940s, And adopted version script of Takri (called Dogri, Dogra or Dogra Akhtar)was the official script for writing the Dogri in the state of Jammu and Kashmir and Kangri, Cahmbeali, Mandeali in Himachal Pradesh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  There are some record of using Takri script in the history of Nepali (Khas Kura).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  Takri has historically been used by a number of Western Pahari, Garhwali and Dardic languages in the Western Himalayas, such as Gaddi or Gaddki (the language of the Gaddi ethnic group), Kashtwari (the dialect centered on the Kashtwar or Kishtwar region of Jammu and Kashmir) and Chamiyali (the language of the Chamba region of Himachal Pradesh).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  Takri used to be most prevalent script for business records and communication in various parts of Himachal Pradesh including Chintpurni, Una, Kangra, Bilaspur and Hamirpur regions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  The aged businessmen can still be found using Takri in these areas, but newer generation has now shifted to Devanagari and even English (Roman).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Takri alphabet
Content:  This shift can be traced to have happened during the period ranging from 1950s to 1980s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Umbrian language
Content: Umbrian is an extinct Italic language formerly spoken by the Umbri in the ancient Italian region of Umbria.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Umbrian language
Content:  Within the Italic languages it is closely related to the Oscan group and is therefore associated with it in the group of Osco-Umbrian languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Umbrian language
Content:  Since that classification was first formulated a number of other languages in ancient Italy were discovered to be more closely related to Umbrian.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Umbrian language
Content:  Therefore, a group was devised to contain them, the Umbrian languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content: The Rejang script, sometimes spelt "Redjang" and locally known as "Surat Ulu" ('upstream script'), is an abugida of the Brahmic family, and is related to other scripts of the region, like Batak, Buginese, and others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  Rejang is a member of the closely related group of Surat Ulu scripts that include the script variants of Bengkulu, Lembak, Lintang, Lebong, and Serawai.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  Other scripts that are closely related, and sometimes included in the Surat Ulu group, are Kerinci and Lampung.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  The script was in use prior to the introduction of Islam to the Rejang area; the earliest attested document appears to date from the mid-18th century CE.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  The Rejang script is sometimes also known as the KaGaNga script following the first three letters of the alphabet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  The term KaGaNga was never used by the users of the script community, but it was coined by the British anthropologist Mervyn A. Jaspan (1926–1975) in his book "Folk literature of South Sumatra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  Redjang Ka-Ga-Nga texts."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rejang script
Content:  Canberra, The Australian National University 1964.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber languages
Content: The Berber languages, also known as Berber or the Amazigh languages (Berber name: "Tamaziɣt", "Tamazight"; Neo-Tifinagh: ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵖⵜ, Tuareg Tifinagh: ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵗⵜ, ⵝⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵗⵝ , ] , ] ), are a family of similar and closely related languages and dialects spoken by the Berbers indigenous to North Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber languages
Content:  The Berber languages constitute a branch of the Afroasiatic family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Berber languages
Content:  They were traditionally written with the ancient Libyco-Berber script, which now exists in the form of Tifinagh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Horpa language
Content: Horpa (Chinese: 道孚语 "Daofu", 爾龔語 "Ergong"), also named Stau, Daofuhua, Bawang, Bopa, Danba, Dawu, Geshitsa, Geshiza, Geshizahua, Hor, Huo’er, Hórsók, Nyagrong-Minyag, Pawang, Rgu, Western Gyarong, Western Jiarong, Xinlong-Muya, rTau"," is one of several closely related Rgyalrongic languages of China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Horpa language
Content:  Horpa is better understood as a cluster of closely related yet unintelligible dialect groups/languages closely related to Horpa Shangzhai or Stodsde skad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Horpa language
Content:  The term "Stodsde skad" is a Tibetan name meaning "language of the people of the far Northwest".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazanderani language
Content: Mazanderani (مازندرانی), also Tabari (طبری) or Geleki (گلکی) is an Iranian language of the Northwestern branch, spoken mainly in Iran's Mazandaran, Tehran, Alborz, Semnan and Golestan provinces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazanderani language
Content:  As a member of the Northwestern branch (the northern branch of Western Iranian), etymologically speaking it is rather closely related to Gilaki, and more distantly related to Persian, which belongs to the Southwestern branch.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazanderani language
Content:  Mazandarani is closely related to Gilaki and the two languages have similar vocabularies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazanderani language
Content:  The Gilaki and Mazandarani languages (but not other Iranian languages) share certain typological features with Caucasian languages (specifically South Caucasian languages), reflecting the history, ethnic identity, and close relatedness to the Caucasus region and Caucasian peoples of the Mazandarani people and Gilaki people.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tugurt language
Content: The Tugurt language, also known as Oued Righ Berber and Temacine Tamazight, is a Zenati Berber variety spoken in some of the oases of the northeastern Oued Righ region around Touggourt in Algeria.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tugurt language
Content:  s of 1893 , its main speech area was in Temacine, Blidet-Amor, Meggarine and Ghomra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tugurt language
Content:  It is closely related to the nearby Tumzabt (Mozabite) and Teggargrent (Ouargli) languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dyula language
Content: Jula (or Dyula, Dioula) is a Mande language spoken in Burkina Faso, Ivory Coast and Mali.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dyula language
Content:  It is one of the Manding languages and is most closely related to Bambara, being mutually intelligible with Bambara as well as Malinke.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dyula language
Content:  It is a trade language in West Africa and is spoken by millions of people, either as a first or second language.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dyula language
Content:  It is written in the Arabic script and the Latin script, as well as in the indigenous N'Ko script.

Title: Charles Fraser-Smith
Content: Charles Fraser-Smith (26 January 1904 – 9 November 1992) was an author and one-time missionary who is widely credited as being the inspiration for Ian Fleming's James Bond quartermaster Q.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Fraser-Smith
Content:  During World War II, Fraser-Smith worked for the Ministry of Supply, fabricating equipment nicknamed "Q-devices" (after Q-ships) for SOE agents operating in occupied Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Fraser-Smith
Content:  Prior to the war, Fraser-Smith had worked as a missionary in North Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Fraser-Smith
Content:  After the war he purchased a dairy farm in Bratton Fleming, Devon, where he died in 1992.

Title: Secret Service Counter-Assault Teams
Content: Secret Service Counter-Assault Teams (CAT) are tactical forces maintained by the U.S. Secret Service which are responsible for repelling coordinated attacks against dignitaries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  This is in contrast to a close protection team whose mission is to shield a dignitary from attackers and evacuate them to a place of safety.

Title: The XYY Man
Content: The XYY Man began life as a series of novels by Kenneth Royce, featuring the character of William (or Willie) 'Spider' Scott, a one-time cat-burglar who leaves prison aiming to go straight but finds his talents still to be very much in demand by both the criminal underworld and the British secret service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Scott has an extra Y chromosome that supposedly gives him a criminal predisposition – although he tries to go straight, he is genetically incapable of doing so.

Title: Secret service
Content: A secret service is a government agency, intelligence agency, or the activities of a government agency, concerned with the gathering of intelligence data.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Secret service
Content:  The tasks and powers of a secret service can vary greatly from one country to another.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Secret service
Content:  For instance, a country may establish a secret service which has some policing powers (such as surveillance) but not others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Secret service
Content:  The powers and duties of a government organization may be partly secret and partly not.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Secret service
Content:  The organization may be said to operate openly at home and secretly abroad, or vice versa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Secret service
Content:  Secret police and intelligence agencies can usually be considered secret services.

Content: His Dark Materials is an epic trilogy of fantasy novels by Philip Pullman consisting of "Northern Lights" (1995, published as "The Golden Compass" in North America), "The Subtle Knife" (1997), and "The Amber Spyglass" (2000).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: Lyra Belacqua
Content:  Lyra is a young girl who inhabits a universe parallel to our own.

Title: Parallel universe (fiction)
Content: A parallel universe is a hypothetical self-contained reality co-existing with one's own.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Parallel universe (fiction)
Content:  A specific group of parallel universes are called a "multiverse", although this term can also be used to describe the possible parallel universes that constitute reality.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Parallel universe (fiction)
Content:  While the terms "parallel universe" and "alternative reality" are generally synonymous and can be used interchangeably in most cases, there is sometimes an additional connotation implied with the term "alternative reality" that implies that the reality is a variant of our own.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Parallel universe (fiction)
Content:  The term "parallel universe" is more general, without any connotations implying a relationship, or lack of relationship, with our own universe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Parallel universe (fiction)
Content:  A universe where the very laws of nature are different – for example, one in which there are no Laws of Motion – would in general count as a parallel universe but not an alternative reality and a concept between both fantasy world and earth.

Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content: The Yakel House and Union Brewery are a historic house and brewery complex located at 1421-1431 Pearl St. in Alton, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  Philip Yakel, a German immigrant, built the brewery soon after coming to America in 1836.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  The brewery was the first in Alton and one of the city's earliest successful industries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  Yakel's son George, who eventually ran the brewery alongside his father, built the house in 1863; it was the family's second home at the site.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  The brick home features a vernacular design influenced by German architectural tradition.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  William Netzhammer, a brewer from St. Louis, purchased the brewery in 1882.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakel House and Union Brewery
Content:  The Netzhammer family ran the brewery until it closed in 1952;, notably, the brewery continued production during Prohibition by making near beer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Hair and Son
Content: John Hair and Son was an English brewer based in Melbourne, Derbyshire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Hair and Son
Content:  It was founded in 1851 and acquired by Offiler's of Derby in 1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaux Breweries
Content: Vaux Breweries was a major brewer based in Sunderland, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaux Breweries
Content:  It was listed on the London Stock Exchange.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chef & Brewer
Content: Chef & Brewer is a chain of around 127 licensed traditional pub restaurants in the United Kingdom, owned by the Greene King subsidiary, the Spirit Pub Company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chef & Brewer
Content:  The concept is providing more up market pub food and those in the chain have been re-furbished.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content: Agnes Bugge (born before 1417) was an English brewer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  Bugge is notable because she was a women who was running a brewery in the 15th/16th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  Usually wives assisted their husbands and their contribution is never identified in the records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  In Agnes' case her husband was a draper and therefore it becomes clear that it was Agnes who brewed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  This is noted in 1419/20 when the brewers were in dispute with the City of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  The brewers agreed to create a fighting fund and Stephen paid the largest contribution for his wife's brewery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  Idonea Hatton's husband also made a contribution but in her case they had the brewery jointly.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  19 of the 24 brewery's involved included women but the Bugge's brewery appeared to the only one operated by a woman, although it was noted that 80% of the breweries were run in part by a married woman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  When Stephen died it becomes clear how the law saw the situation as Stephen had to leave Agnes' brewery to her.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  Coincidentally it is interesting to note that the funds raised won the case against the city of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnes Bugge
Content:  At that time the Lord Mayor of London was Dick Whittington.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ruddles Brewery
Content: Ruddles Brewery (G. Ruddle & Co) was an English brewery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ruddles Brewery
Content:  The brand is now owned by Greene King who still brew beers under the Ruddles name in Suffolk, although the current recipes are not those used at the original brewery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Booth Brewing Co.
Content: The Booth Brewing Co. is a microbrewery headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Booth Brewing Co.
Content:  Sunghoo Yang, a former investment analyst, Heeyoon Kim, a former Korean medicine doctor, and Daniel Tudor, a journalist for The Economist founded the brewery in 2015, after operating a pizza pub since 2013 [].

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Booth Brewing Co.
Content:  They acquired the brewing facility in Eureka, California, previously owned by Lost Coast Brewery, in 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Booth Brewing Co.
Content:  They are well known for Taedonggang Pale Ale, a collaboration beer with Danish microbrewery Mikkeller, and also for being the second foreign craft brewer to produce beer in their own facility in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Castle Rock Brewery
Content: Castle Rock Brewery is a brewery and pub group based in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Castle Rock Brewery
Content:  It was originally set up as a joint venture with Bramcote Brewery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Castle Rock Brewery
Content:  Bramcote Brewery was wound up and a new company was set up, 50% owned by Tynemill and 50% owned by the previous owners of Bramcote Brewery, on a new site next to Tynemill’s pub, The Vat and Fiddle, located on Queensbridge road close to Nottingham railway station.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Castle Rock Brewery
Content:  The Vat and Fiddle became the ‘Brewery Tap’, serving over 10 real ales at any one time, several of which are Castle Rock's.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mill Street Brewery
Content: Mill Street Brewery is a brewery in Toronto, Canada that is a part of Anheuser–Busch InBev.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mill Street Brewery
Content:  During its first decade of operation, as an independent brewer, Mill St. won several awards including Golden Tap Awards for Best Toronto Microbrewery ('04-'08) and Best Toronto Beer (for Tankhouse Ale: '04-'07), and was named "Canadian Brewery of the Year" at the Canadian Brewing Awards in 2007, 2008, and 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mill Street Brewery
Content:  It was purchased in 2015 by Canadian brewer Labatt Brewing Company, which in turn is owned by the global brewing giant Anheuser–Busch InBev.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greene King
Content: Greene King is the UK's largest pub retailer and brewer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greene King
Content:  It is based in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greene King
Content:  The company owns pubs, restaurants and hotels.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greene King
Content:  It is listed on the London Stock Exchange and is a constituent of the FTSE 250 Index.

session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Goes Gaga
Content: "Lisa Goes Gaga" is the twenty-second and final episode of the twenty-third season of the American animated sitcom "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Goes Gaga
Content:  It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on May 20, 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Goes Gaga
Content:  In the episode, American singer-songwriter Lady Gaga makes a visit to Springfield, where all of its residents are in a state of depression.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Goes Gaga
Content:  Main character Lisa Simpson is arguably the most depressed person in the city, prompting Gaga to go out of her way to teach Lisa the meaning of happiness.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mike B. Anderson
Content: Mike B. Anderson (born 1973), sometimes credited as Mikel B. Anderson, is an American television director who works on "The Simpsons" and has directed numerous episodes of the show, and was animated in "The Secret War of Lisa Simpson" as cadet Anderson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mike B. Anderson
Content:  While a college student, he directed the live action feature films "Alone in the T-Shirt Zone" (1986) and "Kamillions" (1989).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mike B. Anderson
Content:  Since 1990, he has worked primarily in animation including being a consulting producer on the series, "The Oblongs", and story consultant on "Tripping the Rift".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yeardley Smith
Content: Martha Maria Yeardley Smith ( ; born July 3, 1964) is a French-American actress, voice actress, writer, author, comedian, and painter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yeardley Smith
Content:  She is best known for her long-running role as Lisa Simpson on the animated television series "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content: "All About Lisa" is the twentieth episode and season finale of "The Simpsons"<nowiki>'</nowiki> nineteenth season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on May 18, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  Lisa Simpson becomes Krusty the Clown's newest assistant and steals his spotlight.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  She wins Entertainer of the Year at the Springfield Media awards, but is warned that with her sudden fame comes a new attitude towards others and herself.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  Meanwhile, Homer and Bart bond over their newfound love of coin collecting.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  The episode features narration by Sideshow Mel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  It was written by John Frink and directed by Steven Dean Moore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All About Lisa
Content:  Drew Carey guest voices as himself, appearing as a guest on Krusty's show.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content: The Itchy & Scratchy Show (often shortened as Itchy & Scratchy) is a running gag and fictional animated television series featured in the American animated television series "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  It usually appears as a part of "The Krusty the Clown Show", watched regularly by Bart Simpson and Lisa Simpson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  Itself an animated cartoon, "The Itchy & Scratchy Show" depicts a sadistic anthropomorphic blue mouse, Itchy (voiced by Dan Castellaneta), who repeatedly maims and kills an anthropomorphic, hapless threadbare black cat, Scratchy (voiced by Harry Shearer).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  The cartoon first appeared in "The Tracey Ullman Show" short "The Bart Simpson Show", which originally aired November 20, 1988.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  The cartoon's first appearance in "The Simpsons" was in the 1990 episode "There's No Disgrace Like Home".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  Typically presented as 15-to-60-second-long cartoons, the show is filled with gratuitous violence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Itchy & Scratchy Show
Content:  "The Simpsons" also occasionally features characters who are involved with the production of "The Itchy & Scratchy Show", including Roger Meyers Jr. (voiced by Alex Rocco, and, later, Hank Azaria), who runs the studio and produces the show.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Matt Groening
Content: Matthew Abraham "Matt" Groening ( ; born February 15, 1954) is an American cartoonist, writer, producer, animator, and voice actor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Matt Groening
Content:  He is the creator of the comic strip "Life in Hell" (1977–2012) and the television series "The Simpsons" (1989–present), "Futurama" (1999–2003, 2008–2013), and the upcoming "Disenchantment" (2018).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Matt Groening
Content:  "The Simpsons" has gone on to become the longest-running U.S. primetime-television series in history, as well as the longest-running animated series and sitcom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ralph Wiggum
Content: Ralph Wiggum is a recurring character on the animated series "The Simpsons", voiced by Nancy Cartwright.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ralph Wiggum
Content:  The son of Police Chief Wiggum and a classmate of Lisa Simpson, Ralph is best known as the show's resident oddball, and is noted for his non sequiturs and erratic behavior.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ralph Wiggum
Content:  His lines range from nonsensical, or bizarre interpretations of a current event, to surprisingly profound statements that go over people's heads; and his behavior varies between blissfully unaware, to dim-witted, to awkwardly spontaneous, even occasionally straightforward.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ralph Wiggum
Content:  The very nature of the character has undergone seemingly differing interpretations over the years and within various media.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content: "Lisa's Rival" is the second episode of "The Simpsons"<nowiki>'</nowiki> sixth season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content:  It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on September 11, 1994.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content:  It was the first episode to be written by Mike Scully, and was directed by Mark Kirkland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content:  Winona Ryder guest stars as Allison Taylor, a new student at Springfield Elementary School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content:  Lisa Simpson begins to feel threatened by Allison because she is smarter, younger, and a better saxophone player than she is.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa's Rival
Content:  The episode's subplot sees Homer steal a large pile of sugar from a crashed truck, and begin selling it door-to-door.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content: Lisa Marie Simpson is a fictional character in the animated television series "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  She is the middle child and most intelligent of the Simpson family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  Voiced by Yeardley Smith, Lisa first appeared on television in "The Tracey Ullman Show" short "Good Night" on April 19, 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  Cartoonist Matt Groening created and designed her while waiting to meet James L. Brooks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  Groening had been invited to pitch a series of shorts based on his comic "Life in Hell", but instead decided to create a new set of characters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  He named the elder Simpson daughter after his younger sister Lisa Groening.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lisa Simpson
Content:  After appearing on "The Tracey Ullman Show" for three years, the Simpson family were moved to their own series on Fox, which debuted on December 17, 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
Content: "The Secret War of Lisa Simpson" is the twenty-fifth and final episode of "The Simpsons"<nowiki>'</nowiki> eighth season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
Content:  It first aired on the Fox network in the United States on May 18, 1997.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
Content:  Bart gets sent to a military academy as punishment for bad behavior.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
Content:  While visiting the academy, Lisa sees that the school is far more challenging than hers and she decides that she wants to attend as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
Content:  It was directed by Mike B. Anderson, written by Richard Appel and featured Willem Dafoe in a guest spot as the school's commandant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content: Jordan Aviation (PSC) is an airline based in Amman, Jordan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content:  It operates worldwide charter flights, provides wet lease services to major airlines seeking additional capacity and is also an important provider of air transportation for UN peacekeeping forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content:  Its main base is Queen Alia International Airport (AMM/OJAM), Amman from where it operates its fleet of Wide Body and Narrow Body aircraft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content:  In addition it has its own MRO which forms part of is Operations & Technical Centre opened in October 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content:  Jordan Aviation is an IATA member and an IOSA certificated airline.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jordan Aviation
Content:  It is also a member of the Arab Air Carriers Organization – AACO, the International Air Carrier Association – IACA and the Flight Safety Foundation – [F

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enimex
Content: Enimex was an airline based in Tallinn, Estonia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enimex
Content:  It operated cargo and passenger charter flights, and wet lease operations worldwide.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enimex
Content:  Its main base was Lennart Meri Tallinn Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles de Gaulle Airport
Content: Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport (French: "Aéroport de Paris-Charles-de-Gaulle" , IATA: CDG, ICAO: LFPG ), also known as Roissy Airport (name of the local district), is the largest international airport in France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles de Gaulle Airport
Content:  It is named after Charles de Gaulle (1890–1970), leader of the Free French Forces during the Second World War, founder of the French Fifth Republic and President of France from 1959 to 1969.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles de Gaulle Airport
Content:  Charles de Gaulle Airport is located within portions of several communes 25 km to the northeast of Paris.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles de Gaulle Airport
Content:  Charles de Gaulle Airport serves as the principal hub for Air France as well as a European hub for fellow SkyTeam alliance partner Delta Air Lines.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles de Gaulle Airport
Content:  Additionally, the airport serves as a focus city for low-cost carriers Vueling and Norwegian Air Shuttle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eagle Aviation France
Content: Eagle Aviation France was a charter airline based in Saint-Nazaire, France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eagle Aviation France
Content:  Its wet lease operations were based in Paris at Charles de Gaulle Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content: Vildanden AS ("The Wild Duck") was a virtual, regional airline based at Skien Airport, Geiteryggen in Norway, where it was the only airline.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content:  With operations starting in 2005, it flew to Bergen, Trondheim and Stavanger using a Jetstream 32 and an ATR 42, which is wet leased from Danish Air Transport (DAT) and Helitrans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content:  Previously, the airline has also served Stockholm and Molde, and has also operated Saab 340 aircraft, operated by Coast Air, Air Aurora and Avitrans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content:  The airline hadbeen in conflict with Coast Air about terminating the wet lease agreement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content:  The company has had to be bailed out several times, including by the municipality, until it managed to make its first profit in 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vildanden (airline)
Content:  It ceased operations and filed for bankruptcy in January 2011.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content: Phuket Air (Phuket Airlines Co. Ltd) is an airline based in Bangkok, Thailand.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  The airline currently specialises in leasing its fleet of Boeing 747 and 737 aircraft on an ACMI (Aircraft, Crews, Maintenance, Insurance) as well as wet lease basis to airlines worldwide needing extra passenger capacity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  Other services provided include international charter services, "ad hoc" wet leasing services, religious pilgrimage charters (Hajj/Umrah) and dry lease services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  Saudi Arabian Airlines is a major long term client of Phuket Air.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  In the past, the airline provided scheduled domestic and international air services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  Its main base is at Suvarnabhumi Airport, Bangkok, with a hub at Phuket International Airport, Phuket.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phuket Air
Content:  The airline was an international scheduled carrier before it shifted its focus to the ACMI/wet lease market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Titan Airways
Content: Titan Airways is a British charter airline founded in 1988 and based at London Stansted Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Titan Airways
Content:  The carrier specialises in short notice ACMI and wet lease operations as well as ad-hoc passenger and cargo charter services to tour operators, corporations, governments and the sports and entertainment sectors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Titan Airways
Content:  The company holds a United Kingdom Civil Aviation Authority Type A Operating Licence, permitting it to carry passengers, cargo and mail on aircraft with 20 or more seats and currently operates a fleet of 10 aircraft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cargo 360
Content: Cargo 360 was a cargo airline based in Seattle, Washington, USA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cargo 360
Content:  It specialised in ACMI (Aircraft, Crew, Maintenance, and Insurance) wet lease operations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cargo 360
Content:  Its main headquarters was Seattle-Tacoma International Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: VIM Airlines
Content: VIM Airlines (legally "VIM Avia") is a Russian airline headquartered in Moscow based at Domodedovo International Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: VIM Airlines
Content:  It offers international scheduled and charter operations for both passengers and cargo as well as wet lease services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content: Bishop International Airport (IATA: FNT, ICAO: KFNT, FAA LID: FNT) is a commercial and general aviation airport located in Flint, Michigan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  It is named after banker and General Motors board member Arthur Giles Bishop (April 12, 1851 – January 22, 1944), who donated 220 acres of his farmland for the airport in 1928.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  The third busiest airport in Michigan, it surpassed competitor MBS International Airport in terms of airline operations in 2002.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  In 2007, 1,071,238 passengers used Bishop International Airport; in 2011, 938,914 passengers used the airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  It is included in the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) National Plan of Integrated Airport Systems for 2017–2021, in which it is categorized as a small hub primary commercial service facility.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  The airport is currently served by several passenger airlines: Allegiant Air, Southwest Airlines and Delta Air Lines operate mainline service out of the airport, as well as affiliates of Delta Connection, United Express and American Eagle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  Additionally, FedEx Express and a FedEx Feeder affiliate operate cargo services out of the airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  Accompanying the airlines is fixed-base operator Av Flight that handles both general aviation and airline operations and the flight school American Wings Aviation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bishop International Airport
Content:  Bishop International Airport is in southwestern Flint, and is surrounded by Flint Township to the north, east and west; and Mundy Township to the south.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Divorce Italian Style
Content: Divorce Italian Style (Italian: "Divorzio all'italiana" ) is a 1961 Italian comedy film directed by Pietro Germi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Divorce Italian Style

Title: Oasis discography

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Oasis discography
Content:  Oasis was formed in 1991 by vocalist Liam Gallagher, guitarist Paul "Bonehead" Arthurs, bassist Paul "Guigsy" McGuigan and drummer Tony McCarroll – they were later joined by guitarist and songwriter Noel Gallagher.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Liberal arts education
Content: The liberal arts (Latin: "artes liberales") are those subjects or skills that in classical antiquity were considered essential for a free person (Latin: "liberalis", "worthy of a free person") to know in order to take an active part in civic life, something that (for Ancient Greece) included participating in public debate, defending oneself in court, serving on juries, and most importantly, military service.

session_0.speaker: System
