session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Skelly Oil
Content:  Skelly Oil became part of Getty Oil Company, Mission Oil Company, Tidewater Oil Company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Skelly Oil
Content:  It became defunct when absorbed by Getty Oil Company in 1974, and the abandoned Skelly brand logo was revived by Nimmons-Joliet Development Corp. in 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Holding
Content: Robert Earl Holding (November 29, 1926 – April 19, 2013) was an American businessman who owned Sinclair Oil Corporation, the Little America Hotels, the Grand America Hotel, the Westgate Hotel in San Diego, California (directed by Georg Hochfilzer), and two ski resorts, Sun Valley in central Idaho since 1977, and Snowbasin near Ogden, Utah, since 1984.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahvaz Field
Content: The Ahvaz oil field is an Iranian oil field located in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahvaz Field
Content:  It was discovered in 1953 and developed by National Iranian Oil Company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahvaz Field
Content:  It began production in 1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahvaz Field
Content:  Ahvaz field is one of the richest oil fields in the world with an estimated proven reserves are around , and production is centered on 750000 oilbbl/d .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahvaz Field
Content:  The field is owned by state-owned National Iranian Oil Company (NIOC) and operated by National Iranian South Oil Company (NISOC).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little America, Wyoming
Content: Little America is a census-designated place (CDP) in Sweetwater County, Wyoming, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little America, Wyoming
Content:  The population was 68 at the 2010 census.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little America, Wyoming
Content:  The community got its name from the Little America motel, which was purposefully located in a remote location as a haven, not unlike the base camp the polar explorer Richard E. Byrd set up in the Antarctic in 1928.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little America, Wyoming
Content:  However, being situated on a coast-to-coast highway and offering travel services, it thrived, launching a chain of travel facilities by the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little America, Wyoming
Content:  Its developer, Robert Earl Holding, died on April 19, 2013, with a personal net worth of over $3 billion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aghajari oil field
Content: The Aghajari oil field is an iranian oil field located in Khuzestan Province.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aghajari oil field
Content:  It was discovered in 1938 and developed by National Iranian Oil Company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aghajari oil field
Content:  It began production in 1940 and produces oil.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aghajari oil field
Content:  The total proven reserves of the Aghajari oil field are around 30 billion barrels (3758×10tonnes), and production is centered on 300000 oilbbl/d .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aghajari oil field
Content:  The field is owned by state-owned National Iranian Oil Company (NIOC) and operated by National Iranian South Oil Company (NISOC).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content: Carabobo is an oil field located in Venezuela's Orinoco Belt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  As one of the world's largest accumulations of recoverable oil, the recent discoveries in the Orinoco Belt have led to Venezuela holding the world's largest recoverable reserves in the world, surpassing Saudi Arabia in July 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  The Carabobo oil field is majority owned by Venezuela's national oil company, Petroleos de Venezuela SA (PDVSA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  Owning the majority of the Orinoco Belt, and its estimated 1.18 trillion barrels of oil in place, PDVSA is now the fourth largest oil company in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  The field is well known for its extra Heavy crude oils, having an average specific gravity between 4 and 16 °API.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  The Orinoco Belt holds 90% of the world's extra heavy crude oils, estimated at 256 billion recoverable barrels.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carabobo Field
Content:  While production is in its early development, the Carabobo field is expected to produce 400,000 barrels of oil per day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinclair Oil Corporation
Content: Sinclair Oil Corporation is an American petroleum corporation, founded by Harry F. Sinclair on May 1, 1916, as the Sinclair Oil and Refining Corporation by combining the assets of 11 small petroleum companies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinclair Oil Corporation
Content:  Originally a New York corporation, Sinclair Oil reincorporated in Wyoming in 1976.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinclair Oil Corporation
Content:  The corporation's logo features the silhouette of a large green dinosaur.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 101 Ranch Oil Company
Content: Founded in 1908 by oil exploration pioneer E. W. Marland, The 101 Ranch Oil Company was located on the Miller Brothers 101 Ranch and headquartered in Ponca City, Oklahoma.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 101 Ranch Oil Company
Content:  The company’s 1911 oil discovery in North Eastern Oklahoma opened up oil development in a great region from Eastern Oklahoma west to Mervine, Newkirk, Blackwell, Billings and Garber and led to the founding of the Marland Oil Company, later renamed the Continental Oil Company, now known as Conoco.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rag Sefid oil field
Content: The Rag Sefid oil field is an oil field located in Khuzestan Province, approximately 6 km in nearest distance from the Persian Gulf, southwest Iran.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rag Sefid oil field
Content:  It was discovered in 1964 and developed by National Iranian Oil Company and began production in 1966.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rag Sefid oil field
Content:  The total proven reserves of the Rag Sefid oil field are around 14,5 billion barrels, and production is centered on 180000 oilbbl/d .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rag Sefid oil field
Content:  The field is owned by state-owned National Iranian Oil Company (NIOC) and operated by National Iranian South Oil Company (NISOC).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Very Best of Macy Gray
Content: The Very Best of Macy Gray is the first greatest hits album by American singer and songwriter Macy Gray, released on August 30, 2004 by Epic Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Very Best of Macy Gray
Content:  It contains all singles from Gray's first three studio albums, as well as two previously unreleased tracks (the single "Love Is Gonna Get You" and a cover of Aerosmith's 1975 song "Walk This Way"), three album tracks, three remixes, and the single "Demons", a collaboration with Fatboy Slim from his 2000 album "Halfway Between the Gutter and the Stars".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Very Best of Macy Gray
Content:  The album peaked at number 36 on the UK Albums Chart and charted moderately in other European countries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Behind the Player: Duff McKagan
Content: Behind The Player: Duff McKagan is an Interactive Music Video featuring Guns N' Roses and Velvet Revolver bassist Duff McKagan

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kissed It
Content: "Kissed It" is a song by the American soul singer Macy Gray.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kissed It
Content:  It is the second US single from her fifth album "The Sellout".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kissed It
Content:  The song was released digitally on May 24, 2010 in the United States and features the musicians of Velvet Revolver and Guns N' Roses, Slash, Duff McKagan and Matt Sorum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kissed It
Content:  In September 2010, the song peaked on the Italian Airplay Chart at number 62.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded (band)
Content: Loaded (also known as Duff McKagan's Loaded) is an American rock band from Seattle, Washington, formed in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded (band)
Content:  Since 2001, the band's line-up has included vocalist and rhythm guitarist Duff McKagan (Velvet Revolver and Guns N' Roses), lead guitarist Mike Squires (formerly of Nevada Bachelors and Alien Crime Syndicate) and bassist Jeff Rouse (formerly of Alien Crime Syndicate, Sirens Sister, and Vendetta Red).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded (band)
Content:  Since 2009, Isaac Carpenter (formerly of Loudermilk, Gosling, and The Exies) has been the band's drummer, replacing Geoff Reading (formerly of New American Shame and Green Apple Quick Step).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Velvet Revolver
Content: Velvet Revolver was an American hard rock supergroup consisting of former Guns N' Roses members Slash (lead guitar), Duff McKagan (bass, backing vocals), and Matt Sorum (drums, backing vocals), alongside Dave Kushner (rhythm guitar) formerly of punk band Wasted Youth and Scott Weiland formerly of Stone Temple Pilots.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Velvet Revolver
Content:  Weiland left the band to rejoin Stone Temple Pilots in 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seattlehead
Content: "Seattlehead" (also typeset Seattle Head) is a song written by American musician Duff McKagan more popularly known as a song by McKagan's band Loaded, from the album "Dark Days", but has also featured on earlier releases by Neurotic Outsiders as well as McKagan's unreleased solo album "Beautiful Disease".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded discography
Content: Loaded (also known as Duff McKagan's Loaded) is an American hard rock band from Seattle, Washington, formed in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded discography
Content:  Since 2001, the band's line-up has included vocalist and rhythm guitarist Duff McKagan (Velvet Revolver and formerly of Guns N' Roses), lead guitarist Mike Squires (formerly of Nevada Bachelors and Alien Crime Syndicate) and bassist Jeff Rouse (formerly of Alien Crime Syndicate, Sirens Sister and Vendetta Red).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded discography
Content:  Since 2009, Isaac Carpenter (formerly of Loudermilk, Gosling and The Exies) has been the band's drummer replacing Geoff Reading (formerly of New American Shame and Green Apple Quick Step).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Loaded discography
Content:  The band has released 3 studio albums, 1 live album, 1 extended play, 4 singles and 4 music videos.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Disease
Content: Beautiful Disease was to be the second solo album released by then ex-Guns N' Roses's bassist Duff McKagan in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Disease
Content:  However, it was shelved after a merger between McKagan's parent label Polygram and Universal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Demons (Fatboy Slim song)
Content: "Demons" is a song by English big beat musician Fatboy Slim, featuring Grammy Award-winning American R&B-soul singer Macy Gray.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Demons (Fatboy Slim song)
Content:  The song was released as a single from Slim's 2000 album "Halfway Between the Gutter and the Stars", and later appeared on Gray's 2004 greatest hits compilation "The Very Best of Macy Gray" as well as Slim's 2006 greatest hits compilation "The Greatest Hits - Why Try Harder".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Demons (Fatboy Slim song)
Content:  It contains elements of Bill Withers' 1973 song "I Can't Write Left-Handed".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Demons (Fatboy Slim song)
Content:  The gospel group The Blind Boys of Alabama covered the song on their 2005 album "Atom Bomb".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Demons (Fatboy Slim song)
Content:  Recently, the song was featured in the Netflix series Sense8.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neurotic Outsiders
Content: Neurotic Outsiders was a supergroup founded in 1995, consisting of Steve Jones of the Sex Pistols, Matt Sorum and Duff McKagan of Guns N' Roses, and John Taylor of Duran Duran.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neurotic Outsiders
Content:  The first line-up featured Billy Idol and Steve Stevens (together with McKagan and Sorum), but they were soon replaced by Jones and Taylor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neurotic Outsiders
Content:  The group was originally called Neurotic Boy Outsiders.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Linda Ronstadt
Content: Linda Maria Ronstadt (born July 15, 1946) is an American popular music and country music singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Linda Ronstadt
Content:  She has earned 11 Grammy Awards, three American Music Awards, two Academy of Country Music awards, an Emmy Award, and an ALMA Award, and many of her albums have been certified gold, platinum or multiplatinum in the United States and internationally.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Linda Ronstadt
Content:  She has also earned nominations for a Tony Award and a Golden Globe award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Linda Ronstadt
Content:  She was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in April 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Linda Ronstadt
Content:  On July 28, 2014, she was awarded the National Medal of Arts and Humanities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content: "They Call the Wind Maria" is an American popular song with lyrics written by Alan J. Lerner and music by Frederick Loewe for their 1951 Broadway musical, "Paint Your Wagon", which is set in the California Gold Rush.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content:  Rufus Smith originally sang the song on Broadway, and Joseph Leader was the original singer in London's West End.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content:  It quickly became a runaway hit, and during the Korean War, the song was among the "popular music listened to by the troops".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content:  Vaughan Monroe and his Orchestra recorded the song in 1951, and it was among the "popular hit singles at the record stores" that year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content:  It has since become a standard, performed by many notable singers across several genres of popular music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: They Call the Wind Maria
Content:  A striking feature of the song in the original orchestration (also used in many cover versions), is a driving, staccato rhythm, played on the string instruments, that evokes a sense of restless motion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eddy Arnold
Content: Richard Edward "Eddy" Arnold (May 15, 1918 – May 8, 2008) was an American country music singer who performed for six decades.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eddy Arnold
Content:  He was a Nashville sound (country/popular music) innovator of the late 1950s, and scored 147 songs on the "Billboard" country music charts, second only to George Jones.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eddy Arnold
Content:  He sold more than 85 million records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eddy Arnold
Content:  A member of the Grand Ole Opry (beginning 1943) and the Country Music Hall of Fame (beginning 1966), Arnold ranked 22nd on Country Music Television's 2003 list of "The 40 Greatest Men of Country Music."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albert Campbell (singer)
Content: Albert Charles Campbell (August 19, 1872 – January 25, 1947) was an American popular music singer who recorded between the late 1890s and the 1920s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albert Campbell (singer)
Content:  He was best known for his many duo recordings with Henry Burr, and as a member of the Peerless Quartet and other vocal groups, but also recorded successfully as a solo singer both under his own name and under various pseudonyms including Frank Howard.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: J. D. Souther
Content: John David Souther, known professionally as J.D. Souther (born November 2, 1945) is an American singer and songwriter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: J. D. Souther
Content:  He has written and co-written songs recorded by Linda Ronstadt and the Eagles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riddles in the Sand
Content: Riddles in the Sand is the thirteenth studio album by American popular music singer-songwriter Jimmy Buffett.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riddles in the Sand
Content:  It was released in September 1984 as MCA 5512 and was produced by noted country music producer Jimmy Bowen and represented a concerted shift toward a more country sound by Buffett.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riddles in the Sand
Content:  He appeared on the album's cover in typical country singer garb and promoted the album at Fan Fair country music festival in Nashville, Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riddles in the Sand
Content:  The album was originally to have been titled "Gulf and Western Music" reflecting the fusion of musical styles seen in much of Buffett's music often called gulf and western music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riddles in the Sand
Content:  In the album's liner notes, Jim Harrison says, "This album has a musical range expanding in an arc from Bob Wills to Bob Marley with the Gulf somehow always there."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Particular Harbour
Content: One Particular Harbour is the twelfth studio album by American popular music singer-songwriter Jimmy Buffett.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Particular Harbour
Content:  It was released in September 1983 as MCA 5447 and was produced by Buffett and Michael Utley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Particular Harbour
Content:  It was Buffett's first involvement producing an album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Particular Harbour
Content:  Stars On The Water was written by and a minor hit for country music songsmith Rodney Crowell and also covered by Texan country music singer George Strait on his 2001 album, "The Road Less Traveled".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content: Howard Andrew Williams (December 3, 1927 – September 25, 2012) was an American popular music singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  He recorded 44 albums in his career, 15 of which have been gold-certified and three of which have been platinum-certified.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  He was also nominated for six Grammy Awards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  He hosted "The Andy Williams Show", a television variety show, from 1962 to 1971, and numerous TV specials.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  "The Andy Williams Show" garnered three Emmy awards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  The Moon River Theatre in Branson, Missouri, is named after the song he is most known for singing—Johnny Mercer and Henry Mancini's "Moon River".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andy Williams
Content:  He sold more than 100 million records worldwide, including 10.5 million certified units in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jimmie Rodgers (pop singer)
Content: James Frederick Rodgers (born September 18, 1933, Camas, Washington) is an American popular music singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jimmie Rodgers (pop singer)
Content:  Rodgers had a brief run of mainstream popularity in the late 1950s with a string of crossover singles that ranked highly on the "Billboard Pop Singles", "Hot Country and Western Sides" and "Hot Rhythm and Blues Sides" charts; in the 1960s, Rodgers had more modest successes with adult contemporary music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Delmore Brothers
Content: Alton Delmore (December 25, 1908 – June 8, 1964) and Rabon Delmore (December 3, 1916 – December 4, 1952), billed as The Delmore Brothers, were country music pioneers and stars of the Grand Ole Opry in the 1930s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Delmore Brothers
Content:  The Delmore Brothers, together with other brother duos such as the Louvin Brothers, the Blue Sky Boys, the Monroe Brothers (Birch, Charlie and Bill Monroe), the McGee Brothers, and The Stanley Brothers, had a profound impact on the history of country music and American popular music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zeitschrift für celtische Philologie
Content: The Zeitschrift für celtische Philologie is an academic journal of Celtic studies, which was established in 1897 by the German scholars Kuno Meyer and Ludwig Christian Stern.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zeitschrift für celtische Philologie
Content:  It was the first journal devoted exclusively to Celtic languages and literature and the oldest significant journal of Celtic studies still in existence today.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zeitschrift für celtische Philologie
Content:  The emphasis is on (early) Irish language and literature and Continental Celtic languages, but other aspects of Celtic philology and literature (including modern literature) also receive attention.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Insular Celtic languages
Content: Insular Celtic languages are a group of Celtic languages that originated in Britain and Ireland, in contrast to the Continental Celtic languages of mainland Europe and Anatolia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Insular Celtic languages
Content:  All surviving Celtic languages are from the Insular Celtic group, including that which is now spoken in Continental Europe; the Continental Celtic languages are extinct.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Insular Celtic languages
Content:  The six Insular Celtic languages of modern times can be divided into:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexei Kondratiev
Content: Alexei Kondratiev (1949–2010) was an American author, linguist, and teacher of Celtic languages, folklore and culture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexei Kondratiev
Content:  He taught the Irish language and Celtic history at the Irish Arts Center in Manhattan, New York from 1985 until his death on May 28, 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexei Kondratiev
Content:  Nine editions of his book, "The Apple Branch", were published in English and Spanish between 1998 and 2004.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexei Kondratiev
Content:  At various times, he taught all six of the living Celtic languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic studies
Content: Celtic studies or Celtology is the academic discipline occupied with the study of any sort of cultural output relating to the Celtic people.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic studies
Content:  This ranges from linguistics, literature and art history, archaeology and history, the focus lying on the study of the various Celtic languages, living and extinct.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic studies
Content:  The primary areas of focus are the six Celtic languages currently in use: Irish, Scottish Gaelic, Manx, Welsh, Cornish, and Breton.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Continental Celtic languages
Content: The Continental Celtic languages are the Celtic languages, now extinct, that were spoken on the continent of Europe, as distinguished from the Insular Celtic languages of the British Isles and Brittany.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Continental Celtic languages
Content:  "Continental Celtic" is a geographic, not a linguistic, grouping of the ancient Celtic languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Continental Celtic languages
Content:  The Continental Celtic languages were spoken by the people known to Roman and Greek writers as "Keltoi", "Celtae", "Galli" and "Galatae".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Continental Celtic languages
Content:  These languages were spoken in an arc stretching across from Iberia in the west to the Balkans and Anatolia in the east.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic nations
Content: The Celtic nations are territories in western Europe where Celtic languages or cultural traits have survived.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic nations
Content:  The term "nation" is used in its original sense to mean a people who share a common identity and culture and are identified with a traditional territory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Journal of Celtic Linguistics
Content: The Journal of Celtic Linguistics is a peer-reviewed annual academic journal established in 1992 with the goal of encouraging and publishing original linguistic research in the Celtic languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Journal of Celtic Linguistics
Content:  The journal is published by the University of Wales Press, but has specialist editors in all six Celtic languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Journal of Celtic Linguistics
Content:  The current editor-in-chief, since volume 16, is Simon Rodway (Aberystwyth University), who replaced Graham Isaac (National University of Ireland, Galway).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content: Amazonian languages is the term used to refer to the indigenous languages of "Greater Amazonia."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  This area is significantly larger than the Amazon and extends from the Atlantic coast all the way to the Andes, while its southern border is usually said to be the Paraná.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  The region is inhabited by societies that share many cultural traits but whose languages are characterized by great diversity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  There are about 330 extant languages in Greater Amazonia, almost half of which have fewer than 500 speakers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  Meanwhile, only Guajiro has a six-digit number of speakers (about 300,000).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  Of the 330 total languages, about fifty are isolates, while the remaining ones belong to about 25 different families.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  Most of the posited families have few members.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  It is this distribution of many small and historically unrelated speech communities that makes Amazonia one of the most linguistically diverse regions in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  The precise reasons for this unusual diversity have not yet been conclusively determined, but it is noteworthy that Amazonian languages seem to have had fewer than 10,000 native speakers even before the invasion of European colonists wrought havoc on the societies by which they were spoken.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  Despite the large-scale diversity, the long-term contact among many of the languages of Greater Amazonia has created similarities between many neighboring languages that are not genetically related.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amazonian languages
Content:  The small tribes can speak English but that would be used as one of their secondary languages

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Celtic art
Content: Celtic art is associated with the peoples known as Celts; those who spoke the Celtic languages in Europe from pre-history through to the modern period, as well as the art of ancient peoples whose language is uncertain, but have cultural and stylistic similarities with speakers of Celtic languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pan Celtic Festival
Content: The Pan Celtic Festival (Irish: "Féile Pan Cheilteach" ) is a Celtic-language music festival held annually in the week following Easter, since its inauguration in 1971.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pan Celtic Festival
Content:  The first Pan Celtic Festival took place in Killarney, County Kerry, Ireland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pan Celtic Festival
Content:  Its aim is to promote the modern Celtic languages and cultures and artists from all six Celtic nations: Brittany, Cornwall, Ireland, Isle of Man, Scotland and Wales.

Title: Batman (score)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  According to the "Batman" DVD Special Edition, Elfman said that producer Jon Peters was not sure about him as a composer until Tim Burton made him play the main titles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  Elfman admitted he was stunned when Peters announced that the score would be released on its own album, as releasing a separate score album for a film was something that was rarely done in the 1980s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  Elfman's "The Batman Theme" went on to become an iconic piece.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  It served as the basis for the theme music of "", which premiered in 1992, although this was later changed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  Some parts of the Elfman score are also heard in "", "" and "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Batman (score)
Content:  Parts are also played in the queue, and on the station platform of Batman the Ride at various Six Flags theme parks.

Content:  Originally released on October 29, 1991, by Walt Disney Records, the album's first half – tracks 2 to 9 – generally contains the film's musical numbers, all of which were written by composer Alan Menken and lyricist Howard Ashman, while its latter half – tracks 10 to 14 – features its musical score, composed solely by Menken.

Title: Beautiful Young Minds
Content: Beautiful Young Minds was a documentary first shown at the BRITDOC Festival on 26 July 2007 and first broadcast on BBC 2 on 14 October 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Young Minds
Content:  The documentary follows the selection process and training for the U.K. team to compete in the 2006 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO), as well as the actual event in Slovenia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Young Minds
Content:  Many of the young mathematicians featured in the film had a form of autism, which the documentary links to mathematical ability.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Young Minds
Content:  The team goes on to win numerous medals at the IMO, including four silver and one bronze.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It was directed by Morgan Matthews, edited by Joby Gee and featured music by Sam Hooper.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beautiful Young Minds
Content:  The documentary inspired the 2014 film X+Y, which was also directed by Morgan Matthews, based on IMO participant Daniel Lightwing.

Title: Downtown Brooklyn Cultural District
Content: The Brooklyn Cultural District (formerly known as the BAM-Downtown Brooklyn Cultural District) is a $100 million development project that focuses on the arts, public spaces and affordable housing in Fort Greene, Brooklyn, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Brooklyn Cultural District
Content:  The project reflected the joint efforts of New York City's Economic Development Corporation, the Department of Cultural Affairs, the Department of Housing Preservation and Development, the Department of City Planning, and the Downtown Brooklyn Partnership to continue to develop the Brooklyn neighborhood area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Brooklyn Cultural District
Content:  Joining the area's longtime institutional stakeholders (BAM, the Brooklyn Museum and the Brooklyn Public Library) are new homes for Mark Morris Dance Group, Theatre for a New Audience (TFANA), UrbanGlass and BRIC Arts and the BAM's Fisher Building.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carlene LeFevre
Content: Carlene LeFevre is a competitive eater from Henderson, Nevada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carlene LeFevre
Content:  She and her husband, Rich LeFevre, are said to form the "First Family of Competitive Eating" in spite of having normal weights and ages around 60, and are both top ranked members of the International Federation of Competitive Eating.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carlene LeFevre
Content:  The childless couple has combined to take two of the top seven places in Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest in 2003, 2004, and 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carlene LeFevre
Content:  She is nicknamed "The Madam of Etiquette" for her relative degree of decorum while consuming mass quantities of food quickly.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carlene LeFevre
Content:  Her trademark technique is called the "Carlene Pop," in which she bounces up and down while eating to get the food to settle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content: Brooklyn Grange is a 2.5-acre organic urban rooftop farm in New York City, growing high quality vegetables and honey for local restaurants, markets, and community-supported agriculture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  The farms span across two rooftops, one on a 43,000 sq. ft. building straddling Astoria and Long Island City, and the other atop the Brooklyn Navy Yard – the world’s largest rooftop soil farm.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  Together, they produce over 40,000 lbs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  of organically-grown vegetables each year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  The Grange also operates New York City’s largest apiary, with over thirty naturally-managed honey beehives, which yields approximately 1,500 pounds of honey annually.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  It was started in the spring of 2010 by transplanted Wisconsinite Ben Flanner, now President and Head Farmer, with the help of Anastasia Plakias, current Vice President, and Gwen Schantz, current Chief Operating Officer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  The group took out loans, contributed their own money and found community investors to fund the project.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  The Brooklyn Navy Yard farm was financed in part by at $592,730 grant from the NYCDEP's Green Infrastructure Grant Program.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Grange
Content:  In addition to growing and distributing local vegetables and herbs, Brooklyn Grange provides urban farming and green roof consulting and installation services to clients worldwide and partner with numerous non-profit organizations throughout New York to promote healthy and strong local communities.

session_0.text:
Title: List of bus routes in Brooklyn
Content: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) operates a number of bus routes in Brooklyn, New York, United States; one minor route is privately operated under a city franchise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of bus routes in Brooklyn
Content:  Many of them are the direct descendants of streetcar lines (see list of streetcar lines in Brooklyn); the ones that started out as bus routes were almost all operated by the Brooklyn Bus Corporation, a subsidiary of the Brooklyn–Manhattan Transit Corporation, until the New York City Board of Transportation took over on June 5, 1940.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of bus routes in Brooklyn
Content:  Of the 55 local Brooklyn routes operated by the New York City Transit Authority, roughly 35 are the direct descendants of one or more streetcar lines, and most of the others were introduced in full or in part as new bus routes by the 1930s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of bus routes in Brooklyn
Content:  Only the eastern section of the B82 (then the B50), the B83, and the B84 were created by New York City Transit from scratch, in 1978, 1966, and 2013, respectively.

session_0.text:
Title: Sports in New York (state)
Content: New York has two Major League Baseball teams, the New York Yankees (based in the Bronx) and the New York Mets (based in Queens).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in New York (state)
Content:  New York is home to three National Hockey League franchises: the New York Rangers in Manhattan, the New York Islanders in Brooklyn and the Buffalo Sabres in Buffalo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in New York (state)
Content:  New York has two National Basketball Association teams, the New York Knicks in Manhattan, and the Brooklyn Nets in Brooklyn.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Sports in New York (state)
Content:  New York has one Major League Soccer team: New York City FC.

Content:  Although the New York Red Bulls represent the New York metropolitan area they play in Red Bull Arena, located in Harrison, New Jersey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content: Queens is the easternmost and largest in area of the five boroughs of New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  It is geographically adjacent to the borough of Brooklyn at the southwestern end of Long Island, and to Nassau County farther east on Long Island; in addition, Queens shares water borders with the boroughs of Manhattan and the Bronx.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  Coterminous with Queens County since 1899, the borough of Queens is the second-largest in population (after Brooklyn), with a census-estimated 2,333,054 residents in 2016, approximately 48% of them foreign-born.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  Queens County also is the second-most populous county in the U.S. state of New York, behind the neighboring borough of Brooklyn, which is coterminous with Kings County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  Queens is the fourth-most densely populated county among New York City's boroughs, as well as in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  If each of New York City's boroughs were an independent city, Queens also would be the nation's fourth most populous, after Los Angeles, Chicago, and Brooklyn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queens
Content:  Queens is the most ethnically diverse urban area in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content: Brooklyn Heights is an affluent residential neighborhood within the New York City borough of Brooklyn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  Originally referred to as Brooklyn Village, it has been a prominent area of Brooklyn since 1834.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  The neighborhood is noted for its low-rise architecture and its many brownstone rowhouses, most of them built prior to the Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  It also has an abundance of notable churches and other religious institutions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  Brooklyn's first art gallery, the Brooklyn Arts Gallery, was opened in Brooklyn Heights in 1958.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  In 1965, a large part of Brooklyn Heights was protected from unchecked development by the creation of the Brooklyn Heights Historic District, the first such district in New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Heights
Content:  The district was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1966.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Greene, Brooklyn
Content: Fort Greene is a neighborhood in the New York City borough of Brooklyn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Greene, Brooklyn
Content:  Part of Brooklyn Community Board 2 and served by the New York City Police Department's 88th Precinct, Fort Greene is listed on the New York State Registry and on the National Register of Historic Places, and is a New York City–designated Historic District.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fort Greene, Brooklyn
Content:  It is located in northwest Brooklyn in the area known as South Brooklyn, just across from Lower Manhattan and north of Prospect Park.

Content:  It is held each year on Independence Day at Nathan's Famous Corporation's original, and best-known restaurant at the corner of Surf and Stillwell Avenues in Coney Island, a neighborhood of Brooklyn, New York City.

Title: Adrian Mannarino

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Adrian Mannarino
Content:  He has a career-high singles ranking of world No. 27 (July 2015) and was the singles runner-up in three ATP World Tour tournaments - Auckland, Bogotá and Antalya.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  Mannarino has achieved victories over Stan Wawrinka, Milos Raonic, Juan Martín del Potro, Gilles Simon, Juan Mónaco, Philipp Kohlschreiber and Gaël Monfils.

session_0.text:
Title: 2010 US Open – Men's Singles
Content: Juan Martín del Potro was the defending champion, but chose not to participate this year, after undergoing a wrist operation in May and only starting to practice again in August.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2010 US Open – Men's Singles
Content:  Del Potro was the third man in the Open Era not to defend his US Open title, after Ken Rosewall in 1971 (due to conflicts between the World Championship Tennis (WCT) and the International Lawn Tennis Federation (ILTF)), and Pete Sampras in 2003 (who unofficially retired after the 2002 final).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2010 US Open – Men's Singles
Content:  For the first time in U.S. Open history, no American player was seeded in the top 8, this was reflected in the result.

Title: Juan Martín del Potro
Content: Juan Martín del Potro (] , born 23 September 1988), also known as Delpo is an Argentinian professional tennis player who is currently ranked world No. 24 in men's singles by the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro
Content:  His biggest achievement has been winning the 2009 US Open, defeating Rafael Nadal in the semifinal and 5-time defending champion Roger Federer in the final.

session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro
Content:  He was the first to defeat both Federer and Nadal during the same major and was the only man outside the Big Four to win a major between the 2005 French Open and the 2013 US Open, a span of 35 tournaments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro
Content:  He is also the second Argentine and the fifth-youngest man to win the US Open in the Open Era.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro
Content:  Other career highlights include winning the bronze medal in men's singles at the 2012 London Olympics and the silver medal at the 2016 Rio Olympics and being part of his country's successful Davis Cup team; but his career has also been hampered by a succession of wrist injuries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 US Open – Men's Singles
Content: Roger Federer was the five-time defending champion, but was defeated by Juan Martín del Potro in the final, 3–6, 7–6, 4–6, 7–6, 6–2.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 US Open – Men's Singles
Content:  This was del Potro's first major title.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro career statistics
Content: This is a list of the main career statistics of Argentine professional tennis player, Juan Martín del Potro.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro career statistics
Content:  To date, Del Potro has won 19 ATP singles titles, including one Grand Slam singles title at the 2009 US Open.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro career statistics
Content:  He was also the runner-up at the 2009 ATP World Tour Finals, a semi-finalist at the 2009 French Open and 2013 Wimbledon Championships, a quarterfinalist at the Australian Open in 2009 and 2012, a bronze medalist at the 2012 London Olympics, and a silver medalist at the 2016 Rio Olympics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Martín del Potro career statistics
Content:  On January 11, 2010, Del Potro achieved a career high singles ranking of world No. 4 for the first time.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: 2017 Laver Cup
Content: On 24 August 2016, Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal were the first of six players to confirm their participation for team Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2017 Laver Cup
Content:  On 15 May 2017, more than eight months later, Milos Raonic was the first of six players to confirm his participation for the World team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2017 Laver Cup
Content:  By 24 August 2017, all six players from each team had been chosen: Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal, Alexander Zverev, Marin Čilić, Dominic Thiem, and Tomáš Berdych for team Europe, and Milos Raonic, John Isner, Jack Sock, Sam Querrey, Juan Martín del Potro, and Denis Shapovalov for team World.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2017 Laver Cup
Content:  Shortly afterwards Raonic withdrew and was replaced by Nick Kyrgios.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2017 Laver Cup
Content:  Later Frances Tiafoe took the place of del Potro who had also withdrawn.

Title: An Na
Content: An Na (born 1972) is a South Korea-born American children's book author.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  Starting her career as a middle school English and History teacher, Na turned to writing novels after taking a young adult literature class while enrolled in an M.F.A. program at Vermont College of Fine Arts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  She gained success with her very first novel "A Step From Heaven", published by Front Street Press in 2001, which won the annual Michael L. Printz Award from the American Library Association recognizing the year's "best book written for teens, based entirely on its literary merit".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  It was also a finalist for the National Book Award, Young People's Literature, and later found its way onto numerous "best book" lists.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  Na still makes frequent visits to middle schools to talk about her works and encourages young Asian-American students to become artists and harness their creativity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  She cites Frank McCourt's "Angela's Ashes" and Sandra Cisneros's "The House on Mango Street" among the influences on her writing and also admires the work of Madeleine L'Engle and of her first writing teacher, Jacqueline Woodson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: An Na
Content:  She divides her time between Oakland, California and Warren, Vermont.

Content: Founded in 2001, the Christian Educational Consortium (CEC) is a private school in Louisville, Kentucky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  A collegiate style school for Christian home educated students, CEC classes are offered to grades 6-12.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christian Educational Consortium
Content:  There are a total of eight 90-min to 120 min.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christian Educational Consortium
Content:  periods, four on Tuesday and four on Wednesday.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.text:
