session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Also in 1969, a new junior high school was built, and Patrick Henry opened that school year as a senior high school serving students in grades ten through twelve.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  When the junior high school was changed to a middle school in 1988, Patrick Henry became a high school enrolling students in grades nine through twelve.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  The school campus of West Patrick Henry Road, which consists of a complex of buildings, began as a campus style school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Additions of an auditorium, classrooms, cafeteria, new gymnasium, and renovations to the media center and administrative offices resulted in an all-enclosed facility in 1992.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  As the population and the needs of the school have changed, so have the dimensions of the school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  A new addition/renovation was added to the facility in the fall of 2001 providing state-of-the-art career and technical education opportunities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  This addition consisted of a broadcasting studio, a bio-technology lab, a communication technology center, a computer-assisted drafting lab, and three classrooms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Patrick Henry celebrated its 50th anniversary in September 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Patrick Henry High has an International Baccalaureate program, as well as a NJROTC program.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Patrick Henry High is especially known for its NJROTC program that is consistently ranked among the top in the state of Virginia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  During the 2010-2011 school year, a program called Rachel's Challenge was introduced.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Henry High School (Ashland, Virginia)
Content:  Patrick Henry High is also noted for its theatre program, being the best in the county, and taken most seriously.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Barnes (bowler)
Content: Chris Barnes (born February 25, 1970 in Topeka, Kansas) is an American professional bowler currently on the Professional Bowlers Association (PBA) Tour.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Barnes (bowler)
Content:  He attended Topeka High School, and then bowled collegiately at Wichita State University, where he earned a Bachelor of Arts degree in Business Management.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Barnes (bowler)
Content:  He was a member of Team USA for four years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content: The history of Over-the-Rhine is almost deep as the history of Cincinnati.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content:  Over-the-Rhine's built environment has undergone many cultural and demographic changes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content:  The toponym "Over-the-Rhine" is a reference to the Miami and Erie Canal as the Rhine of Ohio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content:  An early reference to the canal as "the Rhine" appears in the 1853 book "White, Red, Black", in which traveler Ferenc Pulszky wrote, "The Germans live all together across the Miami Canal, which is, therefore, here jocosely called the 'Rhine.' "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content:  In 1875 writer Daniel J. Kenny referred to the area exclusively as "Over the Rhine."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of Over-the-Rhine
Content:  He noted, "Germans and Americans alike love to call the district 'Over the Rhine.' "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content: Carrier Canal is an irrigation canal in Kern County, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content:  It originates from a common diversion at Manor Street in Bakersfield, which also supplies the Kern Island Canal and Eastside Canal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content:  The common diversion originates from the Kern River about 1 mi south of Gordon's Ferry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content:  There are additional diversions from the Kern River at Golden State Highway (SR 204 freeway) and Coffee Road.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content:  The canal terminates at the Kern River, near Enos Lane west of Bakersfield.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carrier Canal
Content:  For its entire length, it runs roughly parallel to the Kern River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dundee Canal
Content: The Dundee Canal was an industrial canal in Clifton and Passaic in Passaic County, New Jersey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dundee Canal
Content:  It was built between 1858 and 1861 and ran parallel to the Passaic River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dundee Canal
Content:  It supplied hydropower and water for manufacturing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dundee Canal
Content:  There was interest by some members of the business community to modify the canal to support navigational uses, but the canal was never used for that purpose.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kern Island Canal
Content: Kern Island Canal is an irrigation canal in Kern County, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kern Island Canal
Content:  It primarily irrigates farmland located on the Kern Lakebed, south of Bakersfield.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kern Island Canal
Content:  It originates from a common diversion at Manor Street in Bakersfield, which also supplies the Carrier Canal and Eastside Canal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kern Island Canal
Content:  The common diversion originates from the Kern River about 1 mi south of Gordon's Ferry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Miami Canal
Content: The Miami Canal, or C-6 Canal, flows from Lake Okeechobee in the U.S. state of Florida to its terminus at the Miami River, which flows through downtown Miami.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Miami Canal
Content:  The canal flows in a south and southeasterly direction for approximately 77 miles, and passes through three counties: Broward, Palm Beach, and Miami-Dade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Miami Canal
Content:  It was constructed in the early part of the 20th century to drain the Everglades Agricultural Area (EAA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orleans Canal
Content: The Orleans Canal is a drainage canal in New Orleans, Louisiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orleans Canal
Content:  The canal, along with the 17th Street Canal and the London Avenue Canal, form the New Orleans Outfall Canals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orleans Canal
Content:  The current version of the canal is about 2 km long, running along the up-river side of City Park, through the Lakeview and Lakeshore neighborhood, and into Lake Pontchartrain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orleans Canal
Content:  It is part of the system used to pump rain water out of the streets of the city into the Lake.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orleans Canal
Content:  The Canal has also been known as the Orleans Avenue Canal, the Orleans Outfall Canal, the Orleans Tail Race, and early on, the Girod Canal,

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karakum Canal
Content: The Karakum Canal (Qaraqum Canal, Kara Kum Canal, Garagum Canal; Russian: Каракумский канал , "Karakumsky Kanal", Turkmen: Garagum kanaly , گَرَگوُم كَنَلیٛ, "Гарагум каналы") in Turkmenistan is one of the largest irrigation and water supply canals in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karakum Canal
Content:  Started in 1954, and completed in 1988, it is navigable over much of its 1375 km length, and carries 13 km3 of water annually from the Amu-Darya River across the Karakum Desert in Turkmenistan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karakum Canal
Content:  The canal opened up huge new tracts of land to agriculture, especially to cotton monoculture heavily promoted by the Soviet Union, and supplying Ashgabat with a major source of water.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karakum Canal
Content:  Unfortunately, the primitive construction of the canal allows almost 50 percent of the water to escape en route, creating lakes and ponds along the canal, and a rise in groundwater leading to widespread soil salinization problems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karakum Canal
Content:  The canal is also a major factor leading to the Aral Sea environmental disaster.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New River (Broward County, Florida)
Content: The New River is a tidal estuary in South Florida, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New River (Broward County, Florida)
Content:  The river is connected to the Everglades through a series of man made canals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New River (Broward County, Florida)
Content:  After passing through Fort Lauderdale, the river connects to the Atlantic Ocean at Port Everglades cut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New River (Broward County, Florida)
Content:  The river is entirely within Broward County and is composed from the junction of three main canals which originate in the Everglades, splitting off from the Miami Canal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New River (Broward County, Florida)
Content:  They are the North New River Canal, which flows on the north side of State Road 84 / Interstate 595; the South New River Canal, which flows on the north side of Griffin Road and the south side of Orange Drive; and a canal which flows south of Sunrise Boulevard.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content: The Golovnaya Dam is an earth-fill embankment dam on the Vakhsh River just east of Sarband in Khatlon Province, Tajikistan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  It serves to provide water to a system of irrigation canals and generate hydroelectric power.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  The first generator was commissioned in 1962 and the last in 1963.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  Between 1984 and 1989 three of the Kaplan turbines were upgraded from 35 MW to 45 MW.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  Two of the turbines in the 240 MW power station discharge water into a canal on the left bank of the river.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  Water from this canal serves to irrigate but also supplies the 29.9 MW Perepadnaya and 15.1 MW Centralnaya Hydroelectric Power Plants located further down.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golovnaya Dam
Content:  The reservoir has a design storage volume of 96000000 m3 by an estimated 80 percent of this is now silt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content: The Riverside Canal is an irrigation canal in El Paso County beginning southeast of El Paso, Texas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  The canal acquires water from the Riverside Diversion Dam on the Rio Grande 15 mi southeast of El Paso.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  The canal is managed by the US Bureau of Reclamation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  The canal extends for 17.2 mi with a capacity of 900 cubic feet per second.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  Water from the canal irrigates about 39,000 acres (160 km²).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  The canal and diversion dam is the southernmost system on an irrigation project extending along the Rio Grande in New Mexico and Texas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Riverside Canal (El Paso)
Content:  The canal supplies a canal network extending throughout the Upper Rio Grande Valley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Hot Five and Hot Seven Sessions
Content: The Louis Armstrong Hot Five and Hot Seven Sessions were recorded between 1925 and 1928 by Louis Armstrong with his Hot Five and Hot Seven groups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Hot Five and Hot Seven Sessions
Content:  According to the National Recording Registry, "Louis Armstrong was jazz's first great soloist and is among American music's most important and influential figures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Hot Five and Hot Seven Sessions
Content:  These sessions, his solos in particular, set a standard musicians still strive to equal in their beauty and innovation."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Hot Five and Hot Seven Sessions
Content:  These recordings were added to the National Recording Registry in 2002, the first year of the institution's existence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ella Fitzgerald
Content: Ella Jane Fitzgerald (April 25, 1917 – June 15, 1996) was an African - American jazz singer often referred to as the First Lady of Song, Queen of Jazz and Lady Ella.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ella Fitzgerald
Content:  She was noted for her purity of tone, impeccable diction, phrasing and intonation, and a "horn-like" improvisational ability, particularly in her scat singing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport (IATA: MSY, ICAO: KMSY, FAA LID: MSY) is an international airport in Jefferson Parish, Louisiana, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content:  It is owned by the city of New Orleans and is 11 miles west of downtown New Orleans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content:  The airport's address is 900 Airline Drive in Kenner, Louisiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content:  A small portion of Runway 11/29 is in unincorporated St. Charles Parish.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content:  Armstrong International is the primary commercial airport for the New Orleans metropolitan area and southeast Louisiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport
Content:  The airport was formerly known as Moisant Field, and it is also known as Louis Armstrong International Airport and New Orleans International Airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heebie Jeebies (composition)
Content: "Heebie Jeebies" is a composition written by Boyd Atkins which achieved fame when it was recorded by Louis Armstrong in 1926.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heebie Jeebies (composition)
Content:  The recording on Okeh Records by Louis Armstrong and his Hot Five includes a famous example of scat singing by Armstrong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content: "Would You Like to Take a Walk?"

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  is a popular song with music by Harry Warren and lyrics by Mort Dixon and Billy Rose.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  It appeared in the Broadway show "Sweet and Low" starring James Barton, Fannie Brice and George Jessel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  The song was published in 1930 by Remick Music Corporation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  The song has become a pop standard, recorded by many artists including Rudy Vallee in 1931, Annette Hanshaw in 1931 , and Bing Crosby.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  It plays in the 1939 Porky Pig cartoon "Naughty Neighbors" and the 1942 Daffy Duck cartoon "The Daffy Duckaroo".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  Ella Fitzgerald and Louis Armstrong recorded the song for Decca in 1951, accompanied by the Dave Barbour Orchestra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Would You Like to Take a Walk?
Content:  It was later included on Ella's Decca album "Ella and Her Fellas".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Plays W.C. Handy
Content: Louis Armstrong Plays W. C. Handy is a 1954 studio release by Louis Armstrong and His All Stars, described by Allmusic as "Louis Armstrong's finest record of the 1950s" and "essential music for all serious jazz collections".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong Plays W.C. Handy
Content:  Columbia CD released the album on CD in 1986 in a much altered form, with alternative versions in place of many of the original songs, but restored the original with its 1997 re-issue, which also included additional tracks: a brief interview by the producer, George Avakian, with W. C. Handy; a joke told by Louis Armstrong; and several rehearsal versions of the songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong and His Hot Seven
Content: Louis Armstrong and his Hot Seven was a jazz studio group organized to make a series of recordings for Okeh Records in Chicago, Illinois, in May 1927.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong and His Hot Seven
Content:  Some of the personnel also recorded with Louis Armstrong and His Hot Five, including Johnny Dodds (clarinet), Lil Armstrong (piano), and Johnny St. Cyr (banjo and guitar).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong and His Hot Seven
Content:  These musicians were augmented by Dodds's brother, Baby Dodds (drums), Pete Briggs (tuba), and John Thomas (trombone, replacing Armstrong's usual trombonist, Kid Ory, who was then touring with King Oliver).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Louis Armstrong and His Hot Seven
Content:  Briggs and Thomas were at the time working with Armstrong's performing group, the Sunset Stompers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content: "Saint Louis Blues" is a popular American song composed by W. C. Handy in the blues style and published in September 1914.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  It remains a fundamental part of jazz musicians' repertoire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  It was also one of the first blues songs to succeed as a pop song.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  It has been performed by numerous musicians in various styles, including Louis Armstrong, Bessie Smith, Count Basie, Glenn Miller, Guy Lombardo, and the Boston Pops Orchestra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  It has been called "the jazzman's "Hamlet"."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  The 1925 version sung by Bessie Smith, with Louis Armstrong on cornet, was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1993.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Louis Blues (song)
Content:  The 1929 version by Louis Armstrong & His Orchestra (with Red Allen) was inducted in 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Potato Head Blues
Content: "Potato Head Blues" is a Louis Armstrong composition regarded as one of his finest recordings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Potato Head Blues
Content:  It was made by Louis Armstrong and his Hot Seven for Okeh Records in Chicago, Illinois on May 10, 1927.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Potato Head Blues
Content:  It was recorded during a remarkably productive week in which Armstrong's usual Hot Five was temporarily expanded to seven players by the addition of tuba and drums; over five sessions the group recorded twelve sides.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Danny Barcelona
Content: Danny Barcelona (July 23, 1929 – April 1, 2007) was a jazz drummer best known for his years with Louis Armstrong's All-Stars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Danny Barcelona
Content:  He was a Filipino-American born in Waipahu, a community of Honolulu, Hawaii.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Danny Barcelona
Content:  He was also frequently introduced to audiences by Louis Armstrong as The Little Filipino Boy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Danny Barcelona
Content:  Armstrong usually followed up by calling himself "the little Arabian boy".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Faces
Content: Seven Faces is a 1929 American pre-Code drama film with fantasy elements that was released by Fox Film Corporation in the Fox Movietone sound-on-film system on December 1, 1929.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Faces
Content:  Based upon the piece of short fiction "A Friend of Napoleon" which was published in the June 30, 1923, issue of "The Saturday Evening Post" magazine by popular writer Richard Connell (whose best known work, "The Most Dangerous Game", was filmed three years later), it was directed by Berthold Viertel and stars Paul Muni in his second screen appearance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Faces
Content:  "Seven Faces" is a lost film, with no excerpts from its footage known to exist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Burns (paranormal investigator)
Content: Patrick Burns (born 1968) is an American paranormal investigator, best known as star of the TruTV (formerly Court TV) series "Haunting Evidence".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Burns (paranormal investigator)
Content:  He is the founder of the popular website Ghost Hounds, which in 2001 was featured in an Emmy award-winning Turner documentary "Interact Atlanta - 'Ghost Hounds' ".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Burns (paranormal investigator)
Content:  Burns is also the organizer and director of Ghostock, paranormal enthusiast events held at various locations across the USA, and is a professional photographer through Patrick Burns Photography.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thrill of a Romance
Content: Thrill of a Romance (also known as "Thrill of a New Romance") was an American romance film released by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer in 1945, starring Van Johnson, Esther Williams and Carleton G. Young, with musical performances by opera singer Lauritz Melchior.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thrill of a Romance
Content:  The film was directed by Richard Thorpe and written by Richard Connell and Gladys Lehman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Most Dangerous Game
Content: "The Most Dangerous Game", also published as "The Hounds of Zaroff", is a short story by Richard Connell, first published in "Collier's" on January 19, 1924.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Most Dangerous Game
Content:  The story features a big-game hunter from New York City who falls off a yacht and swims to an isolated island in the Caribbean, where he is hunted by a Russian aristocrat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Most Dangerous Game
Content:  The story is inspired by the big-game hunting safaris in Africa and South America that were particularly fashionable among wealthy Americans in the 1920s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content: Cuthy Mede is a Malawian artist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Lonely Planet said "possibly the best-known [Malawian] artist is Cuthy Mede – he is also actively involved in the development and promotion of Malawian art within the country and around the world."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Cuthy Mede grew up on Likoma Island, Lake Malawi where he drew in the rough sands of the beach as a child.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Later he studied Fine Art in Chancellor College and became a lecturer at the College in the 1970s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  By the 1980s Mede established Gallerie Africaine in Lilongwe City Centre, the first art gallery by a local artist in Malawi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede exhibited his work widely in Malawi, becoming a successful artist selling his work to international collectors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede encouraged the work of young Malawian artists struggling to make a living selling folk art and wood carvings as street traders.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  He also brought fine art work from other Malawian artists into his Gallery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  He was commissioned to paint a large mural decorating the City Centre.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede is best known for his modern art styles: modern, futurist, cubist and pointillist, with strong local themes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  His paintings depicted local people, historic events and current events in Malawi, Biblical references with local interpretations, indigenous religious expressions, and paintings about ideas such as Justice, Greed, Man and Machine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  His paintings depict famine, refugees from Mozambique during the Civil War, voting and democracy, wedding celebration, spirits and possession, and the Nyau masquerade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede's less known work is realistic, including a reproduction of the Mona Lisa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  His best known work is dominated by bright primary colors, cubist style, though his pointillist work favors ochres and softer tones in the overall effect.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  In later years Mede painted mostly in shades of blue, then white on white, the purest light.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede is an evangelical Christian and his work begins with a point of light from which the rest of the painting flows, the energy from God.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  This point of light is evident in most of his paintings as a single dot, a sun or moon, or an orb.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Best known for his paintings, Mede also produced sculptural forms such as wood figures covered in beads and pigments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  His garden in Lilongwe was made into a work of art, in white and light, with fluorescent light tubes hanging from trees and white painted rocks lining the drive and entry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cuthy Mede
Content:  Mede's wife, Esther (deceased 2009), served as Principal Secretary for the Ministry of Research and Environmental Affairs in the Malawi government.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Richard P. Gabriel
Content: Richard P. Gabriel (born 1949) is an American computer scientist who is known for his work related to the Lisp programming language (and especially Common Lisp) in computing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Richard P. Gabriel
Content:  His best known work was a 1990 essay “Lisp: Good News, Bad News, How to Win Big”, which incorporated the phrase Worse is Better, and his set of Lisp benchmarks (the "Gabriel Benchmarks"), published in 1985 as "Performance and evaluation of Lisp systems", which became a standard way of benchmarking Lisp implementations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. P. Thompson
Content: Edward Palmer Thompson (3 February 1924 – 28 August 1993), usually cited as E. P. Thompson, was a British historian, writer, socialist and peace campaigner.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. P. Thompson
Content:  He is probably best known today for his historical work on the British radical movements in the late 18th and early 19th centuries, in particular "The Making of the English Working Class" (1963).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. P. Thompson
Content:  He also published influential biographies of William Morris (1955) and (posthumously) William Blake (1993) and was a prolific journalist and essayist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. P. Thompson
Content:  He also published the novel "The Sykaos Papers" and a collection of poetry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. P. Thompson
Content:  His work is considered to have been among the most important contributions to labour history and social history in the latter twentieth-century, with a global impact, including on scholarship in Asia and Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LRRC (Luddite Rural Recording Cooperative)
Content: LRRC (Luddite Rural Recording Cooperative) was an online music mail-order and CDR/vinyl-focused independent record label run by Indiana lo-fi musician Joseph O'Connell, best known for his band Elephant Micah.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LRRC (Luddite Rural Recording Cooperative)
Content:  In addition to some of O'Connell's own work, the label released music by Elephant Micah collaborator Jason Henn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LRRC (Luddite Rural Recording Cooperative)
Content:  The LRRC online store also stocked a variety of what O'Connell called "Homemade Music from Kentuckiana and Abroad," including recordings by regional underground bands like Vollmar, Mt. Gigantic, and Bronze Float.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content: Andrea Alpago (c. 1450 – late 1521 or January 1522) was an Italian physician and arabist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  In publications of his work in Latin his name is frequently given as Andreas Alpagus Bellunensis, where "Bellunensis" refers to his birthplace of Belluno in northeastern Italy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  He worked in Damascus in Syria for decades as physician to the consulate of Republic of Venice in Damascus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  He was appointed professor of medicine in Padua in northeastern Italy in 1521, where he taught for only two or three months before his death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  None of his works were published during his lifetime; after his death they were published on the initiative of his nephew Paolo Alpago.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  His best known work is his commentary and editing of the Latin translation of "The Canon of Medicine" of Ibn Sina.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  This medicine book was translated from Arabic to Latin in the late 12th century by Gerard of Cremona.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  Andrea Alpago's edition and supplements to Gerard of Cremona's translation was widely read in European medical circles during the 16th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andrea Alpago
Content:  It was first published in 1527 and an expanded edition was published in 1544.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton
Content: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton (24 October 1856 – 31 March 1914), sometimes using the pen name Oliphant Smeaton, was a Scottish writer, journalist, editor, historian and educator.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton
Content:  He was popularly known for his writing on Australian life and literature for various British publications as well as for his adventure and children's fiction novels during the 1890s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton
Content:  Later in his career, Smeaton also published books on Scottish antiquities and edited English literary text, ballads and collections of verse and prose.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton
Content:  His best known work, "The Life and Works of William Shakespeare" (1911), was especially successful and enjoyed several reprints.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Henry Oliphant Smeaton
Content:  He also contributed several biographies for the "Famous Scots Series" published by Oliphant, Anderson and Ferrier.

Content: Springer Protocols is a database of life sciences protocols published by Springer Science+Business Media.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springer Protocols
Content:  These "recipes" allow scientists to recreate experiments in their own laboratory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springer Protocols
Content:  Springer Protocols contains more than 33,000 protocols, most of which are derived from the book series Methods in Molecular Biology, published under the Humana Press imprint.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springer Protocols
Content:  That book series, edited by John M. Walker since 1984, contains more than 1,100 volumes and has spawned several related book series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springer Protocols
Content:  "Springer Protocols" replaced "BioMed Protocols", a Humana Press database, in January 2008.

Content: Jesse Stone: Thin Ice is a 2009 American television crime drama film directed by Robert Harmon and starring Tom Selleck, Kathy Baker, and Kohl Sudduth.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jesse Stone: Thin Ice
Content:  Based on the characters from the Jesse Stone book series created by Robert B. Parker, the film is about the police chief of a small New England town who investigates a cryptic letter sent to the mother of a kidnapped child who was declared dead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Filmed on location in Nova Scotia, the story is set in the fictitious town of Paradise, Massachusetts.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  Much of his work is firmly rooted in the landscape, history and folklore of his native county of Cheshire, North West England, being set in the region and making use of the native Cheshire dialect.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Set in eastern Cheshire, they feature one day each in the life of four generations of Garner's family and they span more than a century.

Title: Wimbledon Effect
Content: The Wimbledon effect (Japanese: ウィンブルドン現象, rōmaji: "Uinburudon Genshō", literally "Wimbledon Phenomenon") is a chiefly British and Japanese analogy (which possibly originated in Japan) which compares the tennis fame of the Wimbledon Championships, held at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club in Wimbledon, London, with the economic success of the United Kingdom's financial services industries – especially those clustered in the City of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wimbledon Effect
Content:  The point of the analogy is that a national and international institution (the All England Club) can be highly successful despite the lack of strong native competition, as in modern tennis Britain has produced very few Wimbledon champions, with only Ann Haydon Jones, Virginia Wade (both women's singles), Jonathan Marray (men's doubles), Andy Murray (men's singles), John Lloyd, Jeremy Bates, Jo Durie, Jamie Murray and Heather Watson (mixed doubles) winning titles in the Open Era.

session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content: Zea nicaraguensis is a true grass species in the genus "Zea".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content:  It is considered to be phenotypically the most distinctive, as well as the most threatened teosinte.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content:  This teosinte thrives in flooded conditions along 200 m of a coastal estuarine river in northwest Nicaragua.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content:  Virtually all populations of teosinte are either threatened or endangered: "Z. nicaraguensis" survives as about 6000 plants in an area of 200 x 150 m.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content:  The Mexican and Nicaraguan governments have taken action in recent years to protect wild teosinte populations, using both "in situ" and "ex situ" conservation methods.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea nicaraguensis
Content:  Currently, a large amount of scientific interest exists in conferring beneficial teosinte traits, such as insect resistance, perennialism, and flood tolerance, to cultivated maize lines, although this is very difficult due to linked deleterious teosinte traits.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea (plant)
Content: Zea is a genus of plants in the grass family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea (plant)
Content:  The best-known species is "Z. mays", variously called maize, corn, or Indian corn, one of the most important crops for human societies over much of the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea (plant)
Content:  Several wild species are commonly known as teosintes and are native to Mesoamerica.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content: Zea perennis, the perennial teosinte, is a true grass species in the genus "Zea" and a teosinte.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  It is one of the two perennial species in the genus "Zea".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  The other perennial, "Z. diploperennis," is the sister taxon of "Z. perennis".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  Those two species also form a clade with "Z. luxurians".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  Together, the three species make up the "Luxuriantes" section in the genus "Zea".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  "Z. perennis" is the sole tetraploid in the genus and fertile hybrids with diploid "Zea" species are rare.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  Ribosomal ITS evidence suggested introgression between "Z. perennis" and "Z. mays" that must have come from either crossing the ploidy barrier or been from the diploid ancestral pool.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zea perennis
Content:  "Z. perennis" is generally considered to be an autotetraploid from some ancestral population of "Z. diploperennis".

Content: Microplitis croceipes is a braconid wasp native to the US state of Georgia.

Content:  It is an important parasitoid of caterpillars, including those of major agricultural pests "Helicoverpa zea" (formerly called "Heliothis zea") and "Heliothis virescens".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Disease (song)
Content:  "Disease" was one of two songs written and presented to Mick Jagger, by Rob Thomas in which Jagger actually gives back the song, saying "it would sound better coming from you".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content: The songwriting partnership of Mick Jagger and Keith Richards, known as Jagger/Richards (and occasionally Richards/Jagger), is a musical collaboration whose output has produced the majority of the catalogue of the Rolling Stones.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content:  It is one of the most successful songwriting partnerships in history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content:  Jagger and Richards adopted the nickname "The Glimmer Twins" after a vacation cruise they took to Brazil in December 1968/January 1969 with their then-girlfriends, Marianne Faithfull and Anita Pallenberg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content:  An older English couple on the ship kept asking Richards and Jagger who they were.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content:  When they refused to reveal their identities, the woman reportedly kept asking, "just give us a glimmer" (as in "give us a hint about who you are"), which amused Jagger and Richards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jagger/Richards
Content:  In addition to Jagger and Richards's songwriting partnership, they have also produced or co-produced numerous Rolling Stones albums under the pseudonym The Glimmer Twins, after that experience together.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Memo from Turner
Content: "Memo from Turner" is a solo record by Mick Jagger, featuring the slide guitar by Ry Cooder, from the soundtrack of "Performance", in which Jagger played the leading role of Turner, a reclusive rock star.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Memo from Turner
Content:  It was re-released in October 2007 on a seventeen-song retrospective compilation album "The Very Best of Mick Jagger", making a re-appearance as a Jagger solo effort.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Memo from Turner
Content:  After its original release in 1970, it had been included on Rolling Stones compilations, such as "" as a track credited to the Jagger/Richards songwriting partnership.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Memo from Turner
Content:  "Memo from Turner" was ranked #92 in the 100 Greatest Guitar Songs list of "Rolling Stone".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mick Jagger
Content: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, MBE (born 26 July 1943) is an English singer-songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, and composer, who gained fame as the lead singer and one of the founder members of the Rolling Stones (1962-present).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mick Jagger
Content:  Jagger's career has spanned over five decades, and he has been described as "one of the most popular and influential frontmen in the history of rock & roll".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mick Jagger
Content:  His distinctive voice and performance, along with Keith Richards' guitar style, have been the trademark of the Rolling Stones throughout the career of the band.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mick Jagger
Content:  Jagger gained press notoriety for his admitted drug use and romantic involvements, and was often portrayed as a countercultural figure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elizabeth Jagger
Content: Elizabeth Scarlett "Lizzy" Jagger (born March 2, 1984) is an American-English model and actress.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elizabeth Jagger
Content:  Born in New York City, she is the eldest daughter of Mick Jagger and Jerry Hall.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elizabeth Jagger
Content:  She is the sister of James Leroy Augustin Jagger (born 1985), Georgia May Ayeesha Jagger (born 1992) and Gabriel Luke Beauregard Jagger (born 1997), and paternal half-sister of Karis Hunt Jagger (born 1970), Jade Sheena Jezebel Jagger (born 1971), Lucas Maurice Morad-Jagger (born 1999), and Deveraux Octavian Basil Jagger (born 2016).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joy (Mick Jagger song)
Content: "Joy" was the second track from English singer-songwriter Mick Jagger's fourth solo album, "Goddess in the Doorway" (2001). "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joy (Mick Jagger song)
Content: Rolling Stone" called it "a rocking, gospel-tinged collaboration with Bono of U2" - featuring Pete Townshend on guitar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joy (Mick Jagger song)
Content:  "Joy" was one of three tracks from "Goddess in the Doorway" to be featured within Jagger's greatest hits album, "The Very Best of Mick Jagger".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stray Cat Blues
Content: "Stray Cat Blues" is the eighth song on the Rolling Stones' album "Beggars Banquet".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stray Cat Blues
Content:  It was written by Mick Jagger and Keith Richards and produced by Jimmy Miller.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stray Cat Blues
Content:  Miller's production of the song is very representative of his style, featuring a very prominent hi hat beat, droning piano performed by Nicky Hopkins, a mellotron performed by Brian Jones, all electric guitars (including slide) performed by Richards and vocals from Jagger kept even in the mix.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stray Cat Blues
Content:  According to Mick Jagger, the song was inspired by "Heroin" by the Velvet Underground.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stray Cat Blues
Content:  The intro of Stray Cat and Heroin are similar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Very Best of Mick Jagger
Content: The Very Best of Mick Jagger is a compilation album that was released worldwide on 1 October 2007 and the following day in the United States on WEA/Rhino Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Very Best of Mick Jagger
Content:  This 17-track release is the first ever overview of Mick Jagger's solo career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lucky in Love (Mick Jagger song)
Content: "Lucky in Love" is a song written and performed by English singer-songwriter Mick Jagger released as the second single from his debut album, "She's the Boss", in 1985.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lucky in Love (Mick Jagger song)
Content:  "Lucky in Love" was the seven track on "She's the Boss" and was one of two tracks from "She's the Boss" included on Jagger's greatest hits album, "The Very Best of Mick Jagger".

Title: Rose Kennedy
Content: Countess Rose Elizabeth Fitzgerald Kennedy (July 21, 1890 – January 22, 1995) was an American philanthropist, centenarian and socialite.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Kennedy
Content:  She was deeply embedded in the "lace curtain" Irish Catholic community in Boston, where her father was mayor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Kennedy
Content:  She was the wife of businessman and investor Joseph P. Kennedy Sr., who was United States Ambassador to the Court of St James's.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Kennedy
Content:  Their nine children included President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and longtime Senator Ted Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Kennedy
Content:  She was the sixth American woman to be granted the title of "Countess" by the Vatican.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: John F. Fitzgerald
Content: John Francis "Honey Fitz" Fitzgerald (February 11, 1863 – October 2, 1950) was an American politician, father of Rose Kennedy and maternal grandfather of President John F. Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content: Doyle's Cafe is a bar / restaurant located on Washington Street in the Jamaica Plain neighborhood of Boston, Massachusetts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content:  Doyle's Cafe was established in 1882 and is located near the Samuel Adams Brewery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content:  Its close proximity to the Samuel Adams Brewery affords Doyle's the unique opportunity to serve new or experimental Samuel Adams beers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content:  It is also where Samuel Adams Boston Lager was first put on tap.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content:  Throughout its history, Doyle's has been known as a favorite watering hole for both local and national politicians.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Doyle's Cafe
Content:  On St. Patrick's Day in 1988, Senator Ted Kennedy helped dedicate a new room at the location to his maternal grandfather, John F. Fitzgerald.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Shriver
Content: Anthony Paul Kennedy Shriver (born July 20, 1965) is an American activist for people with intellectual disabilities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Shriver
Content:  In 1989, he founded Best Buddies International, an international organization that helps people with intellectual disabilities to find employment and social opportunities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Shriver
Content:  Through his mother, he is a nephew of World War II casualty Joseph P. Kennedy, Jr., President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and Senator Ted Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Jean Kennedy Smith
Content: Jean Ann Kennedy Smith (born February 20, 1928) is an American diplomat who served as United States Ambassador to Ireland from 1993 to 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Kennedy Smith
Content:  She is the eighth of nine children born to Joseph P. Kennedy, Sr. and Rose Fitzgerald, and is their longest-lived and last surviving child.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Kennedy Smith
Content:  Her siblings include President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, longtime Senator Ted Kennedy, and Special Olympics founder Eunice Kennedy Shriver.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patricia Kennedy Lawford
Content: Patricia Helen "Pat" Kennedy Lawford (May 6, 1924 – September 17, 2006) was an American socialite and the sixth of nine children of Rose and Joseph P. Kennedy Sr. She was a sister of President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and Senator Ted Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patricia Kennedy Lawford
Content:  Pat wanted to be a film producer, a profession not readily open to young women in her time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patricia Kennedy Lawford
Content:  She married English actor Peter Lawford in 1954, but they experienced a serious culture-clash and divorced in 1966.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Kerry Kennedy
Content: Mary Kerry Kennedy (born September 8, 1959) is an American human rights activist and writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kerry Kennedy
Content:  She is the seventh child and third daughter of Robert F. Kennedy and Ethel Skakel Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kerry Kennedy
Content:  After her 15-year marriage to now-New York Governor Andrew Cuomo, she was known as Kerry Kennedy Cuomo from 1991 until 2003.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kerry Kennedy
Content:  She is the president of Robert F. Kennedy Human Rights.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kerry Kennedy
Content:  She is also a niece of the late President John F. Kennedy and United States Senator Ted Kennedy, and a cousin of former U.S. Ambassador to Japan Caroline Kennedy and Maria Shriver, the estranged wife of actor, bodybuilder, and former California Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Kara Kennedy
Content: Kara Anne Kennedy Allen (February 27, 1960 – September 16, 2011) was a member of the American political dynasty, the Kennedy family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kara Kennedy
Content:  She was the oldest of the three children of U.S. Senator Ted Kennedy from Massachusetts and Joan Bennett Kennedy, and a niece of President John F. Kennedy and Senator Robert F. Kennedy.

Title: Patrick Kennedy (1823–1858)
Content: Patrick Kennedy (February 16, 1823 – November 22, 1858) was an Irish farmer, businessman, and politician who moved to East Boston, Massachusetts from County Wexford, Ireland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Patrick Kennedy (1823–1858)
Content:  He was born in New Ross, Ireland.

Title: Patrick Kennedy (1823–1858)
Content:  He was the father of businessman/politician P. J. Kennedy, paternal grandfather of businessman/politician Joseph P. Kennedy Sr., and patrilineal great-grandfather of World War II casualty Joseph P. Kennedy Jr., President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and longtime Senator Ted Kennedy.

Title: Kennedy Compound
Content: The Kennedy Compound consists of three houses on six acres (24,000 m²) of waterfront property on Cape Cod along Nantucket Sound in Hyannis Port, Massachusetts, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kennedy Compound
Content:  It was once the home of American businessman and political figure Joseph P. Kennedy Sr., his wife Rose, and two of their sons, President John F. Kennedy and Senator Robert F. Kennedy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kennedy Compound
Content:  Their youngest son, Senator Ted Kennedy, lived in his parents' house, and it was his main residence from 1982 to 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  He died of brain cancer at the compound in August 2009.

Title: Connie Walker (astronomer)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Connie Walker (astronomer)
Content:  She works in the NOAO's Research Based Science Education department, helping teachers develop curricula to help children learn about Astronomy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Connie Walker (astronomer)
Content:  She is the director of both GLOBE at Night and Project Astro, and is a member of the board of directors of the International Dark Sky Association and the Astronomical Society of the Pacific.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Connie Walker (astronomer)
Content:  She was also chair of the International Year of Astronomy Dark Skies Awareness project, and continues to chair the dark skies awareness programs of Global Astronomy Month.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Connie Walker (astronomer)
Content:  Walker also serves on commission 50 of the International Astronomical Union.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Epistolary novel
Content: An epistolary novel is a novel written as a series of documents.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Epistolary novel
Content:  The usual form is letters, although diary entries, newspaper clippings and other documents are sometimes used.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Epistolary novel
Content:  Recently, electronic "documents" such as recordings and radio, blogs, and e-mails have also come into use.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Epistolary novel
Content:  The word "epistolary" is derived from Latin from the Greek word ἐπιστολή "epistolē", meaning a letter (see epistle).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content: Tobe Levin Freifrau von Gleichen (*Feb 16, 1948), a multi-lingual scholar, translator, editor and activist, is an Associate of the Hutchins Center for African and African American Research at Harvard University; a Visiting Research Fellow at the International Gender Studies Centre, Lady Margaret Hall, University of Oxford; an activist against female genital mutilation (FGM) and professor of English Emerita at the University of Maryland, University College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Having received her PhD in 1979 from Cornell University, she is most known for combining her advocacy against FGM with her academic scholarship in comparative literature.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  She has published peer-reviewed and popular articles and book chapters, edited four books, launched UnCUT/VOICES Press in 2009 and founded "Feminist Europa Review of Books" (1998-2010).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Her most notable works to date are "Empathy and Rage.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Female Genital Mutilation in African Literature" and " Waging Empathy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Alice Walker, Possessing the Secret of Joy, and the Global Movement to Ban FGM."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Alice Walker expressed appreciation for the text that shows worldwide solidarity with the novelist's literary abolition efforts in the early nineties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  Levin has also teamed up with Maria Kiminta and photographer Britta Radike to publish a memoir and sourcebook, "Kiminta.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tobe Levin
Content:  A Maasai's Fight against Female Genital Mutilation."

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  She was particularly active between 1911 and 1914 as one of the leading stars in Solax Studios films directed by Alice Guy-Blaché.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  But already in 1918, in a letter to the editor of the "Motion Picture Magazine", a fan evoked Blanche Cornwall as a delightful "reminiscence" and a forgotten "old star."

Title: Warrior Marks
Content: Warrior Marks: Female Genital Mutilation and the Sexual Blinding of Women is a 1993 book by Alice Walker with Pratibha Parmar, who made an award-winning documentary of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrior Marks
Content:  Following on from her 1992 novel "Possessing the Secret of Joy", Walker undertakes a journey to parts of Africa where clitoridectomy is still practised.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrior Marks
Content:  "Warrior Marks" is a harrowing work as Walker interviews women who have had the operation done and finally interviews a woman—circumcised herself—who performs the operation.

session_0.speaker: System
