session_0.text:
Title: Michael Tait
Content:  He toured as a solo act until 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Tait
Content:  He became lead singer of the Christian pop rock band Newsboys in 2009, and though Peter Furler was the lead vocalist on the album "In the Hands of God", Tait provided supporting vocals on the project.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Tait
Content:  Aside from singing, Tait is also a self-taught guitar player.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Tait
Content:  Tait also has a sister Lynda Randle, who is a Southern Gospel singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bad Books (album)
Content: Bad Books is the debut self-titled album from the folk/indie rock collaboration project by folk artist Kevin Devine and members of indie rock band Manchester Orchestra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bad Books (album)
Content:  It was released digitally on October 19, 2010, and with a physical CD copy following on November 9, 2010 through Manchester Orchestra's own label Favorite Gentlemen Recordings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bad Books (album)
Content:  Six songs on the album were written by Kevin Devine, with the other five written by Andy Hull.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free at Last (DC Talk album)
Content: Free at Last is the third studio album from DC Talk, which was released in 1992.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free at Last (DC Talk album)
Content:  Music videos were filmed for "Jesus Is Just Alright", "The Hardway" and "Luv Is A Verb".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free at Last (DC Talk album)
Content:  In 1994, DC Talk released a long-form video of the "Free at Last World Tour" entitled "Narrow is the Road", in which all three music videos can be seen.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kevin Max
Content: Kevin Max (born August 17, 1967) is an American singer, songwriter, and poet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kevin Max
Content:  He is best known for being a member of the Christian pop group DC Talk.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kevin Max
Content:  As a solo artist following the 2001 dissolution of DC Talk, he has recorded eight full-length studio albums, one Christmas album, and seven EPs, with one releasing in July.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kevin Max
Content:  From 2012 until 2014, he was the lead singer of the band Audio Adrenaline.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bad Books
Content: Bad Books is an American indie rock band formed in early 2010, and is composed of indie folk artist Kevin Devine and members of indie rock band Manchester Orchestra along with drummer Benjamin Homola.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bad Books
Content:  The collaboration began when Kevin toured along with Manchester Orchestra in November–December 2008 in support of his EP "I Could Be with Anyone", and followed by the release of the split EP entitled "I Could Be the Only One" in January 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jaimee Foxworth
Content: Jaimee Monae Foxworth (born December 17, 1979) is an American actress and model.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jaimee Foxworth
Content:  She is best known for her role of Judy Winslow, the youngest daughter of Carl and Harriette Winslow on the ABC/CBS sitcom "Family Matters".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jaimee Foxworth
Content:  She later transitioned to pornographic films using the name Crave, starring in several titles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eric Brown (actor)
Content: Eric Brown (born December 17, 1964) is a former American film and television actor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eric Brown (actor)
Content:  Brown is best known for his role as Phillip Fillmore in the 1981 coming of age film "Private Lessons" and as Vinton "Buzz" Harper, Jr. in the NBC version of the American sitcom "Mama's Family".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don Terry
Content: Don Terry (born Donald Prescott Loker, 8 August 1902 – 6 October 1988) was an American film actor, best known for his lead appearances in B films and serials in the 1930s and early 1940s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don Terry
Content:  His best known role is probably playing the recurring character of Naval Commander Don Winslow in Universal Pictures serials of the early 1940s, including "Don Winslow of the Navy" (1942) and "Don Winslow of the Coast Guard" (1943).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orlando Brown (actor)
Content: Orlando Brown (born December 4, 1987) is an American actor, voice actor, rapper and singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Orlando Brown (actor)
Content:  He is best known for his role as Eddie Thomas in "That's So Raven", 3J Winslow in "Family Matters", Tiger in "Major Payne", Max in "Two of a Kind", Damey Wayne in the short-lived Waynehead, Dobbs in "Max Keeble's Big Move", and Frankie in "Eddie's Million Dollar Cook Off".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Randy J. Goodwin
Content: Randy J. Goodwin, born as Ivan Jerome Goodwin, is a Hollywood actor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Randy J. Goodwin
Content:  He is perhaps best known for his role as "Kennedy Winslow" on "Fast Track" and "Max Ellis" on the show "Abby", as well as his recurring roles on "Girlfriends" as "Davis Hamilton" and "The Vampire Diaries" as "Dr. Jonas Martin".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Family Matters
Content: Family Matters is an American sitcom which originated on ABC from September 22, 1989 to May 9, 1997, before moving to CBS from September 19, 1997 to July 17, 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Family Matters
Content:  A spin-off of "Perfect Strangers," the series revolves around the Winslow family, a middle-class African American family living in Chicago, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Family Matters
Content:  Midway through the first season, the show introduced the Winslows' nerdy neighbor Steve Urkel (Jaleel White), who quickly became its breakout character and eventually the show's main character.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Family Matters
Content:  Having run for nine seasons, "Family Matters" became the second longest-running non-animated US sitcom with a predominantly African American cast, behind only "The Jeffersons" (11).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Family Matters
Content:  Having aired 215 episodes, "Family Matters" is ranked third, behind only "Tyler Perry's House of Payne" (254), and "The Jeffersons" (253).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kellie Shanygne Williams
Content: Kellie Shanygne Williams (born March 22, 1976) is an American actress.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kellie Shanygne Williams
Content:  She is best known for her role as Laura Lee Winslow, the middle child of Carl and Harriette Winslow on the ABC/CBS television series "Family Matters" which ran from 1989–1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kellie Shanygne Williams
Content:  Her middle name is pronounced Sha-neen.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darius McCrary
Content: Darius Creston McCrary (born May 1, 1976) is an American film and television actor and singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darius McCrary
Content:  He is best known for his role as Edward "Eddie" Winslow, the oldest child of Carl and Harriette Winslow on the ABC/CBS television sitcom "Family Matters "which ran from 1989–1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darius McCrary
Content:  Another one of McCrary's notable roles was as Scam in the 1987 comedy film "Big Shots", which was his film debut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darius McCrary
Content:  He provided the voice of Jazz in the 2007 "Transformers" reboot.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darius McCrary
Content:  From December 2009 to October 2011, he portrayed photographer Malcolm Winters on the CBS daytime drama "The Young and the Restless".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rhoda
Content: Rhoda was an American sitcom starring Valerie Harper which aired a total of 109 half-hour episodes and one hour-long episode over five seasons from September 9, 1974 to December 9, 1978.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rhoda
Content:  The show was a spin-off of "The Mary Tyler Moore Show", in which Harper between the years 1970 and 1974 had played the role of Rhoda Morgenstern, a spunky, weight-conscious, flamboyantly fashioned Jewish neighbor and native New Yorker in the role of Mary Richards' best friend.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rhoda
Content:  After four seasons, Rhoda left Minneapolis and returned to her original hometown of New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rhoda
Content:  The series was the winner of two Golden Globes and two Emmy Awards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Glenn Quinn
Content: Glenn Martin Christopher Francis Quinn (May 28, 1970 – December 3, 2002) was an Irish actor in television and film, known for playing Mark Healy in the American sitcom "Roseanne", and Doyle, a half-demon, on "Angel", a spin-off series of "Buffy the Vampire Slayer".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Livesey Hall War Memorial
Content: The Livesey Hall War Memorial commemorates the fallen of World War I and World War II who had been employed by the South Suburban Gas Company of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Livesey Hall War Memorial
Content:  It is also a tribute to those employees who served in the wars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Livesey Hall War Memorial
Content:  The monument was designed and executed by British sculptor Sydney March, of the March family of artists.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alamein Memorial
Content: The Alamein Memorial is a Commonwealth War Graves Commission war memorial in the El Alamein War Cemetery, El Alamein, Egypt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alamein Memorial
Content:  The memorial commemorates 11,866 Commonwealth forces members who died during World War II.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alamein Memorial
Content:  The memorial was designed by Hubert Worthington and unveiled by Viscount Montgomery of Alamein on 24 October 1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rainham War Memorial
Content: The Rainham War Memorial commemorates soldiers killed in both World Wars as well as civilian casualties of World War II.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Portsmouth Naval Memorial
Content: The Portsmouth Naval Memorial, sometimes known as Southsea Naval Memorial, is a war memorial in Portsmouth, Hampshire, England, on Southsea Common beside Clarence Esplanade, between Clarence Pier and Southsea Castle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Portsmouth Naval Memorial
Content:  The memorial commemorates approximately 25,000 British and Commonwealth sailors who were lost in the World Wars, around 10,000 sailors in the First World War and 15,000 in the Second World War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Portsmouth Naval Memorial
Content:  The memorial features a central obelisk, with names of the dead on bronze plaques arranged around the memorial according to the year of death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Freetown Memorial
Content: The Freetown Memorial is a Commonwealth War Graves Commission war memorial located outside the Secretariate Building in Freetown, Sierra Leone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Freetown Memorial
Content:  The memorial generally commemorates Soldiers from Sierra Leone who died whilst serving with the Royal West African Frontier Force in West Africa and whose graves are not known.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Freetown Memorial
Content:  The Freetown Memorial commemorates 1,109 First World War casualties and 247 from the Second World War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vietnam War Memorial, Hanoi
Content: The War Memorial in Hanoi is located across the Ba Dinh Square, across the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and close to Hanoi Citadel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vietnam War Memorial, Hanoi
Content:  Constructed in 1993 in a fusion of traditional Vietnamese and modernist architecture, the memorial commemorates men and women who sacrificed themselves during the Second Indochina War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vietnam War Memorial, Hanoi
Content:  The war is known by many names, e.g. as the American War in Vietnam.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vietnam War Memorial, Hanoi
Content:  The memorial is a focal point for state functions, commemorating the war dead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tower Hill Memorial
Content: The Tower Hill Memorial is a Commonwealth War Graves Commission war memorial on the south side of Trinity Square Gardens, in London, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tower Hill Memorial
Content:  The memorial commemorates those from the Merchant Navy and fishing fleets who died during both world wars and have "no grave but the sea".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tower Hill Memorial
Content:  The memorial was designed by Edwin Lutyens with sculpture work by William Reid Dick, the Second World War extension was designed by Edward Maufe with sculpture work by Charles Wheeler.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arras Flying Services Memorial
Content: The Arras Flying Services Memorial Commonwealth War Graves Commission war memorial in the Faubourg d'Amiens Cemetery, Arras, France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arras Flying Services Memorial
Content:  The memorial commemorates nearly 1,000 airmen from forces of the Commonwealth who were killed on the Western Front during World War I and who have no known grave.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arras Flying Services Memorial
Content:  The memorial was designed by Edwin Lutyens, sculpted by William Reid Dick and unveiled by Hugh Trenchard, 1st Viscount Trenchard, Marshal of the Royal Air Force on 31 July 1932.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masnières Newfoundland Memorial
Content: The Masnières Newfoundland Memorial is a Dominion of Newfoundland war memorial that commemorates the actions of the Royal Newfoundland Regiment during the First Battle of Cambrai, of World War I.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masnières Newfoundland Memorial
Content:  Located at the north end of the town of Masnières, France, the memorial commemorates the participation of the Newfoundlanders in the taking and defense of the town during the First Battle of Cambrai between the 20th of November and 2 December 1917.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content: World War II was the deadliest military conflict in history in absolute terms of total casualties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  Over 60 million people were killed, which was about 3% of the 1940 world population (est. 2.3 billion).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  The tables below give a detailed country-by-country count of human losses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  World War II fatality statistics vary, with estimates of total deaths ranging from 50 million to more than 80 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  The higher figure of over 80 million includes deaths from war-related disease and famine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  Civilians killed totalled 50 to 55 million, including 19 to 28 million from war-related disease and famine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: World War II casualties
Content:  Military deaths from all causes totalled 21 to 25 million, including deaths in captivity of about 5 million prisoners of war.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: HMAS Narani
Content: HMAS "Narani" was an auxiliary minesweeper operated by the Royal Australian Navy (RAN) during World War II.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: HMAS Narani
Content:  "Narani" was requisitioned from the Illawarra & South Coast Steam Navigation Company as auxiliaries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: HMAS Narani
Content:  The 381-ton vessel was armed with a 12-pounder 12cwt QF gun, a 20mm Oerlikon cannon, a .303-inch Vickers machine gun, and four Type D depth charges, and was commissioned into the RAN on 11 June 1941.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vickers machine gun
Content: The Vickers machine gun or Vickers gun is a name primarily used to refer to the water-cooled .303 British (7.7 mm) machine gun produced by Vickers Limited, originally for the British Army.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vickers machine gun
Content:  The machine gun typically required a six to eight-man team to operate: one fired, one fed the ammunition, the rest helped to carry the weapon, its ammunition, and spare parts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vickers machine gun
Content:  It was in service from before the First World War until the 1960s, with air-cooled versions of it on many Allied World War I fighter aircraft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goalkeeper CIWS
Content: Goalkeeper is a Dutch close-in weapon system (CIWS) introduced in 1979.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goalkeeper CIWS
Content:  It is an autonomous and completely automatic weapon system for short-range defence of ships against highly maneuverable missiles, aircraft and fast maneuvering surface vessels.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goalkeeper CIWS
Content:  Once activated the system automatically undertakes the entire air defense process from surveillance and detection to destruction, including selection of the next priority target.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content: The ČZ 2000 is a prototype 5.56 mm caliber Czech weapon system, consisting of a standard rifle, carbine and light machine gun.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  The system was developed in 1991 after adapting the LADA firearm prototype (chambered for the 5.45×39mm M74 cartridge to the standard NATO 5.56×45mm round with the SS109 bullet.)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  J. Denel from the Brno-based Prototypa-ZM company is the chief designer for both systems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  The ČZ 2000 (short for "Česká zbrojovka", and the number 2000 signifies that this is a weapon system of the year 2000) was to be produced by Česká zbrojovka of Uherský Brod.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  It was to be the new service weapon of the Czech Army, replacing: the 7.62 mm vz.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  58 assault rifle, 7.65 mm vz.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  61 Škorpion submachine gun and 7.62 mm vz.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  59 machine gun.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ČZ 2000
Content:  As of 2007, the project has been discontinued.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pershing II
Content: The Pershing II Weapon System was a solid-fueled two-stage ballistic missile designed and built by Martin Marietta to replace the Pershing 1a Field Artillery Missile System as the United States Army's primary nuclear-capable theater-level weapon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pershing II
Content:  The U.S. Army replaced the Pershing 1a with the Pershing II Weapon System in 1983 while the German Air Force retained Pershing 1a until all Pershings were eliminated in 1991.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pershing II
Content:  The U.S. Army Missile Command (MICOM) managed the development and improvements while the Field Artillery Branch deployed the systems and developed tactical doctrine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content: The AK-630 is a Soviet and Russian fully automatic naval close-in weapon system based on a six-barreled 30 mm rotary cannon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content:  In "630", "6" means 6 barrels and "30" means 30 mm.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content:  It is mounted in an enclosed automatic turret and directed by radar and television detection and tracking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content:  The system's primary purpose is defense against anti-ship missiles and other precision guided weapons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content:  However it can also be employed against fixed or rotary wing aircraft, ships and other small craft, coastal targets, and floating mines.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AK-630
Content:  Once operational, this weapon system was rapidly adopted, with up to 8 units installed in every new Soviet warship (from mine-hunters to aircraft carriers), and hundreds produced in total.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: M24 Sniper Weapon System
Content: The M24 Sniper Weapon System (SWS) is the military and police version of the Remington Model 700 rifle, "M24" being the model name assigned by the United States Army after adoption as their standard sniper rifle in 1988.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: M24 Sniper Weapon System
Content:  The M24 is referred to as a "weapon system" because it consists of not only a rifle, but also a detachable telescopic sight and other accessories.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 14th Marine Regiment (United States)
Content: The 14th Marine Regiment (14th Marines) is a reserve artillery regiment of the United States Marine Corps consisting of three artillery battalions and a headquarters battery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 14th Marine Regiment (United States)
Content:  The regiment is based in Fort Worth, Texas however its units are dispersed among 15 different sites in 12 states.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 14th Marine Regiment (United States)
Content:  Its primary weapon system is the M777A2 Howitzer with a maximum effective range of 30 km however one of its battalions has converted to fire the High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) weapon system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weapon system
Content: Weapon System is a United States military term that designated, along with a weapon system number (e.g., WS-110), military experimental (MX) weapons prior to official naming (e.g., under a military aircraft designation system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weapon system
Content:  Preceded by the first Skunk Works program (MX-813 for the Convair XF-92 in 1946), the earliest "WS" designation was the 1954 WS-117L.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weapon system
Content:  Circa February 1950, an Air Research and Development Command ""study prepared by Maj Gen Gordon P. Saville ... recommended that a 'systems approach' to new weapons be adopted "[whereby]" development of a weapon "system" required development of support equipment as well as the actual hardware itself.""

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SWARM
Content: The SWARM Remote Weapon System (Stabilised Weapon And Reconnaissance Mount) is a fully armored remote weapon system designed and built by the Thales Group in Glasgow, Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SWARM
Content:  The SWARM system consists of two main assemblies: the Gun Processing and Interface Unit (GPIU), which is operated inside the vehicle, and the external Weapon and Sensor Platform (WASP).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SWARM
Content:  It can fire a variety of weapons, and utilize multiple sensors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SWARM
Content:  On the US Marine Corps' Gladiator Tactical Unmanned Ground Vehicle (TUGV), it is equipped with a 7.62 mm M240 and day/night sensors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X6
Content: The BMW X6 is a mid-size luxury crossover by German automaker BMW.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW 303
Content: The BMW 303 was a small family saloon produced by BMW in 1933 and 1934.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW 303
Content:  It was the first BMW motor car with a six-cylinder engine and the first BMW motor car with the "kidney grille" associated with the brand.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW 303
Content:  The platform developed for the 303 was used for several other BMW cars, including the BMW 309, a four-cylinder version of the 303, the BMW 315, a 1.5-litre version of the 303 which replaced it in 1934 and was built until 1937, the BMW 319, a 1.9-litre version of the 303 produced alongside the 315 from 1935 to 1937, and the BMW 329, a development of the 319 with styling based on the newer, larger BMW 326, that briefly replaced the 319 in 1937.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW N53
Content: The BMW N53 is a straight 6 DOHC piston engine which was produced from 2006 to 2011.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW N53
Content:  It is the final naturally aspirated straight-6 engine produced by BMW, ending a history of continuous production of this engine configuration since 1968.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW N53
Content:  The N53 released in 2006 in the facelifted E60/E61 523i and 525i.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW N53
Content:  Following the introduction of the N20 turbocharged straight-4 engine in 2011, the N53 began to be phased out.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW N53
Content:  The N54 turbocharged straight-6 engine was produced alongside the N53, therefore the N54 took over as the highest performance six-cylinder engine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5 (E53)
Content: The BMW E53 is the first-generation BMW X5 mid-sized luxury crossover SUV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5 (E53)
Content:  It was produced from 1999-2006 and was replaced by the BMW E70.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5 (E53)
Content:  The E53 was developed at a time when BMW still owned Land Rover and as such shares many components and designs with both the Land Rover Range Rover L322 model (specifically the Hill Descent System and Off Road Engine Management system) and the BMW E39 5 Series (specifically engines and electronic systems).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5 (E53)
Content:  The entire in-car entertainment system (Radio Function, Navigation System, Television and Telecommunications systems) are shared with other BMWs and L322.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Italian cruiser Bolzano
Content: Bolzano was a unique heavy cruiser, sometimes considered to be a member of the "Trento" class , built for the Italian "Regia Marina" (Royal Navy) in the early 1930s, the last vessel of the type to be built by Italy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Italian cruiser Bolzano

"Bolzano" was built by the Gio.

Title: BMW X5

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5
Content:  The first generation of the X5, with the chassis code E53, made its debut in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It was BMW's first SUV and it also featured all-wheel drive and was available with either manual or automatic transmission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X5
Content:  In 2006, the second generation X5 was launched, known internally as the E70, featuring the torque-split capable xDrive all-wheel drive system mated to an automatic transmission, and in 2009 the X5 M performance variant was released as a 2010 model.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X-Coupe
Content:  Based upon the BMW X5 chassis, the X-Coupe featured an aluminium body and a 3.0 litre turbo-diesel engine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: BMW X-Coupe
Content:  Unlike the BMW X5, the X-Coupe had an aluminium body, a trunk opening downwards and two doors that swing outward.

Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow, 542 U.S. 1 (2004), was a case decided by the U.S. Supreme Court.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content:  The lawsuit, originally filed as Newdow v. United States Congress, Elk Grove Unified School District, et al. in 2000, led to a 2002 ruling by the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit that the words "under God" in the Pledge of Allegiance are an endorsement of religion and therefore violate the Establishment Clause of the First Amendment to the United States Constitution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content:  After an initial decision striking the congressionally added "one nation under God" language, [Newdow v. U.S. Congress, 292 F.3d 597 (9th Cir.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content:  2002), the superseding opinion on denial of rehearing en banc was more limited, holding that compelled recitation of the language by school teachers to students was invalid.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content:  [328 F.3d 466 (9th Cir.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elk Grove Unified School District v. Newdow
Content:  2003).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gravel v. United States
Content: Gravel v. United States, 408 U.S. 606 (1972), was a case regarding the protections offered by the Speech or Debate Clause of the United States Constitution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gravel v. United States
Content:  In the case, the Supreme Court of the United States held that the privileges and immunities of the Constitution's Speech or Debate Clause enjoyed by members of Congress also extend to Congressional aides, but not to activity outside the legislative process.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bethel School District v. Fraser
Content: Bethel School District v. Fraser, 478 U.S. 675 (1986), was a landmark decision by the United States Supreme Court involving free speech in public schools.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bethel School District v. Fraser
Content:  High school student Matthew Fraser was suspended from school in the Bethel School District for making a speech including sexual double entendres at a school assembly.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bethel School District v. Fraser
Content:  The Supreme Court held that his suspension did not violate the First Amendment.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Forest Grove School District v. T. A.
Content: Forest Grove School District v. T. A., 557 U.S. 230 (2009), is a case in which the United States Supreme Court held that the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) authorizes reimbursement for private special education services when a public school fails to provide a "free appropriate public education" (FAPE) and the private school placement is appropriate, regardless of whether the child previously received special education services through the public school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Abington School District v. Schempp
Content: Abington School District v. Schempp, 374 U.S. 203 (1963) , was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court decided 8–1 in favor of the respondent, Edward Schempp, and declared school-sponsored Bible reading in public schools in the United States to be unconstitutional.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Abington School District v. Schempp
Content:  The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court during this case was Earl Warren.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content: Cedar Rapids Community School District v. Garret F., 526 U.S. 66 (1999) , was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court ruled that the related services provision in the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) required public school districts to fund "continuous, one-on-one nursing care for disabled children" despite arguments from the school district concerning the costs of the services."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content:  The judges relied heavily on Irving Independent School Dist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content:  v. Tatro, 468 U. S. 883 (1984).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content:  Under the Court's reading of the IDEA's relevant provisions, medical treatments such as suctioning, ventilator checks, catheterization, and others which can be administered by non-physician personnel come within the parameters of the special education law's related services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content:  Disability advocates considered the Court decision to be a "substantial victory for families of children with disabilities."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cedar Rapids Community School Dist. v. Garret F.
Content:  Amendments were made in the Education Flexibility Partnership Act of 1999 to increase IDEA funding as a result of the case.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owasso Independent School District v. Falvo
Content: Owasso Independent School District v. Falvo, 534 U.S. 426 (2002) , was a case in which the United States Supreme Court held in favor of the school district that students scoring each other's tests and calling out the grades do not violate the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974 (FERPA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owasso Independent School District v. Falvo
Content:  Justice Kennedy wrote the opinion for the unanimous court.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owasso Independent School District v. Falvo
Content:  Justice Scalia wrote a concurring opinion in which he agreed with the ruling, but took issue with parts of Kennedy's opinion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content: Henry Washington Sawyer III (December 23, 1918 – July 31, 1999) was an American lawyer, civil rights activist, and Democratic politician.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content:  Born in Philadelphia, he served in World War II and attended the University of Pennsylvania Law School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content:  Sawyer worked as a corporate lawyer but is best known for his advocacy of civil liberties, especially in First Amendment cases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content:  In "Abington School District v. Schempp" and "Lemon v. Kurtzman", he successfully argued cases before the Supreme Court of the United States that became the basis for all modern Establishment Clause jurisprudence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content:  He pursued civil rights causes in Philadelphia and in the South during the Civil Rights movement of the 1960s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry W. Sawyer
Content:  He also served a four-year term on Philadelphia City Council, where he worked for civil service reform and to increase the amount of public art in the city.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Safford Unified School District v. Redding
Content: Safford Unified School District v. Redding, 557 U.S. 364 (2009) , was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court held that a strip search of a middle schooler violated the Fourth Amendment to the United States Constitution where the school lacked reasons to suspect either that the drugs (Ibuprofen) presented a danger or that they were concealed in her underwear.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Safford Unified School District v. Redding
Content:  The court also held, however, that because this was not clearly established law prior to the court's decision, the officials involved were shielded from liability by qualified immunity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Minersville School District v. Gobitis
Content: Minersville School District v. Gobitis, 310 U.S. 586 (1940) , was a decision by the Supreme Court of the United States involving the religious rights of public school students under the First Amendment to the United States Constitution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Minersville School District v. Gobitis
Content:  The Court ruled that public schools could compel students—in this case, Jehovah's Witnesses—to salute the American Flag and recite the Pledge of Allegiance despite the students' religious objections to these practices.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Minersville School District v. Gobitis
Content:  This decision led to increased persecution of Witnesses in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Minersville School District v. Gobitis
Content:  The Supreme Court overruled this decision a mere three years later, in "West Virginia State Board of Education v. Barnette", 319 U.S. 624 (1943).

Title: Double Dragon (video game)
Content: Double Dragon (Japanese: <ruby ><rb>双截龍</rb><rp> (</rp><rt >ダブルドラゴン</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> , Hepburn: Daburu Doragon ) is a 1987 beat 'em up video game developed by Technōs Japan and distributed in North America and Europe by Taito.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Double Dragon (video game)
Content:  The game is a spiritual and technological successor to Technos' earlier beat 'em up, "Nekketsu Kōha Kunio-kun" (released outside of Japan by Taito as "Renegade"), but introduced several additions such as two-player cooperative gameplay and the ability to arm oneself with an enemy's weapon after disarming them.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Double Dragon (video game)
Content:  "Double Dragon" is considered to be one of the first successful examples of the genre, resulting in the creation of two arcade sequels and several spinoffs, as well as inspiring other companies in creating their own beat 'em ups.

Title: Theodore G. Bilbo
Content: Theodore Gilmore Bilbo (October 13, 1877August 21, 1947) was an American politician who twice served as governor of Mississippi (1916–20, 1928–32) and later was elected a U.S. Senator (1935–47).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theodore G. Bilbo
Content:  A master of filibuster and scathing rhetoric, a rough-and-tumble fighter in debate, he made his name a synonym for white supremacy.

session_0.text:
Title: Theodore G. Bilbo
Content:  Like many Southern Democrats of his era, Bilbo believed that black people were inferior; he defended segregation, and was a member of the Ku Klux Klan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1919
Content: The 1919 Mississippi gubernatorial election took place on November 4, 1919, in order to elect the Governor of Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1919
Content:  Incumbent Democrat Theodore G. Bilbo was term-limited, and could not run for reelection to a second term.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1919
Content:  As was common at the time, the Democratic candidate won in a landslide in the general election so therefore the Democratic primary was the real contest, and winning the primary was considered tantamount to election.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1943
Content: The 1943 Mississippi gubernatorial election took place on November 2, 1943, in order to elect the Governor of Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1943
Content:  Incumbent Democrat Paul B. Johnson Sr. was term-limited, and could not run for reelection to a second term (he died less than two months after the election was held).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mississippi gubernatorial election, 1943
Content:  As was common at the time, the Democratic candidate ran unopposed in the general election so therefore the Democratic primary was the real contest, and winning the primary was considered tantamount to election.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelineae
Content: Commelineae is a tribe of monocotyledonous flowering plants in the dayflower family (Commelinaceae).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelineae
Content:  The tribe consists of 13 genera and about 350 species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelineae
Content:  It is one of two tribes in the subfamily Commelinoideae, the other being the Tradescantieae, which is made up of 26 genera and about 300 species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelineae
Content:  The remaining two genera in the family are in a separate subfamily, the Cartonematoideae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinoideae
Content: Commelinoideae is a subfamily of monocotyledonous flowering plants in the dayflower family (Commelinaceae).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinoideae
Content:  The Commelinoideae is one of two subfamilies within the Commelinaceae and includes 39 genera (out of 41 in the family) and all but 12 of the family's several hundred known species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinoideae
Content:  The subfamily is further broken down into two tribes, the Tradescantieae, which includes 26 genera and about 300 species, and the Commelineae, which contains 13 genera and about 350 species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Apiaceae
Content: Apiaceae or Umbelliferae, is a family of mostly aromatic flowering plants named after the type genus "Apium" and commonly known as the celery, carrot or parsley family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Apiaceae
Content:  It is the 16th-largest family of flowering plants, with more than 3,700 species in 434 genera including such well-known and economically important plants such as angelica, anise, asafoetida, caraway, carrot, celery, chervil, coriander, cumin, dill, fennel, hemlock, lovage, cow parsley, parsley, parsnip, sea holly, giant hogweed and silphium (a plant whose identity is unclear and which may be extinct).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Silphium (genus)
Content: Silphium is a genus of North American plants in the sunflower tribe within the daisy family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content: Typhonodorum is a monotypic genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  The single species making up this genus is Typhonodorum lindleyanum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  The genus is native to Madagascar, the Comores, Zanzibar, and Mauritius.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  This genus is believed to be closely related to "Peltandra" even though "Peltandra" is only found in North America and there don't appear to exist closely related genera in the African mainland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  There isn't fossil evidence to link the two genera so it has been proposed that there once was a genus in Africa from which the two genera had originated.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  The African mainland genus spread to North America and to Madagascar 50 million years ago before it broke off.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhonodorum
Content:  Then the African genus became extinct and the North American and Madagascan genera remained.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content: Commelinaceae is a family of flowering plants.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content:  In less formal contexts, the group is referred to as the dayflower family or spiderwort family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content:  It is one of five families in the order Commelinales and by far the largest of these with about 731 known species in 41 genera.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content:  Well known genera include "Commelina" (dayflowers) and "Tradescantia" (spiderworts).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content:  The family is diverse in both the Old World tropics and the New World tropics, with some genera present in both.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commelinaceae
Content:  The variation in morphology, especially that of the flower and inflorescence, is considered to be exceptionally high amongst the angiosperms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cirsium
Content: Cirsium is a genus of perennial and biennial flowering plants in the Asteraceae, one of several genera known commonly as thistles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cirsium
Content:  They are more precisely known as plume thistles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cirsium
Content:  These differ from other thistle genera ("Carduus", "Silybum" and "Onopordum") in having feathered hairs to their achenes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cirsium
Content:  The other genera have a pappus of simple unbranched hairs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cleomaceae
Content: The Cleomaceae are a small family of flowering plants in the order Brassicales, comprising about 300 species in 10 genera, or about 150 species in 17 genera.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cleomaceae
Content:  These genera were previously included in the family Capparaceae, but were raised to a distinct family when DNA evidence suggested the genera included in it are more closely related to the Brassicaceae than they are to the Capparaceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cleomaceae
Content:  The APG II system allows for Cleomaceae to be included in Brassicaceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heliotropium
Content: Heliotropium is a genus of flowering plants in the borage family, Boraginaceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heliotropium
Content:  There are 250 to 300 species in this genus, which are commonly known as heliotropes (sg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heliotropium
Content:  /ˈhiːli.ətroʊp/ ).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content: The Casuarinaceae are a family of dicotyledonous flowering plants placed in the order Fagales, consisting of four genera and 91 species of trees and shrubs native to the Australia, Southeast Asia, Malesia, Papuasia, and the Pacific Islands.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  At one time, all species were placed in the genus "Casuarina".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  Lawrie A. S. Johnson separated out many of those species and renamed them into the new genera of "Gymnostoma" in 1980 and 1982, "Allocasuarina" in 1982, and "Ceuthostoma" in 1988, with some additional formal descriptions of new species in each other genus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  At the time, it was somewhat controversial.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  The monophyly of these genera was later supported in a 2003 genetics study of the family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  In the Wettstein system, this family was the only one placed in the order Verticillatae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Casuarinaceae
Content:  Likewise, in the Engler, Cronquist, and Kubitzki systems, the Casuarinaceae were the only family placed in the order Casuarinales.
