session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Drug War
Content: The Mexican Drug War (also known as the Mexican War on Drugs; Spanish: "guerra contra el narcotráfico en México" ) is the Mexican theater of the United States' War on Drugs, involving an ongoing low-intensity asymmetric war between the Mexican Government and various drug trafficking syndicates.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Drug War
Content:  Since 2006, when the Mexican military began to intervene, the government's principal goal has been to reduce the drug-related violence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Drug War
Content:  The Mexican government has asserted that their primary focus is on dismantling the powerful drug cartels, rather than on preventing drug trafficking, which is left to U.S. functionaries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Indignados Movement
Content: The Mexican Indignados Movement ("Movimiento por la Paz con Justicia y Dignidad") is an ongoing protest movement that began on 28 March 2011 in response to the Mexican Drug War, government and corporate corruption, regressive economic policies, and growing economic inequality and poverty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Indignados Movement
Content:  The protests were called by Mexican poet Javier Sicilia in response to the death of his son in Cuernavaca.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Indignados Movement
Content:  The protesters have called for an end to the Drug War, the legalization of drugs, and the removal of Mexican President Felipe Calderón.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mexican Indignados Movement
Content:  Protests have occurred in over 40 Mexican cities, including an estimated 50,000 in Cuernavaca and 20,000 in Mexico City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content: Juan Nepomuceno Cortina Goseacochea (May 16, 1824 – October 30, 1894), also known by his nicknames Cheno Cortina, the Red Robber of the Rio Grande and the Rio Grande Robin Hood, was a Mexican rancher, politician, military leader, outlaw and folk hero.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  He was an important caudillo, military general and regional leader, who effectively controlled the Mexican state of Tamaulipas as governor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  In borderlands history he is known for leading a paramilitary mounted Mexican Militia in the failed Cortina Wars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  The "Wars" were raids targeting Anglo-American civilians whose settlement Cortina opposed near the several leagues of land granted to his wealthy family on both sides of the Rio Grande.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  Anglo families began immigrating to the Lower Rio Grande Valley after the Mexican Army was defeated by the Anglo-Mexican rebels of the Mexican State of Tejas, in the Texas Revolution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  From 1836 to 1848 when Cortina was 12–24 years old, parts of the Cortina Grant North of the Rio Grande River was in the disputed territory between the Rio Grande and the Nueces Rivers, claimed by both Mexico and the Republic of Texas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  The situation had a big impact on Cortina and his perspective on government and power.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  When the United States defeated Mexico in the Mexican-American War in 1848, Mexico was forced to concede the disputed territory to Texas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  Cortina opposed this concession.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  However, Cortina's Mexican militia was easily defeated and forced to flee into Mexico when the Texas Rangers, the United States Army and the local militia of Brownsville, Texas and Matamoros, Tamaulipas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  According to Robert Elman, author of "Badmen of the West", Cortina was the first "socially motivated border bandit," similar to Catarino Garza and Pancho Villa of later generations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Juan Cortina
Content:  His followers were known as the "Cortinistas."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content: The 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh also known as Operation Red Net (Indonesian: "Operasi Jaring Merah" ) or "Military Operation Area" (Indonesian: "Daerah Operasi Militer/DOM" ) was launched in early 1990s until August 22, 1998, against the separatist movement of Free Aceh Movement (GAM) in Aceh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content:  During that period, the Indonesian army practised large-scale and systematic human rights abuses against the Acehnese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content:  The war was characterised as the dirtiest war in Indonesia involving arbitrary executions, kidnapping, torture and disappearances, and the torching of villages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content:  Amnesty International called the military operations response as a "shock therapy" for GAM.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content:  Villages that were suspected of harboring GAM operatives were burnt down and family members of suspected militants were kidnapped and tortured.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1990–98 Indonesian military operations in Aceh
Content:  It is estimated more than 300 women and children were raped and between 9.000 (9,000) and 12.000 (12,000) people, mostly civilians, were killed between 1989 and 1998 in the operation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content: The First Kurdish–Iraqi War also known as Aylul revolts (Kurdish: شۆڕشی ئەیلوول‎ ) was a major event of the Iraqi-Kurdish conflict, lasting from 1961 until 1970.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  The struggle was led by Mustafa Barzani, in an attempt to establish an independent Kurdish state in northern Iraq.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  Throughout the 1960s, the uprising escalated into a long war, which failed to resolve despite internal power changes in Iraq.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  During the war, 80% of the Iraqi army was engaged in combat with the Kurds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  The war ended with a stalemate in 1970, resulting in between 75,000 to 105,000 casualties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  A series of Kurdish-Iraqi negotiations followed the war in an attempt to resolve the conflict.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: First Iraqi–Kurdish War
Content:  The negotiations led to the Iraqi-Kurdish Autonomy Agreement of 1970.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Plan DN-III-E
Content: The Plan DN-III-E or Plan to Aid Civilian Disaster is a series of measures protocolized and implemented primarily by bodies of the Mexican Secretariat of National Defense, specifically the Mexican Army and the Mexican Air Force organized as a body under the name of Support Force for Disaster (FACD).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Plan DN-III-E
Content:  This acts in response to a disastrous situation that affects or could affect a large group of civilians in Mexico and in some cases abroad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Plan DN-III-E
Content:  It is also known as the 3rd Army mission entrusted to safeguard and protect civilians in case of disaster.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content: The Cristero War or Cristero Rebellion (1926–29), also known as La Cristiada ] , was a widespread struggle in many central-western Mexican states against the secularist, anti-Catholic and anti-clerical policies of the Mexican government.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  The rebellion was set off by enactment under President Plutarco Elías Calles of a statute to enforce the anti-clerical articles of the Mexican Constitution of 1917 (also known as the Calles Law).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  Calles sought to eliminate the power of the Catholic Church and organizations affiliated with it as an institution, and also suppress popular religious celebration in local communities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  The massive, popular rural uprising was tacitly supported by the Church hierarchy and was aided by urban Catholic support.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  US Ambassador Dwight W. Morrow brokered negotiations between the Calles government and the Church.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  The government made some concessions, the Church withdrew its support for the Cristero fighters and the conflict ended in 1929.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cristero War
Content:  It can be seen as a major event in the struggle between Church and State dating back to the 19th century with the War of Reform, but it can also be interpreted as the last major peasant uprising in Mexico following the end of the military phase of the Mexican Revolution in 1920.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: War Against War
Content: In political philosophy and international relations especially in peace and conflict studies the concept of a war against war also known as war on war refers to the reification of armed conflicts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queen Anne's War
Content: Queen Anne's War (1702–1713), as the North American theater of the War of the Spanish Succession was known in the British colonies, was the second in a series of French and Indian Wars fought between France and England, later Great Britain, in North America for control of the continent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queen Anne's War
Content:  The War of the Spanish Succession was primarily fought in Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queen Anne's War
Content:  In addition to the two main combatants, the war also involved numerous American Indian tribes allied with each nation, and Spain, which was allied with France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Queen Anne's War
Content:  It was also known as the Third Indian War or in French as the Second Intercolonial War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Qatari–Bahraini War
Content: The Qatari–Bahraini War also known as the Qatari War of Independence was an armed conflict that took place between 1867 and 1868 in the Persian Gulf.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Qatari–Bahraini War
Content:  The conflict pitted Bahrain and Abu Dhabi against the people of Qatar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Qatari–Bahraini War
Content:  The conflict was the most flagrant violation of the 1835 maritime truce, requiring British intervention.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Qatari–Bahraini War
Content:  The two countries agreed to a truce, mediated by the United Kingdom, which led to Britain recognizing the Al Thani royal family of Qatar as the semi-independent ruler of Qatar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Qatari–Bahraini War
Content:  The conflict resulted in wide-scale destruction in both countries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Early Abstractions
Content: Early Abstractions is a collection of seven short animated films created by Harry Everett Smith between 1939 and 1956.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Early Abstractions
Content:  Each film is between two and six minutes long, and is named according to the chronological order in which it was made.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Early Abstractions
Content:  The collection includes "Numbers 1–5", "7", and "10", while the missing "Numbers 8" and "9" are presumed to have been lost.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bud Abell
Content: Harry Everett "Bud" Abell (born December 21, 1940) is a former American football linebacker in the American Football League for the Kansas City Chiefs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bud Abell
Content:  He played college football at the University of Missouri.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vladimir Danilevich
Content: Vladimir Petrovich Danilevich (Russian: Владимир Петрович Данилевич ; 4 September 1924 — 9 October 2001) was well-known Soviet and Russian Animator: who successfully worked as the film director, the screenwriter, the art director and the animator.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harry Everett Townsend
Content: Harry Everett Townsend (1879–1941) was a war artist for the United States Army during World War I.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harry Everett Smith
Content: Harry Everett Smith (May 29, 1923 in Portland, Oregon – November 27, 1991 in New York City) was a visual artist, experimental filmmaker, record collector, bohemian, mystic, and largely self-taught student of anthropology.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harry Everett Smith
Content:  Smith was an important figure in the Beat Generation scene in New York City, and his activities, such as his use of mind-altering substances and interest in esoteric spirituality, anticipated aspects of the Hippie movement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harry Everett Smith
Content:  Besides his films, Smith is widely known for his influential "Anthology of American Folk Music", drawn from his extensive collection of out-of-print commercial 78 rpm recordings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate
Content: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate (Russian: Ванюша и космический пират ) is a 1991 Soviet Russian stop-motion animation film by Vladimir Danilevich.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate
Content:  This film was produced by Soyuzmultfilm studio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate Content:
The film is about The Friendly Newcomer from another planet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate Content:
The film is The Third Film of the tetralogy, which tells about the adventures of The Newcomer Vaniusha and his friends.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Space Pirate
Content:  Other three films called "The Newcomer in The Cabbage", "Vaniusha The Newcomer" and "Vaniusha and The Giant".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Giant
Content: Vaniusha and The Giant (Russian: Ванюша и великан ) is a 1993 Russian stop-motion animation film by Vladimir Danilevich.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Giant
Content:  This film was produced by Soyuzmultfilm studio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Giant Content:
The film is about The Friendly Newcomer from another planet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Giant Content:
The film is the fourth film of the tetralogy, which tells about the adventures of The Newcomer Vaniusha and his friends.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha and The Giant
Content:  The first three films are "The Newcomer in The Cabbage", "Vaniusha The Newcomer" and "Vaniusha and The Space Pirate".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Newcomer in The Cabbage
Content: The Newcomer in The Cabbage (Russian: Пришелец в капусте ) is a 1989 short Soviet Russian stop-motion animation film by Vladimir Danilevich.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Newcomer in The Cabbage
Content:  It is the first film of a tetralogy about the adventures of The Newcomer Vaniusha and his friends.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Newcomer in The Cabbage
Content:  It was followed by "Vaniusha The Newcomer", "Vaniusha and The Space Pirate" and "Vaniusha and The Giant".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha The Newcomer
Content: Vaniusha The Newcomer (Russian: Пришелец Ванюша ) is a 1990 Soviet Russian stop-motion animation film by Vladimir Danilevich and Olga Panokina.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha The Newcomer
Content:  It was produced by Soyuzmultfilm studio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha The Newcomer Content:
The film is about The Friendly Newcomer from another planet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha The Newcomer Content:
It is the second film of the tetralogy, which tells about the adventures of The Newcomer Vaniusha and his friends.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vaniusha The Newcomer
Content:  The other three films are "The Newcomer in The Cabbage", "Vaniusha and The Space Pirate" and "Vaniusha and The Giant".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heaven and Earth Magic
Content: Heaven and Earth Magic (also called "Number 12", "The Magic Feature", or "Heaven and Earth Magic Feature") is an American avant garde feature film made by Harry Everett Smith.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heaven and Earth Magic
Content:  Originally released in 1957, it was re-edited several times and the final version was released in 1962.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heaven and Earth Magic
Content:  The film primarily uses cut-out-animated photographs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content: Typhoon Dinah, known as Typhoon Luding in the Philippines, was the fourth typhoon to form during August 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content:  An area of low pressure developed near Guam on August 19, and two days later, the low reached tropical storm intensity as it moved generally west.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content:  Intensification was initially gradual, with Dinah becoming a typhoon early on August 24 before it subsequently intensified at a faster pace.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content:  Dinah reached its highest strength on August 26 before turning northward on August 28 and into a less favorable conditions aloft, which prompted weakening.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content:  Dinah entered the Sea of Japan after passing near Okinawa on August 29, where Dinah leveled off in intensity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Typhoon Dinah (1987)
Content:  The system then began to recurve towards southwestern Japan, and after tracking through the area, Dinah transitioned into an extratropical cyclone on August 31, although the remnants could be traced for four more days as it approached the International Date Line.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content: Cyclone Bijli (JTWC designation: 01B, also known as Cyclonic Storm Bijli), was the first tropical cyclone to form during the 2009 North Indian Ocean cyclone season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  Bijli formed from an area of Low Pressure on April 14.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  Later that evening, RSMC New Delhi upgraded the low pressure area to a Depression and designated it as BOB 01.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  The Joint Typhoon Warning Center (JTWC) then issued a Tropical Cyclone Formation Alert for the system and soon after designated it as Tropical Depression 01B.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  On the evening of April 15, both RSMC New Delhi and the JTWC reported that the system had intensified into a tropical storm, with the former naming it Bilji.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  Soon after, Bilji reached its peak intensity as it approached the coast of Bangladesh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  However, on the morning of April 17, Bijli weakened to a deep depression due to land interaction, before making landfall just south of Chittagong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  The remnants of Bilji continued to weaken as they tracked across northern Myanmar, before RSMC New Delhi issued their last advisory on April 18.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyclone Bijli
Content:  The word Bijli refers to lightning in Hindi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content: The November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex was a powerful coastal storm that included the remnants of North Atlantic hurricanes Michelle and Noel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content:  The low intensified as it moved westward into Atlantic Canada on November 6, reaching a minimum pressure of 946 mbars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content:  The storm turned to the northeast and emerged into the Atlantic Ocean on November 8.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content:  It produced strong winds throughout Atlantic Canada, including gusts of up to 96 mph (155 km/h) at the Confederation Bridge in Prince Edward Island.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content:  High waves caused damage along the coastlines, while high winds left up to 100,000 without power.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: November 2001 Atlantic Canada storm complex
Content:  Overall damage was minor, and no casualties were reported.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content: Hurricane Arthur was the earliest known hurricane to make landfall in the U.S. state of North Carolina, and the first hurricane to make landfall in the United States since Hurricane Isaac in 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  The first named storm of the 2014 Atlantic hurricane season, Arthur developed from an initially non-tropical area of low pressure over the Southeastern United States that emerged into the western Atlantic Ocean on June 28.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  After sufficiently organizing, developing a well-defined circulation and deep convection amid a favorable environment, it was classified a tropical depression on July 1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  The system continued to strengthen, and was declared a tropical storm later that day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  Drifting northward, the storm reached hurricane status early on July 3 and curved toward the north-northeast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  Further structural organization resulted in additional intensification, and by 01:00 UTC on July 4, the system attained its peak winds of 100 mph (160 km/h) as a Category 2 hurricane on the Saffir–Simpson hurricane wind scale.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  Arthur made landfall at 03:15 UTC over North Carolina's Shackleford Banks, positioned between Cape Lookout and Beaufort, and intensified slightly further, with a minimum atmospheric pressure of 973 mbar (hPa; 28.70 inHg).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  The storm then trekked swiftly northeast, weakening as it passed by Cape Cod and Nantucket, before transitioning into an extratropical cyclone and coming ashore at Weymouth, Nova Scotia, on July 5.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Arthur
Content:  The remnants continued generally northeastward through Atlantic Canada before ultimately dissipating on July 9 over the Labrador Sea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Case
Content: Robert Allen "Bob" Case (December 16, 1939 – June 19, 2008) was a meteorologist who worked for the National Weather Service (NWS) for 28 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Case
Content:  Over the course of his career, he worked in NWS various offices, developing a diverse background in various types of weather forecasting, including a lengthy stint as a hurricane forecaster.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Case
Content:  He is best known for inspiring the naming of the 1991 Perfect Storm as "The Perfect Storm."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content: Hurricane Bertha caused minor damage in the United States, Bermuda, and Atlantic Canada in July and August 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  The third tropical cyclone, second named storm, and first hurricane of the 1990 Atlantic hurricane season, Bertha developed from a frontal low pressure area offshore of North Carolina on July 24.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  Initially subtropical, it slowly acquired tropical characteristics while tracked southeast and then southwestward.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  By early on July 27, the cyclone was re-classified as a tropical depression.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  Following its transition, the depression intensified and was upgraded to Tropical Storm Bertha on July 28.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  The storm then curved northeastward and rapidly strengthened.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  Bertha became a hurricane early on July 29, though it weakened back to a tropical storm later that day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hurricane Bertha (1990)
Content:  On the following day, Bertha re-intensified into a hurricane and peaked as an 80 mph (130 km/h) Category 1 hurricane on July 31.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christmas 1994 nor'easter
Content: The Christmas 1994 nor'easter was an intense cyclone along the East Coast of the United States and Atlantic Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christmas 1994 nor'easter
Content:  It developed from an area of low pressure in the southeast Gulf of Mexico near the Florida Keys, and moved across the state of Florida.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christmas 1994 nor'easter
Content:  As it entered the warm waters of the Gulf Stream in the Atlantic Ocean, it began to rapidly intensify, exhibiting traits of a tropical system, including the formation of an eye.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christmas 1994 nor'easter
Content:  It attained a pressure of 970 millibars on December 23 and 24, and after moving northward, it came ashore near New York City on Christmas Eve.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christmas 1994 nor'easter
Content:  Because of the uncertain nature of the storm, the National Hurricane Center (NHC) did not classify it as a tropical cyclone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: March 1969 nor'easter
Content: The March 1969 nor'easter was an extratropical cyclone that moved into the Gulf of Mexico on March 5, moving through southern Georgia, then deepened as it moved along the lower Eastern Seaboard, before swinging wide of New England and Atlantic Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: March 1969 nor'easter
Content:  Heavy snows fell across eastern Maryland, southern Delaware, and Martha's Vineyard in Massachusetts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: March 1969 nor'easter
Content:  It was a strong system, with maximum sustained winds of 80 kn a central pressure close to 950 hPa while south of Atlantic Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: March 1969 nor'easter
Content:  The system then moved into the far northern Atlantic Ocean while splitting into two low pressure areas on March 10.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content: Subtropical Storm One was a short-lived subtropical cyclone that brought copious amounts of rainfall to central Florida in late June 1974.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  The storm originated in an area of convection that detached from a tropical depression over the southwestern Gulf of Mexico on June 24.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  A new area of low pressure developed underneath the convection and the system began to intensify.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  Later that day, the storm had become sufficiently organized to be classified as a subtropical depression.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  Early on June 25, it made landfall near Clearwater, Florida with winds of 50 mph (85 km/h) and later attained its peak intensity over eastern Florida with winds of 65 mph (100 km/h).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  After reemerging into the Atlantic Ocean, the system began to weaken as it underwent an extratropical transition.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Subtropical Storm One (1974)
Content:  The storm ultimately dissipated off the coast of North Carolina early on June 26.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content: The 1991 Perfect Storm, also known as The No-Name Storm (especially in the years immediately after it took place) and the Halloween Gale, was a nor'easter that absorbed Hurricane Grace and ultimately evolved back into a small unnamed hurricane late in its life cycle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  The initial area of low pressure developed off Atlantic Canada on October 29.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  Forced southward by a ridge to its north, it reached its peak intensity as a large and powerful cyclone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  The storm lashed the east coast of the United States with high waves and coastal flooding before turning to the southwest and weakening.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  Moving over warmer waters, the system transitioned into a subtropical cyclone before becoming a tropical storm.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  It executed a loop off the Mid-Atlantic states and turned toward the northeast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  On November 1 the system evolved into a full-fledged hurricane with peak winds of 75 miles per hour (120 km/h), although the National Hurricane Center left it unnamed to avoid confusion amid media interest in the predecessor extratropical storm.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  It later received the name "the Perfect Storm" (playing off the common expression) after a conversation between Boston National Weather Service forecaster Robert Case and author Sebastian Junger.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  The system was the fourth hurricane and final tropical cyclone in the 1991 Atlantic hurricane season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1991 Perfect Storm
Content:  The tropical system weakened, striking Nova Scotia as a tropical storm before dissipating.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gerome Ragni
Content: Gerome Ragni (born Jerome Bernard Ragni; September 11, 1935 – July 10, 1991) was an American actor, singer and songwriter, best known as the co-author of the groundbreaking 1960s musical "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Les Misérables (1934 film)
Content: Les Misérables is a 1934 film adaptation of Victor Hugo's novel of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Les Misérables (1934 film)
Content:  It was written and directed by Raymond Bernard and stars Harry Baur as Jean Valjean and Charles Vanel as Javert.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Les Misérables (1934 film)
Content:  The film lasts four and a half hours and is considered by critics to be the greatest adaptation of the novel, due to its in-depth development of the themes and characters in comparison with most shorter adaptations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jerry Orbach
Content: Jerome Bernard "Jerry" Orbach (October 20, 1935 – December 28, 2004) was an American actor and singer, described at the time of his death as "one of the last" bona fide" leading men of the Broadway musical and global celebrity on television" and a "versatile stage and film actor".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Lewis
Content: Ira Lewis Metsky (27 August 1932 — 4 April 2015) was an American actor, writer, and playwright.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Lewis
Content:  Lewis was best known for his one-act play, "Chinese Coffee", which opened at the Circle in the Square Theatre in 1992, starring Al Pacino.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Lewis
Content:  A film adaptation of "Chinese Coffee", also starring Pacino, as well as Jerry Orbach, was released in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Lewis
Content:  Ira Lewis wrote the film's screenplay, while Pacino directed the adaptation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Never Gonna Dance
Content: Never Gonna Dance is a Broadway musical featuring the music of Jerome Kern.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Never Gonna Dance
Content:  The musical was based on the 1936 Fred Astaire/Ginger Rogers film "Swing Time".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Never Gonna Dance
Content:  Lyricists include Oscar Hammerstein, Ira Gershwin, P. G. Wodehouse, Bernard Dougall, Johnny Mercer, Jimmy McHugh, Otto Harbach, and Dorothy Fields.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pride and Prejudice (1940 film)
Content: Pride and Prejudice is a 1940 American film adaptation of Jane Austen's novel "Pride and Prejudice", directed by Robert Z. Leonard and starring Greer Garson and Laurence Olivier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pride and Prejudice (1940 film)
Content:  The screenplay was written by Aldous Huxley and Jane Murfin, adapted specifically from the stage adaptation by Helen Jerome in addition to Jane Austen's novel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pride and Prejudice (1940 film)
Content:  The film is about five sisters from an English family of landed gentry who must deal with issues of marriage, morality, and misconceptions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pride and Prejudice (1940 film)
Content:  The film was released by MGM on July 26, 1940 in the United States, and was critically well received.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pride and Prejudice (1940 film)
Content:  "The New York Times" film critic praised the film as "the most deliciously pert comedy of old manners, the most crisp and crackling satire in costume that we in this corner can remember ever having seen on the screen."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content: Ira Deutchman is best known as a producer, distributor and marketer of independent films, but in 2000, he moved into film exhibition as Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Emerging Pictures—a New York-based digital exhibition company, which was sold in January, 2015 to Vancouver-based 20 Year Media He also served as Chair of the Film Program at Columbia University School of the Arts from 2011 to 2015, where he has been a Professor of Professional Practice for more than 25 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  Deutchman is a member of The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  He was one of the original creative advisors to the Sundance Institute and formerly served on the Board of Advisors for the Sundance Film Festival.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  He has also served as a Board member and former Board chair for the Independent Feature Project, the Board of Advisors for the Los Angeles Independent Film Festival, the Williamstown Film Festival, IFP/West, and the Collective for Living Cinema, and was a member of the Board for Kartemquin Films.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  In 2015, he donated his personal archives to the University of Michigan's Screen Arts Mavericks and Makers Collection.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  Deutchman continues to produce films while consulting on the marketing and distribution of independent films, and teaching producing students in the MFA Film Program at Columbia University's School of the Arts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  Current projects include a film adaptation of Barbara Ehrenreich's best-selling book "Nickel and Dimed," a theatrical adaptation of Joan Micklin Silver's 1976 independent film "Hester Street" and a documentary about art film maverick Donald Rugoff, which is in production.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  He consults for Luce Cinecitta on the marketing of Italian cinema in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ira Deutchman
Content:  Deutchman was awarded the first annual Spotlight Lifetime Achievement Award at the 2017 Sundance Art House Convergence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jerome Karabel
Content: Jerome Bernard Karabel (born 1950) is an American sociologist, political and social commentator, and Professor of Sociology at the University of California at Berkeley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jerome Karabel
Content:  He has written extensively on American institutions of higher education and on various aspects of social policy and history in the United States, often from a comparative perspective.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chinese Coffee
Content: Chinese Coffee is a one-act play, written by Ira Lewis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosemary's Baby (miniseries)
Content: Rosemary's Baby is a 2014 two-part, four-hour television miniseries adaptation of Ira Levin's best-selling horror novel of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosemary's Baby (miniseries)
Content:  Zoe Saldana stars in the NBC production that is directed by Agnieszka Holland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosemary's Baby (miniseries)
Content:  Unlike earlier versions, it is set in Paris rather than New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosemary's Baby (miniseries)
Content:  The work was not well received by critics, many of whom said that it was stretched to fill two two-hour timeslots.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosemary's Baby (miniseries)
Content:  Although there are several notable changes, this miniseries is considered to be a fairly true updating of the original film adaptation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie
Content: Angelina Jolie Pitt ( ; née Voight; born June 4, 1975) is an American actress, filmmaker, and humanitarian.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie
Content:  She has received an Academy Award, two Screen Actors Guild Awards, and three Golden Globe Awards, and has been cited as Hollywood's highest-paid actress.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie
Content:  Jolie made her screen debut as a child alongside her father, Jon Voight, in "Lookin' to Get Out" (1982).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie
Content:  Her film career began in earnest a decade later with the low-budget production "Cyborg 2" (1993), followed by her first leading role in a major film, "Hackers" (1995).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie
Content:  She starred in the critically acclaimed biographical cable films "George Wallace" (1997) and "Gia" (1998), and won an Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress for her performance in the drama "Girl, Interrupted" (1999).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content: The National Association for Chiropractic Medicine (NACM) was a minority chiropractic association founded in 1984 that described itself as a "consumer advocacy association of chiropractors".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  It openly rejected some of the more controversial aspects of chiropractic, including a basic concept of chiropractic, vertebral subluxations as the cause of all diseases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  It also sought to "reform the chiropractic profession away from a philosophical scope of practice and towards an applied science scope of practice."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  It stated that it was "dedicated to bringing the scientific based practice of chiropractic into mainstream medicine" and that its members "confine their scope of practice to scientific parameters and seek to make legitimate the utilization of professional manipulative procedures in mainstream health care delivery."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  "While the NACM is focused on furthering the profession, its primary focus is on the rights and safety of the consumers."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  The NACM was the object of much controversy and criticism from the rest of the profession.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Association for Chiropractic Medicine
Content:  It quietly dropped out of sight and its demise apparently occurred sometime between May 30, 2008 and March 6, 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Château Miraval, Correns-Var
Content: Château Miraval is a château and vineyard located in the village of Correns, just north of Brignoles, a village in the Var "département" in the south of France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Château Miraval, Correns-Var
Content:  The château hit headlines in late May 2008 when it was revealed that Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie had leased it for three years with an option to buy, after surveying prospective properties by helicopter, with the intention of having the twins they expected born in France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Château Miraval, Correns-Var
Content:  It is now owned by Pitt and Jolie, who were married in the château's chapel in August, 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: In the Land of Blood and Honey
Content: In the Land of Blood and Honey is a 2011 American war film written, produced, and directed by Angelina Jolie and starring Zana Marjanović, Goran Kostić, and Rade Šerbedžija.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: In the Land of Blood and Honey
Content:  The film, Jolie's first commercial release as a director, depicts a love story set against the background of the Bosnian War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: In the Land of Blood and Honey
Content:  It opened in the United States on December 23, 2011, in a limited theatrical release.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content: Angelina Jolie is an American actress and filmmaker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  As a child, she made her screen debut in the 1982 comedy film "Lookin' to Get Out", acting alongside her father Jon Voight.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  Eleven years later she appeared in her next feature, the low-budget film "Cyborg 2", a commercial failure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  She then starred as a teenage hacker in the 1995 science fiction thriller "Hackers", which went on to be a cult film despite performing poorly at the box-office.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  Jolie's career prospects improved with a supporting role in the made-for-television film "George Wallace" (1997), for which she received the Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress – Television Film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  She made her breakthrough the following year in HBO's television film "Gia" (1998).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelina Jolie filmography
Content:  For her performance in the title role of fashion model Gia Carangi, she won the Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Television Film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aptostichus angelinajolieae
Content: The Angelina Jolie trapdoor spider ("Aptostichus angelinajolieae", often misspelled "angelinajoleae") is a species of Euctenizidae, nocturnal arthropods who seize their prey after leaping out of their burrows and inject it with venom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aptostichus angelinajolieae
Content:  It was described by the Auburn University professor Jason Bond in 2008, who named it after the American actress Angelina Jolie in recognition of her work on the United Nations High Commission for Refugees.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aptostichus angelinajolieae
Content:  It was one of only seven described species of "Aptostichus" until 2012, when it was joined by Bono's Joshua Tree trapdoor spider and 32 other species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Fosse
Content: Robert Louis Fosse (June 23, 1927 – September 23, 1987) was an American dancer, musical theatre choreographer, director, screenwriter, film director and actor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content: Kathryn Doby is an American dancer, actresses, choreographer, and assistant to Bob Fosse.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  She made her Broadway debut in the ensemble of Fosse’s "Sweet Charity" in January 1966 at the Palace Theatre in Times Square.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  Aside from her performance in the musical "Gregory" (1970), Doby’s work on Broadway continued with Fosse as a Player and Dance Captain in "Pippin" (1972) and as an assistant to Mr. Fosse for "Chicago" (1975) and "Dancin’" (1978).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  Her film credits include "The Night They Raided Minsky's" - “Minsky Girl” (1968), "The Handmaid's Tale (film)" - Aunt Elizabeth (1990), and again worked with Fosse as a dancer in "Sweet Charity" (1969), "Cabaret" – Kit Kat Dancer (1972), and "All That Jazz" – Kathryn (1979).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  She also re-set the Fosse direction and choreography for the 1981 stage production of "Pippin", starring Ben Vereen, William Katt, and Chita Rivera that was filmed for TV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  She was also slated to recreate the choreography for "Dancin" to be revived by the Roundabout Theatre Company in 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  This production was postponed and, as of the date of this entry, does not have a projected start date.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  In 2012 Doby returned to New York from her home in California to restage the "Dancin’" Act One finale, “Beat Me Daddy Eight to the Bar” for the American Dance Machine for the 21st Century (ADM21).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathryn Doby
Content:  She was joined by original cast members Lloyd Culbreath, Valarie Pettiford, Cady Huffman, Roumel Reaux, and Candace Tovar.

session_0.text:
Title: All That Jazz (film)
Content: All That Jazz is a 1979 American musical drama film directed by Bob Fosse.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All That Jazz (film)
Content:  The screenplay by Robert Alan Aurthur and Fosse is a semi-autobiographical fantasy based on aspects of Fosse's life and career as dancer, choreographer and director.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All That Jazz (film)
Content:  The film was inspired by Fosse's manic effort to edit his film "Lenny" while simultaneously staging the 1975 Broadway musical "Chicago".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All That Jazz (film)
Content:  It borrows its title from the Kander and Ebb tune "All That Jazz" in that production.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: All That Jazz (film)
Content:  The film won the Palme d'Or at the 1980 Cannes Film Festival.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  In 2000 she accepted her Oscar for "Girl, Interrupted" in a frock designed by Bouwer.

session_0.text:
Title: Charlie Shrem
Content:  He co-founded the now-defunct startup company BitInstant, and is a founding member of the Bitcoin Foundation, formerly serving as vice chairman.

session_0.text:
Title: Charlie Shrem

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content: Frank Mulhern Ph.D is Associate Dean of Research at the Medill School of Journalism at Northwestern University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  Professor Mulhern specializes in research on marketing communications, marketing research and database analysis and the role of employees in marketing strategy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  He has published papers on retail pricing and promotions, the effectiveness of coupons, and the purchase behavior of ethnic consumer groups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  More recent research involves analysis of the role of employees in brand strategy and the integration of internal communications with traditional and interactive media.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  His research papers have appeared in numerous scholarly journals including the "Journal of Marketing", "Journal of Retailing", "Journal of Advertising", "International Journal of Research in Marketing", "Journal of Interactive Marketing" and "Journal of Business Research".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  Professor Mulhern is the editor of the "Promotion Marketing Academic Quarterly", a publication of the Promotion Marketing Association.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  He is the co-author of the textbook, Marketing Communications: Integrated Theory, Strategy and Tactics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Francis J. Mulhern
Content:  Professor Mulhern also serves as Director of the Forum for People Performance Management and Measurement, a center for scholarly research on employee engagement and internal marketing.

Title: Erik Voorhees
Content: Erik Tristan Voorhees is an American / Panamanian startup founder.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erik Voorhees
Content:  He is co-founder of the bitcoin company Coinapult, worked as Director of Marketing at BitInstant, and was founder and partial owner of the bitcoin gambling website Satoshi Dice (subsequently sold in July 2013 to an undisclosed buyer).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stefan Stremersch
Content: Stefan Stremersch (1972) holds the Desiderius Erasmus Distinguished Chair of Economics and a Chair of Marketing, both at Erasmus University Rotterdam, the Netherlands and is professor of Marketing at IESE Business School, Barcelona, Spain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stefan Stremersch
Content:  His main research interests focus on innovation diffusion, marketing of technology and science, marketing strategy, new product growth, business economics of the life sciences and commercialization of new technologies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stefan Stremersch
Content:  He is the scientific director of the Erasmus Healthcare Business Center and ECMI (European Center of Marketing and Innovation).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stefan Stremersch
Content:  Stremersch is also founder and director at The Marketing Technology and Innovation Institute (MTI²), a consulting company focused on helping companies innovate.

Title: List of Friday the 13th characters
Content: Friday the 13th is an American horror franchise that consists of twelve slasher films, a television show, novels, and comic books.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Friday the 13th characters
Content:  The main villain in the series is Jason Voorhees, who drowned at Camp Crystal Lake as a boy due to the negligence of the teenage counselors.

session_0.text:
Title: List of Friday the 13th characters
Content:  Decades later, the lake is rumored to be "cursed" and is the setting for a series of mass murders.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Recurring characters in the series include Jason's vengeful mother Pamela Voorhees, Alice Hardy, Tommy Jarvis and Crazy Ralph.

session_0.text:

Content:  Founded in 2011 by Gareth Nelson and Charlie Shrem, BitInstant provided a means to rapidly pay traditional funds to bitcoin exchanges.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: BitInstant
Content:  As of January 2014, BitInstant's website is no longer available, displaying only a blank page.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: BitInstant
Content:  Its blog was unavailable as of October 31, 2014.

Title: Alan Meckler
Content: Alan Marshall Meckler (born 1945) is an American internet pioneer and publishing executive.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alan Meckler
Content:  He was the founder and Chairman of Mecklermedia Corporation until the company was acquired by Penton Media in November 1998, and founded several print magazines including "Virtual Reality World", "CDrom World", and "Internet World".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alan Meckler
Content:  Until August 2014, he had been Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Mediabistro Inc. whose media assets were sold to Prometheus Global Partners, leaving a company renamed MecklerMedia Corporation which is producing trade shows world-wide in such fields as 3D Printing, Bitcoin and Facebook marketing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James J. Cooney
Content: Jim Cooney (born 1958) is a direct marketing and media buying executive with 30 years of local, regional, national and international experience.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James J. Cooney
Content:  His mentors were direct marketing pioneers Edward Valenti, Barry Becher and Arthur Schiff whom he began working with directly out of college in 1981.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James J. Cooney
Content:  The trio collaborated on numerous successful marketing campaigns including Armourcote Cookware ($80 million in sales) and the Ginsu Knife ($60 million in sales).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James J. Cooney
Content:  He began his career as a copywriter working directly under Creative Director Schiff at direct marketing agency Dial Media at age 22 and within 3 years succeeded Schiff as the agency's VP/Creative Director.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James J. Cooney
Content:  He is a twenty six time ECHO and New England Direct Marketing Association (NEDMA) award-winning copywriter, broadcast producer, creative director, account executive, media planner/buyer, marketing strategist/consultant, and Account Executive/Supervisor.

session_0.text:
Title: Crossing the Chasm
Content: Crossing the Chasm: Marketing and Selling High-Tech Products to Mainstream Customers or simply Crossing the Chasm (1991, revised 1999 and 2014), is a marketing book by Geoffrey A. Moore that focuses on the specifics of marketing high tech products during the early start up period.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crossing the Chasm
Content:  Moore's exploration and expansion of the diffusions of innovations model has had a significant and lasting impact on high tech entrepreneurship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crossing the Chasm
Content:  In 2006, Tom Byers, director of the Stanford Technology Ventures Program, described it as "still the bible for entrepreneurial marketing 15 years later".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crossing the Chasm
Content:  The book's success has led to a series of follow-up books and a consulting company, The Chasm Group.

Content:  It is the third in the Madden NFL series to include an NFL player, Tennessee Titans running back Eddie George, on its cover (the first being "Madden NFL '95", which featured Erik Williams and Karl Wilson along with Madden himself).

Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content: MV "Ross Revenge" is a radio ship, the home of Radio Caroline, as well as having supported Radio Monique and various religious broadcasters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content:  She was constructed in Bremerhaven in 1960, and initially served as a commercial trawler, notably taking part in the Cod Wars of the 1970s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content:  Following her decommissioning, she was purchased by Radio Caroline and outfitted as a radio ship, complete with 300 ft antenna mast and 50 kW transmitter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content:  Her broadcasts began on 20 August 1983; her final pirate broadcast took place in November 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content:  She ran aground on the Goodwin Sands in November 1991, bringing the era of offshore pirate radio in Europe to an end.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Ross Revenge
Content:  She was, however, salvaged, and is now maintained by the Caroline Support Group, a group of supporters and enthusiasts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billy Parker (singer)
Content: Billy Parker (born July 19, 1939 in Okemah, Oklahoma) is an American country music disc jockey and singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billy Parker (singer)
Content:  Parker was named Disc Jockey of the Year by the Country Music Association in 1974 and by the Academy of Country Music in 1975, 1977, 1978 and 1984.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billy Parker (singer)
Content:  He was inducted into the Country Music Disc Jockey Hall of Fame in 1992, the Western Swing Hall of Fame in 1993, and received the Oklahoma Association of Broadcasters' Lifetime Achievement Award in 1995.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tony Prince
Content: Tony Prince (born Thomas Whitehead; 9 May 1944) is a British radio disc jockey and businessman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tony Prince
Content:  He broadcast on Radio Caroline and Radio Luxembourg in the 1960s and 1970s, later becoming a programme director and then businessman, responsible for establishing the remix label DMC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Lee Travis
Content: David Patrick Griffin (born 25 May 1945), known professionally as Dave Lee Travis, is an English disc jockey, radio presenter and television presenter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content: Christopher Moore was a co-founder of the offshore pirate radio ship Radio Caroline, and the first voice to be heard on the air from that station.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  His opening words were "This is Radio Caroline on 199, your all-day music station".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  The first song played was by The Rolling Stones.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  At its peak in 1967, the station had 23 million listeners, and it revolutionized radio broadcasting in the UK.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  In 1991 Moore was interviewed extensively in the BBC TV show A Pirate's Tale, where he described his key role in detail.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  Moore is a member of the Pirate Radio Hall of Fame.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  Moore, who had variously been a club DJ, merchant naval steward, and photographer had become involved in Radio Caroline when he met the station's founder Ronan O'Rahilly.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  Moore's Chelsea flat mate Ian Ross (later a novelist) introduced O'Rahilly to his father New Zealand born Charles Ross, who in turn helped O'Rahilly raise the £250,000 needed to start what became Britain's first pirate radio station in April 1964.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  <> http://www.flashesandflames.com/2014/03/how-a-radio-ship-and-7-men-shook-up-britain-50-years-ago/</.>

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christopher Moore (DJ)
Content:  Although Chris Moore was the first voice to be heard on Radio Caroline, the first programme was hosted by Simon Dee who subsequently became a TV chat show host of Dee Time on the BBC.

Content:  He was the son of the writer Oliver W F Lodge and his wife Diana, and a grandson of the physicist Sir Oliver Lodge.

Title: Tom Lodge
Content:  He was born on 16 April 1936, in Tanleather Cottage, Forest Green, Surrey.

Title: Excerpt from A Teenage Opera
Content: "Excerpt from 'A Teenage Opera'" (also known as "Grocer Jack") is a 1967 single by Keith West, produced by Mark Wirtz.

session_0.text:

Content:  It was a big hit in Europe, peaking at number two on the UK Singles Chart.

Title: Mark Wirtz
Content: Mark P. Wirtz (born 3 September 1943 in Strasbourg, France) is an Alsatian pop music record producer, composer, singer, musician, author, and comedian.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Wirtz
Content:  As a producer, Wirtz's most famous output is from the mid to late 1960s, when he worked at Abbey Road Studios with Beatles engineer Geoff Emerick, under contract to EMI.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Mark Wirtz

Content:  Another track by Wirtz, the 1966 "A Touch of Velvet, A Sting of Brass" under the name Mood Mosaic, with The Ladybirds as backing singers, became well known in Germany as the theme tune for the Radio Bremen show Musikladen and was used by some radio stations and DJs in the United Kingdom as ident, notably Dave Lee Travis on Radio Caroline.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: Tony Blackburn
Content: ".

Title: Tony Blackburn

Title: Diodorus of Tyre
Content: Diodorus of Tyre (Greek: Διόδωρος ), was a Peripatetic philosopher, and a disciple and follower of Critolaus, whom he succeeded as the head of the Peripatetic school at Athens c. 118 BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diodorus of Tyre
Content:  He was still alive and active there in 110 BC, when Licinius Crassus, during his quaestorship of Macedonia, visited Athens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diodorus of Tyre
Content:  Cicero denies that he was a genuine Peripatetic, because it was one of his ethical maxims, that the greatest good consisted in a combination of virtue with the absence of pain, whereby a reconciliation between the Stoics and Epicureans was attempted.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content: Satyrus (Greek: Σάτυρος ) of Callatis was a distinguished peripatetic philosopher and historian, whose biographies ("Lives") of famous people are frequently referred to by Diogenes Laërtius and Athenaeus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  He came from Callatis Pontica, as we learn from a Herculaneum papyrus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  He lived earlier than the reign of Ptolemy VI Philometor (181–146 BC) when his "Lives" were epitomized by Heraclides Lembus, probably during the 3rd century BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  Athenaeus frequently refers to him as a Peripatetic, but his connection to the Peripatetic school is otherwise unknown.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  His biographies dealt with many eminent people including kings (Dionysius the Younger, Philip), statesmen (Alcibiades), orators (Demosthenes), poets (Aeschylus, Sophocles, Euripides), and philosophers (Bias of Priene, Chilon of Sparta, Pythagoras, Empedocles, Zeno of Elea, Anaxagoras, Socrates, Diogenes, Anaxarchus, Stilpo).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  He also wrote on the population of Alexandria, and a work "On Characters" (Περὶ χαρακτήρων ).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Satyrus the Peripatetic
Content:  Fragments of his biography of the Athenian dramatist Euripides were found at the end of a papyrus scroll discovered at Oxyrhynchus in the early twentieth century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neleus of Scepsis
Content: Neleus of Scepsis ( ; Greek: Νηλεύς ), was the son of Coriscus of Scepsis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neleus of Scepsis
Content:  He was a disciple of Aristotle and Theophrastus, the latter of whom bequeathed to him his library, and appointed him one of his executors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Neleus of Scepsis
Content:  Neleus supposedly took the writings of Aristotle and Theophrastus from Athens to Scepsis, where his heirs let them languish in a cellar until the 1st century BC, when Apellicon of Teos discovered and purchased the manuscripts, bringing them back to Athens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content: Alexander of Aphrodisias (Greek: Ἀλέξανδρος ὁ Ἀφροδισιεύς ; fl.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content:  200 AD) was a Peripatetic philosopher and the most celebrated of the Ancient Greek commentators on the writings of Aristotle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content:  He was a native of Aphrodisias in Caria, and lived and taught in Athens at the beginning of the 3rd century, where he held a position as head of the Peripatetic school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content:  He wrote many commentaries on the works of Aristotle, extant are those on the "Prior Analytics", "Topics", "Meteorology", "Sense and Sensibilia", and "Metaphysics".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content:  Several original treatises also survive, and include a work "On Fate", in which he argues against the Stoic doctrine of necessity; and one "On the Soul".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander of Aphrodisias
Content:  His commentaries on Aristotle were considered so useful that he was styled, by way of pre-eminence, "the commentator" (ὁ ἐξηγητής ).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content: Avicennism is a school in Islamic philosophy which was established by Avicenna.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  According to Henry Corbin and Seyyed Hossein Nasr, there are two kind of Avicennism: Islamic or Iranian Avicennism, and Latin Avicennism.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  According to Nasr, the Latin Avicennism was based on the former philosophical works of Avicenna.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  This school followed the Peripatetic school of philosophy and tried to describe the structure of reality with a rational system of thinking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  In the twelfth century AD, It became influential in Europe, particularly in Oxford and Paris, and affected some notable philosophers such as Thomas Aquinas, Roger Bacon and Duns Scotus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  While the Latin Avicennism was weak in comparison with Latin Averroism, according to Étienne Gilson there was a "Avicennising Augustinism".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  On the other hand, Islamic Avicennism is based on his later works which is known as "The oriental philosophy" (حکمت المشرقیین).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  Therefore, philosophy in the eastern Islamic civilization providing became close to gnosis and tried to provide a vision of spiritual universe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avicennism
Content:  This approach paved the road for the Iranian school of Illuminationism (حکمت الاشراق) by Suhrawardi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phaenias of Eresus
Content: Phaenias of Eresus (Ancient Greek: Φαινίας ὁ Ἐρέσιος , "Phainias"; also Phanias) was a Greek philosopher from Lesbos, important as an immediate follower of and commentator on Aristotle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phaenias of Eresus
Content:  He came to Athens about 332 BCE, and joined his compatriot, Theophrastus, in the Peripatetic school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phaenias of Eresus
Content:  His writings on logic and science appear to have been commentaries or supplements to the works of Aristotle and Theophrastus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phaenias of Eresus
Content:  He also wrote extensively on history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Phaenias of Eresus
Content:  None of his works have survived.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content: Theophrastus ( ; Greek: Θεόφραστος "Theόphrastos"; c. 371 – c. 287 BC ), a Greek native of Eresos in Lesbos, was the successor to Aristotle in the Peripatetic school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  He came to Athens at a young age and initially studied in Plato's school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  After Plato's death, he attached himself to Aristotle who took to Theophrastus his writings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  When Aristotle fled Athens, Theophrastus took over as head of the Lyceum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  Theophrastus presided over the Peripatetic school for thirty-six years, during which time the school flourished greatly.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  He is often considered the "father of botany" for his works on plants.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  After his death, the Athenians honoured him with a public funeral.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theophrastus
Content:  His successor as head of the school was Strato of Lampsacus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coriscus of Scepsis
Content: Coriscus of Scepsis ( ; Greek: Κορίσκος Σκήψιος ) and his brother Erastus were students of Plato.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coriscus of Scepsis
Content:  He was also a friend of Aristotle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coriscus of Scepsis
Content:  Coriscus' son Neleus is mentioned as inheriting Aristotle's library.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aristobulus of Alexandria
Content: Aristobulus of Alexandria (Greek: Ἀριστόβουλος ) also called Aristobulus the Peripatetic (fl.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aristobulus of Alexandria
Content:  181–124 B.C.E.) and once believed to be Aristobulus of Paneas, was a Hellenistic Jewish philosopher of the Peripatetic school, though he also used Platonic and Pythagorean concepts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aristobulus of Alexandria
Content:  Like his successor, Philo, he attempted to fuse ideas in the Hebrew Scriptures with those in Greek thought.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lyco of Troas
Content: Lyco of Troas ( ; Greek: Λύκων "Lykon", "gen".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lyco of Troas
Content: : Λύκωνος; c. 299 – c. 225 BC ), son of Astyanax, was a Peripatetic philosopher and the disciple of Strato, whom he succeeded as the head of the Peripatetic school, c. 269 BC; he held that post for more than forty-four years.

Title: Forward-center
Content: Forward–center or Bigman is a basketball position for players who play or have played both forward and center on a consistent basis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Forward-center
Content:  Typically, this means power forward and center, since these are usually the two biggest player positions on any basketball team, and therefore more often overlap each other.

Title: Delray Brooks
Content: Delray Brooks (October 24, 1965) is an American basketball coach and former basketball player.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  Brooks was an Indiana high school basketball star who was named both 1984 Co-Indiana Mr. Basketball and 1984 USA Today Player of the Year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  After high school, he first attended Indiana University to play basketball for Bobby Knight.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  When he didn't fit into the team plans as he had hoped he transferred to play for Rick Pitino at Providence College, where the team was one of the most successful in school history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  As a professional player, his career floundered in various leagues before he began coaching basketball as an assistant for Pitino at the University of Kentucky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  He reached the NCAA Men's Division I Basketball Championship final four as both a player and assistant coach.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  When Pitino left for the NBA, he moved on to a head coaching position at the University of Texas-Pan American.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  He was eventually caught up in a scandal and fired.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  He has since coached various high school teams.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Delray Brooks
Content:  , he is the head coach for the men's basketball team at Clay High School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blake Hoffarber
Content: Blake Hoffarber (born April 27, 1988) is a former college basketball player who played the shooting guard position on the University of Minnesota men's basketball team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blake Hoffarber
Content:  The 6'4", 200 lbs Hoffarber is a Minnetonka, Minnesota native who gained fame by way of numerous highlight reel shots during the course of his basketball career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blake Hoffarber
Content:  The first occurred during the 2005 Minnesota State High School Basketball Tournament against Eastview High School, for which he won the 2005 Best Play ESPY Award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blake Hoffarber

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blake Hoffarber
Content:  He was a 2011 Academic All-American selection.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Hole set
Content: Hole set is an offensive position in the game of water polo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hole set
Content:  It can be referred to as either just the "hole" position or the "set."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hole set
Content:  Because this player is typically positioned on the two meter (2M) marker and in center of the opposing team's goal, the position can also be called the two-meter or simply 2M.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hole set
Content:  Other names for this position include center forward, due to the similarity between the corresponding basketball position, as well as the pit-man.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hole set
Content:  The defensive player guarding the hole set can be called the hole-D, where D stands for defense, two-meter defender, or 2M-D.

Content:  He is currently the coach of the Rail Coach Factory, Kapurthala team, and has been a long-standing coach of the Indian Railways men's team.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  Ram Kumar is the son of former basketball player Khushi Ram, an Arjuna awardee and the brother of Ashok Kumar, also an India international player.

session_0.speaker: System
