Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content: Yakuza: Dead Souls (Japanese: 龍が如く <ruby ><rb>OF THE END</rb><rp> (</rp><rt >オブ ジ エンド</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> , Hepburn: Ryū ga Gotoku OF THE END , "Like a Dragon of the End") is an action-adventure survival horror video game, developed and published by Sega for the PlayStation 3.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  The game is the sixth installment in the "Yakuza" series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  The game's concept and keyword is "destruction"; a promotional image for the game showed the series' main locale, Kamurocho, in ruins.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  Leaked scans of the Famitsu issue released on September 16, the first day of the 2010 Tokyo Game Show, revealed that the game would be set during a zombie outbreak in Kamurocho and reportedly after the events of "Yakuza 4".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  The game was originally scheduled for release in Japan on March 17, 2011 two days after the release of "Yakuza 4" in North America; however, after the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami, the release was indefinitely delayed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  A new release date, setting the game's release for June 9, was announced on April 7.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yakuza: Dead Souls
Content:  The game was also released in North America and Europe by Sega in March 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Street Mobster
Content:  Shot on location in Kawasaki, the plot centers around Okita, a street thug troublemaker released from prison only to discover that the crime underworld in which he used to operate and the socio-political landscape of Japan has changed dramatically. "

Title: Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival
Content: The Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival (formerly Summer Shakespeare) at the University of Notre Dame is an annual festival that seeks to combine professional productions of the works of William Shakespeare with community outreach and educational programs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival
Content:  The Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival is a part of the University of Notre Dame's Shakespeare initiative entitled "Shakespeare at Notre Dame", a program that recognizes the centrality of the study of Shakespeare in humanistic pedagogy at the University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival
Content:  Its fifteenth season (summer of 2014) was known as the 15/150, also celebrating the 450th birthday of William Shakespeare, and the 150th anniversary of the first full production of Shakespeare at the university in 1864 (Records indicate the first performance of Shakespeare at the University of Notre Dame took place in 1847, a collection of scenes also from "Henry IV)."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Notre Dame Shakespeare Festival
Content:  The anniversary season consisted of the Professional Company production of "Henry IV" (directed by Michael Goldberg), the Young Company performance of "The Merry Wives of Windsor" (directed by West Hyler), and the annual ShakeScenes shows featuring actors of all ages from South Bend and the surrounding community.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame (1996 film)
Content: The Hunchback of Notre Dame is a 1996 American animated musical drama film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation for Walt Disney Pictures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame (1996 film)
Content:  The 34th Disney animated feature film, the film is based on Victor Hugo's novel of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame (1996 film)
Content:  The plot centers on Quasimodo, the deformed bell-ringer of Notre Dame, and his struggle to gain acceptance into society.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame (1996 film)
Content:  Directed by Kirk Wise and Gary Trousdale and produced by Don Hahn, the film's voice cast features Tom Hulce, Demi Moore, Tony Jay, Kevin Kline, Paul Kandel, Jason Alexander, Charles Kimbrough, David Ogden Stiers, and Mary Wickes in her final film role.

session_0.text:

Content: The 2010 Notre Dame Fighting Irish football team represented the University of Notre Dame in the 2010 NCAA Division I FBS football season.

session_0.text:
Title: 2010 Notre Dame Fighting Irish football team
Content:  The team was coached by Brian Kelly and played its home games at Notre Dame Stadium in South Bend, Indiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2010 Notre Dame Fighting Irish football team

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Cumberland Estates
Content: Cumberland Estates is a residential neighborhood in the City of Knoxville, Tennessee, United States, which gained national attention for architectural innovation and research housing in the mid-20th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cumberland Estates
Content:  It began 2.1 miles outside the city limits, in Knox County, as one of many planned suburban neighborhoods in the post-World War II economic expansion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cumberland Estates
Content:  The development soon attracted an innovative young architect and national sponsors who would create new ways to rapidly and affordably fill the demand for residential housing needs for America’s growing population of families.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cumberland Estates
Content:  Their prominent work in the neighborhood influenced the evolution of residential building design.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cumberland Estates
Content:  While the attention received from the research homes waned last century, the neighborhood has maintained its residential character with few changes while avoiding commercial encroachment and blight.

Title: Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib
Content: The Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib is a historic gurdwara near Parliament House in New Delhi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib
Content:  It was built in 1783, after Sikh military leader Baghel Singh (1730–1802) captured Delhi, on 11 March 1783, and his brief stay in Delhi, led to the construction of several Sikh religious shrines within the city.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib
Content:  This one marks the site of cremation of the ninth Sikh Guru, Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur ji, after his martyrdom in November 1675 for saving Hindu Kashmiri Pandits, under orders of Aurangzeb.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib
Content:  The Gurudwara sahib is built near old Raisina village near Raisina Hill, at present Pandit Pant Marg, took 12 years to build.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib
Content:  Prior to that, a mosque had been built near the spot; eventually later Mughal emperor Shah Alam II gave the permission to build a Gurdwara sahib there.

Title: Yalecrest
Content: Yalecrest is a residential neighborhood located on the East Bench of Salt Lake City and is known for the architectural variety and rare collection of turn-of-the-century homes – all within a six block radius bordered by the South Side of Sunnyside Avenue, North Side of 1300 South, East Side of 1300 East and West Side of 1900 East.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  Yalecrest is commonly referred to as the renowned “Harvard-Yale area” and many streets are named after Ivy League or major U.S. universities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  It is a remarkably visually cohesive area with uniform setbacks, historic houses of the same era with comparable massing and landscaping, as well as streets lined with mature shade trees, and a surprising level of contributing structures that retain their historic integrity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  Yalecrest contains 1,487 homes that were built in the early 20th century starting as early as 1912 with the vast majority (74%) built during the period of 1920-1940.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  The remaining homes in the Eastern Most part of the neighborhood were built during the post war boom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  Yalecrest has the largest concentration of period revival English Cottages, English Tudors, French Norman and Spanish Colonial homes anywhere in Utah.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  These houses exhibit a variety of period revival styles with the largest portion being English Tudor and English Cottage.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  According to the Salt Lake City Planning Department, the architectural variety and concentration of period cottages found in Yalecrest are “unrivalled in the state.”

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  Examples from Yalecrest are used to illustrate period revival cottages styles in the only statewide architectural style manual.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  There are 22 subdivisions which were platted and built by the prominent architects and developers of the day responsible for early 20th Century east side Salt Lake City development.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  Yalecrest has been on the National Register of Historic Places since November 8, 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Yalecrest
Content:  One home in the neighborhood, the George Albert Smith home at 1302 Yale Avenue, is listed on the National Register since 1993.

Title: Naval Outlying Field Spencer
Content: Naval Outlying Field Spencer (ICAO: KNRQ, FAA LID: NRQ) is a military airport located two miles (3 km) northeast of Pace, Florida, in Santa Rosa County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Naval Outlying Field Spencer
Content:  It is owned by the United States Navy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Naval Outlying Field Spencer
Content:  NOLF Spencer is one mile north of U.S. Highway 90, 3.5 miles west of the City of Milton, just over 6 miles east of the Escambia River and about 7 miles southwest of NAS Whiting Field.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Naval Outlying Field Spencer
Content:  This airfield is situated on 640 acre and has eight runways, all 1800 feet long by 200 feet wide.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Naval Outlying Field Spencer
Content:  These runways are arranged to make two squares, one whose vertices approximately point North, South, East and West, and another which lies directly on top but is rotated 45°.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Its mission is to support helicopter operations of the Naval Air Training Command and it remains under the control of Commander, Training Air Wing FIVE at nearby NAS Whiting Field.

Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content: Toland is an unincorporated community in Cumberland County, Pennsylvania, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  The very small village is located on Pine Grove Road, east of Mountain Creek Campground.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Michaux State Forest, the site of Laurel Lake, Fuller Lake and Pine Grove Furnace State Park lie a few miles to the west near the intersection of Pine Grove Road and PA route 233.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Toland has a mailing address of Gardners Pennsylvania, because the size of the community doesn't warrant a post-office, nor an official incorporated name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  What is now a sand pit operation was originally a clay bank mining operation that was the reason for the location of the village of Toland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Less than 50 people reside in the close-knit community, which was built for the clay bank company workers in the first quarter of the 20th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  The original community of Toland, Pennsylvania consisted of 11 duplex houses, built side by side along Pine Grove Road, with less than 0.17 of an acre of land to each.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Additional homes have been built since.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  When the community was originally built there was only a common well with a hand pump for all of the families to draw from.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Toland is less than 1/4 of a mile long, and it is located less than half a mile from the Appalachian trail crossing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  The closest town is Mount Holly Springs, Pennsylvania located (3 miles north of Toland).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  The village is roughly 10 miles south of Carlisle, Pennsylvania, 7 miles south east of Boiling Springs, Pennsylvania, and 15 miles South of Gettysburg, Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  The only business in Toland is the Cherokee Campground formerly known as the Tagg Run Campground.

session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Toland Mission is a small non-denominational church that can hold up to 74 persons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  It was originally built by the owner of Beetem Lumber Company in Carlisle for the families of the community of Toland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  A one-room school house near the church originally served the community, but it was converted to a home when residents' children were transported to a township school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toland, Pennsylvania
Content:  Local people in Toland usually travel to the town of Mount Holly Springs for necessities such as gas, food, and toiletries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marshall, Ohio
Content: Marshall is an unincorporated community in central Marshall Township, Highland County, Ohio, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marshall, Ohio
Content:  It lies at the intersection of State Routes 124 and 506.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marshall, Ohio
Content:  Rocky Fork Lake, the site of Rocky Fork State Park, is located 2 miles (3 km) to the north.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marshall, Ohio
Content:  It lies 7 miles (11 km) east-southeast of the city of Hillsboro, the county seat of Highland County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marshall, Ohio
Content:  An early variant name was West Liberty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content: Rose Island is a small island in the Bahamas that lies 3 miles east of Paradise Island, which lies directly off of New Providence Island.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content:  The island has no formal residential infrastructure and no roads.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content:  The center square mile was owned by Claude Turner for around 36 years up until 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content:  The largest mass of the island is made up of a shallow inland lagoon in the center of the island.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content:  The highest elevation on the island is 52 feet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rose Island, Bahamas
Content:  The island has a thin peninsula which juts out 7 miles east.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Fork State Park
Content: East Fork State Park is a state park located in Clermont County, Ohio, United States, about 25 miles east of Cincinnati.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Fork State Park
Content:  It has camping, hiking, swimming and boating opportunities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Fork State Park
Content:  The state park is home to many junior and collegiate rowing races, including the US Rowing Youth National Championships.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Fork State Park
Content:  The main lake in the park is William H. Harsha Lake.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Fork State Park
Content:  The large earthen dam, and smaller saddle dams, are operated by a crew of U.S. Army Corps of Engineers year round.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canoe Creek State Park
Content: Canoe Creek State Park is a 911.91 acre Pennsylvania state park in Frankstown Township in Blair County, Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canoe Creek State Park
Content:  It is 12 miles east of Altoona, the nearest city.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canoe Creek State Park
Content:  Canoe Lake, at 155 acre , is the focus of recreation at the park and is open for fishing year-round.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canoe Creek State Park
Content:  Canoe Creek State Park is a half mile off U.S. Route 22 near the small town of Canoe Creek.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canoe Creek State Park
Content:  The park was opened to the public in 1979 and was developed as part of an expansion effort in the 1970s to improve the state park system in Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Natchez Trace State Park
Content: Natchez Trace State Park is a state park located in western Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Natchez Trace State Park
Content:  It was named for the Natchez Trace woodland path that was an important wilderness road during the late 18th and early 19th centuries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Natchez Trace State Park
Content:  The 48,000-plus acre park features several wilderness trails, camping, sporting, horse-back riding, and water front activities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Granville, Ohio
Content: Granville is a village in Licking County, Ohio, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Granville, Ohio
Content:  The population was 5,646 at the 2010 census.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Granville, Ohio
Content:  The village is located in a rural area of rolling hills in central Ohio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Granville, Ohio
Content:  It is 35 miles east of Columbus, the state capital, and 7 miles west of Newark, the county seat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trace State Park
Content: Trace State Park (formerly Old Natchez Trace Park) is a public recreation area located off Mississippi Highway 6, approximately 7 mi east of Pontotoc and 7 mi west of Tupelo in the U.S. state of Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trace State Park
Content:  The state park surrounds 565 acre Trace Lake and is named for the nearby Natchez Trace trail.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trace State Park
Content:  Famed frontiersman Davy Crockett once lived within the area bounded by the park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tupelo, Mississippi
Content: Tupelo is the county seat and the largest city of Lee County, Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tupelo, Mississippi
Content:  The seventh-largest city in the state, it is situated in Northeast Mississippi, between Memphis, Tennessee, and Birmingham, Alabama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tupelo, Mississippi
Content:  It is accessed by Interstate 22.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tupelo, Mississippi
Content:  As of the 2010 census, the population was 34,546, with the surrounding counties of Lee, Pontotoc and Itawamba supporting a population of 139,671

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amistad Dam Port of Entry
Content: The Amistad Dam Port of Entry is a port of entry into the United States from Mexico.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amistad Dam Port of Entry
Content:  It was built when Amistad Dam was completed in 1969.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amistad Dam Port of Entry
Content:  The Dam was a bi-national effort to establish flood control on the Rio Grande and provide sources of water.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amistad Dam Port of Entry
Content:  Although US Department of Transportation statistics combine traffic counts with Del Rio Texas Port of Entry, approximately 65,000 vehicles crossed the dam into the US in 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pakistan Air Force
Content: The Pakistan Air Force (PAF) (Urdu: ‎ —"Pāk Fizāʾiyah" , , reporting name: PAF) is the aerial warfare branch of the Pakistan Armed Forces, tasked primarily with the aerial defence of Pakistan, with a secondary role of providing air support to the Pakistan Army and the Pakistan Navy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pakistan Air Force
Content:  The PAF also has a tertiary role of providing strategic air transport and logistics capability to Pakistan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pakistan Air Force
Content:  The PAF employs approximately 65,000 full-time personnel (including approximately 3,000 pilots) and currently operates 883 aircraft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Remington Model 51
Content: The Remington Model 51 is a small pocket pistol designed by John Pedersen and manufactured by Remington Arms in the early 20th century for the American civilian market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Remington Model 51
Content:  Remington manufactured approximately 65,000 Model 51 pistols in .32 ACP and .380 ACP calibers from 1918 to 1927, though small numbers were assembled into the mid-1930s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content: The United Negro College Fund, also known as UNCF or the United Fund, is an American philanthropic organization that funds scholarships for black students and general scholarship funds for 37 private historically black colleges and universities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content:  UNCF was incorporated on April 25, 1944 by Frederick D. Patterson (then president of what is now Tuskegee University), Mary McLeod Bethune, and others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content:  UNCF is headquartered at 1805 7th Street, NW in Washington, D.C.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content:  In 2005, UNCF supported approximately 65,000 students at over 900 colleges and universities with approximately $113 million in grants and scholarships.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content:  About 60% of these students are the first in their families to attend college, and 62% have annual family incomes of less than $25,000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United Negro College Fund
Content:  UNCF also administers over 450 named scholarships.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karaboro languages
Content: The Karaboro languages are spoken in Burkina Faso by approximately 65,000 people (SIL 1995/1991).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karaboro languages
Content:  They belong to the Senufo subfamily, but are separated from other Senufo languages by a small band of unrelated languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karaboro languages
Content:  Within Senufo they are thought to be most closely related to the Senari languages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marovoay
Content: Marovoay ] is a city and commune (commune urbaine, Malagasy: "kaominina" ) in north-western Madagascar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marovoay
Content:  It belongs to the district of Marovoay, which is a part of Boeny Region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marovoay
Content:  The population of the commune was estimated to be approximately 65,000 in 2001 commune census.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darayim District
Content: Darayim is a district in Badakhshan Province, Afghanistan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Darayim District
Content:  It was created in 2005 from part of Fayzabad District and is home to approximately 65,000 residents.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hanna Varis
Content: Hanna Leena Kristiina Varis (b. 1959 in Kuusankoski) is a Finnish graphic artist and painter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hanna Varis
Content:  She earned a Master of Arts degree from the Aalto University School of Arts, Design and Architecture in 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hanna Varis
Content:  She participated in the NUROPE, Nomadic University for Art, Philosophy and Enterprise in Europe, in 2006-2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hanna Varis
Content:  She has held over 70 solo exhibitions and participated at over 140 group exhibitions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hanna Varis
Content:  Her works are part of major art collections in Finland and abroad, such as the Kiasma, Amos Anderson Art Museum, and Helsinki Art Museum in Helsinki, Wäinö Aaltonen Museum of Art in Turku, and Albertina Museum in Vienna.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albertina
Content: The Albertina is a museum in the Innere Stadt (First District) of Vienna, Austria.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albertina
Content:  It houses one of the largest and most important print rooms in the world with approximately 65,000 drawings and approximately 1 million old master prints, as well as more modern graphic works, photographs and architectural drawings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albertina
Content:  Apart from the graphics collection the museum has recently acquired on permanent loan two significant collections of Impressionist and early 20th-century art, some of which will be on permanent display.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albertina
Content:  The museum also houses temporary exhibitions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gwangjang Market
Content: Gwangjang Market, previously Dongdaemun Market, is a traditional street market in Jongno-gu, Seoul, South Korea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gwangjang Market
Content:  The market is one of the oldest and largest traditional markets in South Korea, with more than 5000 shops and 20,000 employees in an area of 42000 m2 .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gwangjang Market
Content:  Approximately 65,000 people visit the market each day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Walter Lovell
Content: Walter Lovell (September 9, 1884 – September 10, 1937) was a World War I volunteer pilot and an American serviceman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Walter Lovell
Content:  He was born in Newton, Massachusetts to Wallace D. and Josephine (Hastings) Lowell.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Walter Lovell
Content:  Walter attended Newton High School and graduated from Harvard College with Bachelor of Arts degree, Harvard College Class of 1907.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Walter Lovell
Content:  He stayed in Boston and went into brokerage business after graduation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harvard College
Content: Harvard College is the undergraduate liberal arts college of Harvard University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harvard College
Content:  Founded in 1636 in Cambridge, Massachusetts, it is the oldest institution of higher learning in the United States and one of the most prestigious in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content: The University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma, or USAO, is a public liberal arts college located in Chickasha, Oklahoma.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content:  It is the only public college in Oklahoma with a strictly liberal arts-focused curriculum and is a member of the Council of Public Liberal Arts Colleges.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content:  USAO is an undergraduate-only institution and grants Bachelor's Degrees in a variety of subject areas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content:  The school was founded in 1908 as a school for women and from 1912 to 1965 was known as Oklahoma College for Women.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content:  It became coeducational in 1965 and today educates approximately 1,000 students.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of Science and Arts of Oklahoma
Content:  In 2001, the entire Oklahoma College for Women campus was listed as a National Historic District.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Liberal arts college
Content: A liberal arts college is a college with an emphasis on undergraduate study in the liberal arts and sciences.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Liberal arts college
Content:  A liberal arts college aims to impart a broad general knowledge and develop general intellectual capacities, in contrast to a professional, vocational, or technical curriculum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Liberal arts college
Content:  Students in a liberal arts college generally major in a particular discipline while receiving exposure to a wide range of academic subjects, including sciences as well as the traditional humanities subjects taught as liberal arts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Liberal arts college
Content:  Although it draws on European antecedents, the liberal arts college is strongly associated with American higher education, and most liberal arts colleges around the world draw explicitly on the American model.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macalester College
Content: Macalester College ( ) is a private, coeducational liberal arts college located in Saint Paul, Minnesota, US.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macalester College
Content:  It was founded in 1874 as a Presbyterian-affiliated but nonsectarian college.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macalester College
Content:  Its first class entered September 15, 1885.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macalester College
Content:  Macalester is exclusively an undergraduate four-year institution and enrolled 1,978 students in the fall of 2013 from 50 U.S. states and 90 countries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macalester College
Content:  In 2015, "U.S. News & World Report" ranked Macalester as tied for the 23rd best liberal arts college in the United States, 6th for undergraduate teaching at a national liberal arts college, and 19th for best value at a national liberal arts college.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Sisters (colleges)
Content: The Seven Sisters is a loose association of seven liberal arts colleges in the Northeastern United States that are historically women's colleges.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Sisters (colleges)
Content:  Five of the seven institutions continue to offer all-female undergraduate programs: Barnard College, Bryn Mawr College, Mount Holyoke College, Smith College, and Wellesley College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Sisters (colleges)
Content:  Vassar College has been co-educational since 1969.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Sisters (colleges)
Content:  Radcliffe College and its all-male coordinate school Harvard College (both of which were part of Harvard University) effectively merged in 1977, although Radcliffe did not take its current form as the Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study until 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seven Sisters (colleges)
Content:  Barnard College was Columbia University's women's liberal arts undergraduate college until its all-male coordinate school Columbia College went co-ed in 1983; to this day, Barnard continues to be an all-women's undergraduate college affiliated with Columbia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content: Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and functioned as a female coordinate institution for the all-male Harvard College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content:  It was also one of the Seven Sisters colleges, among which it shared with Bryn Mawr College the popular reputation of having a particularly intellectual, literary, and independent-minded student body.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content:  Radcliffe conferred Radcliffe College diplomas to undergraduates and graduate students for the first 70 or so years of its history and then joint Harvard-Radcliffe diplomas to undergraduates beginning in 1963.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content:  A formal "non-merger merger" agreement with Harvard was signed in 1977, with full integration with Harvard completed in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content:  Today, within Harvard University, Radcliffe's former administrative campus (Radcliffe Yard) is home to the Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study, and former Radcliffe housing at the Radcliffe Quadrangle (Pforzheimer House, Cabot House, and Currier House) has been incorporated into the Harvard College house system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe College
Content:  Under the terms of the 1999 consolidation, the Radcliffe Yard and the Radcliffe Quadrangle retain the "Radcliffe" designation in perpetuity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content: Beaufille is a Canadian fashion label specializing in fashion, jewelry and accessories for women.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content:  It was founded as Chloé comme Parris in 2009 by sisters Chloé Gordon and Parris Gordon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content:  The name was changed to Beaufille in April 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content:  Both sisters studied at the Nova Scotia College of Art and Design.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content:  Chloé Gordon graduated in 2010 with a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree majoring in Textiles while Parris graduated with a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree majoring in jewelry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaufille
Content:  Chloe Gordon designs the ready-to-wear while Parris Gordon creates all of the accessories for the label.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content: Jennifer Gordon founded the Workplace Project in 1992, a non-profit worker center in Hempstead, New York, which organizes immigrant workers, mostly from Central and South America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  The Workplace Project lobbied for and won a strong wage enforcement law in New York state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  Gordon was the executive director of the Workplace Project from 1993 to 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  Gordon was a MacArthur Fellow from 1999-2004.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  She is the author of "Suburban Sweatshops: The Fight for Immigrant Rights", as well as several articles on immigrants, politics, and labor unions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  She received a bachelor of arts degree from Radcliffe College of Harvard University in 1987 and a Juris Doctor degree from Harvard Law School in 1992.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jennifer Gordon
Content:  She is currently an associate professor at Fordham University School of Law, where she teaches courses on immigration and labor law.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James Vorenberg
Content: James Vorenberg (October 1, 1928 – April 12, 2000) was the Roscoe Pound Professor of Law and Dean of Harvard Law School, former Watergate Associate Special Prosecutor, and first chair of the Massachusetts State Ethics Commission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James Vorenberg
Content:  Born in Boston, Massachusetts, Vorenberg attended Harvard College, from which he graduated "magna cum laude" with a Bachelor of Arts degree in 1948, and Harvard Law School, which bestowed on him the Juris Doctor degree in 1951.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James Vorenberg
Content:  In his first year at Harvard Law, he achieved the highest grades in his class and was awarded the Sears Prize.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James Vorenberg
Content:  He served as the president of the "Harvard Law Review" while attending the school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James Vorenberg
Content:  In 1953, he clerked for Justice Felix Frankfurter at the U.S. Supreme Court.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Georgetown University faculty
Content: This is a list of notable Georgetown University faculty, including both current and past faculty at the Washington, D.C. school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Georgetown University faculty
Content:  As of 2007, Georgetown University employs approximately 1,202 full-time and 451 part-time faculty members across its three campuses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Georgetown University faculty
Content:  Many former politicians choose to teach at Georgetown, including U.S. Agency for International Development administrator Andrew Natsios, National Security Advisor Anthony Lake, U.S. Senator and Senate Democratic Leader Tom Daschle, Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Douglas Feith, and CIA director George Tenet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Georgetown University faculty
Content:  Politically, Georgetown's faculty members give more support to liberal candidates, and their donation patterns are consistent with those of other American university faculties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Georgetown University faculty
Content:  All of Georgetown University's presidents have been faculty as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of New Mexico Hospital
Content: The University of New Mexico Hospital (locally known as either University Hospital or UNM Hospital) is a public teaching hospital located in Albuquerque, New Mexico, immediately north of the main campus of the University of New Mexico.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of New Mexico Hospital
Content:  The hospital is the only Level I trauma center in the state of New Mexico, and also houses the only certified burn unit and designated stroke center in the state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of New Mexico Hospital
Content:  In addition, UNMH also contains the only children's hospital in New Mexico, and is the state's sole source of 13 pediatric sub-specialties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of New Mexico Hospital
Content:  As a "safety net hospital", UNMH serves a large percentage of the uninsured and under-insured population of the state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of New Mexico Hospital
Content:  The hospital is the main teaching facility for the University of New Mexico School of Medicine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Mobile, Alabama)
Content: Providence Hospital is a 349-bed high-rise hospital in the U.S. city of Mobile, Alabama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Mobile, Alabama)
Content:  The hospital tower was completed in 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Mobile, Alabama)
Content:  The building sits at the center of a 277 acre campus, it rises approximately 170 ft and 11 stories.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Mobile, Alabama)
Content:  It was designed by noted American architect Bertrand Goldberg, best known for the Marina City complex in Chicago.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Washington Hospital Center
Content: MedStar Washington Hospital Center is the largest private hospital in Washington, D.C. A member of MedStar Health, the not-for-profit Hospital Center is licensed for 926 beds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Washington Hospital Center
Content:  Health services in primary, secondary and tertiary care are offered to adult and neonatal patients.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Washington Hospital Center
Content:  It also serves as a teaching hospital for Georgetown University School of Medicine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Washington, D.C.)
Content: Providence Hospital is a 408 bed hospital located in the District of Columbia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Washington, D.C.)
Content:  Founded in 1861, it is the longest continuously operating hospital in the District.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Providence Hospital (Washington, D.C.)
Content:  Providence Hospital is a member of Ascension Health, the largest non-profit health care organization in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Oslo University Hospital, Ullevål
Content: Oslo University Hospital, Ullevål (Norwegian: "Oslo universitetssykehus, Ullevål" ), formerly Ullevål University Hospital (Norwegian: "Ullevål universitetssykehus" ) in Oslo, Norway is one of the four main campuses of Oslo University Hospital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Oslo University Hospital, Ullevål
Content:  It was opened in 1887, and was an independent hospital owned by Oslo municipality and then by the state until it became part of Oslo University Hospital in 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Oslo University Hospital, Ullevål
Content:  It is the largest hospital in Norway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University Hospital (Augusta, Georgia)
Content: University Hospital is a non-profit 581-bed private hospital located in downtown Augusta, Georgia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University Hospital (Augusta, Georgia)
Content:  Established in 1818, it is the second-oldest hospital in Georgia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University Hospital (Augusta, Georgia)
Content:  Although University Hospital is considered a teaching institution, it does not currently sponsor an academic program resulting in a degree.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University Hospital (Augusta, Georgia)
Content:  University Hospital is no longer directly affiliated with the Medical College of Georgia or Augusta University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University Hospital (Augusta, Georgia)
Content:  University Hospital is a fully private hospital receiving no local or state funding.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgetown University School of Medicine
Content: Georgetown University School of Medicine, a medical school opened in 1851, is one of Georgetown University's five graduate schools.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgetown University School of Medicine
Content:  It is located on Reservoir Road in the Georgetown neighborhood of Washington, DC, adjacent to the University's main campus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgetown University School of Medicine
Content:  The School of Medicine works in association with the 609-bed Georgetown University Hospital, Washington Hospital Center, and nine other affiliated federal and community hospitals in the Washington metropolitan area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgetown University School of Medicine
Content:  Georgetown is the oldest Catholic medical school in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital is one of the national capital area's oldest academic teaching hospitals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content:  It is a not-for-profit, acute care teaching and research facility located in the Georgetown neighborhood of the Northwest Quadrant of Washington, D.C.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content:  MedStar Georgetown is co-located with the Georgetown University Medical Center and is affiliated with the Georgetown University School of Medicine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content:  Its clinical services represent one of the largest, most geographically diverse, and fully integrated healthcare and delivery networks in the area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content:  MedStar Georgetown is home to the internationally known Lombardi Comprehensive Cancer Center, as well as centers of excellence in the neurology, neurosurgery, psychiatry, gastroenterology, transplant and vascular surgery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Content:  Originally named Georgetown University Hospital, it became part of the MedStar Health network in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lenny B. Robinson
Content: Lenny's mission was "to entertain ill and terminally ill children by appearing to them as Batman and teaching them that just as Batman fights battles, no matter how hard or long their health battles may be, with strength of will and determination, there is always hope!"

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lenny B. Robinson
Content:  Lenny visited sick children in hospitals, handing out Batman paraphernalia to them, and was sure to sign every book, hat, T-shirt, and backpack he handed out ""Batman"".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lenny B. Robinson
Content:  Some of the hospitals he visited included the Children's National Medical Center, Sinai Hospital, and Georgetown University Hospital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lenny B. Robinson
Content:  In 2016, Laurie Strongin and her non-profit, Hope For Henry Foundation, started the LENNY "BATMAN" ROBINSON HOPE FOR HENRY PROGRAM at Sinai Hospital in Baltimore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Howard (author)
Content: John Howard is an English author, born in London in 1961.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Howard (author)
Content:  His fiction has appeared in anthologies, magazines, and the collections "The Silver Voices", "Written by Daylight", and "Cities and Thrones and Powers".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Howard (author)
Content:  The majority of Howard's stories have central and eastern European settings; many are set in the fictional Romanian town of Steaua de Munte.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Howard (author)
Content:  "The Defeat of Grief" is a novella set in Steaua de Munte and the real Black Sea resort of Balcic; the novellas "The Fatal Vision" (in "Cities and Thrones and Powers") and "The Lustre of Time" form part of an ongoing series with Steaua de Munte architect and academic Cristian Luca as protagonist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Howard (author)
Content:  "Numbered as Sand or the Stars" attempts a 'secret history' of Hungary between the World Wars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sten Forshufvud
Content: Sten Gabriel Bernhard Forshufvud (9 February 1903 – 25 June 1985) was a Swedish dentist and physician, and amateur toxicologist (expert on poisons) who formulated and supported the controversial theory that Napoleon was assassinated by a member of his entourage while in exile.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sten Forshufvud
Content:  He wrote a book, in Swedish, about this in 1961, which was translated the following year as "Who Killed Napoleon?"

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sten Forshufvud
Content:  He later published his ideas in English, in the 1983 book "Assassination At St. Helena: The Poisoning Of Napoleon Bonaparte," a book on whose authorship Ben Weider, co-author (with David Hapgood) of the book "The Murder Of Napoleon," published the year earlier, which also advanced Forshufvud's ideas, collaborated.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fatal Vision (goggles)
Content: Fatal Vision goggles are a line of training tools for simulating the effects of alcohol and drug intoxication without actually using these substances.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joe McGinniss
Content: Joseph R. McGinniss, Sr. (December 9, 1942 – March 10, 2014), known as Joe McGinniss, was an American non-fiction writer and novelist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joe McGinniss
Content:  The author of twelve books, he first came to prominence with the best-selling "The Selling of the President 1968" which described the marketing of then-presidential candidate Richard Nixon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joe McGinniss
Content:  He is popularly known for his trilogy of bestselling true crime books — "Fatal Vision", "Blind Faith" and "Cruel Doubt" — which were adapted into TV miniseries in the 1980s and 90s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joe McGinniss
Content:  His last book was "The Rogue: Searching for the Real Sarah Palin", an account of Sarah Palin, the former governor of Alaska who was the 2008 Republican vice-presidential nominee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Going to Extremes (book)
Content: Going to Extremes is a non-fiction book by Joe McGinniss.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Going to Extremes (book)
Content:  It was first published in 1980.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Going to Extremes (book)
Content:  The book is about McGinniss' travels through Alaska for a year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Going to Extremes (book)
Content:  The book became a best-seller.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Journalist and the Murderer
Content: The Journalist and the Murderer is a study by Janet Malcolm about the ethics of journalism, published by Alfred A. Knopf/Random House in 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Journalist and the Murderer
Content:  It is an examination of the professional choices that shape a work of non-fiction, as well as a rumination on the morality that underpins the journalistic enterprise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Journalist and the Murderer
Content:  The journalist in question is Joe McGinniss; the murderer is the former Special Forces captain Dr. Jeffrey R. MacDonald, who became the subject of McGinniss' 1983 book "Fatal Vision".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fatal Vision (miniseries)
Content: Fatal Vision is a 1984 American television miniseries based on the account, in the book of the same name, of the murders in 1970 at Fort Bragg of the wife and daughters of U.S. Army officer Jeffrey R. MacDonald.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeffrey R. MacDonald
Content: Jeffrey Robert MacDonald (born October 12, 1943) is an American medical doctor who was convicted in 1979 of murdering his pregnant wife and two daughters in February 1970.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fatal Vision controversy
Content: The controversy over Fatal Vision, journalist and author Joe McGinniss's best-selling 1983 true crime book, is a decades-long dispute spanning several court cases and discussed in several other published works.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fatal Vision (disambiguation)
Content: Fatal Vision is the 1983 true crime book by Joe McGinniss which lies at the center of the "Fatal Vision" controversy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dark Side of the Moon (film)
Content: Dark Side of the Moon is a French mockumentary by director William Karel which originally aired on Arte in 2002 with the title Opération Lune.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dark Side of the Moon (film)
Content:  The basic premise for the film is the theory that the television footage from the Apollo 11 Moon landing was faked and recorded in a studio by the CIA with help from director Stanley Kubrick.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dark Side of the Moon (film)
Content:  It features some surprising guest appearances, most notably by Donald Rumsfeld, Dr. Henry Kissinger, Alexander Haig, Vernon Walters, Buzz Aldrin and Stanley Kubrick's widow, Christiane Kubrick.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes is a 2008 documentary film directed by Jon Ronson about the film director Stanley Kubrick.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content:  Ronson's intent was not to create a biography of the filmmaker but rather to understand Kubrick by studying the director's vast personal collection of memorabilia related to his feature films.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content:  The documentary came about in 1998 when Ronson received a request from Kubrick's estate for a copy of a documentary Ronson made about the Holocaust (Ronson was unaware that it was Kubrick who was asking for the film until months later).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content:  A year later, as Ronson was making plans to conduct a rare interview with the director, Kubrick suddenly died after completing work on his final film "Eyes Wide Shut".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content:  To his surprise, Ronson was invited to Kubrick's house by his widow.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick's Boxes
Content:  When he arrived, he found that half the house was filled by more than one thousand boxes containing snap shots, newspaper clippings, film out-takes, notes, and fan letters which the director used for research towards each of his films.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
Content: High School Musical 3: Senior Year is a 2008 American musical film and is the third installment in the "High School Musical" trilogy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
Content:  Produced and released on October 24, 2008, by Walt Disney Pictures, the film is a sequel to Disney Channel Original Movie 2006 television film "High School Musical".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
Content:  It was the only film in the series to be released theatrically.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
Content:  Kenny Ortega returned as director and choreographer, as did all six primary actors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick Archive
Content: The Stanley Kubrick Archive is held by the University of the Arts London in their Archives and Special Collection Centre at the London College of Communication.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick Archive
Content:  The Archive opened in October 2007 and contains material collected and owned by the film director Stanley Kubrick (1928–1999).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick Archive
Content:  It was transferred from his home in 2007 through a gift by his family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick Archive
Content:  It contains much of Kubrick's working material that was accumulated during his lifetime.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick
Content: Stanley Kubrick ( ; July 26, 1928 – March 7, 1999) was an American film director, screenwriter, producer, cinematographer, editor, and photographer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick
Content:  He is frequently cited as one of the greatest and most influential directors in cinematic history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stanley Kubrick
Content:  His films, which are mostly adaptations of novels or short stories, cover a wide range of genres, and are noted for their realism, dark humor, unique cinematography, extensive set designs, and evocative use of music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Influence of Stanley Kubrick
Content: Stanley Kubrick is regarded by film critics and historians as one of the most influential directors of all time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Influence of Stanley Kubrick
Content:  Leading directors, including Martin Scorsese, Steven Spielberg, George Lucas, James Cameron, Woody Allen, Terry Gilliam, the Coen Brothers, Ridley Scott, Paul Thomas Anderson, Christopher Nolan, David Lynch, and George A. Romero, have cited Kubrick as a source of inspiration, and in the case of Spielberg, collaboration.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Influence of Stanley Kubrick
Content:  In an interview for the "Eyes Wide Shut" DVD release, Steven Spielberg comments that "nobody could shoot a picture better in history", and that Kubrick told stories in a way "antithetical to the way we are accustomed to receiving stories".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Influence of Stanley Kubrick
Content:  Writing in the introduction to a recent edition of Michel Ciment's "Kubrick", film director Martin Scorsese notes most of Kubrick's films were misunderstood and under-appreciated when first released, only to be considered masterpieces later on.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content: Josann McGibbon is an American screenwriter working in partnership with Sara Parriott.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  The team's first major success as a screenwriter was the early Brad Pitt film, "The Favor".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  Their biggest hits since then include "Three Men and a Little Lady" and "Runaway Bride".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  In 2007, McGibbon and Parriott co-wrote and produced the hit Debra Messing miniseries, "The Starter Wife".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  "The Starter Wife" received 10 Emmy nominations in 2007, including for best screenwriting, and won one Emmy Award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  It was also nominated for Golden Globe and Writers Guild awards, and was then produced as a series, also on USA Network.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  McGibbon and Parriott wrote and co-produced the Disney Channel movie, "Descendants" which was directed by Kenny Ortega and premiered in July, 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  On February, 2013, it won the Writers Guild of America Award in television for Outstanding Children's Long Form.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josann McGibbon
Content:  They then wrote and executive-produced "Descendants 2", also directed by Kenny Ortega, which was simulcast on the Disney Channel, ABC, and the other cable channels owned by Disney-ABC in July, 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kenny Ortega
Content: Kenneth John "Kenny" Ortega (born April 18, 1950) is an American producer, director, and choreographer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kenny Ortega
Content:  He is best known for directing "Hocus Pocus", the "High School Musical" trilogy, "Descendants" and Michael Jackson's "This Is It" concerts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: S Is for Stanley
Content: S is for Stanley (Italian: "S Is for Stanley - Trent'anni dietro al volante per Stanley Kubrick" ) is a 2016 Italian documentary film co-written and directed by Alex Infascelli.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: S Is for Stanley
Content:  It depicts the relationship between celebrated director Stanley Kubrick and his personal chauffeur and assistant, Emilio D'Alessandro.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: S Is for Stanley
Content:  It was produced by Kinethica and Lock And Valentine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: S Is for Stanley
Content:  It is based on D'Alessandro's autobiography "Stanley Kubrick and Me".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Travis Payne
Content: Travis Payne (born July 5, 1971) is an American choreographer, director, and producer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Travis Payne
Content:  He was the choreographer for Michael Jackson's This Is It until Jackson's death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Travis Payne
Content:  Payne also served as the associate producer for "This Is It", and along with the director, Kenny Ortega, was extensively and intimately involved in the making of the film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Travis Payne
Content:  To date, "This Is It" worldwide gross revenue totaled $261.3 million during its theatrical run making it the highest grossing documentary or concert movie of all time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad
Content: The Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad (also known as the St. Paul & Pacific Railroad and the SP&P) was a shortline railroad in the state of Minnesota in the United States which existed from 1857 to 1879.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad
Content:  Founded as the Minnesota and Pacific Railroad, it was the state's first active railroad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad
Content:  It went bankrupt, and the state changed its name to the Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad
Content:  The SP&P went bankrupt as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Paul and Pacific Railroad
Content:  It was taken over by James Jerome Hill and others, who used the railroad as the basis for building the Great Northern Railway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content: Svenska Automobilfabriken (SAF) was a Swedish car manufacturer founded in Bollnäs in December 1919.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  It assembled US cars based on Pullman Motor Co chassis bought when Pullman went bankrupt in 1917, and fitted them with coachworks and adapted them for Swedish conditions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  The engine was a Golden, Belknapp & Swartz giving 32 hp.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  It was fitted with a Stromberg carburettor and had a 50-litre gasoline tank.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  People who earlier had worked on Rengsjöbilen were among the employees.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  SAF bought 40 Pullman chassis, and built 28 SAF cars before the company went bankrupt in 1921.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Svenska Automobilfabriken
Content:  The remaining stock (with or without bodywork) was sold out over a few years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content: Modern Music was an American record company and label formed in 1945 in Los Angeles by the Bihari brothers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  In the 1960s, Modern Records went bankrupt and ceased operations, but the catalogue went with the management into what became Kent Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  This back catalogue was eventually licensed to the UK label Ace Records in the 1980s and later sold outright during the 1990s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  Modern was also one of the big R&B labels with artists including Etta James, Joe Houston, Little Richard, Ike and Tina Turner and John Lee Hooker in the 1950s and 1960s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  Having started as an R&B label, Modern was later one of the few R&B labels to routinely cover rhythm and blues hits on other labels, apparently in an attempt to broaden their appeal and reach the popular market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  Here, they had less success and the company eventually went bankrupt, although the catalogue was maintained under similar management at Kent Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Modern Records
Content:  Ace Records of the U.K. now owns the tapes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content: Exelon Corporation is an American Fortune 100 energy company headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  It generates revenues of approximately $34.5 billion and employs approximately 34,000 people.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  Exelon is the largest electric holding company in the United States by revenue, the largest regulated utility in the United States with approximately 10 million customers, and is also the largest operator of nuclear power plants in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  It was created in October 2000 by the merger of PECO Energy Company and Unicom, of Philadelphia and Chicago respectively.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  Unicom owned Commonwealth Edison.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  Exelon operates regulated utilities in Illinois, Pennsylvania, Maryland, Delaware, New Jersey, and Washington, DC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  In October 2009, Exelon had full or majority ownership of 17 nuclear reactors in 10 nuclear power plants. Exelon has operations and business activities in 47 states, the District of Columbia and Canada and is the largest competitive U.S. power generator with approximately 35,000 megawatts of owned capacity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  Exelon merged with Constellation Energy Group in March 2012 and acquired Pepco Holdings in March 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exelon
Content:  Exelon consists of eight main operating subsidiaries with Exelon Generation, a deregulated energy generator, Constellation, a deregulated competitive energy supplier, and six regulated utilities, Commonwealth Edison (Illinois), Philadelphia Electric Company (Pennsylvania), Baltimore Gas and Electric (Maryland), Delmarva Power & Light (Delaware and Maryland), Atlantic City Electric (New Jersey), and Potomac Electric Power Company (Washington, DC and Maryland).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nissan Life
Content: Nissan Mutual Life Insurance Company (日産生命保険相互会社) was a Japanese company established in 1909 that went bankrupt on April 25, 1997.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nissan Life
Content:  It was the first time since the postwar period that a life insurer went bankrupt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Hood Energy
Content: Robin Hood Energy is a Not for Profit Energy Company launched in September 2015 by Nottingham City Council as a competitor to the Big Six Energy Suppliers (UK).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Hood Energy
Content:  The company operates as both an energy generator and supplier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Hood Energy
Content:  Nottingham City Council wholly own the company making it the first local authority energy company in the UK.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Hood Energy
Content:  Prior none had existed since the UK energy system was nationalised in 1948 under the Electricity Act 1947 and subsequently privatised in 1990 under the Electricity Act 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enron
Content: Enron Corporation was an American energy, commodities, and services company based in Houston, Texas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enron
Content:  It was founded in 1985 as the result of a merger between Houston Natural Gas and InterNorth, both relatively small regional companies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enron
Content:  Before its bankruptcy on December 2, 2001, Enron employed approximately 20,000 staff and was one of the world's major electricity, natural gas, communications and pulp and paper companies, with claimed revenues of nearly $101 billion during 2000. "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Enron
Content: Fortune" named Enron "America's Most Innovative Company" for six consecutive years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lou Pai
Content: Lou Lung Pai () born in Nanjing, China in 1947, is a Chinese-American businessman and former Enron executive.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lou Pai
Content:  He was CEO of Enron Energy Services from March 1997 until January 2001 and CEO of Enron Xcelerator, a venture capital division of Enron, from February 2001 until June 2001.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lou Pai
Content:  He left Enron with over $280 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lou Pai
Content:  Pai was the second largest land owner in Colorado after he purchased the 77500 acre Taylor Ranch for  million in 1999, though he sold the property in June 2004 for  million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fuller case
Content: E. M. Fuller and Co. bankruptcy trial, or the Fuller case was a criminal trial referring to the prosecution of Edward M. Fuller and William F. McGee for using their brokerage firm E. M. Fuller and Co. as a "bucket shop" in the early 1920s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fuller case
Content:  United States Attorney William Hayward was assisted in the case by assistant US Attorney John E. Joyce.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fuller case
Content:  The case started when the firm went bankrupt in 1922, and creditors petitioned to recover assets from E. M. Fuller & Co., as the assets "mysteriously disappeared" when the firm went bankrupt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fuller case
Content:  Ultimately Fuller and McGee pled guilty, and were convicted of operating a bucketshop in connection with E.M. Fuller Co., for defrauding its customers around $4,000,000 by bucketing the orders of customers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fuller case
Content:  The case also resulted in trials for high-profile New Yorkers such as Consolidated Exchange president William S. Silkworth, attorney William J. Fallon, sports broker Charles A. Stoneham, and gambler Arnold Rothstein.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content: The Denver, South Park, and Pacific Railroad (later called the Denver, Leadville and Gunnison Railway) was a historic narrow gauge railroad that operated in Colorado in the western United States in the late 19th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  The railroad opened up the first rail routes to a large section of the central Colorado mining district in the decades of the mineral boom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  The railroad took its name from the fact that its main line from Denver ascended the Platte Canyon and traversed South Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  Founded in 1872 by Colorado Governor John Evans, the company was purchased by the Union Pacific Railway in 1880, though it continued to be operated independently.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  The line went bankrupt in 1889 and was reorganized under the new moniker the Denver, Leadville and Gunnison Railway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  When the Union Pacific went bankrupt in 1893, the DL&G lines went into receivership and were eventually sold to the Colorado and Southern Railway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  In the first half of the 20th century, nearly all the company's original lines were dismantled or converted into .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  The last train to run the old DSP&P tracks was from Como, Colorado on April 11, 1937.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  A section of the standard gauge line between Leadville and Climax is still operated as a passenger excursion railroad called the Leadville, Colorado and Southern Railroad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad
Content:  At its peak the Denver, South Park and Pacific Railroad had 335 mi of narrow gauge line, making it the largest narrow gauge railroad in the state of Colorado.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  The script was written by Ray in 1967, based on "Bankubabur Bandhu", a Bengali story he had written in 1962 for "Sandesh", the Ray family magazine.

Title: Science fiction films in India

session_0.text:

Title: Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation
Content: The Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation is given each year for theatrical films, television episodes, or other dramatized works related to science fiction or fantasy released in the previous calendar year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation
Content:  Originally the award covered both works of film and of television but since 2003, it has been split into two categories: "Best Dramatic Presentation (Long Form)" and "Best Dramatic Presentation (Short Form)".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation
Content:  The Dramatic Presentation Awards are part of the broader Hugo Awards, which are given every year by the World Science Fiction Society for the best science fiction or fantasy works and achievements of the previous year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation
Content:  The awards is named after Hugo Gernsback, the founder of the first science fiction magazine, "Amazing Stories", and was once officially known as the Science Fiction Achievement Award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation
Content:  The award has been described as "a fine showcase for speculative fiction".

session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg (27 July 1852 in Buenos Aires – 4 November 1937) was an Argentine natural historian and novelist, one of the leading figures in Argentine biology.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  Together with Florentino Ameghino he undertook the inventory of Argentine flora and fauna, and explored all the ecoregions in the country, summarizing for the first time the biodiversity of its territory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  The son of botanical aficionado and grandson of the Baron Holmberg, Holmburg accompanied Argentine "Libertador" Manuel Belgrano on his campaigns and introduced the cultivation of the camellia to Argentina.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  As director of the Buenos Aires Zoological Garden he greatly developed its scientific aspect, publishing booklets and providing printed media for a learned appreciation of its contents.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  He also directed the Natural History Cabinet of the University of Buenos Aires and published the standard reference works on botany and zoology used in his country for most of the 20th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  While less distinguished for his writing, he was arguably the first science fiction writer in Latin America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  He wrote the first science fiction novel, "Viaje maravilloso del señor Nic-Nac al planeta Marte" ("Eng."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  "The Marvellous Journey of Mr. Nic-Nac to the Planet Mars").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  In 1879, he wrote "Horacio Kalibang o los autómatas" ("Eng."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
Content:  "Horacio Kalibang or The Automatons"), the first short science fiction story of Latin America.
