Content: Krrish is a franchise of Indian science fiction films, superhero films, television series, comics and video games.

session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  The films are centred, initially, on a mentally handicapped boy who has an encounter with an extraterrestrial being, and later, his son, who grows up to be a reluctant superhero.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  The first two films were blockbusters in the Indian market, and hits in the overseas markets.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  The third film was released on 1 November 2013 and was declared a blockbuster shattering many box office records grossing over () at the box office.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  In 2013, an animated television series based on this "Krrish" film series, and named "Kid Krrish", aired on Cartoon Network India.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  It also spawned a spin-off animation-cum-live-action series titled "J Bole Toh Jadoo" that aired on Nickelodeon (India).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Krrish (franchise)
Content:  "Krrish 3" was the first Indian film to launch its own official Facebook Emoticons as part of the promotion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Women in speculative fiction
Content: In 1948, 10–15% of science fiction writers were female.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Women in speculative fiction
Content:  Women's role in speculative fiction (including science fiction) has grown since then, and in 1999, women comprised 36% of the Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers of America's professional members. "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Women in speculative fiction
Content: Frankenstein" (1818) by Mary Shelley has been called the first science fiction novel, although women wrote utopian novels even before that, with Margaret Cavendish publishing the first ("The Blazing World") in the seventeenth century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Women in speculative fiction
Content:  Early published fantasy was written by and for both genders.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Women in speculative fiction
Content:  However, speculative fiction, with science fiction in particular, has traditionally been viewed as a male-oriented genre.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content: The Battle of Kuwait International Airport occurred on February 27, 1991 during the 1st Gulf War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  It was a tank battle between the United States and Ba'athist Iraq.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  Despite being a very large battle it is often overlooked compared to the other battles which took place during the war.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  No less than elements of 18 divisions total participated in this battle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  U.S. Army Special Forces units and multiple Iraqi Commando units were also in theatre.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  In reality the battle took place over a span of three days despite the primary battle at Kuwait International Airport lasting only one day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  Much of the combat actually took place en route to the airport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Kuwait International Airport
Content:  The battle featured the "Reveille Engagement" which went on to become the biggest and fastest tank battle in United States Marine Corps' entire history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Tarawa
Content: The Battle of Tarawa was a battle in the Pacific Theater of World War II that was fought on 20–23 November 1943.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Tarawa
Content:  It took place at the Tarawa Atoll in the Gilbert Islands, and was part of Operation Galvanic, the U.S. invasion of the Gilberts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Tarawa
Content:  Nearly 6,400 Japanese, Koreans, and Americans died in the fighting, mostly on and around the small island of Betio, in the extreme southwest of Tarawa Atoll.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Dalrigh
Content: The Battle of Dalrigh, also known as the Battle of Dail Righ, Battle of Dalry or Battle of Strathfillan, was fought in the summer of 1306 between the army of King Robert I of Scotland against the Clan MacDougall of Argyll who were allies of Clan Comyn and the English.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Dalrigh
Content:  It took place at the hamlet of Dalrigh (the "King's Field" in the Scottish Gaelic language) near Tyndrum in Perthshire, Scotland (not to be confused with Dalry, Ayrshire).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Dalrigh
Content:  Bruce's army, reeling westwards after defeat by the English at the Battle of Methven, was intercepted and all but destroyed, with Bruce himself narrowly escaping capture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Dalrigh
Content:  The battle took place sometime between late July and early August, but the exact date is unknown.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Wawon
Content: The Battle of Wawon (Turkish: "Kunuri Muharebeleri" ), also known as the Battle of Wayuan (), was a series of delay actions of the Korean War that took place from November 27–29, 1950 near Wawon in present-day North Korea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Wawon
Content:  After the collapse of the US Eighth Army's right flank during the Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River, the Chinese 38th Corps advanced rapidly towards the critical road junction at Kunu-ri in an effort to cut off United Nations forces' retreat route.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Wawon
Content:  In what was considered to be Turkey's first real combat action since the aftermath of World War I, the Turkish Brigade attempted to delay the Chinese advances at Wawon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Wawon
Content:  Although during the battle the Turkish Brigade was crippled after being encircled by Chinese forces with superior numbers, they were still be able to breach the Chinese trap and rejoin the US 2nd Infantry Division.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content: The Battle of the Allia was fought between the Senones (one of the Gallic tribes which had invaded northern Italy) and the Romans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  It was fought at the confluence of the rivers Tiber and Allia, eleven Roman miles (16 km) north of Rome.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  The Romans were routed and subsequently the Senones sacked Rome.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  The common date given for the battle is 390 BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  This is based on the account of the battle by the Roman historian Livy and the Varronian Chronology, a Roman dating system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  The ancient Greek historian Polybius, who used a Greek dating system, derived the date 387/6 BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  Plutarch wrote that the battle took place just after the summer solstice when the moon was near the full, a little more than three hundred and sixty years from the foundation of Rome.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  That would be shortly after 393 BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Allia
Content:  Tacitus said that the battle took place the 15 before the Kalends of August, which is 18 July.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Third Battle of Seoul
Content: The Third Battle of Seoul, also known as the Chinese New Year's Offensive, the January–Fourth Retreat (Korean: 1•4 후퇴 ) or the Third Phase Campaign Western Sector (), was a battle of the Korean War, which took place from December 31, 1950 to January 7, 1951 around the South Korean capital of Seoul.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Third Battle of Seoul
Content:  In the aftermath of the major Chinese victory at the Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River, the United Nations Command started to contemplate the possibility of evacuation from the Korean Peninsula.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Third Battle of Seoul
Content:  Upon learning of this development, China's Chairman Mao Zedong ordered the Chinese People's Volunteer Army to cross the 38th parallel in an effort to pressure the United Nations forces to withdraw from South Korea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Berestechko
Content: The Battle of Berestechko (Polish: "Bitwa pod Beresteczkiem" ; Ukrainian: Берестецька битва, Битва під Берестечком ) was fought between the Ukrainian Cossacks, led by Hetman Bohdan Khmelnytsky, aided by their Crimean Tatar allies, and a Polish army under King John II Casimir.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Berestechko
Content:  It was a battle of a Cossack rebellion in Ukraine that took place in the years 1648–1657 after the expiration of a two-year truce.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Berestechko
Content:  Fought from 28 to 30 June 1651, the battle took place in the province of Volhynia, on the hilly plain south of the Styr River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Berestechko
Content:  The Polish camp was on the river opposite Berestechko and faced south, towards the Cossack army about two kilometers away, whose right flank was against the River Pliashivka (Pliashova) and the Tatar army on their left flank.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Berestechko
Content:  It was probably the largest European land battle of the 17th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content: The Battle of Clitheroe was a battle between a force of Scots and English knights and men at arms which took place on 10 June 1138 during the period of The Anarchy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  The battle was fought on the southern edge of the Bowland Fells, at Clitheroe, Lancashire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  It took place in the course of an invasion of England by King David I of Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  In the summer of 1138, King David split his army into two forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  One of them, commanded by William fitz Duncan, Mormaer of Moray, marched into Lancashire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  There he harried Furness and Craven.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  On 10 June, William fitz Duncan was met by a force of knights and men-at-arms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  A pitched battle took place and the result was that the English army was routed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Clitheroe
Content:  The battle was a prelude to the Battle of the Standard later in the summer, where the result was reversed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River
Content: The Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River, also known as the Battle of the Ch'ongch'on () or the Second Phase Campaign Western Sector (), was a decisive battle in the Korean War, and it took place from November 25 to December 2, 1950, along the Ch'ongch'on River Valley in the northwestern part of North Korea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River
Content:  In response to the successful Chinese First Phase Campaign against the United Nations forces, General Douglas MacArthur launched the Home-by-Christmas Offensive to evict the Chinese forces from Korea and to end the war.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River
Content:  Anticipating this reaction, the Chinese People's Volunteer Army Commander Peng Dehuai planned a counteroffensive, dubbed the "Second Phase Campaign", against the advancing UN forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content: The Battle of Ticinus was a battle of the Second Punic War fought between the Carthaginian forces of Hannibal and the Romans under Publius Cornelius Scipio in November 218 BC.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  The battle took place in the flat country of Pavia county on the right bank of the Ticino River, not far north from its confluence (from the north) with the Po River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  The battle is named from the river, not the nearby contemporaneous settlement of Ticinum (today's Pavia).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  Although the precise location is not known, it is generally accepted that a settlement known today as Vigevano is mentioned in Livy's text and that Scipio's camp was to the south at Gambolo, whose coordinates are given on the map.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  The conflict would have been west of there.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  It was the first battle of the war against the Romans that was fought on Italian soil and the first battle of the war to employ legion-sized forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Battle of Ticinus
Content:  Its loss by the Romans, and the temporary disablement of Scipio's command, set the stage for the Roman disaster at the Battle of Trebbia in December.

Title: Tom Draper
Content: Thomas Edward Draper (nicknamed "Drapes" or in Finland "Toppi") (born November 20, 1966 in Outremont, Quebec) is a retired Canadian professional ice hockey goaltender.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  He was chosen in the eighth round, 165th overall,and the 8th pick by the Winnipeg Jets in the 1985 NHL Entry Draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  On February 28, 1991 the Jets traded Tom to the St. Louis Blues for future considerations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  Which on May 24, 1991 turned out being for Jim Vesey and in the same contract he was traded back to the Winnipeg Jets.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  Then on June 22, 1991 the Buffalo Sabres acquired him from the Jets for the seventh round draft pick in the 1992 entry draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  On September 30, 1993 the Sabres traded him to the New York Islanders for a seventh round draft pick, Steve Plouffe in the 1994 entry draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tom Draper
Content:  He then became a free agent and the Winnipeg Jets picked him up again on December 14, 1995.

Title: Robin Cole
Content: Robin Cole, a former professional American football player, was born September 11, 1955 in Compton, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He was the seventh of ten children born to Obediah and Georgia Mae Cole.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He attended high school at Compton High, graduating in 1973.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He furthered his education at the University of New Mexico where he became an All American and the first person to be a first round draft pick out of the University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He was drafted by the Pittsburgh Steelers, the 21st pick in the first round of the draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  At Pittsburgh, he was a part of the Steel Curtain defense, replacing Andy Russell as right outside linebacker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He played linebacker and defensive end for twelve seasons for the Pittsburgh Steelers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He played in two Super Bowls - Super Bowl XIII and Super Bowl XIV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  Mr. Cole was in the starting lineup in Super Bowl XIV in 1980 and was elected to the Pro Bowl in 1984.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He was runner up for MVP in Super Bowl XIV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robin Cole
Content:  He played one season, 1988, with the New York Jets.

Title: Jeris White
Content: Jeris Jerome White (born September 3, 1952) is a former professional American football cornerback in the National Football League (NFL) for nine seasons for the Miami Dolphins, Tampa Bay Buccaneers, and Washington Redskins.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeris White
Content:  He played football at Radford High School, Honolulu, Hawaii, and at the University of Hawaii.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeris White
Content:  He was drafted by Miami in the second round of the 1974 NFL Draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeris White
Content:  He is the first person from a Hawaii high and collegiate school to ever play in a Super Bowl.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeris White
Content:  White was a holdout when the Redskins opened the 1983 season, so Washington replaced him with its first-round draft pick Darrell Green.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Champ Bailey
Content: Roland "Champ" Bailey Jr. (born June 22, 1978) is a former American football cornerback in the National Football League (NFL).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Champ Bailey
Content:  He played college football for Georgia, where he earned consensus All-American honors, and was drafted by the Washington Redskins in the first round of the 1999 NFL Draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Champ Bailey
Content:  He is the brother of former NFL linebacker Boss Bailey.

Title: Marc Parenteau
Content: Marc Parenteau (born December 4, 1980 in Sherbrooke, Quebec) is a professional Canadian football offensive lineman who last played for the Winnipeg Blue Bombers of the Canadian Football League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  He was drafted 36th overall by the Ottawa Renegades in the 2003 CFL Draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  He was signed by the Saskatchewan Roughriders to a contract on February 12, 2007 and played for five seasons before being released on February 10, 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  He was soon after signed by the Toronto Argonauts on February 13, 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  Parenteau would go on to win the 100th Grey Cup with the Argonauts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  On September 9, 2013, Parenteau was traded to the Winnipeg Blue Bombers, along with a 3rd round draft pick in 2014, in exchange for running back Anthony Woodson and a fifth round draft pick in 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  He retired after 9 years in the CFL in January 2014 after winning 2 Grey Cups (2007 & 2012) and he is the only offensive lineman to ever score a touchdown in a Grey Cup game (2010).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  Before his CFL career, Marc was an All-Star (All Big East) lineman with the Boston College Eagles from 1999-2003.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marc Parenteau
Content:  He continues to be a real estate sales representative in the Ottawa, ON area.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  He played college football for Louisiana State University (LSU), and earned consensus All-American honors.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Title: LaRon Landry

Title: Ron Snidow
Content: Ronald Wayne Snidow (December 30, 1941 – May 17, 2009) was an American football defensive tackle in the National Football League for the Washington Redskins and the Cleveland Browns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  He attended San Rafael High School in California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  He played college football at the University of Oregon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  The Washington Redskins drafted Snidow in the third round of the 1963 NFL draft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  After five seasons with the Redskins, he was traded to the Cleveland Browns in exchange for a second round draft choice, just prior to the opening of the 1968 season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  Snidow was first-team All-Pro with the Browns in 1969.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  He appeared in 126 career regular season games.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  After suffering a broken leg while playing with the Browns, he retired at the end of the 1972 season, having played 10 years in the NFL.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  After retiring from the NFL, Snidow worked as a commercial real estate broker in Southern California, until he retired.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ron Snidow
Content:  In 2008, Snidow was diagnosed with Lou Gehrig's disease, which he died from a year later on May 17, 2009, while on a vacation cruise off the coast of Italy on the island of Elba.

Title: Anthony Woodson
Content: Anthony Woodson (born January 7, 1988) is a professional Canadian football running back for the Calgary Stampeders of the Canadian Football League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Woodson
Content:  He was drafted 29th overall by the Winnipeg Blue Bombers in the 2010 CFL Draft and played for parts of two seasons with the club.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Woodson
Content:  On September 9, 2013, Woodson was traded to the Toronto Argonauts, along with a fifth round draft pick in 2014, in exchange for offensive lineman Marc Parenteau and a third round draft pick in 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anthony Woodson
Content:  He signed with the Hamilton Tiger-Cats as a free agent on February 11, 2015 and spent two years with the team before signing with his hometown Stampeders on February 17, 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content: Fred John Hageman (born June 30, 1937 in Bunkie, Louisiana) is a former American football linebacker in the National Football League for the Washington Redskins.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He played college football at the University of Arkansas and University of Kansas, and was drafted in the 2nd round in 1959 by the Oakland Raiders but did not report and returned to Kansas to finish his undergraduate degree and play out his senior season where he was a 2 time All Big 8 selection as a center and middle linebacker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He was a Tri-Captain and played in 4 post season games including the College All-Star game with numerous All Americans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  Was drafted in the 7th round of the 1960 NFL Draft by the New York Giants and was immediately traded to Washington for cash and a high draft pick.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He was the "Tribe's" defensive leader and starting middle linebacker upon reporting to camp.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He was a runner-up for Rookie of the Year as a middle linebacker and played more minutes than any other player in the NFL in 1961.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  After his first stellar season, he was moved to starting Center where he played at an elite level.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He was traded to the Chicago Bears in 1965, where he was injured in a pre-season game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  Although urged to return by many, Fred returned to Kansas and earned his master's degree in Education.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He went on to a very successful business career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  The "gentle giant" at a huge 6 foot 5 and 255 pounds of solid muscle with world class speed, Fred was named as Kansas University's "Center of the Century" and was named to its first team "All-Time KU Football Team" along with the likes of Gayle Sayers, John Hadl and other NFL greats.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  Known as "Pappy" to many, he led KU's team, to a #2 Ranking and a Big 8 Championship in 1960.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  Some believe the teams he led in 1959 and 1960, along with John Hadl, were the best in KU's history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  Fred was enshrined in the Batesville, Arkansas Area Sports Hall of Fame in 1992.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fred Hageman
Content:  He was the first All-State Athlete at Batesville, H.S.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content: Fun Pier opened up as the Wildwood Convention Hall Pier in 1924 by being leased out by Holly Beach Realty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In 1957 Holly Beach Realty ended there contract with the Wildwood Convention Hall Pier eventually rented the pier to Joe Barnes where he turned it into Fun Pier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  When Barnes first started to rent the pier he had to destroy the entrance of the Wildwood Convention Hall to accommodate enough room to build some new rides.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  The end of the Wildwood Convention Hall which was an arcade and some shops was converted to a dark ride.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  Eventually by the mid 60's the building was torn down.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  The pier eventually got a Monorail, Ski Ride, Sky Tower and some other kiddie rides.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In 1973 the Seascape ride way redeveloped into Castle Frankenstein.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  Later in 1975 the Devil's Inn was transformed into Lost World.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  On November 15, 1976 Joe Barnes sold the pier to the Howard's family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  When he came some renovations came to the pier when he got rid of the classic monorail.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In the 80's the pier added two water slides which would last until the final years of the pier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In 1984 there was a fire in the back of the pier (still unknown the cause) which burned Castle Frankenstein to the beach.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  Later that year on November 24, 1984 the Lost World was completely burned.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  This fire also destroyed the Crazy House, Jet 400, and the bumper cars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In 1984 Fun Pier removed the Sky Tower.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  The pole would remain on the pier until May 6, 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In the 1985 Season the two water slides were removed to make a difference on the pier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  Later on that year Fun Pier would close down.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  In April 1, 1987 the pier was sold to Morey's Piers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fun Pier
Content:  During the 1987 season the Hanneford Family Circus rented the pier.

Title: Bart to the Future
Content: "Bart to the Future" is the seventeenth episode of the eleventh season of the American animated television sitcom "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on March 19, 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  In the episode, after their picnic in the park is cut short due to a mosquito infestation, the Simpsons stop by at an Indian casino.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  There, Bart is prevented from entering because of his age.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  He manages to sneak in but is caught by the guards and sent to the casino manager's office.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  The Native American manager shows Bart a vision of his future as a washed-up, wannabe rock musician living with Ralph Wiggum, while Lisa has become the President of the United States and tries to get the country out of financial trouble.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bart to the Future
Content:  "Bart to the Future" was the second episode of "The Simpsons" after "Lisa's Wedding" to be set in the future.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Back to the Future: The Ride
Content: Back to the Future: The Ride was a simulator ride at Universal Studios theme parks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Back to the Future: The Ride
Content:  It was based on and inspired by the "Back to the Future" film series and is a mini-sequel to 1990's "Back to the Future Part III".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Back to the Future: The Ride
Content:  It was previously located at Universal Studios Florida and Universal Studios Hollywood, where it has since been replaced by "The Simpsons Ride", and at Universal Studios Japan where it has since been replaced by "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ride Tonight!
Content: Ride Tonight!

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ride Tonight!
Content:  (Swedish: Rid i natt) is a 1942 Swedish historical drama film directed by Gustaf Molander and starring Lars Hanson, Oscar Ljung, Gerd Hagman and Eva Dahlbeck.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ride Tonight!
Content:  It is an adaptation of the 1941 novel "Ride This Night" by Vilhelm Moberg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ride Tonight!
Content:  The film, like the original novel, alluded directly to events in occupied Europe during the Second World War and helped to bolster anti-Nazi sentiment in neutral Sweden.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Days of Future Future
Content: "Days of Future Future" is the eighteenth episode of the 25th season of the American animated sitcom "The Simpsons", and the 548th episode of the series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Days of Future Future
Content:  It first aired on the Fox network in the United States on April 13, 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Days of Future Future
Content:  It was written by J. Stewart Burns and directed by Bob Anderson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Days of Future Future
Content:  The episode is a sequel to "Future-Drama" and a continuation of "Holidays of Future Passed", set 30 years from the present.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Days of Future Future
Content:  In this futuristic installment, Bart goes to a clinic to rid himself of his feelings for his ex-wife Jenda (who is now dating a xenomorph-like alien named Jerry), Lisa must choose whether or not to cure her zombie husband Milhouse after he gets bitten by a homeless zombie, and Marge (after putting up with years of Homer dying and being cloned back to life by Professor Frink) loads Homer onto a flatscreen monitor and throws him out of the house.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springfield (Universal Studios Hollywood)
Content: Springfield is a themed area at the Universal Studios Hollywood theme park near Los Angeles, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springfield (Universal Studios Hollywood)
Content:  The area is themed around the fictional town of the same name American animated sitcom, "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Springfield (Universal Studios Hollywood)
Content:  It opened on May 13, 2015 adjacent to The Simpsons Ride.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content: "The Simpsons" Ride is a simulator ride featured at the Universal Studios Florida and Universal Studios Hollywood theme parks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  The ride is based on the animated television series "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  It was first announced in 2007 and replaced the "" at both locations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  The ride at Universal Studios Florida soft opened on April 23, 2008, and the official ceremonies took place on May 15.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  The ride at Universal Studios Hollywood opened on May 19, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  "The Simpsons" Ride was collaborated on by the producers of "The Simpsons", and uses CGI animation, which was provided by Blur Studio and Reel FX.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  2D animation was provided by Film Roman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Simpsons Ride
Content:  The ride uses state of the art technology, including a new projection system and new hydraulics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Entertain Magazine
Content: Entertain Magazine was a British newsstand magazine devoted to Film, DVD, Video Games and Themed Entertainment.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Entertain Magazine
Content:  It was published every two months, with the first issue going on sale in January 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Entertain Magazine
Content:  The publication was notable as the first UK news trade, entertainment magazine to be published on DVD format.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Entertain Magazine
Content:  During its run, "Entertain" featured exclusive video features on "", "Star Trek", "The Simpsons Ride" at Universal Studios Hollywood, The Wizarding World of Harry Potter at Universal Orlando and "Thunderbirds" "Stand By for Action".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Back to the Future Trilogy (soundtrack)
Content: The Back to the Future Trilogy is a 1999 licensed soundtrack to the "Back to the Future" film series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Back to the Future Trilogy (soundtrack)
Content:  It was released under Varèse Sarabande on September 21, 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Back to the Future Trilogy (soundtrack)
Content:  The album consists of numerous Alan Silvestri cues from all three "Back to the Future" films and a rare track from Universal Studios Theme Park's "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Back to the Future Trilogy (soundtrack)
Content:  Whilst the tracks from "Back to the Future Part III" are from the , those for "Part I", "Part II" and "The Ride" are re-recordings performed by John Debney and the Royal Scottish National Orchestra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Duff Beer
Content: Duff Beer is a brand of beer that originally started as a fictional beverage on the animated series "The Simpsons".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Duff Beer
Content:  Since then it has become a real brand of beer in a number of countries without permission or consent from its original creator, Matt Groening, and has resulted in legal battles with varying results.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Duff Beer
Content:  An official version of the beer is sold in three variations near the Simpsons Ride at Universal Studios.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Duff Beer
Content:  In 2015, 21st Century Fox, producer of "The Simpsons", began selling licensed Duff beer in Chile, with a view to driving out unlicensed brandjacking.

Title: Catholic Discipline
Content: Catholic Discipline was an American punk rock band from Los Angeles, California, formed by "Slash Fanzine" editor Claude Bessy in 1979.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catholic Discipline
Content:  The initial line-up of the band featured Bessy on vocals, Phranc on guitar, Rick Brodey on bass guitar, Richard Meade on keyboards and Craig Lee on drums.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Crimson
Content: King Crimson are an English rock band formed in London in 1968.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Crimson
Content:  The band has undergone numerous formations throughout its history of which 21 musicians have been members; since January 2017 it has consisted of Robert Fripp, Jakko Jakszyk, Tony Levin, Mel Collins, Pat Mastelotto, Gavin Harrison, Bill Rieflin and Jeremy Stacey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Crimson
Content:  Fripp is the only consistent member of the group, and is considered the band's leader and driving force.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Crimson
Content:  The band has earned a large cult following.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Crimson
Content:  They were ranked No. 87 on VH1's "100 Greatest Artists of Hard Rock".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nomeansno
Content: Nomeansno (sometimes stylized as NoMeansNo or spelled No Means No) was a Canadian punk rock band formed in Victoria, British Columbia and later relocated to Vancouver.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nomeansno
Content:  They issued ten LPs, one collaborative LP, numerous EPs and singles, and gained an international audience following extensive touring.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nomeansno
Content:  Critic Martin Popoff described their music as "the mightiest merger between the hateful aggression of punk and the discipline of heavy metal."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nomeansno
Content:  Nomeansno's distinct hardcore punk sound, complex instrumentation, and dark, "savagely intelligent" lyrics inspired subsequent musicians.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nomeansno
Content:  They are often considered foundational in the punk jazz and post-hardcore movements, and have been cited as a formative influence on the math rock and emo genres.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (band)
Content: Discipline is a United States progressive rock band formed in 1987 by singer/songwriter Matthew Parmenter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (band)
Content:  Based in Detroit, Michigan the band has released five studio albums, two live albums, a live DVD, and a live concert motion picture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (band)
Content:  Discipline may be best known for their 1997 release "Unfolded Like Staircase."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (instrumental)
Content: "Discipline" is an instrumental composition by the progressive rock band King Crimson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (instrumental)
Content:  It is the title track on "Discipline", the band's return album after a seven-year hiatus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Discipline (instrumental)
Content:  The piece is about five minutes in length and serves as the album's conclusion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sandy and Beaver Canal
Content: The Sandy and Beaver Canal ran 73 mi from the Ohio and Erie Canal at Bolivar, Ohio, to the Ohio River at Glasgow, Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sandy and Beaver Canal
Content:  It had 90 locks, was chartered in 1828 and completed in 1848.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sandy and Beaver Canal
Content:  However, the middle section of the canal had many problems from the beginning and fell into disrepair.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sandy and Beaver Canal
Content:  The canal ceased to operate in 1852, when the Cold Run Reservoir Dam outside of Lisbon, Ohio, broke, ruining a large portion of the canal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content: Owensmouth, California was a town founded in 1912 in the Western part of the San Fernando Valley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content:  Owensmouth joined the city of Los Angeles in 1917, and was renamed Canoga Park on March 1, 1931.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content:  Owensmouth was named for the 1913 Owens River aqueduct's terminus in current Canoga Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content:  The town was started by the Los Angeles Suburban Homes Company as part of an extraordinary real estate development in Southern California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content:  Los Angeles Suburban Homes Company was owned by a syndicate of rich Los Angeles investors, developers, and speculators: including Harrison Gray Otis, Harry Chandler, Moses Sherman, Hobart Johnstone Whitley, and others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content:  It anticipated possible connections to but was planned independent of the soon to be completed (1913) Los Angeles Aqueduct from the Owens River watershed to the City of Los Angeles through the San Fernando Valley in Los Angeles County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth
Content: The newly built Sherman Way double drive and the Pacific Electric street cars, opened on December 7, 1912, gave new access to the town and to the other new towns in the valley Van Nuys (1911) and Marion (now Reseda);

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Eaton
Content: Frederick Eaton (1856 – March 11, 1934), known as Fred Eaton, was a major individual in the transformation and expansion of Los Angeles in the latter 19th century through early 20th century, in California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Eaton
Content:  Eaton was the political mastermind behind the early 20th century Los Angeles Aqueduct project, designed by William Mulholland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rush Creek (Mono County, California)
Content: Rush Creek is a 27.2 mi creek in California on the eastern slope of the Sierra Nevada, running east and then northeast to Mono Lake.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rush Creek (Mono County, California)
Content:  Rush Creek is the largest stream in the Mono Basin, carrying 41% of the total runoff.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rush Creek (Mono County, California)
Content:  It was extensively diverted by the Los Angeles Aqueduct system in the twentieth century until California Trout, Inc., the National Audubon Society, and the Mono Lake Committee sued Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) for continuous low flows in Rush Creek to maintain trout populations in good condition, which was ordered by the court in 1985.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaver Creek State Park
Content: Beaver Creek State Park is a 2722 acre Ohio state park in Columbiana County, Ohio in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaver Creek State Park
Content:  The park is near East Liverpool on the banks of Little Beaver Creek.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaver Creek State Park
Content:  It is open for year-round recreation including, camping, boating, hunting, fishing and hiking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaver Creek State Park
Content:  Historic remnants of the Sandy and Beaver Canal can be found throughout the park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beaver Creek State Park
Content:  Confederate General John Hunt Morgan was captured near what is now the park after conducting raids across the state during the American Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth (Pacific Electric)
Content: The Pacific Electric streetcar service to Owensmouth (present day Canoga Park) was part of an extraordinary real estate development in Southern California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth (Pacific Electric)
Content:  Nearly the entire southern San Fernando Valley was bought in 1910 by the Los Angeles Suburban Homes Co., owned by a syndicate of rich Los Angeles investors, developers, and speculators: including Harrison Gray Otis, Harry Chandler, Moses Sherman, Hobart Johnstone Whitley, and others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Owensmouth (Pacific Electric)
Content:  It anticipated possible connections to but was planned independent of the soon to be completed (1913) Los Angeles Aqueduct from the Owens River watershed to the City of Los Angeles through the San Fernando Valley in Los Angeles County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct
Content: The Los Angeles Aqueduct system, comprising the Los Angeles Aqueduct (Owens Valley aqueduct) and the Second Los Angeles Aqueduct, is a water conveyance system, built and operated by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct
Content:  The Owens Valley aqueduct was designed and built by the city's water department, at the time named The Bureau of Los Angeles Aqueduct, under the supervision of the department's Chief Engineer William Mulholland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct
Content:  The system delivers water from the Owens River in the Eastern Sierra Nevada Mountains to Los Angeles, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct
Content:  In 1971 it was recognized by the American Society of Civil Engineers on the List of Historic Civil Engineering Landmarks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St. Francis Dam
Content: The St. Francis Dam was a curved concrete gravity dam, built to create a large regulating and storage reservoir for the city of Los Angeles, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St. Francis Dam
Content:  The reservoir was an integral part of the city's Los Angeles Aqueduct water supply infrastructure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St. Francis Dam
Content:  It was located in San Francisquito Canyon of the Sierra Pelona Mountains, about 40 mi northwest of Downtown Los Angeles, and approximately 10 mi north of the present day city of Santa Clarita.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Mulholland
Content: William Mulholland (September 11, 1855 – July 22, 1935) was responsible for building the infrastructure to provide a water supply that allowed Los Angeles to grow into one of the largest cities in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Mulholland
Content:  As the head of a predecessor to the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power, Mulholland designed and supervised the building of the Los Angeles Aqueduct, a 233 mi -long system to move water from Owens Valley to the San Fernando Valley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Mulholland
Content:  The creation and operation of the aqueduct led to the disputes known as the California Water Wars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Mulholland
Content:  In March 1928, Mulholland's career came to an end when the St. Francis Dam failed just over 12 hours after he and his assistant gave it a safety inspection.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dry Canyon Reservoir
Content: Dry Canyon Reservoir is a small reservoir formed by an embankment dam on Dry Canyon Creek in the Sierra Pelona Mountains of northern Los Angeles County, California, just over 6 mi north the city of Santa Clarita.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dry Canyon Reservoir
Content:  It was designed a part of the Los Angeles Aqueduct system.

Content:  It is the oldest continuously operating institution of higher education in Hungary (since 1538).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Pole–West Pole divide
Content: The East Pole–West Pole divide in the fields of cognitive psychology and cognitive neuroscience is an intellectual schism between researchers subscribing to the nativist and empiricist schools of thought.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Pole–West Pole divide
Content:  The term arose from the fact that much of the theory and research supporting nativism, modularity of mind, and computational theory of mind originated at several universities located on the East Coast, including Harvard University, the University of Michigan, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, and Tufts University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Pole–West Pole divide
Content:  Conversely, much of the research and theory supporting empiricism, emergentism, and embodied cognition originated at several universities located on the West Coast, including the University of California, Berkeley, the Salk Institute, and, most notably, the University of California, San Diego.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Pole–West Pole divide
Content:  In reality, the divide is not so clear, with many universities and scholars on both coasts (as well as the Midwest and around the world) supporting each position, as well as more moderate positions in between the two extremes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: East Pole–West Pole divide
Content:  The phrase was coined by Jerry Fodor at an MIT conference on cognition, at which he referred to another researcher as a "West Coast theorist," apparently unaware that the researcher worked at Yale University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bond Market Association
Content: The Bond Market Association (TBMA, previously Public Securities Association or PSA until 1997) was the international trade association for the bond market industry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bond Market Association
Content:  It had headquarters in New York City and offices in London and Washington, D.C. Twenty per cent of the membership was located outside the United States, while 70 per cent was located outside New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bond Market Association
Content:  TBMA acted as a global voice for bond issuers and traders, and co-ordinated with governments, corporations, and investors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bond Market Association
Content:  It also had a code of ethics, which required members to behave in a fashion of fairness.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bond Market Association
Content:  On November 1, 2006, The Bond Market Association merged with the Securities Industry Association to form the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Massachusetts International Academy

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  As a result of a Royal Decree signed by Philip V of Spain, the Central University of Venezuela—the country's oldest—was founded in 1721 as "Universidad Real y Pontificia de Caracas".

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Polytech'Paris-UPMC
Content: Within the French University "Pierre et Marie Curie" (Paris VI) there is an engineering school offering courses backed by research laboratories and leading to a state-recognised degree.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Polytech'Paris-UPMC
Content:  The Polytech'Paris-UPMC is a member of the Polytech' Network, which is a network of 11 graduate engineering schools within France's leading science universities located in Marseille, Nantes, Montpellier, Nice, Grenoble, Paris, Chambéry, Clermont-Ferrand, Orléans, Tours and Lille.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commonwealth System of Higher Education
Content: The Commonwealth System of Higher Education is a statutory designation by the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania that confers "state-related" status on four universities located within the state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commonwealth System of Higher Education
Content:  The designation establishes the schools as an "instrumentality of the commonwealth" and provides each university with annual, non-preferred financial appropriations in exchange offering tuition discounts to students that are residents of Pennsylvania and a minority state-representation on each school's board of trustees.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commonwealth System of Higher Education
Content:  Legally, however, the universities remain separate and private entities, operating under their own charters, governed by independent boards of trustees, and with its assets under their own ownership and control thereby retaining much of the freedom and individuality of private institutions, both administratively and academically.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Commonwealth System of Higher Education
Content:  It is the only public-private hybrid system of higher education in the United States that is so construed, although Cornell University, the University of Delaware, and Rutgers University represent alternative types of public-private university hybrids.

Title: Acute HME syndrome
Content: In many districts of western Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Odisha and Haryana, India, outbreaks of an acute “encephalopathy” syndrome dubbed as a “mysterious disease” have been an annual feature for many years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Acute HME syndrome
Content:  The disease affects rural young children during the winter months of September to December, and carries case fatality rates of around 75-80%.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Acute HME syndrome
Content:  Most investigators assume it to be viral “encephalitis”.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Acute HME syndrome
Content:  According to a rough estimate, at least 500-700 young previously healthy children have been losing their lives every year for many years in this region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Acute HME syndrome
Content:  Many national investigating agencies failed to diagnose the entity despite investigating for many consecutive years.

Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content: Alcoholic hallucinosis (or alcohol-related psychosis or alcohol-induced psychotic disorder) is a complication of alcohol withdrawal in alcoholics. Descriptions of the condition date back to at least 1907.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content:  They can occur during acute intoxication or withdrawal with the potential of having delirium tremens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content:  Alcohol hallucinosis is a rather uncommon alcohol-induced psychotic disorder only being seen in chronic alcoholics who have many consecutive years of severe and heavy drinking during their lifetime.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content:  Alcoholic hallucinosis develops about 12 to 24 hours after the heavy drinking stops suddenly, and can last for days.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content:  It involves auditory and visual hallucinations, most commonly accusatory or threatening voices.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alcoholic hallucinosis
Content:  The risk of developing alcoholic hallucinosis is increased by long-term heavy alcohol abuse and the use of other drugs.

Content: Letters is the fourth album by American singer-songwriter Jimmy Webb, released in 1972 by Reprise Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content: Letters was a more sedate, piano-oriented album than its predecessors, and soaked in the influence of Webb's peer and eventual close friend Joni Mitchell.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  "I was tremendously influenced by Joni Mitchell," he admitted to Peter Doggett of Record Collector in 1994.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  "She was a good friend, and I was fortunate enough to be around her when she was working on For the Roses and Court and Spark.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  We were just part of each other's lives for a while.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  I definitely envied that part of her work -- the idea that this is just a conversation you're listening in on.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  It can still be poetry, but not self-conscious or forced poetry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  I got extremely under her spell as a writer -- I still am.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letters (Jimmy Webb album)
Content:  I used to go to the studio and listen to her record, sit quietly in the back of the control room.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  After the Beatles, Joni was the next big blip on my radar screen, in terms of, 'Hey, pay attention: this girl is doing something a little bit different.'"

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Mitchell's longtime engineer Henry Lewy also did some engineering work for Letters as well.

Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content: Let There Be Love is Joni James debut album, recorded in 1953 and released by MGM Records at the end of the year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  It was released in a four-disc 10-inch 78-rpm record box, in both a two-disc 7-inch 45-rpm extended-play foldout album and a four-disc 45-rpm regular-play box and on a 10-inch 33⅓-rpm album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  The serial number, 222, coincidentally included James's lucky number, "22," which appeared in many of her record serial numbers all over the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  The album is the first to present its songs as a book in music, opening with "Let There Be Love" and closing with "I'll Be Seeing You", with the songs telling a story start to finish.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  The memorable cover was done at M-G-M Pictures Studios in Culver City by artist Russ Gale.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  From the album a single of "Let There Be Love" and "You're Nearer" was shipped to radio stations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  Then, by public demand, a single of "You're My Everything" and "You're Nearer" was released.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  This album offered Joni's second recording of "Let There Be Love," which had been released in an earlier version in 1952 as her first single on Sharp Records in Chicago, then going to M-G-M Records for further distribution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  Both recordings were arranged and conducted by Lew Douglas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  "Let There Be Love" went to the top of the album charts and was the sixth-best-selling album of 1953.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  In 1956 the album was reissued as a 12-inch L.P. and in three single EPs; there was no EP set including the entire album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  For this release, four Joni James singles were added, but one of them had never been released.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  That was "I Need You Now," which was to have followed the hit "My Love, My Love" but was canceled when Eddie Fisher came out with a version recorded several weeks after Joni's.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  M-G-M had expected Joni's original version to go straight to #1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  For the new album the first four songs and second four songs were switched to get "You're My Everything" in the outside groove of the L.P. to facilitate disc jockey play.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  So, for 50 years, the story the album tells has been garbled.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  In 1961 the album got new cover art, a new serial number (E3931), and an electronically simulated stereo release.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  Released again on compact disk with yet more bonus tracks, the album is in its fifth decade as a best seller.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  Joni James hopes for yet another release which will restore the original song order.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  Significantly, for her last M-G-M album, "Bossa Nova Style", Joni included new recordings of several songs from "Let There Be Love", including a new single of "You're Nearer."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  That album was arranged by Lew Douglas's protégé Chuck Sagle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Let There Be Love (1953 Joni James album)
Content:  This information comes from Wayne Brasler, longtime President of the Joni James International Fan Club and the writer of the album notes for all Joni James' CD releases.

session_0.text:

Content:  The season started well before taking a turn for the worse when the club was placed into administration at the end of January 2010, culminating in manager Neil Warnock leaving his job just over a month later and being replaced by Paul Hart, whose tenure saw the club survive relegation on the final day of the season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2010–11 Crystal Palace F.C. season
Content:  The CPFC 2010 consortium completed a takeover of the club in the close season and installed former Scotland manager George Burley as the club's new boss, with club legend Dougie Freedman continuing his role as assistant manager.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  However, after a poor start to the season, Burley was sacked on New Year's Day and Freedman named manager the following week.

Title: David Bradley (UK journalist)
Content:  After graduating in 1988 with a degree in Chemistry from Newcastle University, he began his career in technical editing at the Royal Society of Chemistry in 1989 and built up a freelance writing business in his spare time before going full-time freelance in the mid-1990s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: Li Sizhong (ichthyologist)
Content: Li Sizhong (; 2/19 1921 – 1/11, 2009) was an ichthyologist with the Institute of Zoology (中国科学院动物研究所 ) at the Chinese Academy of Sciences.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Li Sizhong (ichthyologist)
Content:  Throughout his research career, he made numerous discoveries of new fish species (or subspecies), and published many books and research papers describing the fauna and geographical distribution of fishes in China and beyond.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Li Sizhong (ichthyologist)
Content:  He translated and helped publication of the Chinese editions of "Fishes of the World" (2nd edition, by Joseph S. Nelson) and "Fish Migration" (a popular science book by Russian zoologist ).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Li Sizhong (ichthyologist)
Content:  Li was the major author of two published volumes in the Fauna Sinica monograph series, systematically reviewing and describing orders of bony fishes that include flat fish, cod, silverside, pearlfish, killifish, flying fish, etc. in or near China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Li Sizhong (ichthyologist)
Content:  He had written over 40 popular science articles about fish on Chinese newspapers and magazines, and been responsible for compiling and editing fish-related entries in several standard reference books (including Encyclopedia of China).

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Pavel Iustinovich Marikovsky
Content: Pavel Iustinovich Marikovsky (Russian: Павел Иустинович Мариковский ; July 28, 1912 – November 10, 2008) was a Russian entomologist, arachnologist, and popular science author known for his research in ants and the insect fauna of Kazakhstan and Central Asia, as well as over 60 popular science books, which made him one of the most popular science promoters of the former Soviet Union.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pavel Iustinovich Marikovsky
Content:  His 1954 monograph "Tarantula and Karakurt" ("Тарантул и Каракурт ") is still used by zoologists, and his most popular general science books include "For the Young Entomologist", "In Talas Alatau" and "Across the Semirechye".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pavel Iustinovich Marikovsky
Content:  He served in the Great Patriotic War (the Eastern Front of World War II), earning several honors including Order of the Red Star, Order of the Patriotic War, and Order of the Badge of Honour.

Title: Popular Science
Content: Popular Science (also known as PopSci) is an American bi-monthly magazine carrying popular science content, which refers to articles for the general reader on science and technology subjects.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular Science
Content:  "Popular Science" has won over 58 awards, including the American Society of Magazine Editors awards for its journalistic excellence in both 2003 (for General Excellence) and 2004 (for Best Magazine Section).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular Science
Content:  With roots beginning in 1872, "Popular Science" has been translated into over 30 languages and is distributed to at least 45 countries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular science
Content: Popular science (also pop-science or popsci) is interpretation of science intended for a general audience.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular science
Content:  While science journalism focuses on recent scientific developments, popular science is more broad-ranging.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular science
Content:  It may be written by professional science journalists or by scientists themselves.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Popular science
Content:  It is presented in many forms, including books, film and television documentaries, magazine articles, and web pages.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Damien Broderick
Content: Damien Francis Broderick (born 22 April 1944) is an Australian science fiction and popular science writer and editor of some 70 books.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Damien Broderick
Content:  His science fiction novel "The Dreaming Dragons" (1980) introduced the trope of the generation time machine, his "The Judas Mandala" (1982) contains the first appearance of the term "virtual reality," and his 1997 popular science book The Spike was the first to investigate the technological Singularity in detail.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Astrobiology Magazine
Content: Astrobiology Magazine (exploring the solar system and beyond), or Astrobiology Mag, is an American NASA-sponsored international online popular science magazine containing popular science content, which refers to articles for the general reader on science and technology subjects.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Astrobiology Magazine
Content:  The magazine reports on missions of NASA and other space agencies, as well as presents news of relevant research conducted by various institutions, universities, and non-profit groups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Astrobiology Magazine
Content:  In addition, the magazine provides a forum through which researchers and the general public can oversee the progress made in fields of study that are associated with the science of astrobiology.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Astrobiology Magazine
Content:  According to Phys.org, the magazine has a "vast archive of stories covering a broad array of topics ... [and] covers science and nature topics relevant to space, innovation and biology, with an emphasis on the existence, detection and exploration of life in the universe".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Astrobiology Magazine
Content:  The magazine was created by Helen Matsos, presently Chief Editor and Executive Producer, and began publication in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System

Content: The 2016 MTV Movie Awards was held on June 15, 2016 from Warner Brothers Studios in Burbank, California, as the first such event in 21 years as well as being the first Movie Awards to be held outdoors.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: 2016 MTV Movie Awards
Content:  In addition, this year also became the first since the 2006 MTV Movie Awards not to be aired live as the event was pre-recorded on April 9 prior to its April 10 date and the first since the 2003 MTV Movie Awards to include two hosts instead of one.

Title: Newbold Verdon
Content: Newbold Verdon is a village and civil parish in the county of Leicestershire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Newbold Verdon
Content:  The parish includes Newbold Heath to the north and Brascote to the south.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Newbold Verdon
Content:  Originally an agricultural centre Newbold Verdon grew in size during the 1850s with the expansion of coal mining in the area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Newbold Verdon
Content:  That industry has now ceased leaving Newbold Verdon as primarily a commuter village.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Newbold Verdon
Content:  The 2001 census recorded a population of 3,193, reducing to 3,012 at the 2011 census.

Content: Whetstone is a village and civil parish in the Blaby district of Leicestershire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It has a population of 12,000 and largely acts as a commuter village for Leicester, five miles to the north.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whetstone, Leicestershire
Content:  The population at the 2011 census was 6,556.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Its proximity to Leicester causes Whetstone to form part of the Leicester Urban Area.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Wood End, Kingsbury, Warwickshire
Content: Wood End is an old mining village in North Warwickshire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wood End, Kingsbury, Warwickshire
Content:  It lies to the south east of Tamworth and close to the border with Staffordshire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wood End, Kingsbury, Warwickshire
Content:  It grew around the former Kingsbury Colliery but now it serves as a commuter village to Tamworth.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It has a church, a primary school, a co-operative store, a working men's club and a village hall.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wood End, Kingsbury, Warwickshire
Content:  The population of Wood End is 2,205, but from the 2011 Census has been included in Kingsbury, Warwickshire.

Title: Woodhouse, Leicestershire
Content: Woodhouse, often known to locals as Old Woodhouse, is a small village in the heart of Charnwood, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Woodhouse, Leicestershire
Content:  The population of the civil parish at the 2011 census was 2,319, including around 300 term-time boarders at the Defence College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Woodhouse, Leicestershire
Content:  Located between the larger Woodhouse Eaves and Quorn villages, the village contains a mixture of small cottages and large modern houses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Woodhouse, Leicestershire
Content:  It is a commuter village for both Leicester and Loughborough, as well as further afield.

Title: Backford Cross
Content: Backford Cross is a village on the Wirral Peninsula, Cheshire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Backford Cross
Content:  It is a suburb of the town of Ellesmere Port and part of Cheshire West and Chester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Backford Cross
Content:  Backford Cross is located around the A41/A5117 junction, south of Great Sutton and about 1.5 mi north of the village of Backford, near Chester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Backford Cross
Content:  Backford Cross is largely made up of residential homes built from 1990 onwards and serves as a commuter village to Ellesmere Port and Chester, although inhabitants show no allegiance to either locality.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Backford Cross
Content:  The area is split between postcode districts, with parts of the village in Great Sutton, Ellesmere Port CH66 and other areas in Backford, Chester CH1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bracebridge Heath
Content: Bracebridge Heath is a commuter village located approximately 2 mi south from the city and county town of Lincoln, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bracebridge Heath
Content:  It lies at the junction of two major roads the A15 to Sleaford and the A607 to Grantham, and was (until modern systems of local government were introduced in the 19th century) part of the Boothby Graffoe Wapentake.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bracebridge Heath
Content:  The village sits on top of Lincoln Cliff, overlooking Lincoln and the valley of the River Witham.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bracebridge Heath
Content:  The Viking Way runs along the cliff top, a 147 mi long footpath, which runs from the Humber Bridge to Oakham.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bracebridge Heath
Content:  Its population at the 2001 census was 4,530, increasing to 5,656 at the 2011 census.

session_0.text:
Title: Robey-Peters Gun-Carrier
Content: The Robey-Peters Gun-Carrier was a British three-seater armed tractor biplane designed and built by Robey & Company Limited at Bracebridge Heath, Lincoln for the Royal Naval Air Service (RNAS).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content: Markfield is a commuter village in both the National Forest and Charnwood Forest and in the Hinckley and Bosworth district of Leicestershire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  The settlement dates back to at least the time of the Norman conquest and is mentioned in the Domesday Book under the name Merchenefeld.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  A variant of this is still used as the name for the village primary school, Mercenfeld.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  It is to the south-east of Junction 22 of the M1, and to the south of the A50.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  The highest point in Markfield is shown on OS sheet 129 at 222 metres above sea level.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  Nearby places are Newtown Linford, Groby, Field Head, and Stanton under Bardon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  In the 1841 census its population was recorded at 1,203.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  In the 2011 census the parish had a population of 5681.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  Markfield is within the LE67 postcode district.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Markfield
Content:  In 2012 Hinckley & Bosworth Borough Council published an overview of Markfield conservation area.

Title: Waddington, Lincolnshire

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Waddington, Lincolnshire
