session_0.text:
Title: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
Content: Johann Gottlieb Fichte ( ; ] ; May 19, 1762 – January 27, 1814), was a German philosopher who became a founding figure of the philosophical movement known as German idealism, which developed from the theoretical and ethical writings of Immanuel Kant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
Content:  Recently, philosophers and scholars have begun to appreciate Fichte as an important philosopher in his own right due to his original insights into the nature of self-consciousness or self-awareness.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
Content:  Fichte was also the originator of "thesis–antithesis–synthesis", an idea that is often erroneously attributed to Hegel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
Content:  Like Descartes and Kant before him, Fichte was motivated by the problem of subjectivity and consciousness.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
Content:  Fichte also wrote works of political philosophy; he has a reputation as one of the fathers of German nationalism.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahmad Fardid
Content: Seyyed Ahmad Fardid (Persian: سید احمد فردید‎ ‎ ) (Born in 1912, Yazd – 16 August 1994, Tehran), born Ahmad Mahini Yazdi, was a prominent Iranian philosopher and an inspiring and dedicated professor of Tehran University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahmad Fardid
Content:  He is considered to be among the philosophical ideologues of the Islamic government of Iran which came to power in 1979.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahmad Fardid
Content:  Fardid was under the influence of Martin Heidegger, the influential German philosopher, whom he considered "the only Western philosopher who understood the world and the only philosopher whose insights were congruent with the principles of the Islamic Republic.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahmad Fardid
Content:  These two figures, Khomeini and Heidegger, helped Fardid argue his position."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ahmad Fardid
Content:  What he decried was the anthropocentrism and rationalism brought by classical Greece, replacing the authority of God and faith with human reason, and in that regard he also criticized Islamic philosophers like al Farabi and Mulla Sadra for having absorbed Greek philosophy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends
Content: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends (リトル・ルルとちっちゃい仲間 , Ritoru Ruru to Chitchai Nakama ) is a 26-episode Japanese anime television series produced by Nippon Animation which aired on ABC and NET from October 3, 1976 to April 3, 1977.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends
Content:  The series was directed by Fumio Kurokawa and featuring voice actresses Eiko Masuyama and Minori Matsushima as the voice of Lulu.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends
Content:  It is also based on Little Lulu comics by American cartoonist Marjorie Henderson Buell.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends
Content:  An English-dubbed version of the anime was made for the American market by ZIV International in 1978, and the series was also released in Italian, German, Hebrew, Arabic, Spanish, and Brazilian Portuguese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Little Lulu and Her Little Friends
Content:  The "Little Lulu" anime is extremely rare and has been out of print in the United States for years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content: Karl W. Aschenbrenner (November 20, 1911 in Bison, Kansas – July 4, 1988 in Budapest, Hungary) was an American philosopher, translator (into English of works in Latin and German) and prominent American specialist in analytic philosophy and aesthetics, author and editor of more than 48 publications including five monographs, 27 articles and 16 book reviews.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  His principal academic post was at the University of California, Berkeley in the Department of Philosophy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  Aschenbrenner co-edited, with Arnold Isenberg, a collection of essays on the subject of aesthetic theory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  As co-translator with William B. Holther, Aschenbrenner published the principal work of Alexander Gottlieb Baumgarten and, with Donald Nicholl, assisted in completing the second edition of an important work of the German philosopher Joseph M. Bocheński.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  He is particularly noted for his authoritative commentary on the Kritik der Reinen Vernunft of Immanuel Kant as well as the commentary he and Nicholl supplied in their translation of Baumgarten’s "Meditationes philosophicae de nonnullis ad poema pertinentibus" introducing that work.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  Except for his sabbaticals, Aschenbrenner resided in Berkeley, California from 1943 to 1986 and in Los Angeles from 1986 to 1988.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  During sabbatical leaves Aschenbrenner taught at the Universität Wien, University College London and the Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  He remained Professor Emeritus at the University of California at Berkeley until his death in 1988.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  Aschenbrenner died in Budapest while doing research and is buried in Farkasréti Cemetery in that city.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Karl Aschenbrenner
Content:  The Aschenbrenner papers are held by the Doe Library of the University of California at Berkeley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Theodor W. Adorno
Content: Theodor W. Adorno ( ; ] ; born Theodor Ludwig Wiesengrund; September 11, 1903 – August 6, 1969) was a German philosopher, sociologist, and composer known for his critical theory of society.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christian Wolff (philosopher)
Content: Christian Wolff (less correctly Wolf, ] ; also known as Wolfius; ennobled as Christian Freiherr von Wolff; 24 January 1679 – 9 April 1754) was a German philosopher.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christian Wolff (philosopher)
Content:  Wolff was the most eminent German philosopher between Leibniz and Kant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Christian Wolff (philosopher)
Content:  His main achievement was a complete "oeuvre" on almost every scholarly subject of his time, displayed and unfolded according to his demonstrative-deductive, mathematical method, which perhaps represents the peak of Enlightenment rationality in Germany.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Valerie Spencer
Content: Valerie Spencer is a fictional character from "General Hospital", an American soap opera on the ABC network, portrayed by Brytni Sarpy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Valerie Spencer
Content:  Introduced as the niece of the legendary Luke Spencer (Anthony Geary), Valerie is the daughter of the late Pat Spencer (Dee Wallace).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Valerie Spencer
Content:  Upon losing her mother, Valerie relocates to Port Charles and moves in with her cousin, Lulu Spencer (Emme Rylan), and bonds with her husband Dante Falconeri (Dominic Zamprogna).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Valerie Spencer
Content:  The two end up having a one-night stand, only for Dante to reconcile with Lulu and Valerie briefly dates Lulu's ex-boyfriend Dillon Quartermaine (Robert Palmer Watkins).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nietzsche: Philosopher, Psychologist, Antichrist
Content: Nietzsche: Philosopher, Psychologist, Antichrist is a book about the German philosopher Friedrich Nietzsche by the philosopher Walter Kaufmann, first published in 1950 by Princeton University Press, with revised editions in 1956, 1968, and 1974.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nietzsche: Philosopher, Psychologist, Antichrist
Content:  The book was republished by Princeton University Press in 2013, with a new introduction by Alexander Nehamas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Todtnauberg
Content: Todtnauberg is a German village in Black Forest ("Schwarzwald") belonging to the municipality of Todtnau, in Baden-Württemberg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Todtnauberg
Content:  It is named after the homonym mount ("berg" means hill or mountain in German) and is famous because it is the place where the German philosopher Martin Heidegger had a chalet and wrote portions of his major work, "Being and Time".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lulu (opera)
Content: Lulu (composed from 1929–1935, premièred incomplete in 1937 and complete in 1979) is an opera in three acts by Alban Berg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lulu (opera)
Content:  The German-language libretto was adapted by Berg himself from Frank Wedekind's two "Lulu" plays, "Erdgeist" ("Earth Spirit", 1895) and "Die Büchse der Pandora" ("Pandora's Box", 1904).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lulu (opera)
Content:  Berg died before completing the third and final act, and in the following decades, the opera was typically performed incomplete.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lulu (opera)
Content:  Since its publication in 1979, however, the Friedrich Cerha orchestration has become popular.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lulu (opera)
Content:  Theodor W. Adorno wrote "The opera "Lulu" is one of those works that reveals the extent of its quality the longer and more deeply one immerses oneself in it."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sanctuary (band)
Content: Sanctuary is an American heavy metal band founded in Seattle in 1985.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sanctuary (band)
Content:  They split up in 1992, but reformed 18 years later.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sanctuary (band)
Content:  The band consists of Warrel Dane (vocals), Lenny Rutledge (guitar), Nick Cordle (guitar), George Hernandez (bass), and Dave Budbill (drums).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sanctuary (band)
Content:  To date, they have released four studio albums and one live EP.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content: Dreaming Neon Black is the third full-length album released by Seattle progressive metal band Nevermore, and was released through Century Media in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  Unlike its predecessor, "The Politics of Ecstasy", this album is very emotional and contains many slower, ballad-type songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  It is also notable that "Dreaming Neon Black" is a concept album of sorts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  According to Nevermore's lead singer, Warrel Dane, "it's a very simple story about a man who slowly goes insane after losing a woman that he was very close to.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  Progressive levels of insanity are expressed in the songs, he goes through phases of denial and self-blame, blaming God, then denouncing God.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  The ending is a little...tragic, a little depressing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  Shakesperian.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  Everybody dies, it's all happy."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content: , which may be based on an event in the life of Dane.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  Supposedly, his old girlfriend left him when she joined a religious cult and was never heard from again, and he began having nightmares of her crying out to him as she drowned.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  This has been confirmed by Warrel himself in an older interview.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreaming Neon Black
Content:  The spoken word samples from the tracks "Ophidian" and "Forever" are from the Clive Barker movie, "Lord of Illusions".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content: Into The Mirror Live / Black Reflections is live promotional EP of the band Sanctuary recorded on May 12, 1990 at The Country Club in Reseda, California, during their "Into the Mirror Black" tour.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  Approximately 1000 CD copies of this were made and less than 500 were ever distributed to radio stations and record stores.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  Because of contractual problems with Epic Records, the full version was never released for public consumption.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  Epic Records destroyed 500+ copies of this CD.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  Warrel Dane has stated on several occasions that he has the master tapes and copyrights of the full show and intended to have it finally released.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  To date that has not happened.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Into the Mirror Live
Content:  This CD has been heavily pirated over the years as a 2-on-1 CD with the Satan's Host "Metal From Hell" album on the notorious Reborn Classics record label.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore (album)
Content: Nevermore is the eponymous debut studio album by American heavy metal band Nevermore, released on February 14, 1995 by Century Media Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore (album)
Content:  It was singer Warrel Dane and bassist Jim Sheppard's first release after departing from their previous band, Sanctuary, in 1994.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore (album)
Content:  Having recruited drummer Van Williams and former Sanctuary touring guitarist Jeff Loomis, they formed the band Nevermore and began anew.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore
Content: Nevermore is an American progressive metal band from Seattle, Washington, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore
Content:  Formed in 1991, they are known to incorporate elements from various styles such as power metal, modern hard rock, classic heavy metal and technical thrash metal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore
Content:  The band has been on hiatus since 2011, due to personal issues between the band members.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nevermore
Content:  In April 2015, lead singer Warrel Dane confirmed that Nevermore has not disbanded, and there is a possibility for them to continue in the next two years with another album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Year of the Voyager
Content: The Year Of The Voyager is a double DVD/CD released by Nevermore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Year of the Voyager
Content:  It was released in Europe on October 20, 2008 and in North America on November 25, 2008 via Century Media.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Year of the Voyager
Content:  The set covers the "This Godless Endeavor" touring cycle, starting with live footage from the U.S. Gigantour 2005, the Metal Mania festival 2006 in Poland, the Wacken Open Air festival in 2006 in Germany and the main DVD show recorded at the Zeche in Bochum, Germany.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Year of the Voyager
Content:  Bonus material includes two songs from Century Media USA 10th Anniversary Party 2001, all promo videos and an interview with singer Warrel Dane which was recorded at the Roax Film Studios in Berlin in the spring of 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrel Dane
Content: Warrel Dane (born March 7, 1969) is the lead singer of the American power metal band Sanctuary.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrel Dane
Content:  He is also known as the former lead singer of currently inactive progressive metal band Nevermore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrel Dane
Content:  He is a natural baritone, though he was known for his high-pitched vocals with Serpent's Knight and on the first two Sanctuary albums.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Warrel Dane
Content:  Later in his career, Dane became more notable for his distinctively deep, dramatic voice.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toshi (musician)
Content: Toshimitsu Deyama (出山利三 , Deyama Toshimitsu , born October 10, 1965 in Tateyama, Chiba) , known exclusively by his stage name Toshi, is a Japanese singer and songwriter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toshi (musician)
Content:  He is best known as the lead vocalist and a co-founder of the heavy metal band X Japan, which disbanded in 1997.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toshi (musician)
Content:  Toshi then went on to have an extensive solo career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toshi (musician)
Content:  In 2007, X Japan reunited and began an ongoing world tour in 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toshi (musician)
Content:  His distinctive, impassioned tenor vocals have been compared to those of Steve Perry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peter Wichers
Content: Peter Wichers is best known as one of the founding members of Swedish melodic death metal band, Soilwork.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peter Wichers
Content:  From 1995 until late 2005, when he announced his departure from the band, he was known as the lead guitarist and also a songwriter, especially on their "Stabbing the Drama" album., He rejoined Soilwork in 2008 and played on 2010s The Panic Broadcast and in June 2012 he quit Soilwork for the second time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peter Wichers
Content:  After leaving Soilwork, he filled in for Adam Dutkiewicz when he was injured on Killswitch Engage's European tour.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peter Wichers
Content:  More recently, he has collaborated with singers from across the melodic death metal genre including Anders Fridén, former band mate Björn "Speed" Strid and John Bush (vocalist of Anthrax) to make a CD much in the same style as "Roadrunner United", entitled "Nuclear Blast All-Stars".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peter Wichers
Content:  He also co-wrote Warrel Dane's solo record "Praises to the War Machine".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content: Praises to the War Machine is the debut and only solo release/album by heavy metal vocalist Warrel Dane from Nevermore, released on April 25, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  The album was produced by Peter Wichers, who was tapped by Dane to work on the album while Wichers was still a member of Soilwork.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  After leaving that band in 2007, Wichers co-wrote the album with Dane and played on eight of its tracks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  Soilwork drummer Dirk Verbeuren and former Himsa guitarist Matt Wicklund also played on the album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  Nevermore guitarist Jeff Loomis and the band's touring guitarist at the time Chris Broderick make guest appearances, along with James Murphy, who had performed on the Nevermore album "This Godless Endeavor".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  "Praises to the War Machine" includes two cover songs, The Sisters of Mercy's "Lucretia My Reflection" and "Patterns" by Paul Simon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Praises to the War Machine
Content:  Dane had previously covered a Simon song on Nevermore's album "Dead Heart in a Dead World".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mahmood Sariolghalam
Content: Mahmood Sariolghalam (Persian: محمود سریع‌القلم‎ ‎ ) is a professor of International Relations at the School of Economics and Political Science in Shahid Beheshti University (formerly Iran National University) since 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mahmood Sariolghalam
Content:  He was born in Tehran, Iran in 1959.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mahmood Sariolghalam
Content:  He received his B.A. degree in Political Science/Management from California State University, Northridge in 1980 and his M.A. and Ph.D. degrees in International Relations from the University of Southern California in 1982 and 87, respectively.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mahmood Sariolghalam
Content:  Sariolghalam also completed a Post Doctorate program at the Ohio University in 1997.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mahmood Sariolghalam
Content:  During the 2009-2010 academic year, he taught at the Kuwait University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs
Content: The Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs is a London-based scholarly institution furthering the study of Muslims in non-Muslim nations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs
Content:  It holds conferences and publishes books and journals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs
Content:  Pakistani-born Dr. Saleha Mahmood Abedin, the mother of Hillary Clinton aide Huma Abedin, is Director of the Institute.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs
Content:  It was founded in 1978 by Dr. Syed Zainul Abedin, from India who was educated at Aligarh Muslim University and University of Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Institute of Muslim Minority Affairs
Content:  Abdullah Omar Naseef, then president of the Muslim World League and president of King Abdulaziz University, provided backing to Abedin for the institute's formation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huma Abedin
Content: Huma Mahmood Abedin (born July 28, 1976) is an American political staffer who was vice chair of Hillary Clinton's 2016 campaign for President of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huma Abedin
Content:  Prior to that, Abedin was deputy chief of staff to Clinton, who was U.S. Secretary of State, from 2009 to 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huma Abedin
Content:  She was also the traveling chief of staff and former assistant for Clinton during Clinton's campaign for the Democratic nomination in the 2008 presidential election.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content: The eldest of the eleven children of Dr. William Henry Twort, Frederick Twort was born in Camberley, Surrey on 22 October 1877.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  The three eldest sons went to Tomlinson's Modern School in Woking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  From 1894 Frederick studied medicine at St Thomas's Hospital, London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  After qualifying in medicine (Membership of the Royal College of Surgeons, Licentiate of the Royal College of Physicians) in 1900, Twort took the first paid post available, assistant to Dr. Louis Jenner, Superintendent of the Clinical Laboratory of St Thomas' Hospital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  There he trained in pathological techniques.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  In 1902 he became assistant to the Bacteriologist of the London Hospital, Dr. William Bulloch, later F.R.S., and carried out single-handed the whole diagnostic routine of the Hospital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  In 1909, Twort became the superintendent of the Brown Animal Sanatory Institution, a pathology research centre, and remained there for the duration of his career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Frederick Twort
Content:  In 1919 Twort married Dorothy Nony, daughter of Frederick J. Banister, and together they had three daughters and a son.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content: On April 28, 1833, Willson was born in Fitzroy, Ontario.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Going to New York, he attended a district school and taught for a year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Moving on to Olean, New York, he was involved in making daguerreotypes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  In the fall of 1855, Willson move to Ann Arbor to study at the University of Michigan medical department.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  In 1857, he started practicing medicine in the office of Dr. R. D. Lamond in Flint.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Returning in 1858 to the University, Willson graduated in 1859 with a Doctor of Medicine degree.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Back in Flint, he joined his cousin's, Dr. George B. Willson, practice.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  He serve as a Union Army major in the American Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  In 1861, he was a surgeon to the Tenth Michigan Infantry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Willson was transfer to the Eight Michigan Infantry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  He was honorable discharged in March 1863 because of ill health.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  On May 18, 1865, he married Rhoda Crapo, daughter of Governor Henry H. Crapo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  That same year, he was sent to Washington, D.C. as the appointed Michigan military agent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  Willson joined and was active in two Civil War organizations: the Grand Army of the Republic and the Loyal Legion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James C. Willson
Content:  On the death of Henry Crapo, the Willson move into the Crapo Mansion in Flint at First and Clifford (now Wallenberg Street) Streets, now known as Willson Park and part of the University of Michigan–Flint Campus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazher Mahmood
Content: Mazher Mahmood (born 22 March 1963) is an undercover British journalist who has worked mainly for the tabloid press.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazher Mahmood
Content:  He spent 20 years working for the "News of the World" and the "Sunday Times", during which time he was responsible for numerous investigations, including a reputed 94 that led to convictions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazher Mahmood
Content:  He won Reporter of the Year in 2011, as well as Scoop of the Year and the Sports Journalists' Association award, for an investigation of cricket match-fixing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mazher Mahmood
Content:  Later, from its foundation in 2012, he worked for the "Sun on Sunday", successor to the "News of the World".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content: Univ.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  Prof. Dr. Eva Schönbeck-Temesy (August 16, 1930 – August 27, 2011) was an eminent, Austrian botanist of Hungarian descent who made notable contributions to Karl Heinz Rechinger's magisterial Flora Iranica.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  The fourth daughter of General Stefan Temesy and Ludovika Temesy née Messek, she was born in Győr, the most important city in northwestern Hungary.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  She attended elementary school and afterwards (1940–44) "Szent Margit Leánygimnázium" (St. Margaret's School for Girls) in Székesfehérvár in central Hungary.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  In the turmoil of the invasion of Hungary by the Red Army, the Temesy family left the country and settled in the Styrian capital Graz in eastern Austria, where the young Eva resumed her secondary education at BRG II for Girls in 1946 - from which she graduated with distinction in 1949.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  In the same year, she enrolled at the University of Graz to study botany.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  Her doctoral thesis, entitled "The Polytypic Species "Saxifraga stellaris" Linné" , which she wrote under the guidance of Professor Felix Joseph Widder, earned her in 1954 the degree of Ph.D. "Sub auspiciis Praesidentis" - the highest honour which may be bestowed by the Austrian University system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  On July 9, 1956 she married the zoologist Horst Schönbeck (1929–2009) by whom she had three children: Susanne (1957-), Stefan (1959-) and Pippa (1961-).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  Up until 1964, the young Schönbeck-Temesy family lived in the municipality of Judendorf-Straßengel, in the district of Graz-Umgebung.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  There then followed a work-related move to Vienna.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  From January 1 1965 to December 1 1970 Eva worked on a research fellowship and then up until March 30 1973 on an "Arbeitsauftrag" in the botany department of the Natural History Museum, Vienna under the Directorship of Professor Karl Heinz Rechinger.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  On March 1 1974 she became Keeper of the museum's Herbarium - a post in which she remained until her retirement in April 1993.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  Dr. Schönbeck-Temesy benefitted greatly, during her academic career, from her polyglotism : in addition to her mother tongues of Hungarian and German she was also fluent in English, French, Italian, and Russian.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Eva Schönbeck-Temesy
Content:  A true "Grande Dame" of the world of botany, she is commemorated in the name of the rubiaceous plant "Galium schoenbeck-temesyae" Ehrend.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content: Myra Celestina Philomena Portia McQueen is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera, "Hollyoaks", played by Nicole Barber-Lane.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  She made her debut screen appearance during the episode broadcast on 19 June 2006 and was introduced by series producer Bryan Kirkwood as part of the McQueen family.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  Her storylines have included discovering her son John Paul (James Sutton) was gay, the revenge of her abandoned son Niall (Barry Sloane), which led to the death of her daughter Tina (Leah Hackett), a relationship with Dirk Savage (David Kennedy), faking her own death, coming to terms with the death of her daughter Carmel (Gemma Merna) following a train crash and the disappearance of her daughter Mercedes (Jennifer Metcalfe).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  On 1 August 2013, it was announced that Barber-Lane had quit the serial and Myra made her on-screen departure during the episode broadcast on 5 September 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  The character was shot by Dr. Paul Browning (Joseph Thompson), but survived and then emigrated so he thought she was dead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  On 31 January 2014, Myra made a brief return to the show following the death of Jim McGinn (Dan Tetsell).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  In July 2014, it was announced that Myra would return again later in the year, but for a longer stint.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  It was crossed with the return of Theresa McQueen (Jorgie Porter).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Myra McQueen
Content:  Myra returned to the serial on 12 September 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sevil Atasoy
Content: Prof. Dr. Sevil Atasoy (Hafize Hikmet Sevil Atasoy Ekinci), immediate past president of the UN International Narcotics Control Board born in İstanbul, Turkey (February 25, 1949), is an internationally distinguished leader in the field of Forensic Sciences, substance abuse and addiction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sevil Atasoy
Content:  Atasoy is the daughter of forensic pathologist Prof. Dr. Şemsi Gök (1921–2002) and bacteriologist Dr. Ferda Gök (1924 - 2003).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sevil Atasoy
Content:  She is currently the executive director of the Innocence Project (Turkey), the International Forensic Science Services, Vice-Rector of Uskudar University, Istanbul, Dean of the Faculty of Social Sciences and chairs the Crime & Violence Prevention Center.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content: Dr. Hossein Gharib was born in Tehran, Iran on February 2, 1940.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  His father was Dr. Mohammad Gharib (1909–1975), a former Professor and Chair of Pediatrics at Tehran University (1938–1975), generally regarded as the "Father of Pediatrics" in Iran.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  In 1998, Hossein Gharib edited and published a book in Persian about his father which includes a collection of essays from his father's family, patients, friends, and colleagues.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  The second edition is scheduled for publication in late 2011.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  For his lifetime of service, Dr. Mohammad Gharib was honored on a stamp issued by the government of Iran in 1991.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  Hossein's mother was Zahra Gharib (1915–2010), daughter of Ostad Abdolazim Gharib (1877–1965), a renowned professor of Persian literature and the first person to develop a modern grammar for the Iranian language.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  Ostad Abdolazim Gharib was recognized for his major contributions to Iranian literature by a stamp issued by Iran in 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  Dr. Gharib has two sisters, Nahid Ziai and Mayram Comninos, and one brother, Dr. Mohsen Gharib.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hossein Gharib
Content:  In 1976, he married Minoo Jalili and they have three children.

Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content: Moby Zazà is a ferry operated by Moby Lines between Nice and Bastia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  Until 2015 she was MS "Wind Perfection, an accommodation ship owned and operated by C-bed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  She was built in 1982 as MS "Olau Britannia by Weser Seebeckswerft in Bremerhaven for Olau Line, who used her on Sheerness—Vlissingen service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  In 1990 she was sold to Fred.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  Olsen Lines and renamed MS "Bayard for services between Norway and Denmark.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  In 1991 Fred.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  Olsen Lines was sold to Color Line and the "Bayard" passed under their ownership under the name MS "Christian IV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  In 2008, she was sold to Stella Lines and renamed MS "Julia" for service between Helsinki and St. Petersburg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  This was a failure and after just two months the "Julia" was laid up.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Zazà
Content:  In 2009, she was sold to Fastnet Line and entered service on their Swansea—Cork route in 2010, retaining her earlier name.

Title: Armada Asset Management
Content: Armada Asset Management, LLC is a business conglomerate holding company providing asset management services, consulting, and threat reduction/security services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Armada Asset Management
Content:  Their main office is located in Newport Beach, CA, with operational locations in Pleasant Grove, UT, Copperas Cove, TX, Hattiesburg, MS, Hampton Roads, VA, and Washington, D.C. Armada Asset Management is a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business that bids primarily on low-budget defense contracts relating to military and operational consulting.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content: Terren Scott Peizer dubbed the "Zelig of Wall Street" is currently the Chairman of his personal Los Angeles-based investment company, Acuitas Group Holdings (AGH), which in turn owns 100% of Crede Capital Group (CCG) which invests in public companies, and provides growth capital to small and medium-sized enterprises.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  Since its inception in June 2009, CCG has provided companies with capital commitments and funding in excess of $1.2 Billion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  Besides its ownership of Crede Capital, Acuitas Group owns 72% of Catasys, Inc., a provider of proprietary big data based analytics and predictive modeling driven behavioral health management services for health plans; and owns 100% of NeurMedix, Inc., a biotech company that develops and commercializes disease modifying small molecules to treat neuro-degenerative diseases, such as Parkinson’s disease, Alzheimer’s disease, Migraine disease, Huntington’s disease, ALS, MS, Epilepsy, Traumatic Brain Injury, and Retina disease.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  Peizer is Founder, Chairman of the three Acuitas companies, and is CEO of Catasys, Inc. and Crede Capital Group.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  Having developed a bioscience and healthcare expertise, Peizer’s Crede Capital became the largest shareholder in 22nd Century Group, Inc., a public company that is commercializing bio-plant technology to harm-reduce tobacco, creating very low nicotine and very low tar tobacco products which affect levels of addiction and cancer causing carcinogens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  On October 1, 2015 New England Journal of Medicine published a landmark study on the company’s very low nicotine spectrum cigarettes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  In October 2014, Peizer and 22nd Century formed a JV to commercialize the company’s products in China with China National Tobacco Company, the largest tobacco company in the world and the largest monopoly in China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terren Peizer
Content:  China represents over 50% of the worldwide tobacco market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content: MS "Arberia is a cruiseferry owned by Chryses Finance Co. and operated by Ilion Lines on their Trieste–Durres–Bari -service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  She was built in 1975 by Dubegion-Normandie S.A., Nantes, France for Steamship Company Bore, Finland as MS "Bore Star for Steamship Company Bore who used her in Silja Line services on the Baltic Sea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  During the northern hemisphere winter months she was chartered to Finnlines for cruise services on African west coast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  In 1980 she was sold to Finland Steamship Company and renamed MS "Silja Star but retained in Silja Line service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  Between 1986 and 1992 she was used in different cruise and ferry services around the world for various operators under the names MS "Orient Express, MS "Club Sea, MS "Eurosun and MS "Orient Sun.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  In 1992 her ownership passed to Wasa Line and she was renamed MS "Wasa Queen for Baltic Sea ferry service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  In 1993 Wasa Line was merged into Silja Line and "Wasa Queen" returned to the Silja Line fleet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  In 2001 she was sold to Star Cruises for use in Far Eastern ferry service and later casino cruising with their daughter company Cruise Ferries without a change in name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Arberia
Content:  In 2008 "Wasa Queen" was withdrawn from service, and in 2009 sold to her current owners.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Dada
Content: MS "Moby Dada" is a cruiseferry operated by Moby Lines, under charter from DFDS Seaways.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Dada
Content:  She was built in 1981 as "Finlandia" for Effoa at Wärtsilä's Perno shipyard in Turku, Finland, and placed in service on Silja Line's Helsinki—Stockholm service.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Dada
Content:  In 1990 she was sold to DFDS Seaways and renamed "Queen of Scandinavia".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Dada
Content:  From 2010 until 2016, she operated under the name of "Princess Maria" for St. Peter Line between Helsinki and St. Petersburg, Russia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content: Burton Speiser (January 24, 1946 – October 18, 2015) was an american medical researcher.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  He earned an AB in Biology from Queens College and directly afterward completed an MD from the New Jersey College of Medicine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  He finished residency in Radiation Oncology and earned a MS in Radiation Biology from the University of Rochester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  Dr. Speiser was a Major in the U.S. Air Force where he served as Chief of Radiation Oncology at David Grant Medical Center from '74-'76 at Travis Air Force Base.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  He was Associate Director of Radiation Oncology at LDS Hospital '76-'79 in Salt Lake City, UT.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  He was Assistant Professor of Radiation Oncology at Indiana University '79-'80 in Indianapolis, Indiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  Dr. Speiser moved to Phoenix, Arizona in 1980 where he worked as Director of Radiation Oncology at St. Joseph's Hospital and Director of Brachytherapy at Humana Hospital and St. Luke's Hospital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  While in Arizona, Dr. Speiser developed Arizona Oncology Services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  The company provided medical services to patients as well as conducting research into cancer treatments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  That research was subsequently published in a variety of medical journals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burton Speiser
Content:  Dr. Speiser held the position of President of the American Brachytherapy Society from 1996 to 1997

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DFDS Seaways
Content: DFDS Seaways is a large Danish shipping company that operates passenger and freight services across northern Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DFDS Seaways
Content:  Following the acquisition of Norfolkline in 2010, DFDS restructured its other shipping divisions (DFDS Tor Line and DFDS Lisco) into the previously passenger-only operation of DFDS Seaways.

session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Tommy
Content: MS "Moby Tommy is a fast passenger roll-on/roll-off (Ro-Ro) cruiseferry, currently owned by the Italy-based shipping company Moby Lines and operated on their Piombino and Livorno–Olbia route.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MS Moby Tommy
Content:  She was built in 2002 by Samsung Heavy Industries Co. Ltd., Geoje, South Korea for the Greek company, Minoan Lines as MS "Ariadne Palace.

Title: Brandy filmography
Content: As an actress, Brandy has appeared in feature films and television shows.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  She made her television debut in 1993 in the ABC sitcom "Thea", as the daughter of a single mother (Thea Vidale).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  Broadcast to low ratings, the series ran for only one season, but earned her a Young Artists Award nomination for Outstanding Youth Ensemble alongside her co-stars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  In 1996, her short-lived engagement on "Thea" led Brandy to star in her own show, the UPN-produced sitcom "Moesha", in which she played the title role of Moesha Mitchell, a Los Angeles girl coping with a stepmother as well as the pressures and demands of becoming an adult.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  The program debuted on UPN in January 1996, and soon became their most-watched show.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  The network decided to cancel the show after six seasons on the air, leaving it ending with a cliffhanger for a scrapped seventh season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  Brandy was awarded an NAACP Image Award for her performance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  In 1997, Brandy was hand-picked by producer Whitney Houston to play the title character in Rodgers and Hammerstein’s television version of "Cinderella" featuring a multicultural cast that also included Jason Alexander, Whoopi Goldberg, and Houston.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brandy filmography
Content:  The two-hour "Wonderful World of Disney" special garnered an estimated 60 million viewers, giving the network its highest ratings in the time period in 16 years, and won an Emmy Award the following year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of best-selling singles
Content: According to "Guinness World Records", "White Christmas" (1942) by Bing Crosby is the best-selling single worldwide, with estimated sales of over 50 million copies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of best-selling singles
Content:  The song recognized as "the best-selling single of all time" was released before the pop/rock singles-chart era and "was listed as the world's best-selling single in the first-ever "Guinness Book of Records" (published in 1955) and—remarkably—still retains the title more than 50 years later."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of best-selling singles
Content:  "Guinness World Records" states that double A-side charity single "Candle in the Wind 1997"/"Something About the Way You Look Tonight" (1997) by Elton John, a tribute to the late Diana, Princess of Wales, is "the biggest-selling single since UK and US singles charts began in the 1950s, having accumulated worldwide sales of 33 million copies," making it the second-best-selling single of all time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whitney Houston
Content: Whitney Elizabeth Houston (August 9, 1963 – February 11, 2012) was an American singer, actress, producer, and model.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whitney Houston
Content:  In 2009, "Guinness World Records" cited her as the most awarded female act of all-time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whitney Houston
Content:  Houston is one of the best-selling music artists of all-time, with 200 million records sold worldwide.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whitney Houston
Content:  She released seven studio albums and two soundtrack albums, all of which have diamond, multi-platinum, platinum, or gold certification.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Whitney Houston
Content:  Houston's crossover appeal on the popular music charts, as well as her prominence on MTV, starting with her video for "How Will I Know", influenced several African American women artists who follow in her footsteps.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content: Guinness World Records Gone Wild, also known as "Guinness World Records Unleashed", is an American reality television series on truTV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content:  The series debuted on February 7, 2013 and is hosted by Dan Cortese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content:  The series' first season averaged more than 1.3 million viewers and ranked as one of ad-supported cable's Top 3 programs in the Thursday 8 p.m. timeslot with key adult and male demos.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content:  It was also cable's No.1 unscripted entertainment program in the timeslot with men 18-49 and adults 18-34.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content:  It was announced in April 2013 that truTV has ordered an additional ten episodes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records Gone Wild
Content:  Season 2 premiered on November 7, 2013, and features a title change to "Guinness World Records Unleashed".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Farrow
Content: David Andrew Farrow (born January 10, 1975) is a two-time Canadian Guinness World Record Holder for Most Decks of Playing Cards Memorized in a Single Sighting, entrepreneur, memory coach, speed reader and keynote speaker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Farrow
Content:  He is best known for winning the Guinness World Records for Most Decks of Playing Cards Memorized in a Single Sighting in 1996 and again in 2007 when he set out to reclaim his record after it was beaten in 2002.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Farrow
Content:  The initial record was set at the Guinness World Records museum in Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada while the latter was performed for Discovery Channel Canada at CTV Television Network studios.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Farrow
Content:  Both records were accomplished under the controlled supervision of multiple cameras and multiple independent witnesses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bao Xishun
Content: Bao Xishun (also known as Xi Shun; born 1951) is a herdsman from Inner Mongolia, China, recognized by "Guinness World Records" as one of the world's tallest living men.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bao Xishun
Content:  On September 17, 2009, Turkey's Sultan Kösen overtook Bao Xishun as the tallest living man in the world, when he was measured by Guinness World Records, standing 2.51 m tall.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records – Ab India Todega
Content: Guinness World Records – Ab India Todega (English: "Guinness World Records – Now India will Break") is a reality TV show based on the Guinness Book of World Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records – Ab India Todega
Content:  The show, which was hosted by Preity Zinta and Shabbir Ahluwalia, premiered on 18 March 2011 to an audience measurement of 3.3 rating points.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guinness World Records – Ab India Todega
Content:  Each episode presents different individuals trying to break official world records.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  It operates the Twin Galaxies website and publishes the "Twin Galaxies' Official Video Game & Pinball Book of World Records", with the Arcade Volume released on June 2, 2007.

Content:  "The Guinness World Records - Gamers Edition 2008" was released in March, 2008 in conjunction with Twin Galaxies, who Guinness World Records considers to be the official supplier of verified world records to the annual volume.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ashrita Furman
Content:  As of 2017, Furman has set more than 600 official Guinness Records and currently holds 200 records, including the record for holding the most Guinness world records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ashrita Furman
Content:  He has been breaking records since 1979.

Content: Kong: The Animated Series was an American-Canadian television series for children that follows King Kong, the monster of the 1933 film of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kong: The Animated Series
Content:  "Kong" was created to compete with "", and first aired on BKN in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kong: The Animated Series
Content:  In May 2001, Fox Kids began airing reruns of the first 13 episodes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Then, Jetix began airing reruns on September 9, 2005, as a prelude to the release of Peter Jackson's "King Kong".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kong: The Animated Series
Content:  Additionally, two direct-to-DVD movies based on the series were released: "Kong: King of Atlantis", where Kong fights to save the mythic island from a vicious snake sorceress (and which also contains three musical numbers; the only Kong film to do so) in 2005 and "Kong: Return to the Jungle", where poachers capture Kong and other animals from his island to sell to a zoo, in 2007.

Title: King Kong 2: Yomigaeru Densetsu

session_0.text:
Title: King Kong 2: Yomigaeru Densetsu

session_0.text:
Title: King Kong 2: Yomigaeru Densetsu
Content:  Similar to Konami's later "Hi no Tori" games, two separate games were developed together and released for the MSX and Famicom respectively.

session_0.text:
Title: King Kong 2: Yomigaeru Densetsu
Content:  While the Famicom "" has players playing as King Kong in a more action-oriented format similar to Nintendo's "The Legend of Zelda", "Yomigaeru Densetsu" has players playing as Mitchell in a role-playing style.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Here Lies Arthur
Content: Here Lies Arthur is a young-adult novel by Philip Reeve, published by Scholastic in 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Here Lies Arthur
Content:  Set in fifth or sixth century Britain and the Anglo-Saxon invasion, it features a girl who participates in the deliberate construction of legendary King Arthur during the man's lifetime, orchestrated by a bard.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Here Lies Arthur
Content:  Reeve calls it a back-creation: not a genuine historical novel as it is not based on actual specific events; rather it is "back-created" from the legends, giving them a "realistic" origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content: King Kong is a giant movie monster, resembling a giant ape, that has appeared in various media since 1933.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  The character first appeared in the 1933 film "King Kong" from RKO Pictures, which received universal acclaim upon its initial release and re-releases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  A sequel quickly followed that same year with "The Son of Kong", featuring Little Kong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  In the 1960s, Toho produced "King Kong vs. Godzilla" (1962), pitting a much larger Kong against Toho's own Godzilla, and "King Kong Escapes" (1967), based on "The King Kong Show" (1966–1969) from Rankin/Bass Productions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  In 1976, Dino De Laurentiis produced a modern remake of the original film directed by John Guillermin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  A sequel, "King Kong Lives", followed a decade later featuring a Lady Kong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King Kong
Content:  Another remake of the original, this time set in 1933, was released in 2005 from filmmaker Peter Jackson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  He first met Peter Jackson as a 17-year-old, and storyboarded all of Jackson's films since "Braindead".

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Christian Rivers

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Christian Rivers

Title: M. D. Bright
Content: Mark D. Bright (born 1955) is an American comic book and storyboard artist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: M. D. Bright
Content:  Often credited as M.D. Bright, and sometimes as Doc Bright (a play on his initials), he is best known for pencilling the Marvel Comics Iron Man story "Armor Wars", the two "" miniseries for DC Comics, for painting the iconic cover to Marvel Comics' "Transformers" #5, (featuring the Decepticon Shockwave and the haunting words "Are All Dead" underneath the series title), and for co-creating Quantum and Woody with writer Christopher J. Priest.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: M. D. Bright
Content:  Mostly out of comics, Bright is now a freelance storyboard artist, although he and Priest reunited for a 5-issue "Quantum and Woody" miniseries published by the new incarnation of Valiant Comics in 2014-2015, but set in the continuity of the original "Quantum and Woody" series.

Title: The Quiet American
Content: The Quiet American is a 1955 novel by English author Graham Greene which depicts French colonialism in Vietnam being uprooted by the Americans during the 1950s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  The novel implicitly questions the foundations of growing American involvement in Vietnam in the 1950s and is unique in its exploration of the subject topic through the links among its three main characters - Fowler, Pyle and Phuong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  The novel has received much attention due to its prediction of the outcome of the Vietnam War and subsequent American foreign policy since the 1950s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  Graham Greene portrays a U.S. official named Pyle as so blinded by American exceptionalism that he cannot see the calamities he brings upon the Vietnamese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  It was adapted as two different movies during 1958 and 2002.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  The book uses Greene's experiences as a war correspondent for "The Times" and "Le Figaro" in French Indochina 1951–1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  He was apparently inspired to write "The Quiet American" during October 1951 while driving back to Saigon from Ben Tre province.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Quiet American
Content:  He was accompanied by an American aid worker who lectured him about finding a "third force in Vietnam” .

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Seattle Smooth
Content:  Seattle Smooth's dam is the unraced mare, Our Seattle Star, by Seattle Song who was bred to Quiet American; a Grade 1 winner by Fappiano who was in turn sired by the great Mr. Prospector.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seattle Smooth
Content:  Being sired by Quiet American makes her-half sister to Kentucky Derby and Preakness Stakes winner Real Quiet, who lost the 1998 Triple Crown by a nose.

session_0.text:

Content:  He is out of the Dr. Fager mare Demure, by the Grade I winner Fappiano, son of the great Mr. Prospector.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content: Audie Murphy (20 June 1925 – 28 May 1971) was a highly decorated American soldier and Medal of Honor recipient who turned actor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  He portrayed himself in the film ""To Hell and Back"", the account of his World War II experiences.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  During the 1950s and 1960s he was cast primarily in westerns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  While often the hero, he proved his ability to portray a cold-blooded hired gun in "No Name on the Bullet".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  A notable exception to the westerns was "The Quiet American" in which he co-starred with Michael Redgrave.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  Murphy made over 40 feature films and often worked with directors more than once.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  Jesse Hibbs who directed "To Hell and Back" worked with the star on six films, only half of which were westerns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  When promoting his 1949 book "To Hell and Back" he appeared on the radio version of "This Is Your Life".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  To promote the 1955 film of the same name, he appeared on Ed Sullivan's "Toast of the Town".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  He was a celebrity guest on television shows such as "What's My Line?

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content: " and appeared in a handful of television dramas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  Murphy's only television series "Whispering Smith" had a brief run in 1961.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Film career of Audie Murphy
Content:  For his cooperation in appearing in the United States Army's "Broken Bridge" episode of "The Big Picture" television series he was awarded the Outstanding Civilian Service Medal.

Content: The Quiet American is a 1958 American film and the first film adaptation of Graham Greene's bestselling novel of the same name, and the first major American attempt to deal with the geo-politics of Indochina.

Content:  It was critically well-received, but was not considered a box office success.

Title: Play-by-post role-playing game
Content: A play-by-post role-playing game (or sim) is an online text-based role-playing game in which players interact with each other and a predefined environment via text.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Play-by-post role-playing game
Content:  It is a subset of the online role-playing community which caters to both gamers and creative writers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Play-by-post role-playing game
Content:  Play-by-post games may be based on other role-playing games, non-game fiction including books, television and movies, or original settings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Play-by-post role-playing game
Content:  This activity is closely related to both interactive fiction and collaborative writing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Play-by-post role-playing game
Content:  Compared to other roleplaying game formats, this type tends to have the loosest rulesets.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AmberMUSH
Content: AmberMUSH, sometimes abbreviated as Amber or AM, was a MUSH — a kind of online text-based role-playing game — based on "The Chronicles of Amber" by Roger Zelazny and, to some extent, the "Amber Diceless Roleplaying Game".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AmberMUSH
Content:  Founded in 1992 by Jennifer "Jasra" Smith and several associates, it operated until 2009, remaining online for 17 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AVATAR (MUD)
Content:  It combines elements of role-playing games, hack and slash style computer games, adventure games and social gaming.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Torn (online game)
Content: Torn, previously known as "Torn City" before the change to "TORN", is a free, online text-based massive multiplayer online role-playing game (MMORPG) created by British online gaming entrepreneur Joe Chedburn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Torn (online game)
Content:  The game was launched in 2003.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Torn (online game)
Content:  In 2010, over 24,000 people played daily, and over 1 million accounts existed in total.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Torn (online game)
Content:  "Torn" is a virtual world based around gang violence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Torn (online game)
Content:  Like many RPGs, players start at the bottom of the ladder and make their way to the top by earning experience points.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Torn (online game)
Content:  The game focuses on crimes and player versus player combat as a way of earning XP or experience points.

session_0.text:
Title: Online text-based role-playing game
Content: An online text-based role playing game is a role-playing game played online using a solely text-based interface.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Online text-based role-playing game
Content:  Online text-based role playing games date to 1978, with the creation of "MUD1", which began the MUD heritage that culminates in today's MMORPGs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Online text-based role-playing game
Content:  Some online-text based role playing games are video games, but some are organized and played entirely by humans through text-based communication.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Online text-based role-playing game
Content:  Over the years, games have used TELNET, internet forums, IRC, email and social networking websites as their media.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TinyMUCK
Content: TinyMUCK or, more broadly, a MUCK, is a type of user-extendable online text-based role-playing game, designed for role playing and social interaction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TinyMUCK
Content:  Backronyms like "Multi-User Chat/Created/Computer/Character/Carnal Kingdom" and "Multi-User Construction Kit" are sometimes cited, but are not the actual origin of the term; "muck" is simply a play on the term MUD.

session_0.speaker: System
