session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content: Charles Cecil MBE (born 11 August 1962) is a British video game designer and co-founder of Revolution Software.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  Cecil was brought to the Democratic Republic of the Congo when he was still very young, but was evacuated at two years after Mobutu Sese Seko's coup d'état.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  He was then educated at Bedales School in Hampshire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  In 1980 he began his studies in Engineering Manufacture and Management at Manchester University, where he met student Richard Turner who invited him to write some text adventures for Artic Computing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  After completing his degree in 1985 he decided to continue his career in game development and became director of Artic.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  In the following year he established Paragon Programming, a game development company working with British publisher U.S. Gold.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  In 1987 he moved into publishing as software development manager for U.S. Gold.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Cecil
Content:  One year later he was approached by Activision and was offered the position of manager of their European development studio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider II
Content: Strider II (released in North America under the title of Journey from Darkness: Strider Returns), is a side-scrolling platform game published by U.S. Gold (under license from Capcom USA) and originally released for various computer platforms in 1990 .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider II
Content:  It is a European-developed sequel to Capcom' s arcade game "Strider", which U.S. Gold previously ported to home computers in Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider II
Content:  The game was developed by Tiertex, as with "Human Killing Machine", the U.S. Gold sequel to "Street Fighter".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content: U.S. Gold Limited was a British video game publisher based in Holford, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  The company was founded in 1984 by Geoff Brown in parallel to his distributor firm CentreSoft, and, like CentreSoft, became part of the Woodward Brown Holdings (later renamed CentreGold).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  The company primarily aimed to publish games imported from the United States for a lower price tag in Europe and especially the United Kingdom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  By 1985, U.S. Gold projected a tourover of US$ for their first fiscal year, and expected to release further 150 games in the year to come.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  In 1988, U.S. Gold received the Golden Joystick Award for "Software House of the Year".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  The company also operated the budget range label Kixx.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: U.S. Gold
Content:  In April 1996, Eidos Interactive acquired the entire CentreGold umbrella (including U.S. Gold) for GB£ , as a result of which all operations of U.S. Gold were merged into Eidos Inteactive, and the company closed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider 2
Content: Strider 2, released in Japan as Strider Hiryū 2 (ストライダー飛竜２ , Sutoraidā Hiryū Tsū ) , is Capcom's 1999 sequel to the original "Strider".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider 2
Content:  This game is actually the second sequel to "Strider" produced, following U.S. Gold's 1990 noncanonical "Strider II" ("Journey from Darkness: Strider Returns" in America), a game with which Capcom was not directly involved.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider 2
Content:  The Capcom-produced "Strider 2" makes no references to the Western-only "Strider Returns"/"Strider II".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Strider 2
Content:  The game was released for arcades in 1999 and was ported to the PlayStation in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Flashback (1992 video game)
Content: Flashback, released as Flashback: The Quest for Identity in the United States, is a 1992 science fiction cinematic platform game developed by Delphine Software of France and published by U.S. Gold in United States and Europe, and Sunsoft in Japan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content: Kaboom Studios Limited (formerly Geoff Brown Holdings Limited) was a British holding company established by Geoff Brown in 1996 to pursue video game development company investment.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  The company was started for Brown's December 1996 acquisition of a 75% stake in Silicon Dreams Studio for at least GB£ .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  Silicon Dreams Studio was founded by Brown in March 1994, as in-house development team for his publishing company, U.S. Gold, until the entire CentreGold umbrella (including U.S. Gold and Silicon Dreams) was sold to Eidos Interactive in April 1996.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  In January 1997, Geoff Brown Holdings also acquired Attention to Detail, and a motion capture studio Audiomotion, as well as another developer, Pivotal Games, were founded under the Kaboom umbrella in 1997 and March 2000, respectively.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  In 2003, Kaboom Studios faced major financial struggles, resulsting in the colsure of Attention to Detail and Silicon Dreams Studio on 28 August 2003 and 3 September 2003, respectively.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  Meanwhile, Audiomotion's managing director Mick Morris performed a management buyout, in order to "reboot" itself and avoid closure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  Kaboom Studios went into receivership on 9 September 2003, with all remaining assets, including still-active Pivotal Games, being transferred to Ernst & Young for sale.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kaboom Studios
Content:  On 29 September 2003, SCi acquired Pivotal Games from Ernst & Young for a total of GB£ .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masters of the Universe: The Arcade Game
Content: Masters of the Universe: The Arcade Game is a video game developed by Adventure Soft for several home computer platforms, and published by U.S. Gold in 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masters of the Universe: The Arcade Game
Content:  The game is part of the Masters of the Universe media franchise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masters of the Universe: The Arcade Game
Content:  In the same year, U.S. Gold published another Masters of the Universe video game: "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Human Killing Machine
Content: Human Killing Machine, commonly abbreviated as "HKM", is a 2D fighting video game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Human Killing Machine
Content:  The game was developed by Tiertex who hired external team Blue Turtle (Nick Pavis and Leigh Christian) to produce the graphics, and published by U.S. Gold, released in March 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Human Killing Machine
Content:  It was touted as a sequel to Tiertex's home computer conversion of "Street Fighter" (as the two companies later did with "Strider II").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Human Killing Machine
Content:  It was released on the 8 and 16-bit home computer formats.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dropzone
Content: Dropzone is a shoot 'em up video game developed by Archer Maclean (under the name Arena Graphics) and published in 1984 by U.S. Gold.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dropzone
Content:  It was released for the Atari 8-bit family and Commodore 64, then later ported to the Nintendo Entertainment System, Game Boy, Game Gear and Game Boy Color.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dropzone
Content:  It was Maclean's first commercial game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hammock Music
Content: Hammock Music, based in Nashville, Tennessee, is the label imprint owned and operated by the band Hammock.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hammock Music
Content:  The label is distributed by Redeye Worldwide.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hammock Music
Content:  In 2015, Hammock Music signed Slow Meadow, an ambient music project by Matt Kidd, to their label imprint.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hammock Music
Content:  Hammock Music released Slow Meadow's self-titled debut in August 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content: Black Cat Music is a punk rock band from San Francisco Bay Area, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  They were formed in the summer of 1998 by former members of bands such as The Criminals and the Receivers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  The music combined elements of punk & rock and roll with dark, poetic lyrics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  After its formation, Black Cat Music released an EP & first full-length record on Cheetah's records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  Soon thereafter, Black Cat Music signed to Lookout!

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  The band played live shows up and down the west coast, and on several national tours.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Black Cat Music
Content:  In 2004, the band released a video for the song "the Jet Trash", directed by Vincent Haycock.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Homemade Jamz Blues Band
Content: Homemade Jamz Blues Band is an American, Tupelo, Mississippi-based blues trio, consisting of siblings Ryan (vocal and guitar), Kyle (bass) and Taya (drums) Perry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Homemade Jamz Blues Band
Content:  In December 2007, the trio made music history as the youngest blues band to achieve a record deal: the Toronto-based NorthernBlues Music signed the band when Ryan was aged 16, Kyle was 14 and Taya was 9.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Homemade Jamz Blues Band
Content:  Their debut album, "Pay Me No Mind," was released in June 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content: The Brilliance, stylized as the BRILLIANCE, are an American worship music duo of David Gungor and John Arndt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content:  They come from various locations in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content:  The band started making music in 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content:  Integrity Music signed the band. "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content: Brother" is the first studio album by The Brilliance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Brilliance
Content:  This charted on the "Billboard" Christian Albums chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate (Mike Burns & Mark Lewis)
Content: Interstate is an LA-based electronic music production duo made up of Mike Burns (born 1972 in Miami Beach) and Mark Lewis (born in South London).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate (Mike Burns & Mark Lewis)
Content:  The production pair became known to fans and the industry back in 2005 when Armin Van Buuren's Netherlands based record label Armada Music signed them to a 3 track deal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate (Mike Burns & Mark Lewis)
Content:  Their breakthrough single "I Found U" was later featured on Paul Oakenfold's Grammy nominated Creamfields compilation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate (Mike Burns & Mark Lewis)
Content:  They have had releases on Paul Van Dyk's Vandit Records as well as System Recordings in the US.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Private Years
Content: The Private Years is a box set released by Private Music in 1999.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Private Years
Content:  It features five of Yanni's albums for Private Music: "Reflections of Passion", "In Celebration of Life", "Dare to Dream", "In My Time", and "Live at the Acropolis", as well as the DVD of his concert film, "Live at the Acropolis".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content: Weathermaker Music is the record label owned by the American rock band Clutch and their manager Jack Flanagan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Weathermaker Music LLC was formed in June 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Weathermaker Music was mostly known to work with Clutch and their side project, the psychedelic jazz-rock alter ego The Bakerton Group.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  In 2012 Weathermaker signed and released product by The Company Band, and The Mob.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Both groups include Weathermaker Music principal owners.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  On March 19th, 2013 Weathermaker Music released the Clutch record ” Earth Rocker” on CD and vinyl.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  At the end of 2013 Weathermaker Music released “Earth Rocker Live” which is a double 12 inch vinyl picture disc version of the “Earth Rocker” studio release on one LP, and the same track listing recorded live from various cities on their 2013 USA tour on the second LP.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  In 2013 Weathermaker Music signed Deep Swell, featuring Clutch member Tim Sult on guitar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Their record, “Lore of the Angler” was released October 15th, 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Weathermaker Music signed the Maryland-based group Lionize in 2013 as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  Entitled “Jetpack Soundtrack”, this Lionize record was released on February 18th, 2014 in North America, April 11th, 2014 in Europe, and February 14th, 2014 in the UK.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Weathermaker Music
Content:  The Lionize signing marks the label’s first signing of an independent artist to Weathermaker Music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Private Music
Content: Private Music was an American independent record label founded in 1984 by musician Peter Baumann as a "home for instrumental music."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Private Music
Content:  Baumann signed Yanni, Suzanne Ciani, Andy Summers, Patrick O'Hearn, Leo Kottke, and his former bandmates, Tangerine Dream.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Private Music
Content:  The label specialized in new-age music, but made a sharp turn to the mainstream, with signings of Taj Mahal, Ringo Starr, Etta James, and A. J. Croce.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Private Music
Content:  Its releases were distributed by then-emerging BMG (the label's earliest recordings having been distributed by RCA), which bought Private Music in 1996.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ringo Starr
Content: Richard Starkey, {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} (born 7 July 1940), known professionally as Ringo Starr, is an English drummer, singer, songwriter and actor who gained worldwide fame as the drummer for the Beatles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ringo Starr
Content:  He occasionally sang lead vocals, usually for one song on an album, including "With a Little Help from My Friends", "Yellow Submarine", "Good Night", and their cover of "Act Naturally".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ringo Starr
Content:  He also wrote the Beatles' songs "Don't Pass Me By" and "Octopus's Garden", and is credited as a co-writer of others, including "What Goes On" and "Flying".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content: Christian Webster (born October 13, 1988), better known by his stage name Chris Webby, is an American rapper from Norwalk, Connecticut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content:  Chris Webby has released many mixtapes such as the DJ Drama-hosted "Bars On Me" (2012) and his EP "There Goes the Neighborhood" (2011), which peaked at number 101 on the "Billboard" 200.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content:  He has worked with various artists such as Freeway, Mac Miller, Joell Ortiz, Big K.R.I.T., Method Man, Prodigy, Gatzby, Bun B, Tech N9ne, and Kid Ink.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content:  In 2013 he and his label Homegrown Music signed a deal with E1 Music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content:  He then released "Homegrown", another EP, in November 2012.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chris Webby
Content:  Webster released his debut studio album "Chemically Imbalanced" on October 27, 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dee Does Broadway
Content:  It was produced by Dee, grammy winning Patti LuPone and Bebe Neuwirth.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dee Does Broadway
Content:  Guests in the album include the Grammy and Emmy award winning Cyndi Lauper, Clay Aiken, Nick Adams and many others.

Title: Kwak Yi-kyong
Content: Kwak Yi-kyong (곽이경 , born 1979) is a South Korean LGBT human rights activist, civil rights activist, and labor rights activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kwak Yi-kyong
Content:  From 2012 to the present, she has been a representative of Solidarity for LGBT Human Rights of Korea(SLRK).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Be An Angel
Content: Be An Angel Romania (BAAR) is a Romanian human rights organisation based in Cluj-Napoca founded by Lucian Dunăreanu, a LGBT rights activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Be An Angel
Content:  Although the organisation's mission is to combat discrimination in Romanian society in all its forms, its main field of activity is in LGBT rights and dealing with discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian LGBT Network
Content: The Russian LGBT Network (Russian: Российская ЛГБТ-сеть ) is a non-governmental LGBT rights organization working for the social acceptance of and protection of the rights of LGBT people in Russia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian LGBT Network
Content:  Founded in 2006, it was reformed into the first (and only) Russian inter-regional LGBT rights organization on October 19, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian LGBT Network
Content:  The organization is a member of the International Lesbian and Gay Association (ILGA) and is led by Russian LGBT rights activist Igor Kochetkov.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Chong
Content: Jean Chong is a Singaporean LGBT rights activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Chong
Content:  She co-founded Sayoni, a LGBT rights organization, and serves as one of the leaders of the ASEAN Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity and Gender Expression Caucus, an activist collective.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyndi Lauper
Content: Cynthia Ann Stephanie "Cyndi" Lauper (born June 22, 1953) is an American singer, songwriter, actress and LGBT rights activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyndi Lauper
Content:  Her career has spanned over 30 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyndi Lauper
Content:  Her debut solo album "She's So Unusual" (1983) was the first debut female album to chart four top-five hits on the "Billboard" Hot 100—"Girls Just Want to Have Fun", "Time After Time", "She Bop", and "All Through the Night"—and earned Lauper the Best New Artist award at the 27th Grammy Awards in 1985.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyndi Lauper
Content:  Her success continued with the soundtrack for the motion picture "The Goonies" and her second record "True Colors" (1986).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cyndi Lauper
Content:  This album included the number one single "True Colors" and "Change of Heart", which peaked at number 3.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vicki Gabriner
Content: Vicki Gabriner (born 1942-) is an American-Jewish civil rights activist, feminist and LGBT rights activist who became an activist when she was just a student.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vicki Gabriner
Content:  She was one of the members of "The Weathermen" which was the radical student activist group.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vicki Gabriner
Content:  In 2002, she was honored at the 2002 Women Who Dared event in Boston.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terry Bean
Content: Terrence Patrick "Terry" Bean is an American political fundraiser, a civil rights activist, and a pioneer of the LGBT rights movement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terry Bean
Content:  He is known for co-founding several national LGBT rights organizations, including the Human Rights Campaign and the Gay & Lesbian Victory Fund.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Terry Bean
Content:  s of 2012 , he is the CEO and President of Bean Investment Real Estate and resides in Portland, Oregon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lois Galgay Reckitt
Content: Lois Galgay Reckitt (born December 31, 1944) is an American feminist, human rights activist, LGBT rights activist, and domestic violence advocate.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lois Galgay Reckitt
Content:  Called "one of the most prominent advocates in Maine for abused women", she served as executive director of Family Crisis Services in Portland, Maine for more than three decades.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lois Galgay Reckitt
Content:  From 1984 to 1987 she served as executive vice president of the National Organization for Women in Washington, D.C. She is a co-founder of the Human Rights Campaign Fund, the Maine Coalition for Human Rights, the Maine Women's Lobby, and the first Maine chapter of the National Organization for Women.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lois Galgay Reckitt
Content:  She was inducted into the Maine Women's Hall of Fame in 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Equality Matters
Content: Equality Matters is a media and communications initiative in support of LGBT rights in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Equality Matters
Content:  According to its website, Equality Matters is a partner organization with Media Matters for America, a progressive media watchdog group.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Equality Matters
Content:  Richard Socarides, longtime LGBT rights activist and former senior advisor to President Bill Clinton on gay civil rights issues, served as president of Equality Matters from its founding, and Kerry Eleveld, journalist for "The Advocate", served as editor beginning in 2011.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Equality Matters
Content:  Both announced their departure from the organization in November 2011.

Title: 2017 Fed Cup Europe/Africa Zone Group I – Pool C
Content: Pool C of the 2017 Fed Cup Europe/Africa Zone Group I was one of four pools in the Europe/Africa zone of the 2017 Fed Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2017 Fed Cup Europe/Africa Zone Group I – Pool C
Content:  Four teams competed in a round robin competition, with the top team and the bottom team proceeding to their respective sections of the play-offs: the top team played for advancement to the World Group II Play-offs, while the bottom team faced potential relegation to Group II.

session_0.text:
Title: Georgia at the Rugby World Cup
Content: The Georgia national rugby union team has competed in four consecutive Rugby World Cup tournaments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgia at the Rugby World Cup
Content:  Starting in 2003, they were placed in Pool C with eventual winners England, South Africa, Uruguay and Samoa, where they lost all four matches.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgia at the Rugby World Cup
Content:  Georgia qualified for the 2007 World Cup and were placed in Pool D with Argentina, France, Ireland and Namibia, where they beat Namibia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgia at the Rugby World Cup
Content:  In 2011 Rugby World Cup, Georgia qualified for their third straight tournament.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  They were placed in Pool B with Scotland, England, Argentina and Romania and won the game against rivals Romania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Their best performance was in 2015 tournament, when they were drawn against eventual world champions New Zealand, Argentina, Tonga and Namibia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Georgia at the Rugby World Cup
Content:  Georgia won games against Tonga and Namibia, finished third in the group and automatically qualified for 2019 Rugby World Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: NBA G League
Content: The NBA G League is the National Basketball Association's official minor league basketball organization.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NBA G League
Content:  The league was known as the National Basketball Development League (NBDL) from 2001 to 2005, and the NBA Development League (NBA D-League) from 2005 until 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NBA G League
Content:  The league started with eight teams until NBA commissioner David Stern announced a plan to expand the NBA D-League to fifteen teams and develop it into a true minor league farm system, with each NBA D-League team affiliated with one or more NBA teams in March 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NBA G League
Content:  At the conclusion of the 2013–14 NBA season, 33% of NBA players had spent time in the NBA D-League, up from 23% in 2011.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NBA G League
Content:  As of the 2017–18 season, the league consists of 26 teams, all of which are either single-affiliated or owned by an NBA team.

Title: Kadeem Jack
Content:  He played college basketball for Rutgers.

Title: Hashtag United F.C.
Content: Hashtag United F.C. is an English YouTube-based football club that was founded in 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hashtag United F.C.
Content:  They gained notability due to recording their matches, making videos around them and posting them to their player/manager, Spencer Owen's YouTube channel, known as Spencer FC, which has just under 2 million subscribers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hashtag United F.C.
Content:  They have played many teams including several Premier League staff teams such as Manchester City, West Ham United and Crystal Palace FC as well as the staff teams of Major League Soccer clubs, Atlanta United and New York City F.C..

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hashtag United F.C.
Content:  They have also played and beaten the youth teams of some semi-professional clubs, Biggleswade United F.C. and Newhaven F.C. As of September 2017, their Twitter and Instagram accounts have over 100,000 followers each.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hashtag United F.C.
Content:  The club also controls a YouTube channel where they upload 5-a-side matches that the club participates in and vlogs.

Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content: The NCAA Division I Men's Tournament is a basketball tournament that has been played annually since 1939.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  Teams were placed in the tournament based on their records and performance against other teams.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  The spots in which the teams were placed are referred to as "seeds."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  When the tournament first started, seeds were assigned 1-8, based on how many teams played in a conference.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  As the number of teams in the tournament grew, more seeds were added.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  Currently, seeds are assigned 1-16.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NCAA March Madness: School Appearances by Seed
Content:  This list is a compilation of the seeds held by teams each time they competed in the tournament.

Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, has been home to many teams and events in professional, semi-professional, amateur, college, and high-school sports.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Philadelphia is one of twelve cities that hosts teams in all four major sports leagues in North America, and Philadelphia is one of just three cities in which one team from every league plays within city limits.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  These major sports teams are the Philadelphia Phillies of Major League Baseball, the Philadelphia Eagles of the National Football League, the Philadelphia 76ers of the National Basketball Association and the Philadelphia Flyers of the National Hockey League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Each team has played in Philadelphia since at least the 1960s, and each team has won at least one championship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Since 2010, Philadelphia has been the home of the Philadelphia Union of Major League Soccer which plays in suburban Chester, Pennsylvania, making the Philadelphia market one of nine cities that hosts a team in the four major sports leagues and the MLS.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Philadelphia hosts several college sports teams, including the Philadelphia Big 5 schools and Temple's Division I FBS football team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Many of these teams have fan bases in both Philadelphia and the surrounding Delaware Valley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  In addition to the major professional and college sports, numerous semi-pro, amateur, community, and high school teams play in Philadelphia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  The city hosts numerous sporting events, such as the Penn Relays and the Collegiate Rugby Championship, and Philadelphia has been the most frequent host of the annual Army-Navy football game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Philadelphia has also been the home of several renowned athletes and sports figures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in Philadelphia
Content:  Philly furthermore has played a historically significant role in the development of cricket and extreme wrestling in the United States.

Title: Albanian Third Division
Content: Kategoria e Tretë is the fourth and lowest professional level of football in Albania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albanian Third Division
Content:  In the 2015-16 season, Kategoria e Tretë had 19 teams participating, which were divided into 2 groups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albanian Third Division
Content:  Before the start of 2016-17 season, many teams withdrew, which was mainly due to financial problems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albanian Third Division
Content:  The competition format changed and now has 10 teams playing in a single league structure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albanian Third Division
Content:  The winner and the runner-up are automatically promoted to the Albanian Second Division and the third and fourth placed teams qualify for the play-offs against the teams ranked 11th from the A & B groups of the Albanian Second Division.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Albanian Third Division
Content:  From the 2017-18 season, teams ranked in last spot will relegate to Albanian Amateur Division I

Title: Mark Snell
Content: Mark Snell is a retired American soccer goalkeeper who played professionally in the North American Soccer League and the Major Indoor Soccer League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  He coached in the FC Dallas youth system for eight years, and is widely considered one of the top youth development coaches in the country for Zone 1 (6-12 yr olds).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  At FC Dallas he developed the curriculum that included teaching age appropriate tactics through small sided games.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  His philosophy was that the "best train with the best" and created the Premier team format grouping all the top players in each age group onto a team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  Initially, players of all levels were scattered across many teams within each age group.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  Snell felt the best development model was having teams made up of "like" talent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  That way training would be competitive for all teams regardless of talent level.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  He also authored and managed the street soccer based "FCD LIGA" which was a 3v3/4v4 in house league on Sundays.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  He grew the wildly successful program from 5 teams to over 30 teams to preach the small sided games format as the best player development tool.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Snell
Content:  He also organized and managed the FC Dallas Development League which hosted the top U9 teams in the Dallas Metroplex.

session_0.speaker: System

Content: Belarusian Second League is the third division of professional football in Belarus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Belarusian Second League
Content:  A strict number of teams is not set for the competition and every season it depends on how many teams are able to obtain a license or have an intention to apply for one.

Title: Belarusian Second League
Content:  As of 2017, the league consists of 14 teams.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Belarusian Second League
Content:  At the end of the season two best teams will be promoted to Belarusian First League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Belarusian Second League
Content:  The competition is organized by the Belarusian Football Federation.

Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content: Evans Russ Mensah (born 25 July 1988) is a former Ghanaian football player.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  He played for New Radiant SC, a Dhivehi League team in Maldives.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  He played his academy football in Ghana and South Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  He was the top scorer for his academy teams; he played both in Ghana and South Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  From academy he went straight to Ghana premier league, scoring twice on his debut for Okwahu United.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  He caught the eyes of many teams, which took him to Malaysia to Perlis F.C for his first international trials.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  He came to Ghana to continue his career and later landed a big contract in Thailand Premiere League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  His speed, pace and skills made him break into the first team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Evans Mensah (born 1988)
Content:  He was awarded players player of his team on his first season in the Thai Premieer League

Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content: The Northern League was founded in 1929 when it was known as the English Dirt Track League, the earliest league (along with the Southern League) in speedway racing in the United Kingdom, comprising teams from Northern Britain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content:  The addition of two Scottish teams prompted a name change in 1930.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content:  The league existed between 1929 and 1931, after which, with many teams folding, it was amalgamated with the Southern League to form the National League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content:  In the 1929 season, White City (Manchester) won all 18 matches but resigned from the league after a dispute, leaving Leeds Lions as champions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content:  The season was beset with problems with Warrington being expelled, Bolton completing only one match before their fixtures were taken over by Hanley, and Long Eaton entering the league but not completing a match.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northern League (speedway)
Content:  Belle Vue won the league in both 1930 and 1931.

session_0.text:
Title: Suzanne Simone Baptiste Louverture
Content: Suzanne Simone Baptiste Louverture (around 1742 – May 19, 1816 in Agen, France) was the wife of Toussaint Louverture and the "Dame-Consort" of the French colony of Saint-Domingue.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: War of Knives
Content: The War of Knives (French: "Guerre des couteaux"), also known as the War of the South, was a civil war from June 1799 to July 1800 between the Haitian revolutionary Toussaint Louverture, a black ex-slave who controlled the north of Saint-Domingue (modern-day Haiti), and his adversary André Rigaud, a free colored person of mixed race who controlled the south.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: War of Knives
Content:  Louverture and Rigaud fought over de facto control of the French colony of Saint-Domingue during the war, which took place after the two men had successfully expelled foreign forces from the colony as part of the Haitian Revolution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: War of Knives
Content:  The war resulted in Toussaint taking control of the entirety of Saint-Domingue, and Rigaud fleeing into exile.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Baptiste Brunet
Content: Jean Baptiste Brunet (7 July 1763 – 21 September 1824) was a French general of division in the French Revolutionary Army.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Baptiste Brunet
Content:  He was responsible for the arrest of Toussaint Louverture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Baptiste Brunet
Content:  He was promoted to command a light infantry demi-brigade at the Fleurus in 1794.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Baptiste Brunet
Content:  He led the unit in François Joseph Lefebvre's division in the 1795, 1796 and 1799 campaigns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jean Baptiste Brunet
Content:  He was the son of French general Gaspard Jean-Baptiste Brunet who was guillotined in 1793.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint L'Overture County Cemetery
Content: The Toussaint L'Ouverture County Cemetery is an historical African-American cemetery located in Franklin, Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint L'Overture County Cemetery
Content:  It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1995.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint L'Overture County Cemetery
Content:  It is named for Toussaint Louverture, the leader of the Haitian Revolution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint L'Overture County Cemetery
Content:  The earliest recorded burials date from 1869, but it wasn't officially incorporated until 1884.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint L'Overture County Cemetery
Content:  It is "the oldest African American institution in continuous use" in Williamson County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sanité Bélair
Content: Suzanne Bélair, called "Sanité Bélair", (1781 – 5 October 1802), was a Haitian Freedom fighter and revolutionary, lieutenant in the army of Toussaint Louverture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint Louverture
Content: François-Dominique Toussaint Louverture (] 20 May 1743 – 7 April 1803), also known as Toussaint L'Ouverture or Toussaint Bréda, was the best-known leader of the Haitian Revolution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint Louverture
Content:  His military and political acumen saved the gains of the first Black insurrection in November 1791.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint Louverture
Content:  He first fought for the Spanish against the French; then for France against Spain and Great Britain; and finally, for Saint-Domingue against Napoleonic France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toussaint Louverture
Content:  He then helped transform the insurgency into a revolutionary movement, which by 1800 had turned Saint-Domingue, the most prosperous slave colony of the time, into the first free colonial society to have explicitly rejected race as the basis of social ranking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Revolution (Nina Simone song)
Content: "Revolution" is a 1969 song by American jazz musician Nina Simone and Weldon Irvine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Revolution (Nina Simone song)
Content:  It was released as a single in 1969 and on the album "To Love Somebody" in 1969.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Revolution (Nina Simone song)
Content:  The single release was split over two sides of a 45 rpm disc and these two edits were used as separate tracks on the album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Revolution (Nina Simone song)
Content:  The song was released the year after the Beatles' "Revolution", and is seen by some as a variation of that song.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Revolution (Nina Simone song)
Content:  "Revolution" didn't do as well as expected and Simone has expressed surprise and disappointment at its lack of success.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content: Simone Torres arrives in the fictional town of Pine Valley, Pennsylvania in 2001 at the behest of Edmund Grey in order to aid his brother-in-law Mateo Santos uncover the identity of the drug lord "Proteus".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  An investigative reporter, Simone works to find Proteus's true identity while pretending to be engaged in an affair with Mateo, in order to protect the latter's wife and son from Proteus's wrath.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  Simone falls in love with Mateo, but it is clear his heart lies with his wife Hayley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  Simone mistakenly believes that a legitimate book publisher is interested in her authoring a book on the investigation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  Revealing inside information to the publisher, Simone is shocked when it turns out to be a front set up by "Vanessa Cortlandt", the real Proteus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  As a result of Simone's leak, FBI agent "Chris Stamp" is almost killed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Simone Torres
Content:  A furious Mateo distances himself from Simone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content: The Haitian Revolution provoked mixed reactions in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  In June 1793 when the Haitian people, led by Toussaint Louverture, overthrew the French colonial rule and declared themselves an independent colony, it made the neighboring United States uneasy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The slaves in Saint-Domingue’s were able to observe the growing disunity among the white colonists and themselves.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  They realized that they would need to seek an opportunity to stop the tyranny that was being placed upon them, thus they took a stand and revolted.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The Haitian Revolution of 1804 impacted the United States of America, led by Thomas Jefferson, instilling fear of racial instability in the US, and the possible problematic effect the revolution could have on the early foreign relations and trade between the US and the new independent Haiti.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  Thomas Jefferson realized that the Haitian Revolution had the potential to cause an upheaval against slavery in the US not only by the slaves themselves, but by white abolitionists as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  Southern slaveholders feared that the revolt might spread from the island of Hispaniola to the slave plantations of the Southern United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The primary goal of the US was to maintain social order in the country, so the United States attempted to suppress the Haitian Revolution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The US even went as far as to refuse acknowledgement of Haitian independence until 1862, which was during the heat of the North American civil war; coincidentally the main causal factor for the war between the states was slavery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The second major impact that the Haitian Revolution had on the United States was on early foreign relations and trade that had been conducted with Haiti.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The United States had conducted trade and commerce with the Haitian island under French rule during the eighteenth century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  Haiti was the main producer of the United States supply of sugar and coffee, and once the Haitian slave population had broken from slavery, the US was reluctant to continue trade with them in fear that they would upset the French and the Southern slaveholders.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  American merchants conducted a substantial trade with the plantations on Hispaniola (aka the French colony of Saint Domingue or Haiti).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  But there were anti-slavery advocates in northern cities who believed that consistency with the principles of the American Revolution — life, liberty and equality for all—demanded that the U.S. support the slave insurgents.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  An extremely beneficial aspect and real estate triumph that resulted from the Haitian Revolution and impacted the United States was the Louisiana Purchase.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  Once Napoleon had lost his control of the land holding in the Caribbean to the Haitian rebellion, he felt that the French territory in the southern part of the United States was useless to the French Empire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  The US was only interested in the New Orleans area; however, the revolution enabled the sale of the entire territory west of the Mississippi River for around $15 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: United States and the Haitian Revolution
Content:  This purchase more than doubled the United States’ territory.

session_0.text:
Title: Chrysanthemum japonense
Content:  It is a perennial flowering plant with leaves between 3–5 cm in length and flower heads that are 3-4.5 cm with white petals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chrysanthemum japonense
Content:  Typically, flowering occurs in October to December annually.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chrysanthemum japonense
Content:  It the floral emblem of Hyōgo Prefecture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crocosmia aurea
Content: Crocosmia aurea, common name Falling Stars, Valentine Flower, or Montbretia, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the family Iridaceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content: Wolffia arrhiza is a species of flowering plant known by the common names spotless watermeal and rootless duckweed, belonging to the Araceae, a family rich in water-loving species, such as "Arum" and "Pistia".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  It is the smallest vascular plant on Earth.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  It is native to Europe, Africa, and parts of Asia, and it is present in other parts of the world as a naturalized species.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  It is an aquatic plant which grows in quiet water bodies such as ponds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  The green part of the plant, the frond, is a sphere measuring about 1 mm wide, but with a flat top that floats at the water's surface.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  It has a few parallel rows of stomata.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  There is no root.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  The plant produces a minute flower fully equipped with one stamen and one pistil.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  It often multiplies by vegetative reproduction, however, with the rounded part budding off into a new individual.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  In cooler conditions the plant becomes dormant and sinks to the bed of the water body to overwinter as a turion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolffia arrhiza
Content:  The plant is a mixotroph which can produce its own energy by photosynthesis or absorb it from the environment in the form of dissolved carbon.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Crocosmia
Content: Crocosmia ( ; J. E. Planchon, 1851) (montbretia) is a small genus of flowering plants in the iris family, Iridaceae.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crocosmia
Content:  It is native to the grasslands of southern and eastern Africa, ranging from South Africa to Sudan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Crocosmia
Content:  One species is endemic to Madagascar.

Title: Cimicifuga
Content: Cimicifuga (bugbane or cohosh) was a genus of between 12-18 species of flowering plants belonging to the family Ranunculaceae, native to temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere.

session_0.text:
Title: Lycium ruthenicum
Content: Lycium ruthenicum (), is a flowering plant commonly known as Russian box thorn in the West.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lycium ruthenicum
Content:  is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family which can be found in Central Asia, southern part of Russia, throughout Northwest China, and Pakistan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lycium ruthenicum
Content:  Also commonly known as black fruit wolfberry, goji nero, siyah goji, kaokee, თეთრეკალა, (Tibetan name) “next to Mary”, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family which can be found in Central Asia, southern part of Russia, throughout Northwest China, and Pakistan.

Title: Actaea arizonica
Content: Actaea arizonica is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family known by the common name Arizona bugbane.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Actaea arizonica
Content:  It is endemic to Arizona in the United States, where it occurs in Coconino, Gila, and Yavapai Counties.

Content:  Like some other species in genus "Actaea", this plant was formerly included in the genus "Cimicifuga".

session_0.text:
Title: New Republic Party (South Africa)
Content: The New Republic Party (NRP) was a South African political party.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Republic Party (South Africa)
Content:  It was formed as the successor to the disbanded United Party (UP) in 1977 and as a merger with the smaller Democratic Party.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Republic Party (South Africa)
Content:  It drew its support mainly from the then Province of Natal, and tried to strike a moderate course between the apartheid policy of the ruling National Party (NP) and the liberal policies of the Progressive Federal Party (PFP).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content: Zollie Malindi (1924 – 21 April 2008) was a South African trade unionist and anti-apartheid activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  His life journey started in the small village of Gqogqora , near Tsomo in the former Transkei.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  While growing up, Zollie became aware of racial segregation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  Years later, after completing his Teaching Training Diploma, he set off to look for work in Cape Town; but in those days it was not easy for an African male to find employment even with a professional's qualification.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  Zollie became actively involved in the African National Party (ANC) and the South African Communist Party (SACP) in the 1940s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  As members of the ANC were encouraged to join unions, in no time, Zollie joined the Food and Canning Workers Union (FAWU) where he met political leaders like Oscar Mpetha and Ray Alexander.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  Zollie was a dedicated man and a powerful leader with a commanding presence and a high degree of responsibility.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  At the time of the ANC’s banning in 1960, Malindi was the Western Cape Provincial President.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  Following the passage of the 90-day detention laws in 1963, Malindi was arrested and detained together with Looksmart Ngudle, who was the first person to die in detention.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  In the years after his detention, Zollie became even more involved in the fight against apartheid and subsequently became a mentor to the younger generation of political activists that included well-known political figures such as former Minister of Finance Trevor Manuel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  After the 1994 elections, Zollie continued working as a member of the ANC, but his declining health forced him to retire alongside his wife, activist Letitia Malindi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zollie Malindi
Content:  A day after his death in 2008, Zollie Malindi was awarded the Order of Luthuli by former South African president Thabo Mbeki in recognition of his contribution to the trade union movement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Democratic Party (South Africa, 1973)
Content: The Democratic Party was a South African political party formed in 1973 by former Interior Minister Theo Gerdener after he broke away from the ruling National Party of South Africa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Democratic Party (South Africa, 1973)
Content:  The party hoped to be a rallying point for the "verligte" (enlightened) Nationalists who had grown disillusioned with the hardline apartheid government of John Vorster and attracted support from younger Afrikaners.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Democratic Party (South Africa, 1973)
Content:  The party advocated liberalizing the country's apartheid laws to some degree and emancipating Asian and "Coloured" South Africans and had a goal of re-establishing South Africa as a confederation of ethnic groupings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Union (South Africa)
Content: The National Union was a short-lived South African political party founded in 1960 by Japie Basson after he was expelled from the ruling National Party.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Union (South Africa)
Content:  It was meant to provide a political home for Nationalists who had become disillusioned with J. G. Strydom and Hendrik Verwoerd's increasingly hard-line apartheid policies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Union (South Africa)
Content:  Basson recruited former Chief Justice of South Africa Henry Allan Fagan to stand as the party's candidate for President in the 1961 general election, in which the party won 6.26% of the vote but only one parliamentary seat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: National Union (South Africa)
Content:  The party soon fizzled out and was absorbed into the United Party.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lesotho Liberation Army
Content: The Lesotho Liberation Army (LLA) was a guerrilla movement in Lesotho, formed in the mid-1970s and connected to both the anti-Apartheid Azanian People's Liberation Army (APLA) and the South African National Party government.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lesotho Liberation Army
Content:  It was the armed wing of the Basutoland Congress Party (BCP), a pan-Africanist and left-wing political party founded in 1952, which opposed the regime of Prime Minister Leabua Jonathan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Sisulu
Content: Samuel Sisulu (June 1956 - June 2003) was a South African anti-apartheid activist, Soweto uprising student leader and founder of South African Freedom Organisation (SAFO).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Sisulu
Content:  He was jailed at various prisons including Robben Island in 1978, two years after the 1976 Soweto uprising.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Sisulu
Content:  Samuel Sisulu was charged under the Terrorism Act.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Sisulu
Content:  Reported in WIP 3.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Sisulu
Content:  He was mentioned in the indictment of Paul Langa and found guilty of founding South African Freedom Organisation (SAFO), attempting to cripple the economy of the country by bombing the apartheid government, inciting persons to persuade taxi drivers not to transport workers to place of work, aid strikes, unlawfully aid students in their fight against Bantu education and was also found guilty of recruiting people for military training.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Democratic Party (South Africa)
Content: The Democratic Party (DP) was the name of the South African political party now called the Democratic Alliance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Democratic Party (South Africa)
Content:  Although the Democratic Party name dates from 1989, the party existed under other labels throughout the apartheid years, when it was the Parliamentary opposition to the ruling National Party's policies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Internal resistance to apartheid
Content: Internal resistance to "apartheid" in South Africa originated from several independent sectors of society and alternatively took the form of social movements, passive resistance, or guerrilla warfare.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Internal resistance to apartheid
Content:  Mass action against the ruling National Party government, coupled with South Africa's growing international isolation and economic sanctions, were instrumental factors in ending racial segregation and discrimination.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Internal resistance to apartheid
Content:  Both black and white South African activists such as Steve Biko, Desmond Tutu, Nelson Mandela, Harry Schwarz, and Joe Slovo were involved with various anti-apartheid causes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Internal resistance to apartheid
Content:  By the 1980s, there was continuous interplay between violent and non-violent action, and this interplay was a notable feature of resistance against apartheid from 1983 until South Africa's first multiracial elections under a universal franchise in 1994.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Desmond Tutu
Content: Desmond Mpilo Tutu {'1': ", '2': ", '3': 'OMSG CH GCStJ', '4': "} (born 7 October 1931) is a South African anti-apartheid and social rights activist and Anglican bishop.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Desmond Tutu
Content:  He was the first black Archbishop of Cape Town and bishop of the Church of the Province of Southern Africa (now the Anglican Church of Southern Africa).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letitia Sibeko
Content: Letitia Sibeko (born 1930) was a South African anti-apartheid activist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letitia Sibeko
Content:  She was member of the African National Congress, the ANC Women's League as well as the Federation of South African Women.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Letitia Sibeko
Content:  She later joined the Communist Party.

Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content: Jackie Chan Hill or Jackie Chan Village (Indonesian: "Kampung Jackie Chan"; formally: "The Friendship Village of Indonesia-China") is a neighbourhood in Banda Aceh, Indonesia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  It is named for Chinese actor Jackie Chan who, with other Hong Kong actors, helped fund the building of the community and purchase of the hill.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  Jackie Chan also campaigned with the Hong Kong Red Cross to raise additional relief funds that went to reconstruction of the site.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  Officially, the government does not allow villages to be named after individuals, hence the official name not bearing "Jackie Chan".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The neighbourhood is built up on a hill, high enough to avoid being inundated by a tsunami, thus being safe from tsunamis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The village is a green field construction, where only treed hills and farmers' fields once stood.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  It is located 25 minutes, some 17 km, outside of central Banda Aceh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The village is 1.5 km inland and elevated 300m.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The village has a clinic and kindergarten and a covered village square for a market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  However the market has not worked out.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  There are 606 mostly single family homes in the village.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The village was built by a Chinese contractor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The quality of the build is reasonable, unlike some other similar reconstruction efforts in Aceh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  There is no local high school, and the public transport system is insufficient to needs, as most jobs are located far from the village.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  The village opened in 2007 with 2400 residents from a variety of villages and a variety of ethnic groups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  They have lived harmoniously and built a community together.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  As of 2014, the community's kindergarten is currently unused.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  Some 1200 people remain, others having moved away to be closer to work or services.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackie Chan Hill
Content:  Those that still hold title to their homes have rented them out to others.

Content:  She made her acting debut in Puri Jagannadh's 2005 Telugu film "Super", and appeared in "Mahanandi", released later the same year.

Rajamouli's "Vikramarkudu", which helped her gain recognition, followed by "Astram" (a remake of the 1999 Hindi film "Sarfarosh"), the Sundar C.-directed "Rendu", (which marked her debut in Tamil cinema), and a special appearance in AR Murugadoss' Telugu film "Stalin".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  She had two releases in 2007: "Lakshyam" and "Don".

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  In 2008, she appeared in six films, including "Okka Magaadu", "Swagatam" and "Souryam".

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.text:

Content: Shruti Haasan is an Indian film actress, composer and playback singer who works in Telugu, Hindi, and Tamil cinema.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  Born into the prominent Haasan family, she is the daughter of actors Kamal Haasan and Sarika Thakur.

session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  Shruti Haasan started her career as a playback singer at the age of six in the 1992 Tamil film "Thevar Magan".

session_0.text:

Content:  She later made a cameo appearance in her father's Tamil-Hindi bilingual directorial "Hey Ram" (2000).

session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  Haasan's first major appearance was in Soham Shah's Hindi film "Luck" (2009), in which she played a dual role of a woman avenging her twin sister's death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  She played the female lead in the films "Anaganaga O Dheerudu" and "7aum Arivu"; both were released in 2011 and together earned her the Best Female Debut – South at the 59th Filmfare Awards South ceremony.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Her subsequent releases "Oh My Friend" (2011) and "3" (2012) were commercially unsuccessful.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  The latter earned her a nomination for the Best Actress – Tamil at the 60th Filmfare Awards South ceremony.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  A turning point came in Hassan's career with Harish Shankar's commercially successful Telugu film "Gabbar Singh" (2012).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shruti Haasan filmography
Content:  The release was followed by a series of successful films such as "Balupu" (2013) and "Yevadu" (2014).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anton Ginsburg
Content:  A disciple of Heinrich Neuhaus, he graduated from the Moscow Conservatory in 1953.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anton Ginsburg
Content:  Four years later he won the Smetana Competition in Prague.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Anton Ginsburg
Content:  Ginsburg has been active as a concert pianist both in the USSR and abroad, but is best remembered for his work as an accompanist with Daniil Shafran.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elem Klimov
Content: Elem Germanovich Klimov (Russian: Эле́м Ге́рманович Кли́мов ; 9 July 1933 – 26 October 2003) was a Soviet Russian film director.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elem Klimov
Content:  He studied at VGIK, and was married to film director Larisa Shepitko.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elem Klimov
Content:  Klimov is best known in the West for his final film, 1985's "Come and See" ("Иди и смотри"), which follows a teenage boy in German-occupied Belarus during the German-Soviet War and is often considered one of the greatest war films ever made.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Elem Klimov
Content:  He also directed dark comedies, children's movies, and historical pictures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Daniil Shafran
Content: Daniil Borisovich Shafran (Russian: Даниил Борисович Шафран , January 13, 1923February 7, 1997) was a Soviet Russian cellist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Boris Mozhayev
Content: Boris Andreyevich Mozhayev (Борис Андреевич Можаев, June 1, 1923, Pitelino village, Ryazan Governorate - March 2, 1996, Moscow) was a Soviet Russian author, dramatist, script-writer and editor, the USSR State Prize (1989) laureate, best known for his novel "Zhivoy" (Alive, 1966) and the two-part epic "Peasant Men and Women" (Muzhiki i babyi, 1972-1980).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Boris Mozhayev
Content:  Supported by Alexander Tvardovsky and admired by Alexander Solzhenitsyn, Mozhayev experienced serious difficulties with publishing his harshly realistic, tinged with bitter humour Village prose, dealing with trials and tribulations of the Soviet peasantry in the years of collectivisation and beyond.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dmitry Buchkin
Content: Dmitry Petrovich Buchkin (Russian: Дмитрий Петрович Бучкин ; July 23, 1927, Leningrad, USSR) is a Soviet Russian painter, a member of the Saint Petersburg Union of Artists (before 1992 — the Leningrad Union of Artists), who lives and works in Saint Petersburg.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dmitry Buchkin
Content:  He is also regarded as one of the representatives of the Leningrad school of painting, being best known for his genre and landscape paintings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dmitry Buchkin
Content:  In the 1960s and 1980s he repeatedly worked in the House of creativity «Staraya Ladoga», later he worked in his own cottage with his art studio in Staraya Ladoga.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Razumnik Ivanov-Razumnik
Content: Razumnik Vasilyevich Ivanov-Razumnik (real surname - Ivanov; Разумник Васильевич Иванов-Разумник; December 24 1878, Tiflis, Georgia, then Russian Empire, - July 9, 1946, Munich, Germany was a Soviet Russian writer, philosopher and literary critic, best known for his book "History of Russian Social Thought" (1907, in two volumes) and the series of essays on post-Revolution literary life in the Soviet Russia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mstislav Rostropovich
Content: Mstislav Leopoldovich "Slava" Rostropovich (Russian: Мстисла́в Леопо́льдович Ростропо́вич , "Mstislav Leopol'dovič Rostropovič", ] ; 27 March 192727 April 2007) was a Soviet and Russian cellist and conductor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mstislav Rostropovich
Content:  He is considered to be one of the greatest cellists of the 20th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mstislav Rostropovich
Content:  In addition to his interpretations and technique, he was well known for both inspiring and commissioning new works, which enlarged the cello repertoire more than any cellist before or since.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mstislav Rostropovich
Content:  He inspired and premiered over 100 pieces, forming long-standing friendships and artistic partnerships with composers including Dmitri Shostakovich, Sergei Prokofiev, Henri Dutilleux, Witold Lutosławski, Olivier Messiaen, Luciano Berio, Krzysztof Penderecki, Alfred Schnittke, Norbert Moret, Andreas Makris and Benjamin Britten.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aleksandr Medvedkin
Content: Aleksandr Ivanovich Medvedkin (Russian: Александр Иванович Медведкин ; 24 February 1900 – 20 February 1989), was a Soviet Russian film director, best known for his 1934 film "Happiness".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Aleksandr Medvedkin
Content:  His life and art are the subject of Chris Marker's film "The Last Bolshevik" (1992).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander Belyaev
Content: Alexander Romanovich Belyaev (Russian: Алекса́ндр Рома́нович Беля́ев , ] ; 16 March 1884 – 6 January 1942) was a Soviet Russian writer of science fiction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander Belyaev
Content:  His works from the 1920s and 1930s made him a highly regarded figure in Russian science fiction, often referred to as "Russia's Jules Verne".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alexander Belyaev
Content:  Belyaev's best known books include "Professor Dowell's Head", "Amphibian Man", "Ariel", and "The Air Seller".

Title: LanSlide Gaming PCs
Content: LanSlide Gaming PCs, LLC was founded in 2005 by a group of gamers tired of moving large gaming desktops to LAN parties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LanSlide Gaming PCs
Content:  The company focuses on computers designed to be portable and sells a wide range of desktop gaming computers, all of which are built into cases with carrying handles for ease of transport.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LanSlide Gaming PCs
Content:  Each computer comes with a special backpack to hold everything needed to run a desktop computer with the exception of the computer case.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: LanSlide Gaming PCs
Content:  In addition to portable computers, LanSlide Gaming PCs also sells a line of computers designed for 3D gaming.

Title: MacBook Pro
Content: The MacBook Pro (sometimes abbreviated MBP) is a line of Macintosh portable computers introduced in January 2006 by Apple Inc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook Pro
Content:  Replacing the PowerBook G4, the MacBook Pro was the second model to be announced during the Apple–Intel transition, after the iMac.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook Pro
Content:  It is the high-end model of the MacBook family and is currently available in 13- and 15-inch screen sizes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook Pro
Content:  A 17-inch version was available between April 2006 and June 2012.
