session_0.text:
Title: Single-board computer
Content: A single-board computer (SBC) is a complete computer built on a single circuit board, with microprocessor(s), memory, input/output (I/O) and other features required of a functional computer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Single-board computer
Content:  Single-board computers were made as demonstration or development systems, for educational systems, or for use as embedded computer controllers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Single-board computer
Content:  Many types of home computers or portable computers integrate all their functions onto a single printed circuit board.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content: The Amstrad PPC512 and Amstrad PPC640 were the first portable IBM PC compatible computers made by Amstrad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content:  Released in 1988, they were a development of the desktop PC-1512 and PC-1640 models.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content:  As portable computers, they contained all the elements necessary to perform computing on the move.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content:  They had a keyboard and a monochrome LCD display built in and also had space for disposable batteries to power the PC where a suitable alternative power source (i.e. mains or 12 volt vehicle power) was not available.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content:  The PCs came with either one or two double density double side floppy disc drives and the PPC640 model also featured a modem.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: PPC 512
Content:  Both models were supplied with 'PPC Organiser' software and the PPC640 was additionally supplied with the 'Mirror II' communications software.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of laptops
Content: The history of laptops describes the efforts in the 1970s and 1980s to build small, portable personal computers that combine the components, inputs, outputs and capabilities of a desktop computer in a small chassis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of laptops
Content:  Before laptop/notebook computers were technically feasible, similar ideas had been proposed, most notably Alan Kay's Dynabook concept, developed at Xerox PARC in the early 1970s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of laptops
Content:  One of the first reasonably portable computers was the Xerox NoteTaker, again developed at Xerox PARC, in 1976.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of laptops
Content:  However, only 10 prototypes were built.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook (Retina)
Content: The MacBook is a line of Macintosh portable computers introduced in March 2015 by Apple Inc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook (Retina)
Content:  The MacBook has a similar appearance to the MacBook Air, but is thinner and lighter, and is available in colours called space grey, silver, gold, and rose gold.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook (Retina)
Content:  It offers a high-resolution Retina Display, a Force Touch trackpad, a redesigned keyboard, and only two ports: a headphone jack and a USB 3.1 Type-C port for charging, data transfer and video output.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chuck Colby
Content: Chuck Colby is an electronics engineer and chief-inventor, founder and president of Colby Systems Corporation, a company that created the first DVR-based video surveillance systems but is also very notable as a pioneer in portable computing, being the first to market both DOS and Macintosh portable computers, as well as a remarkable number of other technological firsts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Apricot Portable
Content: The Apricot Portable was a computing device manufactured by Apricot Computers, and was released to the public in November 1984.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Apricot Portable
Content:  It was Apricot Computers' first attempt at manufacturing a portable computer, which were gaining popularity at the time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Apricot Portable
Content:  Compared to other portable computers of its time like the Compaq Portable and the Commodore SX-64, the Apricot Portable was the first computer to have an 80-column and 25-line LCD screen and an input/output speech recognition system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook family
Content: The MacBook family is a brand of Macintosh laptop computers by Apple Inc. that merged the PowerBook and iBook lines during Apple's transition to Intel processors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook family
Content:  The current lineup consists of the MacBook (2006–2012; 2015–present), the MacBook Air (2008–present), and the MacBook Pro (2006–present).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MacBook family
Content:  The MacBook and redesigned MacBook Pro are the only MacBooks that do not incorporate the use of a MagSafe charger, but rather a USB Type-C port.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Retina Display
Content: Retina Display is a brand name used by Apple for its series of IPS panel displays that have a higher pixel density than traditional displays.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Retina Display
Content:  Apple has applied to register the term "Retina" as a trademark in regard to computers and mobile devices with the United States Patent and Trademark Office, Canadian Intellectual Property Office, and in Jamaica.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Retina Display
Content:  On November 27, 2012 the US Patent and Trademark office approved Apple's application and "Retina" is now a registered trademark for computer equipment.

Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content: Arrival is the fourth studio album by the Swedish pop group ABBA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content:  It was originally released in Sweden on 11 October 1976 by Polar Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content:  Recording sessions began in August 1975 and continued until September 1976 at Metronome and Glen studios in Stockholm, Sweden.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content:  It became one of ABBA's most successful albums to date, producing three of their biggest hits: "Dancing Queen", "Money, Money, Money" and "Knowing Me, Knowing You".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content:  Upon its original 1976 release, "Fernando", released as a single earlier the same year, did not appear on "Arrival", but it was included on the Australian and New Zealand versions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arrival (ABBA album)
Content:  "Arrival" was the best-selling album of 1977 in the United Kingdom and was certified gold by the Recording Industry Association of America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Money, Money, Money
Content: "Money, Money, Money" is a song recorded by Swedish pop group ABBA, written by Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Money, Money, Money
Content:  It was released as a single on 1 November 1976, as the follow-up to "Dancing Queen" (both from the album "Arrival").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Money, Money, Money
Content:  The B-side, "Crazy World", was recorded in 1974 during the sessions for the album "ABBA".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ABBA: The Movie
Content: ABBA: The Movie is a 1977 documentary film about the pop group ABBA's Australian tour.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ABBA: The Movie
Content:  It was directed by Lasse Hallström, who directed most of the group's videos.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ABBA: The Movie
Content:  The film has become a cult film among ABBA fans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ABBA: The Movie
Content:  Its release coincided with the release of "", the group's fifth studio album, and features many songs from that album as well as many of their earlier hits, and one, "Get on the Carousel", unavailable anywhere else.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm a Marionette
Content: "I'm a Marionette" is a song recorded by the Swedish pop group ABBA from their fifth album, "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm a Marionette
Content:  Written by Björn Ulvaeus and Benny Andersson, this song was part of a mini-musical called "The Girl with the Golden Hair" that ABBA performed at several of their live concerts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm a Marionette
Content:  The other songs in the "mini-musical" were "Thank You for the Music", "I Wonder (Departure)" and "Get on the Carousel".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm a Marionette
Content:  With the exception of the last track, studio versions of these appeared on the 1977 ABBA album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm a Marionette
Content:  "I'm a Marionette" was the B-side of "Take a Chance on Me", when it was released as a single.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content: "The Piper" is a 1980 track off the "Super Trouper" album by Swedish pop group ABBA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content:  The song is loosely based on the famous story of The Pied Piper of Hamelin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content:  It is regarded by some ABBA fans as being very different from the more mainstream songs they had recorded until this time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content:  In particular, the dark lyrics dealing with the seduction by fascistic leaders and a somewhat medieval sound (drums, flute, choral) are not seen in their earlier songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content:  It is also the only ABBA song where a part of the refrain is in Latin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Piper (ABBA song)
Content:  The song has gained a small cult following among ABBA fans.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Disillusion (ABBA song)
Content: "Disillusion" is a ballad by the pop group ABBA, on their first album "Ring Ring" (1973).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Disillusion (ABBA song)
Content:  It is notable as the only song ABBA recorded to have a song-writing credit from Agnetha Faltskog.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Disillusion (ABBA song)
Content:  She, unlike Anni-Frid Lyngstad, was a songwriter as well as a singer, and had dabbled in that in her pre-ABBA career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Disillusion (ABBA song)
Content:  She wrote the music, with lyrics added by fellow ABBA member Björn Ulvaeus.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: I Have a Dream (song)
Content:  It was featured on the group's sixth studio album "Voulez-Vous" and released as a single in December 1979.

Content: Oz the Great and Powerful is a 2013 American fantasy adventure film directed by Sam Raimi and produced by Joe Roth, from a screenplay written by David Lindsay-Abaire and Mitchell Kapner.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Oz the Great and Powerful
Content:  Based on L. Frank Baum's "Oz" novels and set 20 years before the events of the original novel, "Oz the Great and Powerful" is a spiritual prequel to the 1939 Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer film, "The Wizard of Oz".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Stølsdalsnutane
Content: Stølsdalsnutane or Støylsdalsnutene is a mountain on the border of Aust-Agder and Telemark counties in southern Norway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stølsdalsnutane
Content:  The 1438 m tall mountain actually has 3 peaks, all three are just slightly over the border inside Bykle municipality in Aust-Agder, but much of the mountain lies in neighboring Tokke municipality in Telemark.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stølsdalsnutane
Content:  The highest peak, known as "Nordvestre Stølsdalsnuten", is the 7th highest peak in Aust-Agder.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stølsdalsnutane
Content:  The second highest of the three peaks, called "Nordre Stølsdalsknuten" is the 9th highest peak in the county at 1424 m , and the third peak, known as "Sørvestre Stølsdalsknuten", is the 10th highest peak in the county at 1420 m .

Title: Mount Foraker
Content: Mount Foraker is a 17400 ft mountain in the central Alaska Range, in Denali National Park, 14 mi southwest of Denali.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Foraker
Content:  It is the second highest peak in the Alaska Range, and the third highest peak in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Foraker
Content:  It rises almost directly above the standard base camp for Denali, on a fork of the Kahiltna Glacier also near Mount Hunter in the Alaska Range.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Le Conte (Tennessee)
Content: Mount Le Conte (or LeConte) is a mountain in Sevier County, Tennessee located in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Le Conte (Tennessee)
Content:  At 6593 ft it is the third highest peak in the national park, behind Clingmans Dome (6,643 ft, 2,024 m) and Mount Guyot (6,621 ft, 2,018 m).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Le Conte (Tennessee)
Content:  It is also the highest peak that is completely within Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Le Conte (Tennessee)
Content:  However, from its immediate base to its summit, Mount Le Conte is one of the highest peaks in the Appalachian Mountains rising 5,301 ft (1,616 m) from its base, near Gatlinburg, Tennessee (1,292 ft/394 m).

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Mount Pinchot (Montana)
Content: Mount Pinchot (9310 ft ) is located in the Lewis Range, Glacier National Park in the U.S. state of Montana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Pinchot (Montana)
Content:  Mount Pinchot is less than 1.5 mi SSE of Mount Stimson while Beaver Woman Lake is southeast of Mount Pinchot.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Stimson
Content: Mount Stimson (10142 ft ) is the second highest peak in Glacier National Park, located in Montana, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Stimson
Content:  It is part of the Lewis Range, which spans much of the park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Stimson
Content:  It is located in the remote southwestern portion of the park, approximately 5 mi west of the Continental Divide and 12 mi southeast of Lake McDonald.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Stimson
Content:  It is drained by Pinchot Creek (on the south) and Nyack Creek (on the other sides), both of which flow into the Middle Fork of the Flathead River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content: Mount Schurz el.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  11007 ft is a mountain peak in the Absaroka Range in Yellowstone National Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  Mount Schurz is the second highest peak in Yellowstone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  The mountain was originally named Mount Doane by Henry D. Washburn during the Washburn–Langford–Doane Expedition in 1871.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  Later the name Mount Doane was given to another peak in the Absaroka Range by geologist Arnold Hague.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  In 1885, Hague named the mountain for the 13th U.S. Secretary of the Interior, Carl Schurz (1877–1881).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Schurz
Content:  Schurz was the first Secretary of the Interior to visit Yellowstone and a strong supporter of the national park movement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Glacier National Park (U.S.)
Content: Glacier National Park is a national park located in the U.S. state of Montana, on the Canada–United States border with the Canadian provinces of Alberta and British Columbia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Glacier National Park (U.S.)
Content:  The park encompasses over 1 million acre and includes parts of two mountain ranges (sub-ranges of the Rocky Mountains), over 130 named lakes, more than 1,000 different species of plants, and hundreds of species of animals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Glacier National Park (U.S.)
Content:  This vast pristine ecosystem is the centerpiece of what has been referred to as the "Crown of the Continent Ecosystem", a region of protected land encompassing 16000 mi2 .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Glacier National Park (U.S.)
Content:  The current Superintendent is Jeff Mow.

Title: Mount Hubley (Alaska)
Content: Mount Hubley is the second highest peak in the Brooks Range, Alaska, USA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Hubley (Alaska)
Content:  Located in the eastern Brooks Range, in what are known as the Romanzof Mountains, Mount Hubley is 5 mi north of Mount Isto, the tallest peak in the Brooks Range.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Hubley (Alaska)
Content:  Mount Hubley is within the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge and was named in 1958 for Dr. Richard Carleton Hubley, a coordinator for the International Geophysical Year who died in 1957 while doing research on the adjacent McCall Glacier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Hubley (Alaska)
Content:  In 2014 new measurement technology established that Mount Hubley is the second highest peak in the Brooks Range after Mount Isto.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Hubley (Alaska)
Content:  Previously, Mount Chamberlin was believed to be the tallest, but it is now ranked third.

Title: Mount Tehama
Content: Mount Tehama (also called Brokeoff Volcano or Brokeoff Mountain) is an eroded andesitic stratovolcano in the Cascade Volcanic Arc and the Cascade Range in Northern California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Tehama
Content:  Part of the Lassen volcanic area, its highest remaining remnant, Brokeoff Mountain, is itself the second highest peak in Lassen Volcanic National Park and connects to the park's highest point, Lassen Peak.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Tehama
Content:  Located on the border of Tehama County and Shasta County, Tehama's peak is the highest point in the former.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Tehama
Content:  The hikers that summit this mountain each year are treated to "exceptional" views of Lassen Peak, the Central Valley of California, and many of the park's other features.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mount Tehama
Content:  On clear days, Mount Shasta can also be seen in the distance.

Title: Thompson Peak (California)
Content: Thompson Peak is a mountain (a high point on a tall granite ridge) in Trinity County, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Peak (California)
Content:  It is the highest peak in the Trinity Alps Wilderness.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It is the highest point in a ridge that also features Wedding Cake, another well-known Trinity Alps peak.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Peak (California)
Content:  Thompson Peak is the highest Peak in the Trinity Alps Wilderness, a vast assemblage of craggy granite mountains in northwestern California (Trinity Alps Wilderness is the seventh largest designated wilderness area in California).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Peak (California)
Content:  Thompson Peak is the second highest mountain in Northern California west of the Cascades, after 9027 foot; Mount Eddy.

Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content: Watford Football Club is an English association football club based in Watford, Hertfordshire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  Formed as Watford Rovers in 1881, and renamed West Hertfordshire in 1893, the team joined the Southern League in 1896.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  West Hertfordshire merged with local rivals Watford St. Mary's for the start of the 1898–99 season, adopting the club's present name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  Between 1898 and 1920, Watford competed in the Southern League, winning the championship in 1914–15.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  The Southern League was suspended for the next four seasons due to the First World War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  On the league's resumption in 1919–20, Watford finished as runners up on goal average.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  At the start of 1920–21, Watford joined the Football League Third Division, and transferred to the Third Division South when the league was reorganised the following season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  They have played in the Football League ever since, with the exception of 1939–1946, when competitive football was suspended due to the Second World War, and the 1999–2000 and 2006–07 seasons, when they competed in the Premier League.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Watford F.C. players
Content:  In addition to the latter two seasons, the club also competed in the top division of English football between 1982 and 1988, achieving their highest league placing of second in the 1982–83 season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007–08 Watford F.C. season
Content: Following relegation from the Premier League in 2006–07, Watford started the 2007–08 season strongly, winning eleven of their opening sixteen games and opening up an 8-point gap at the top of the Championship table.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007–08 Watford F.C. season
Content:  However, after the departure of Adam Johnson to Middlesbrough on 11 November 2007 Watford's form declined; they won six of their next sixteen Championship games, and only one of their final sixteen.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007–08 Watford F.C. season
Content:  In January 2008 Watford sold captain Gavin Mahon and top scorer Marlon King, but purchased Mat Sadler, Leigh Bromby and John Eustace.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007–08 Watford F.C. season
Content:  Watford finished the 2007–08 season in 6th position, and were eliminated in the play-off semi finals to Hull City.

Title: Vincas Kudirka
Content: Vincas Kudirka (31 December [O.S. 19 December] 1858 – 16 November [O.S. 4 November] 1899 ) was a Lithuanian poet and physician, and the author of both the music and lyrics of the Lithuanian National Anthem, "Tautiška giesmė".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Vincas Kudirka
Content:  He is regarded in Lithuania as a National Hero.

session_0.text:
Title: Vincas Kudirka
Content:  Kudirka used pen names V. Kapsas, Paežerių Vincas, Vincas Kapsas, P.Vincas, Varpas, Q.D, K., V.K, Perkūnas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tautiška giesmė
Content: Tautiška giesmė ("The National Hymn"; ] ) is the national anthem of Lithuania, also known by its opening words "Lietuva, Tėvyne mūsų" (official translation of the lyrics: "Lithuania, Our Homeland", literally: "Lithuania, Our Fatherland") and as "Lietuvos himnas" (Hymn of Lithuania).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tautiška giesmė
Content:  The music and lyrics were written in 1898 by Vincas Kudirka, when Lithuania was still part of the Russian Empire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tautiška giesmė
Content:  The fifty-word poem was a condensation of Kudirka's conceptions of the Lithuanian state, the Lithuanian people, and their past.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tautiška giesmė
Content:  Shortly before his death in 1899, the anthem was performed for Lithuanians living in Saint Petersburg, Russia.

Title: List of historical national anthems
Content: The oldest national anthem, defined as "a song, as of praise, devotion, or patriotism", is the Dutch national anthem "Het Wilhelmus", which was written between 1568 and 1572, but not then given any official status.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of historical national anthems
Content:  The first anthem to be officially proclaimed as such was "God Save The Queen", adopted by Great Britain in 1745.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of historical national anthems
Content:  "Het Wilhelmus" was declared the national anthem of the Netherlands in 1932; both of these anthems remain in use today.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of historical national anthems
Content:  A royal or imperial anthem is a song that is similar in patriotic character to a national anthem, but which specifically praises a monarch, or royal dynasty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of historical national anthems
Content:  Some states have doubled their royal or imperial anthem as their national anthem.

Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content: Many words that existed in Old English did not survive into Modern English.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  There are also many words in Modern English that bear little or no resemblance in meaning to their Old English etymons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Some linguists estimate that as much as 80 percent of the lexicon of Old English was lost by the end of the Middle English period, including a large number of words formed by compounding, e.g. "bōchūs" ('bookhouse', 'library'), yet we still retain the component parts 'book' and 'house'.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Certain categories of words seem to have been especially vulnerable.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Nearly all words relating to sexual intercourse and sexual organs were supplanted by words of Latin or Ancient Greek origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Many, if not most, of the words in Modern English that are used in polite conversation to describe body parts and bodily functions are of Latin or Greek origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  The words which were used in Old English for these same purposes are now mostly either extinct or considered crude or vulgar, such as arse/ass.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content: Circumlocution (also called circumduction, circumvolution, periphrasis, or ambage) is speech that circles around an idea with many words instead of stating it directly and simply.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  It is sometimes necessary in communication (for example, to avoid lexical gaps that would cause untranslatability), but it can also be undesirable (when an uncommon or easily misunderstood figure of speech is used).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Roundabout speech is the use of many words to describe something that already has a common and concise term (for example, saying ""a tool used for cutting things such as paper and hair"" instead of "scissors").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Most dictionaries use circumlocution to define words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Circumlocution is often used by people with aphasia and people learning a new language, where simple terms can be paraphrased to aid learning or communication (for example, paraphrasing the word "grandfather" as "the father of one's father").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Euphemism, innuendo, and equivocation are different forms of circumlocution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Varpas
Content: Varpas (literally: "The Bell") was a monthly Lithuanian-language newspaper published during the Lithuanian press ban from January 1889 to December 1905.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Varpas
Content:  Because its publication was illegal in Lithuania, then part of the Russian Empire, it was printed in Tilsit (current Sovetsk) and Ragnit (current Neman) in German East Prussia and smuggled into Lithuania by the knygnešiai (book smugglers).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Varpas
Content:  "Varpas", with circulation of about 500–1,000, played a pivotal role in the Lithuanian National Revival. "

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Varpas
Content: Tautiška giesmė", one of poems by founder and editor Vincas Kudirka written to commemorate the 10th anniversary of "Varpas", became the Lithuanian national anthem.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Varpas
Content:  Editorial staff of "Varpas" later started two more specialized publications: more practical "Ūkininkas" ("The Farmer", 1890–1905) for less educated peasants and apolitical "Naujienos" ("News", 1901–1903) for general public.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: He Mele Lahui Hawaii
Content: "He Mele Lāhui Hawaiʻ i" ("The Song of the Hawaiian Nation") was composed by Liliuokalani in November 1866 at the request of Kamehameha V, who wanted a national anthem to replace the British anthem "God Save the King".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: He Mele Lahui Hawaii
Content:  It replaced Lunalilo's composition "E Ola Ke Aliʻ i Ke Akua" as the national anthem.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: He Mele Lahui Hawaii
Content:  Liliʻ uokalani wrote: "The king was present for the purpose of Criticising my new composition of both words and music, and was liberal in his commendations to me on my success.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: He Mele Lahui Hawaii
Content:  He admired not only the beauty of music, but spoke enthusiastically of the appropriate words, so well adapted to the air and to the purpose for which they were written.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: He Mele Lahui Hawaii
Content:  This remained in use as our national anthem for some twenty years or more when my brother composed the words "Hawaiʻ i Ponoʻ ī"."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lithuanian National Revival
Content: Lithuanian National Revival, alternatively Lithuanian National Awakening (Lithuanian: "Lietuvių tautinis atgimimas" ), was a period of the history of Lithuania in the 19th century at the time when a major part of Lithuanian-inhabited areas belonged to the Russian Empire (the Russian partition of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lithuanian National Revival
Content:  It was expressed by the rise of self-determination of the Lithuanians that led to formation of the modern Lithuanian nation and culminated in the re-establishment of an independent Lithuanian state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lithuanian National Revival
Content:  Most active participants of national revival were Vincas Kudirka, Jonas Basanavičius and others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lithuanian National Revival
Content:  The period largely corresponded to the rise of romantic nationalism and other national revivals of the 19th century Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content: "O Canada" (French: "Ô Canada" ) is the national anthem of Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content:  The song was originally commissioned by Lieutenant Governor of Quebec Théodore Robitaille for the 1880 Saint-Jean-Baptiste Day ceremony; Calixa Lavallée composed the music, after which, words were written by the poet and judge Sir Adolphe-Basile Routhier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content:  The lyrics were originally in French; an English version was created in 1906.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content:  Robert Stanley Weir wrote in 1908 another English version, which is the official and most popular version, one that is not a literal translation of the French.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content:  Weir's lyrics have been revised twice, taking their present form in 1980, but the French lyrics remain unaltered.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O Canada
Content:  "O Canada" had served as a "de facto" national anthem since 1939, officially becoming Canada's national anthem in 1980 when the Act of Parliament making it so received royal assent and became effective on July 1 as part of that year's Dominion Day (now known as Canada Day) celebrations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great British Garden Revival
Content: Great British Garden Revival is a British documentary television series that was first broadcast on BBC Two on 9 December 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great British Garden Revival
Content:  The series was presented by Monty Don, Carol Klein, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, James Wong, Tom Hart Dyke, Chris Beardshaw, Alys Fowler, Charlie Dimmock, Diarmuid Gavin, Christine Walkden, Toby Buckland, Sarah Raven and Matt James.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great British Garden Revival
Content:  Each episode shows two presenters focusing on an endangered part of gardens.

Title: Matt James (game designer)

Content:  As a game designer, James is best known for his online and print works for the "Dungeons & Dragons" fantasy role-playing game published by Wizards of the Coast, "Pathfinder" role-playing game by Paizo, and Privateer Press.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  In 2012 James won an ENnie Award for "" and has been nominated for several Origins Awards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Matt James (game designer)
Content:  In 2014, James won an ENnie Award for "".

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Taskasaplidis
Content: Taskasaplidis (Greek:Τασκασαπλίδης) is a Greek last name (or family name).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Taskasaplidis
Content:  It is believed that the name derived from a local area of the city of Istanbul (formerly Constantinople) Turkey, called "Taşkasap (Tashkasap)".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Taskasaplidis
Content:  The patronymic suffix -idis, used mostly from Greeks in the Pontus and Asia Minor regions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Taskasaplidis
Content:  The patronymic infix -li, sometimes is used to declare that the name derived from a location or origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Taskasaplidis
Content:  For example, the common Greek family name "Tokatlidis", uses the infix -li (also the suffix -idis) and the name derives from the city name Tokat (Greek:Τοκάτη), Turkey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content: Geddes is a surname of English and Scottish origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  In Scotland and northern Ireland the name may be derived from the place-name Geddes in Nairn, Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  However, according to the "Dictionary of American Family Names", the surname is more likely a patronymic name derived from the name "Geddie".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  This name ("Geddie"), may be an altered form of MacAdam.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  In this way, the letter "G" represents the Gaelic "mac" "son of" and "Eddie" is a variant of Adam.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  "Geddie" may also be a nickname meaning "greedy", derived from "gedd" meaning "pike", this could also refer to a voracious eater.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  The earliest written record of the surname "Geddes" is of William "Ged", from Shropshire, England, recorded within the Pipe Rolls in the year 1230.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geddes (surname)
Content:  The surname Geddes can be represented in Scottish Gaelic as "Geadasach" and "Geadais".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Matthew Carlson
Content: Matthew Carlson (birth name: Matt James Carlson) (born February 10, 1951) is an American television producer and writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northwest Passage (book)
Content: Northwest Passage is a book based upon the famous Canadian song "Northwest Passage".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northwest Passage (book)
Content:  The song is a story of a man's travel through the arctic of Canada while following famed explorers like Alexander Mackenzie, David Thompson, John Franklin, and Henry Kelsey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northwest Passage (book)
Content:  The book is written and narrated by Matthew James, who is an award-winning Canadian musician, illustrator, and author and has been nominated for, and won, the Governor General's Award for children's literature.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northwest Passage (book)
Content:  The book is full of unique illustrations, also done by Matt James, showing the story as it is mentioned in the song.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Northwest Passage (book)
Content:  It also includes a timeline of Canadian exploration, miniature biographies on explorers of the Northwest Passage, and portraits of major explorers.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Dąbrowski
Content: Dąbrowski (] ; feminine Dąbrowska, plural Dąbrowscy) or Dabrowski is the 11th most common surname in Poland (87,304 people in 2009); this is down from an apparent rank of 4th in 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dąbrowski
Content:  "Dąbrowski" is a habitational name derived from the placename 'Dąbrowa' or 'Dąbrówka', which is used for several specific places in Poland or generically as "oak grove", the English meaning for these Polish words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dąbrowski
Content:  Variants of the surname include Dombrowski, Dobrowski, and Dobrosky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dąbrowski
Content:  "Dobrowski" also has an independent origin as a habitational name derived from the placename 'Dobrów'.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dąbrowski
Content:  The text-figure below summarizes the relationships among these various words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dąbrowski
Content:  In other Slavic countries, the same surname takes the form Dubrovsky, as the Polish "ą" corresponds to "u" in most other Slavic languages.

Title: ENnies
Content: The Annual Gen Con EN World RPG Awards (or ENnie Awards) are annual, fan-based awards for role-playing game products and publishers hosted at Gen Con in Indianapolis, Indiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ENnies
Content:  The name of the award is derived from the EN World web site that has hosted the awards since their inception in 2001.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: ENnies
Content:  The ENnies were created by Russ Morrissey and Eric Noah and have been run and owned by Russ Morrissey since 2001.

Title: Harvey (name)
Content: Harvey is an English family and given name derived from the Breton name "Huiarnuiu", derived from an Old Breton first name "Huiarnviu", derived from the elements "hoiarn", "huiarn" (Breton "houarn") meaning "iron" and "viu" (Breton "bev") meaning "blazing".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Harvey (name)
Content:  It is related to Old Welsh "Haarnbiu".

Title: Seth Curry
Content: Seth Adham Curry (born August 23, 1990) is an American professional basketball player for the Dallas Mavericks of the National Basketball Association (NBA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seth Curry
Content:  A native of Charlotte, North Carolina, Curry played collegiately for one year at Liberty University before transferring to Duke.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seth Curry
Content:  He is the son of former NBA player Dell Curry and the younger brother of current NBA player Stephen Curry.

Title: Klay Thompson
Content: Klay Alexander Thompson (born February 8, 1990) is an American professional basketball player for the Golden State Warriors of the National Basketball Association (NBA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  The son of former NBA player Mychal Thompson, he played college basketball for three seasons at Washington State University, where he was a two-time first-team all-conference selection in the Pac-10.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  Thompson was selected in the first round of the 2011 NBA draft by Golden State with the 11th overall pick.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  In 2014, he and teammate Stephen Curry set a then NBA record with 484 combined three-pointers in a season, as the pair were given the nickname the "Splash Brothers".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  Thompson is a three-time NBA All-Star and a two-time All-NBA Third Team honoree.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  In 2015, he helped lead the Warriors to their first NBA Championship since 1975.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klay Thompson
Content:  Thompson helped the Warriors return to the NBA Finals for a third straight year in 2017, winning his second NBA Championship.

Content: The 1995–96 NBA season was the Magic's seventh season in the National Basketball Association.

Content:  During the offseason, the Magic signed unrestricted free agent Jon Koncak.

Content:  Coming off their trip to the NBA Finals, the Magic clinched the Atlantic Division title with a 60–22 record.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  The season saw Dennis Scott take a place in the league history books by scoring 267 three-point field goals, a single season record since broken by Stephen Curry, while O'Neal and Hardaway were both selected for the 1996 NBA All-Star Game.

Title: 50–40–90 club
Content: Informally, the 50–40–90 club is the group of National Basketball Association (NBA) players who have had a shooting percentage at or above 50% for field goals, 40% for three-pointers, and 90% for free throws during an entire NBA regular season while also achieving the NBA minimum number of makes in each category.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 50–40–90 club
Content:  Only seven players have had 50–40–90 seasons.

Title: Stephen Curry
Content: Wardell Stephen Curry II (born March 14, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Golden State Warriors of the National Basketball Association (NBA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Curry
Content:  Many players and analysts have called him the greatest shooter in NBA history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Curry
Content:  In 2014–15, Curry won the NBA Most Valuable Player Award and led the Warriors to their first championship since 1975.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Curry
Content:  The following season, he became the first player in NBA history to be elected MVP by a unanimous vote and to lead the league in scoring while shooting above 50–40–90.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Curry
Content:  That same year, the Warriors broke the record for the most wins in an NBA season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Curry
Content:  Curry helped the Warriors return to the NBA Finals for a third straight year in 2017, where he won his second NBA championship.

Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content: The Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry is a National Basketball Association (NBA) rivalry between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Golden State Warriors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content:  While the two teams have played each other since the Cavaliers joined the league in 1970, their rivalry did not develop until the 2014–15 season, when they met in the first of three consecutive NBA Finals series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content:  The two teams have met in three straight NBA Finals, becoming the only two teams in NBA history to do so.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content:  Of these three series, the Warriors have won two, most recently in 2017the Cavaliers have won one, in 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content:  The two teams feature 11 NBA All-Stars: LeBron James, Kyrie Irving, Kevin Love, Deron Williams and Kyle Korver (Cleveland), and Stephen Curry, Kevin Durant, Draymond Green, Klay Thompson, David West and Andre Iguodala (Golden State).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cavaliers–Warriors rivalry
Content:  Two players, Andrew Bogut and Anderson Varejão, played for both teams during this time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2016–17 Golden State Warriors season
Content:  The Warriors won their fifth NBA Championship, setting the best postseason record in NBA history by going 16–1 .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2016–17 Golden State Warriors season
Content:  They entered the season as runners-up in the 2016 NBA Finals, after a record breaking regular-season in 2015–16.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2016–17 Golden State Warriors season
Content:  With the acquisition of free agent Kevin Durant in the offseason, the Warriors were hailed as a "Superteam" by the media and fans, forming a new All-Star "Fantastic Four" of Durant, Stephen Curry, Klay Thompson and Draymond Green.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2016–17 Golden State Warriors season
Content:  The Warriors broke over 20 NBA records on their way to equaling their 2014–15 regular-season record of 67–15 , their second most wins in franchise history.

Title: Duke of Norfolk
Content: The Duke of Norfolk is the premier Duke in the peerage of England, and also, as Earl of Arundel, the premier Earl.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Duke of Norfolk
Content:  The Duke of Norfolk is, moreover, the Earl Marshal and hereditary Marshal of England.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  The seat of the Duke of Norfolk is Arundel Castle in Sussex, although the title refers to the county of Norfolk.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Viscount FitzAlan of Derwent

Title: Viscount FitzAlan of Derwent
Content:  Born Lord Edmund FitzAlan-Howard, he was the second son of Henry Fitzalan-Howard, 14th Duke of Norfolk.

session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content: Sexual racism is the "sexual rejection of the racial minority, the conscious attempt on the part of the majority to prevent interracial cohabitation."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  It is the discrimination between potential sexual or romantic partners on the basis of perceived racial identity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  However, not everyone agrees that this should be classified as racism, some argue that distinguishing among partners on the basis of perceived race is not racism at all but a justifiable personal preference.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  The origins of sexual racism can be explained by looking at its history, especially in the USA, where the abolition of slavery and the Reconstruction Era had significant impacts on interracial mixing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  Attitudes towards interracial relationships, and indeed marriage, have increased in positivity in the last 50 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  In 1968, 73% of US citizens disapproved of the right to marry inter-racially, whereas this figure dropped to 17% by 2007, this illustrating the reduction in discriminatory attitudes towards interracial dating.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  Irrespective of this, there still remains the issue of sexual racism in the online dating world, in that preferences appear to follow a racial hierarchy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  The exclusion of races dissimilar to one's own is a main feature of sexual racism, however a reluctance to date inter-racially predominantly spans from the discriminatory views often possessed by those in society, as opposed to purely a same-race individual preference.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  Moreover, this racial discrimination also deviates into the form of the sexual dehumanisation of individuals of other racial identities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  Sharing the basic premise, originating from the 'taboo' nature of interracial relations, individuals of other racial groups are classified as forbidden sexual objects; the result of a racial fetish.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sexual racism
Content:  This sexualised reductionism is, concurrently, a form of sexual racism.

Title: Sex-positive movement
Content: The sex-positive movement is a social movement which promotes and embraces sexuality and sexual expression, with an emphasis on safe sex and the importance of consent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sex-positive movement
Content:  Sex-positivity is "an attitude towards human sexuality that regards all consensual sexual activities as fundamentally healthy and pleasurable, encouraging sexual pleasure and experimentation".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sex-positive movement
Content:  The sex-positive movement is a social and philosophical movement that advocates these attitudes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sex-positive movement
Content:  The sex-positive movement also advocates sex education and safer sex as part of its campaign".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sex-positive movement
Content:  Part of its original use was in an effort to get rid of the frightening connotation that the term 'positive' had during the height of the AIDS epidemic.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sex-positive movement
Content:  The movement generally makes no moral distinctions among types of sexual activities, regarding these choices as matters of personal preference.

Title: Saint Genet
Content: Saint Genet, Actor and Martyr (French: "Saint Genet, comédien et martyr" ) is a book by the French philosopher Jean-Paul Sartre about the writer Jean Genet especially on his "The Thief's Journal".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Genet
Content:  It was first published in 1952.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Genet
Content:  Sartre described it as an attempt "to prove that genius is not a gift but the way out that one invents in desperate cases."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Genet
Content:  Sartre also based his character Goetz in his play "The Devil and the Good Lord" (1951) on his analysis of Genet's psychology and morality.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saint Genet
Content:  Sartre has been credited by David M. Halperin with providing, "a brilliant, subtle, and thoroughgoing study of the unique subjectivity and gender positioning of gay men".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Transcendence of the Ego
Content: The Transcendence of the Ego (French: "La Transcendance de l'ego: Esquisse d'une description phénomenologique" ) is a philosophical and psychological essay written by Jean-Paul Sartre in 1934 and published in 1936.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Transcendence of the Ego
Content:  The essay demonstrates Sartre’s transition from traditional phenomenological thinking and most notably his break from Edmund Husserl’s school of thought (phenomenology), and into his own.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Transcendence of the Ego
Content:  This transition is more apparent after Sartre’s military service from 1939 where we observe a rather more sympathetic view of being in the world, a topic that is dealt with in much greater detail in his 1943 work "Being and Nothingness".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Transcendence of the Ego
Content:  This essay begins Sartre's study and hybridisation of phenomenology and ontology.

Title: Bianca Lamblin
Content:  Her book, "Mémoires d'une Jeune Fille Dérangée" (published in English under the title, "A Disgraceful Affair"), is an account of her long-lasting involvement with two of the most prominent French thinkers of the twentieth century.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Lamblin later lamented of being abused by both Sartre and Beauvoir.

Title: George Bernard Shaw
Content: George Bernard Shaw ( ; 26 July 1856 – 2 November 1950), known at his insistence simply as Bernard Shaw, was an Irish playwright, critic and polemicist whose influence on Western theatre, culture and politics extended from the 1880s to his death and beyond.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  He wrote more than sixty plays, including major works such as "Man and Superman" (1902), "Pygmalion" (1912)" and Saint Joan" (1923).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
