session_0.text:
Title: Sports in the Las Vegas metropolitan area
Content:  The Las Vegas Valley has one major league professional team: the Vegas Golden Knights of the National Hockey League (NHL) who began play in 2017 as the region's first major pro team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in the Las Vegas metropolitan area
Content:  The Oakland Raiders of the National Football League (NFL) will begin play in Las Vegas as the "Las Vegas Raiders" by 2020 and become the region's second major professional team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in the Las Vegas metropolitan area
Content:  Las Vegas is also home to one minor league sports team: the Las Vegas 51s of the Triple-A Pacific Coast League (Minor League Baseball).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in the Las Vegas metropolitan area
Content:  The 51s are currently the only team to actually play in the city of Las Vegas, playing at the city owned Cashman Field.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sports in the Las Vegas metropolitan area
Content:  They will be joined in 2018 by the Las Vegas Lights FC of the United Soccer League, one of two leagues that collectively make up the second level of the U.S. men's soccer league system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SkyJump Las Vegas
Content: SkyJump Las Vegas holds the Guinness World Record for highest commercial decelerator descent with an official height of 829 ft and is located at Stratosphere Las Vegas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SkyJump Las Vegas
Content:  As part of its grand opening event, Las Vegas Mayor Oscar Goodman presented a written proclamation deeming April 20, 2010 as SkyJump Day in Las Vegas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SLS station
Content: SLS station (originally Sahara) is a station on the Las Vegas Monorail, in Las Vegas, Nevada, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SLS station
Content:  The station is a side platform located at the Sahara Hotel and Casino.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SLS station
Content:  The Sahara Station could be reached in two ways: from inside the hotel via a hallway located behind the Casbar Theatre Lounge (closed on May 16, 2011) or from street level on Paradise Road behind the Sahara.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: SLS station
Content:  The tracks just north of Sahara station were designed to provide access to a possible downtown extension of the monorail via the northern portion of the Las Vegas Strip in the area of the Circus Circus Las Vegas and the Riviera.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Las Vegas Uncork'd
Content: Las Vegas Uncork'd (also referred to as Vegas Uncork'd and Vegas Uncorked) is an annual culinary and wine event in Las Vegas, Nevada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Las Vegas Uncork'd
Content:  The concept was developed by the Las Vegas Convention and Visitors Authority, their advertising agency R&R Partners and Las Vegas resort partners who originally considered a number of magazine partners such as Bon Appetit, Food & Wine and Gourmet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Las Vegas Uncork'd
Content:  Bon Appetit was selected as the magazine partner after a review with each magazine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Las Vegas Uncork'd
Content:  The event was launched in 2007 by the Las Vegas Convention and Visitors Authority, "Bon Appétit" magazine led by Editor-in-Chief Barbara Fairchild and co-creator and Executive Director Rob O'Keefe who led the first five years of development of what Eater.com called "the world's most innovative culinary event".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Las Vegas Uncork'd
Content:  Las Vegas resort partners over the years include Bellagio, Caesars Palace and Wynn Las Vegas, MGM Grand, Mandalay Bay, The Venetian, Las Vegas and The Palazzo and each year the event features more than 80 celebrated chefs and over 25 events occurring over a spectacular four-day weekend.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trump International Hotel Las Vegas
Content: The Trump International Hotel Las Vegas is a 64-story luxury hotel, condominium, and timeshare located on Fashion Show Drive near Las Vegas Boulevard, just off the Las Vegas Strip in Paradise, Nevada, named for real estate developer and the 45th and current President of the United States Donald Trump.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trump International Hotel Las Vegas
Content:  It is located across the street from Wynn Las Vegas, behind Alon Las Vegas on 3.46 acre , near the Fashion Show Mall, and features both non-residential hotel condominiums and residential condominiums.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trump International Hotel Las Vegas
Content:  The exterior glass is infused with gold.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Trump International Hotel Las Vegas
Content:  The hotel is a member of The Leading Hotels of the World.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: El Rancho Vegas
Content: El Rancho Vegas was a hotel and casino on the Las Vegas Strip.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: El Rancho Vegas
Content:  It was located at 2500 Las Vegas Boulevard, at the southwest corner of Las Vegas Boulevard and Sahara Avenue, and opened on April 3, 1941.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: El Rancho Vegas
Content:  Until 1942, it was the largest hotel in Las Vegas with 110 rooms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: El Rancho Vegas
Content:  On June 17, 1960, the hotel was destroyed by fire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: El Rancho Vegas
Content:  In 1982, the El Rancho Hotel and Casino formerly known as the Thunderbird and later as the Silverbird opened across the street from the former site of the El Rancho Vegas, creating some confusion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content: Macau Tower Convention & Entertainment Centre (; Portuguese: "Centro de Convenções e Entretenimento da Torre de Macau" ), also known as Macau Tower, is a tower located in Sé, Macau, China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content:  The tower measures 338 m (1,109 ft) in height from ground level to the highest point.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content:  An observation deck with panoramic views, restaurants, theaters, shopping malls and the Skywalk X, a thrilling walking tour around the outer rim.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content:  It offers the best view of Macau and in recent years has been used for a variety of adventurous activities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content:  At 233 metres, the Macau Tower's tethered "skyjump" and Bungee jump by AJ Hackett from the tower's outer rim, is the highest commercial skyjump in the world (233 metres), and the second highest commercial decelerator descent facility in the world, after Vegas' Stratosphere skyjump at 252 metres.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Macau Tower
Content:  The tower was created by the architecture firm of Craig Craig Moller.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Las Vegas
Content: Downtown Las Vegas (commonly abbreviated as DTLV) is the central business district and historic center of Las Vegas, Nevada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Las Vegas
Content:  It is the original townsite and was the gambling district of Las Vegas prior to the Strip, and the area still incorporates downtown gaming.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Las Vegas
Content:  As the urban core of the Las Vegas Valley, it features a variety of hotel and business highrises, cultural centers, historical buildings and government institutions, as well as residential and retail developments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Las Vegas
Content:  Downtown is located in the center of the Las Vegas Valley and just north of the Las Vegas Strip, centered on Fremont Street, the Fremont Street Experience and Fremont East.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Las Vegas
Content:  The city defines the area as bounded by I-15 on the west, Washington Avenue on the north, Maryland Parkway on the east and Sahara Avenue on the south.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stratosphere Las Vegas
Content: The Stratosphere Las Vegas (formerly Vegas World) is a hotel, casino, and tower located on Las Vegas Boulevard just north of the Las Vegas Strip in Las Vegas, Nevada, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tropicana – Las Vegas Boulevard intersection
Content: The Tropicana – Las Vegas Boulevard intersection on the Las Vegas Strip (Tropicana Avenue and Las Vegas Boulevard), is noteworthy for several reasons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tropicana – Las Vegas Boulevard intersection
Content:  It was the first intersection in Las Vegas completely closed to street level pedestrian traffic and its four corners are home to four major resorts: Excalibur Hotel and Casino, Tropicana Las Vegas, New York-New York Hotel and Casino and MGM Grand Las Vegas—the latter has 5,044 rooms and was once the largest hotel in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tropicana – Las Vegas Boulevard intersection
Content:  The resorts at the four corners have a total of 12,536 hotel rooms as of 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geronimo (2014 film)
Content: Geronimo is a 2014 French drama film directed by Tony Gatlif.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Geronimo (2014 film)
Content:  It premiered in the Special Screenings section at the 2014 Cannes Film Festival on 20 May.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tony Gatlif
Content: Tony Gatlif (born as Michel Dahmani on 10 September 1948 in Algiers) is a French film director of Romani ethnicity who also works as a screenwriter, composer, actor, and producer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Korkoro
Content: Korkoro ("Alone" in the Romanes) is a 2009 French drama film written and directed by Tony Gatlif, starring French actors Marc Lavoine, Marie-Josée Croze and James Thiérrée.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Korkoro
Content:  The film's cast were of many nationalities such as Albanian, Kosovar, Georgian, Serbian, French, Norwegian, and nine Romani people Gatlif recruited in Transylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Latcho Drom
Content: Latcho Drom ("safe journey") is a 1993 French documentary film directed and written by Tony Gatlif.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Latcho Drom
Content:  The movie is about the Romani people's journey from north-west India to Spain, consisting primarily of music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Latcho Drom
Content:  The film was screened in the Un Certain Regard section at the 1993 Cannes Film Festival.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exils
Content: Exiles is a 2004 French film by Tony Gatlif.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exils
Content:  The film follows two young bohemians, Zano and Naima.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exils
Content:  After having sex, the two spontaneously decide that they will travel to Algeria, where Naima's parents come from, and where Zano's (Romain Duris) "pied-noir" parents were once exiled.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exils
Content:  Their adventurous journey to Algiers is full of character exploration, relationship hiccups and imagery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Exils
Content:  The film was also a homecoming for Gatlif himself, seeing him return to Algeria 43 years after he left.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Je suis né d'une cigogne
Content: Je suis né d'une cigogne (English: Children of the Stork ) is a 1999 French road movie directed by Tony Gatlif, starring Romain Duris, Rona Hartner, Ouassini Embarek, Christine Pignet and Marc Nouyrigat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Je suis né d'une cigogne
Content:  Following its French release, it received mixed reviews but was nominated for a "Golden Bayard" at the "International Festival of Francophone Film" in Namur, Belgium.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tchavolo Schmitt
Content: Tchavolo Schmitt (born 1954 in Paris) is a gypsy jazz guitarist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tchavolo Schmitt
Content:  Schmitt performed as a member of various ensembles in the 1970s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tchavolo Schmitt
Content:  Then he settled in Strasbourg and left the professional circuit for a time, releasing solo albums in 2000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tchavolo Schmitt
Content:  He played Miraldo in the Tony Gatlif film "Swing."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rona Hartner
Content: Rona Hartner (born March 9, 1973, Bucharest) is a Romanian actress, painter and singer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rona Hartner
Content:  She is best known for her role in Tony Gatlif's film Gadjo Dilo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rona Hartner
Content:  Hartner currently focuses on her music career, specializing in Gypsy music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Transylvania (film)
Content: Transylvania is a 2006 French drama film starring Asia Argento.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Transylvania (film)
Content:  In 2006, Director Tony Gatlif and composer Delphine Mantoulet won the "Georges Delerue Prize" at the Flanders International Film Festival for the score, and Gatlif was nominated for the "Grand Prix" award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Transylvania (film)
Content:  "Transylvania" premiered at the 2006 Cannes Film Festival in France on May 28, and premiered in the United States on March 16, 2007 at the Cleveland International Film Festival and in the United Kingdom at the Cambridge Film Festival on July 6, 2007 (with a later theatrical release on August 10, 2007).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David O. Russell
Content: David Owen Russell (born August 20, 1958) is an American film director, screenwriter, and producer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David O. Russell
Content:  His early directing career includes the comedy films "Spanking the Monkey" (1994), "Flirting with Disaster" (1996), "Three Kings" (1999) and "I ♥ Huckabees" (2004).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Hundred Days in Palermo
Content: One Hundred Days in Palermo (Italian: "Cento giorni a Palermo" ) is a 1984 non-fiction film directed by Giuseppe Ferrara with Giuseppe Tornatore as screenplay writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Hundred Days in Palermo
Content:  The film is a France/Italy coproduction and tells about the last hundred days in the life of the Italian "Generale dei Carabinieri" and anti-mafia highest authority Carlo Alberto Dalla Chiesa as prefect of Palermo, the capital of the Italian island of Sicily.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One Hundred Days in Palermo
Content:  Dalla Chiesa's life ended with his barbaric murder, shot by the machine guns of a mafia squad (along his wife and his bodyguard) on September 3, 1982.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnese Nano
Content: Agnese Nano (born 5 November 1965 in Rome) is an Italian film, TV and theater actress.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnese Nano
Content:  Her first appearance was in 1987 but she became famous after her role as the young "Elena" in "Cinema Paradiso" by Giuseppe Tornatore, in 1988.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Agnese Nano
Content:  Nano felt that playing Elena "was a deeply nurturing experience, crucial for the development of her future career."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cinema Paradiso
Content: Cinema Paradiso (Italian: Nuovo Cinema Paradiso , ] , "New Paradise Cinema") is a 1988 Italian drama film written and directed by Giuseppe Tornatore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cinema Paradiso
Content:  The film stars Jacques Perrin, Philippe Noiret, Leopoldo Trieste, Marco Leonardi, Agnese Nano and Salvatore Cascio, and was produced by Franco Cristaldi and Giovanna Romagnoli, while the music score was composed by Ennio Morricone along with his son, Andrea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cinema Paradiso
Content:  It won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film at the 62nd Academy Awards.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Il camorrista
Content: Il camorrista (English: "The Professor") is a 1986 Italian drama directed by Giuseppe Tornatore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Il camorrista
Content:  His film debut, it is based on the true story of the Italian crime boss Raffaele Cutolo, and adapted from the novel by Giuseppe Marrazzo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Il camorrista
Content:  The International version is shorter than the original Italian release.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Baarìa (film)
Content: Baarìa is a 2009 Italian film directed by Giuseppe Tornatore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Baarìa (film)
Content:  It was the opening film of the 66th Venice International Film Festival in September 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Best Offer
Content: The Best Offer (Italian: La migliore offerta – entitled Deception in the UK) is a 2013 Italian English-language romantic mystery film written and directed by Giuseppe Tornatore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Best Offer
Content:  The film stars Geoffrey Rush, Jim Sturgess, Sylvia Hoeks, and Donald Sutherland, and the music score is composed by Ennio Morricone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Giuseppe Tornatore
Content: Giuseppe Tornatore (born 27 May 1956) is an Italian film director and screenwriter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Giuseppe Tornatore
Content:  He is considered as one of the directors who brought critical acclaim back to Italian cinema.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Giuseppe Tornatore
Content:  In a career spanning over 30 years he is best known for directing and writing drama films such as "The Legend of 1900", "Malèna", "Baarìa" and "The Best Offer".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Giuseppe Tornatore
Content:  Probably his most noted film is "Nuovo Cinema Paradiso", for which Tornatore won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Giuseppe Tornatore
Content:  He directed also several advertising campaigns for Dolce & Gabbana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Star Maker (1995 film)
Content: The Star Maker (Italian: L'Uomo delle stelle ) is a 1995 Italian film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Star Maker (1995 film)
Content:  It was produced by Rita Cecchi Gori, Vittorio Cecchi Gori, directed by Giuseppe Tornatore, while the title role was played by Sergio Castellitto.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Star Maker (1995 film)
Content:  It was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Everybody's Fine (2009 film)
Content: Everybody's Fine is a 2009 American drama film written and directed by Kirk Jones, and starring Robert De Niro, Drew Barrymore, Sam Rockwell and Kate Beckinsale.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Everybody's Fine (2009 film)
Content:  It is a remake of the Giuseppe Tornatore's Italian film "Everybody's Fine".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Everybody's Fine (2009 film)
Content:  In Brazil, Russia and Japan, the film was released direct-to-DVD.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Legend of 1900
Content: The Legend of 1900 (Italian: La leggenda del pianista sull'oceano , The Legend of the Pianist on the Ocean) is a 1998 Italian drama film directed by Giuseppe Tornatore and starring Tim Roth, Pruitt Taylor Vince and Mélanie Thierry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Legend of 1900
Content:  It was Tornatore's first English-language film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Legend of 1900
Content:  The film is inspired by "Novecento", a monologue by Alessandro Baricco.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Legend of 1900
Content:  The film was nominated for a variety of awards worldwide, winning several for its soundtrack.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content: Southeast Kansas is a region of the U.S. state of Kansas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  It can be roughly defined by Woodson County in the northwest, Bourbon County in the northeast, Cherokee County in the southeast, and Montgomery County in the southwest.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  Geographically it is dominated by a broad rolling landscape located between the Flint Hills to the west and includes the Ozarks to the southeast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  Some notable towns there include Pittsburg, Parsons, Coffeyville, Independence, Chanute, Fort Scott, and Iola.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  The region has a land area of 13,624.13 km² (5,260.30 sq mi) and a 2000 census population of 180,815 inhabitants.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  It has 6.43% of the state's land area and 6.726% of its population.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  It is dominated by a broad rolling landscape that includes the Ozarks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  It receives more precipitation than any other part of Kansas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  Southeast Kansas is located in the tallgrass prairie ecosystem of North America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  Originally inhabited by several Native American tribes, frontier towns largely dependent on cattle ranching, and mining, were wracked by violence over the issue of slavery both before and during the American Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Southeast Kansas
Content:  Southeast Kansas offers one of the premier reconstructions of early life in the United States Army at Fort Scott National Historic Site.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octagon City
Content: Octagon City is a ghost town in Allen County, Kansas, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octagon City
Content:  It was a failed intentional community that was founded in 1856 about six miles (10 km) south of Humboldt, Kansas near the Neosho River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octagon City
Content:  It was created by the Vegetarian Kansas Emigration Company, headed by prominent vegetarian Henry Clubb and entrepreneurs Charles DeWolfe and John McLaurin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octagon City
Content:  The original intent was to build a vegetarian commune on the south side of the Neosho River for vegetarians only, but investor interest in a non-vegetarian moral community was much higher and so the decision was made to build Octagon City on the north side of the Neosho River to make the entire project sustainable.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Octagon City
Content:  Members of Octagon City were under oath to educate their children and uphold a moral lifestyle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iola, Kansas
Content: Iola (pronounced ) is a city situated along the Neosho River in the northwestern part of Allen County, located in Southeast Kansas, in the Central United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iola, Kansas
Content:  As of the 2010 census, the city population was 5,704.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iola, Kansas
Content:  Iola is the county seat of Allen County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iola, Kansas
Content:  It is named in honor of Iola Colborn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cofachique, Kansas
Content: Cofachique (pronounced "ko-fa-chee") was an unincorporated community situated along the Neosho River near the present-day city of Iola in the western part of Allen County, located in southeast Kansas, in the central United States of America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cofachique, Kansas
Content:  Being the first town established in Allen County in 1855, it was the original county seat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cofachique, Kansas
Content:  However, within five years the greater part of the town was moved to the new town of Iola, while the old site of Cofachique became farm land.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cofachique, Kansas
Content:  The town was named in honor of an Osage chief known as Cofachique, who is said to have been particularly helpful to early settlers, bringing aid to the distressed and homeless.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cofachique, Kansas
Content:  The name "Cofachique" appears to have origins with the Cofachiqui (or Cofitachiqui) tribe in South Carolina, who were Siouan speakers, and the Osage who settled this area were closely affiliated with the Siouan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Humboldt, Kansas
Content: Humboldt is a city in Allen County, Kansas, United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Humboldt, Kansas
Content:  It is situated along the Neosho River.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Humboldt, Kansas
Content:  As of the 2010 census, the city population was 1,953.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Grand River (Oklahoma)
Content: The Grand River is an alternate name for the lower section of the Neosho River, a tributary of the Arkansas River in Oklahoma.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Grand River (Oklahoma)
Content:  "Grand River" refers to the section of river below the confluence of the Neosho and Spring rivers in Ottawa County near Miami.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Grand River (Oklahoma)
Content:  It empties into the Arkansas northeast of Muskogee, just downstream from the confluence of the Verdigris River with the Arkansas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Grand River (Oklahoma)
Content:  The area of convergence of the three rivers Arkansas, Verdigris and Neosho is called "Three Forks".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: USS Neosho (AO-23)
Content: USS "Neosho" (AO-23) was a "Cimarron"-class fleet oiler serving with the United States Navy, the second ship to be named for the Neosho River in Kansas and Oklahoma.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Labette County, Kansas
Content: Labette County (county code LB) is a county located in the U.S. state of Kansas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Labette County, Kansas
Content:  As of the 2010 census, the county population was 21,607.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Labette County, Kansas
Content:  Its county seat is Oswego, and its most populous city is Parsons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Labette County, Kansas
Content:  The county was named after LaBette creek, the second-largest creek in the county, which runs roughly NNW-SSE from near Parsons to Chetopa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Labette County, Kansas
Content:  The creek in turn was named after French-Canadian fur trapper Pierre LaBette who had moved to the area, living along the Neosho River, and marrying into the Osage tribe in the 1830s and 1840s.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content: TV's 50 Greatest Stars was a one-off British television awards show which invited the viewing public to vote for their favourite on-screen stars from a list compiled by the broadcaster ITV.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  Fifty actors, actresses, presenters and comedians, both alive and dead, were featured on the list, the number 50 being chosen to coincide with ITV's fiftieth birthday celebrations in September 2005 (though technically there were 55 individuals on the list due to partnerships like The Two Ronnies and Richard and Judy being counted as one entry).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  The two-hour show, directed by Mark Robinson, was hosted by Coronation Street actor Bradley Walsh, who ran through the stars in reverse order – the order was determined by the public, who could vote, either online or in the "TV Times", for five (no more and no fewer) of their favourite celebrities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  Members of the public could also nominate other television celebrities who had not been included on the list.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  Actor David Jason won with Morecambe and Wise and John Thaw coming in second and third place respectively; the majority of nominees were comedians, a fifth were deceased and men outnumbered women by almost five to one.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  Walsh was quoted as saying "I'm delighted to be involved in this show.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  It will be a great celebration of some of the most fantastic stars from the last 50 years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  It will be interesting to see who the viewers vote to come out on top."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: TV's 50 Greatest Stars
Content:  The show was narrated by Mel Giedroyc.

Title: The David Lee Roth Show
Content: The David Lee Roth Show is a former short-lived American radio show that aired from January 3 to April 21, 2006, hosted by rock musician David Lee Roth.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The David Lee Roth Show
Content:  It was the replacement of "The Howard Stern Show" following its departure from terrestrial to Sirius Satellite Radio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The David Lee Roth Show
Content:  It was simulcast on seven stations across the United States owned by CBS Radio, who launched the show as part of its Free FM radio format.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The David Lee Roth Show
Content:  Notably different from Howard Stern's program, or corporate "morning zoo" and "shock jock" formats which dominated morning radio at the time, Roth's show sounded similar to pirate radio, featuring ethnic and non-commercial rock music integrated with personal stories, occasionally uncomfortable debates on intellectual matters, and interviews with people Roth admired, such as guitarist Brian May, baseball player Johnny Damon, and Roth's uncle, Manny Roth.

Title: Ken Hakuta

Title: Foreign relations of Italy
Content: Foreign relations of the Italian Republic are the Italian government's external relations with the outside world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Foreign relations of Italy
Content:  Located in Europe, Italy has been considered a major Western power since its unification in 1861.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Foreign relations of Italy
Content:  Its main allies are the NATO countries, the EU states and the G7 nations, three entities of which Italy is a founding member.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NATO Quint
Content: The Quint is an informal decision-making group consisting of the United States and the Big Four of Western Europe (France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: NATO Quint
Content:  It operates as a "directoire" of various entities such as NATO and the G7/ G20.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Group of Seven
Content: The Group of 7 (G7) is a group consisting of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom and the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Group of Seven
Content:  These countries represent more than 64% of the net global wealth ($263 trillion) and all have a very high Human Development Index.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Group of Seven
Content:  The G7 countries also represent 46% of the global GDP evaluated at market exchange rates and 32% of the global purchasing power parity GDP.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Group of Seven
Content:  The European Union is also represented within the G7.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Least of the Great Powers
Content: The least of the Great Powers is a label used to conceptualize Italy's international status.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Least of the Great Powers
Content:  Italy is part of great power concerts such as the EU trio, the NATO Quint, the G7, the G20 and various International Contact Groups. Italy, one of the UN's major funders, is the leading nation of the Uniting for Consensus and serves as one of the states of "chief" importance in providing shipping services, air transport and Industrial development.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Least of the Great Powers
Content:  Alternative terms used by academics and observers to describe this concept include "intermittent Major power" or "small Great power", asserting that Italy's position in the international arena can be described in this way.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Power (international relations)
Content: Power in international relations is defined in several different ways.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Power (international relations)
Content:  Modern discourse generally speaks in terms of state power, indicating both economic and military power.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Power (international relations)
Content:  Those states that have significant amounts of power within the international system are referred to as small powers, middle powers, regional powers, great powers, superpowers, or hegemons, although there is no commonly accepted standard for what defines a powerful state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Power (international relations)
Content:  NATO Quint, The G7, the BRICS nations and the G20 are seen by academics as forms of governments that exercise varying degrees of influence within the international system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: G20
Content: The G20 (or G-20 or Group of Twenty) is an international forum for the governments and central bank governors from 20 major economies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: G20
Content:  Currently, these are Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Canada, China, France, Germany, India, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, South Korea, Turkey, United Kingdom, United States, and the European Union.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: G20
Content:  Founded in 1999, the G20 aims to discuss policy issues pertaining to the promotion of international financial stability.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: G20
Content:  It seeks to address issues that go beyond the responsibilities of any one organization.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: G20
Content:  The G20 heads of government or heads of state have periodically conferred at summits since their initial meeting in 2008, and the group also hosts separate meetings of finance ministers and foreign ministers due to the expansion of its agenda in recent years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canada–Italy relations
Content: Canada–Italy refers to the current and historical relations between Canada and Italy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canada–Italy relations
Content:  Both nations enjoy friendly relations, the importance of which centres on the history of Italian migration to Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canada–Italy relations
Content:  Approximately 1.5 million Canadians claim to have Italian ancestry (approximately 4.6% of the population).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Canada–Italy relations
Content:  Both nations are members of the G7, G20, NATO and the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: EU three
Content: The EU three, also known as EU big three or EU trio, refers to Germany, France and Italy, a group that consists of the three large founding members of the European Union; or France, Germany, and the United Kingdom, a group of countries of the European Union, especially during the negotiations with Iran.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content: The Financial Stability Forum (FSF) was a group consisting of major national financial authorities such as finance ministries, central bankers, and international financial bodies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  The Forum was founded in 1999 to promote international financial stability.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  Its founding resulted from discussions among Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors of the G7 countries, and a study which they commissioned.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  The Forum facilitated discussion and co-operation on supervision and surveillance of financial institutions, transactions and events.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  FSF was managed by a small secretariat housed at the Bank for International Settlements in Basel, Switzerland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  The FSF membership included about a dozen nations who participate through their central banks, financial ministries and departments, and securities regulators, including: the United States, Japan, Germany, the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands and several other industrialized economies as well as several international economic organizations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  At the G20 summit on November 15, 2008 it was agreed that the membership of the FSF will be expanded to include emerging economies, such as China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  The 2009 G-20 London summit decided to establish a successor to the FSF, the Financial Stability Board.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Financial Stability Forum
Content:  The FSB includes members of the G20 who were not members of FSF.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: France–Italy relations
Content: France–Italy relations refer to the interstate relations as well as the historical links between the French Republic and the Italian Republic (since 1946) and its predecessor the Kingdom of Italy (1861—1946).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: France–Italy relations
Content:  Both countries were among the Inner six that founded the European Community, the predecessor of the EU.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: France–Italy relations
Content:  They are also founding members of the G7/G8 and NATO.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: France–Italy relations
Content:  Since April 9, 1956 Rome and Paris are exclusively and reciprocally twinned with each other:

Title: Russians in Kyrgyzstan
Content: Russians in Kyrgyzstan are a minority ethnic group numbering 419,600 individuals according to 2009 Census, representing 9.1% of the total population.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russians in Kyrgyzstan
Content:  Most ethnic Russians migrated to the country during the 20th Century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russians in Kyrgyzstan
Content:  The Russian population has been declining since the breakup of the Soviet Union due to low fertility rates and emigration.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1951 Polish–Soviet territorial exchange
Content: The 1951 Polish–Soviet territorial exchange or Polish-Soviet border adjustment treaty of 1951 was a border adjustment signed in Moscow between the People's Republic of Poland and the Soviet Union regarding roughly 480 km2 of land, along their mutual border.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1951 Polish–Soviet territorial exchange
Content:  The exchange was made to the decisive economic benefit of the Soviet Union due to rich deposits of coal given up by Poland; these deposits were discovered well before World War II.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1951 Polish–Soviet territorial exchange
Content:  Within eight years following the agreement, the Soviets built four large coal mines there with the total mining capacity of 15 million tons annually.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rollins Air
Content: Rollins Air was an airline charter company based in Honduras.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rollins Air
Content:  It had Lockheed Tristars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rollins Air
Content:  In 2011 it was banned from operating in the European Union due to safety concerns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rollins Air
Content:  On 24 September 2012, its AOC (Air Operator's Certificate) was revoked and it subsequently expired.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rollins Air
Content:  On 4 December 2012 (after the AOC expired) the airline was removed from EU list of banned air carriers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian avant-garde
Content: The Russian avant-garde was a large, influential wave of avant-garde modern art that flourished in the Russian Empire and Soviet Union, approximately from 1890 to 1930—although some have placed its beginning as early as 1850 and its end as late as 1960.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian avant-garde
Content:  The term covers many separate, but inextricably related, art movements that flourished at the time; namely Suprematism, Constructivism, Russian Futurism, Cubo-Futurism, Zaum and Neo-primitivism.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Russian avant-garde
Content:  Given that many avant-garde artists involved were born or grew up in what is present day Belarus and Ukraine (including Kazimir Malevich, Aleksandra Ekster, Vladimir Tatlin, Wassily Kandinsky, David Burliuk, Alexander Archipenko), some sources also talk about Ukrainian avant-garde.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Compromise of 1850
Content: The Compromise of 1850 was a package of five separate bills passed by the United States Congress in September 1850, which defused a four-year political confrontation between slave and free states on the status of territories acquired during the Mexican–American War (1846–1848).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Compromise of 1850
Content:  The compromise, drafted by Whig Senator Henry Clay of Kentucky and brokered by Clay and Democratic Senator Stephen Douglas of Illinois, reduced sectional conflict.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Compromise of 1850
Content:  Controversy arose over the Fugitive Slave provision.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Compromise of 1850
Content:  The Compromise was greeted with relief, but each side disliked some of its specific provisions:

Title: Āne
Content: Āne is a large village in Ozolnieki Municipality, Latvia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Āne
Content:  The village is located on the Lielupe River approximately 38 km from the capital city of Riga and 7 km from city of Jelgava.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Āne
Content:  It is known for the amount of clay that is dug up in local lakes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Āne
Content:  There is a brick factory, which was very prominent during the Soviet era; since the factory was privatised it still produces bricks albeit on a reduced scale.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Āne
Content:  The town was very well known in the Soviet Union due to this.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Āne
Content:  It is sometimes referred to by locals as "Sarkanais māls" literally meaning "Red clay".

session_0.text:
Title: Joseph Sokolsky
Content: Joseph Sokolsky (Bulgarian: Йосиф Соколски , Gabrovo, Ottoman Empire 1786 – died in Kiev, Russian Empire September 30, 1879) was the first senior Eastern Orthodox Bulgarian clergyman who convert to Catholicism, thus becoming a pioneer of the Bulgarian Byzantine Catholic Church.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joseph Sokolsky
Content:  Sokolsky negotiated with Vatican a formal union due to Phanariotes domination over Bulgarian Orthodoxy and gained Catholic recognition 1861 when Pope Pius IX named him Archbishop for the Bulgarians of the Byzantine Rite.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Joseph Sokolsky
Content:  He was also accepted in that capacity by the Ottoman Empire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content: Dr. Leonard Jan Le Vann (1 August 1915 – 29 September 1987) was the medical superintendent at the Alberta Provincial Training School for Mental Defectives (also known as the Michener Center) from the years 1949–1974.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  Although he was born and raised in the United States, Le Vann trained as a physician in Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  Throughout his career Le Vann wrote many articles, the majority of which were published during his 25-year career at the Provincial Training School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  These articles covered a broad range of topics that include alcoholism, schizophrenia and experimental treatments of antipsychotic drugs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  In 1974 Le Vann resigned from the training center, which was due to the Conservative Party of Alberta’s repeal against the Sexual Sterilization Act of Alberta.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  Furthermore, there has been plenty of controversy about how he ran the school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  This controversy has been brought to attention mainly because of the Leilani Muir trial that took place in 1995.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Leonard Jan Le Vann
Content:  Although Le Vann was already deceased at the time of the trial, his name was brought to the court’s attention on many separate occasions.

Title: History of California before 1900
Content: Human history in California begins with indigenous Americans first arriving in California some 13,000–15,000 years ago.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of California before 1900
Content:  Exploration and settlement by Europeans along the coasts and in the inland valleys began in the 16th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of California before 1900
Content:  California was acquired by the United States under the terms of the 1848 Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo following the defeat of Mexico in the Mexican–American War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of California before 1900
Content:  American westward expansion intensified with the California Gold Rush, beginning in 1849.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of California before 1900
Content:  California joined the Union as a free state in 1850, due to the Compromise of 1850.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: History of California before 1900
Content:  By the end of the 19th century, California was still largely rural and agricultural, but had a population of about 1.4 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Breckland Forest
Content: Breckland Forest is an 18,126 hectare biological and geological Site of Special Scientific Interest in many separate areas between Swaffham in Norfolk and Bury St Edmunds in Suffolk.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Breckland Forest
Content:  It is part of the Breckland Special Protection Area under the European Union Directive on the Conservation of Wild Birds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Breckland Forest
Content:  It contains two Geological Conservation Review sites, Beeches Pit, Icklingham and High Lodge.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Breckland Forest
Content:  Barton Mills Valley is a Local Nature Reserve in the south-west corner of the site.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rob Waiz
Content: Robert L. Waiz, Jr (born January 30, 1963) is a politician of Jeffersonville, Indiana.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rob Waiz
Content:  He works in real estate and has been on the city council and served as mayor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rob Waiz
Content:  Waiz, a Democrat, was first elected mayor in 2003, defeating two-term incumbent Tom Galligan in the May Democratic primary and then defeating Republican Monty Snelling in the November general election.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rob Waiz
Content:  On May 8, 2007, he lost the Democrat primary against Galligan; who went on to win the general election in November 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rob Waiz
Content:  Rob Waiz was the youngest mayor elected in the city's modern history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. H. Crump
Content: Edward Hull "Boss" Crump (October 2, 1874 – October 16, 1954) was an American politician from Memphis, Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. H. Crump
Content:  Representing the Democratic Party, he was the dominant force in the city's politics for most of the first half of the 20th century, during which the city had a commission form of government.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. H. Crump
Content:  He also dominated Tennessee state politics for most of the time from the 1920s to the 1940s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. H. Crump
Content:  He was elected and served as mayor of Memphis from 1910 through 1915, and again briefly in 1940.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: E. H. Crump
Content:  However, he effectively appointed every mayor elected from 1915 to 1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Brooks (mayor)
Content: John Brooks (June 13, 1785 – February 19, 1869) was the ninth mayor of Columbus, Ohio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Brooks (mayor)
Content:  He was the first mayor elected by popular vote in the City of Columbus on April 14, 1834.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Brooks (mayor)
Content:  He was elected to a two-year term, but only served for one year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Brooks (mayor)
Content:  He resigned from office on April 21, 1835.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Brooks (mayor)
Content:  John Bailhache was appointed by city council to complete the unexpired term of Brooks's tenure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Box
Content: Charles Box was elected mayor of Rockford, Illinois, United States in 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Box
Content:  A Democrat, Box was the first African-American Mayor elected in Rockford.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Box
Content:  Box was elected to three terms as mayor before choosing not to run in 2001.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Box
Content:  In 2006, Illinois Governor Rod Blagojevich appointed Box as head of the Illinois Commerce Commission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson mayoral election, 2017
Content: The 2017 mayoral election in Jackson, Mississippi took place on June 6, 2017, alongside other Jackson municipal races.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson mayoral election, 2017
Content:  Chokwe Antar Lumumba, son of late former mayor Chokwe Lumumba was elected mayor in a landslide in the general election after defeating eight other candidates, including incumbent mayor Tony Yarber in the primary.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chokwe Lumumba
Content: Chokwe Lumumba ( ; August 2, 1947 – February 25, 2014) was an American attorney and politician, affiliated with the Republic of New Afrika and serving as its second vice president.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chokwe Lumumba
Content:  He served as a human rights lawyer in Michigan and Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chokwe Lumumba
Content:  In 2013, after serving on the City Council, he was elected as Mayor of Jackson, Mississippi.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chokwe Lumumba
Content:  He was born in Detroit, Michigan, as Edwin Finley Taliaferro, and was raised there, attending local schools.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriette Reker
Content: Henriette Reker (born 9 December 1956) is a German lawyer and independent politician.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriette Reker
Content:  She is known for her pro-immigration stance and for being the victim of an assassination attempt in 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriette Reker
Content:  A day after the attack, Reker was elected mayor of Cologne after gaining 52.66% of the votes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriette Reker
Content:  She is the first female mayor elected in Cologne's history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josh Edward Cobangbang
Content: Josh Edward S. Cobangbang (born Josh Edward Seguban Cobangbang on (1994--) 1, 1994 ) is a Filipino politician who is presently the youngest mayor elected in the history of Philippines at the age of 21 and 7 months of the Municipality of Cabugao, Province of Ilocos Sur.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Josh Edward Cobangbang
Content:  The previous youngest mayor title was held by Jono Jumamoy who was elected as mayor of Inabanga, Bohol on none }} at the age of 21 and 8 months.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James W. Rutherford
Content: James W. Rutherford (April 23, 1925 – January 14, 2010) was a mayor of the City of Flint, Michigan serving as the first "strong" mayor elected under Flint's 1974 charter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James W. Rutherford
Content:  Rutherford served for two terms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: James W. Rutherford
Content:  Rutherford was elected as a caretaker mayor after the recall of Mayor Stanley was recalled and an Emergency Financial Manager, Ed Kurtz, was appointed by the state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mayor of Watford
Content: The Mayor of Watford is the head of the borough council of Watford, Hertfordshire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mayor of Watford
Content:  The holder of the position is a directly elected mayor elected using the supplementary vote, every four years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mayor of Watford
Content:  The current Mayor of Watford is Dorothy Thornhill.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mayor of Watford
Content:  Thornhill is Watford's first directly elected mayor, and was also the first female directly elected mayor in the United Kingdom and the Liberal Democrats' first directly elected mayor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mayor of Watford
Content:  She has been Mayor of Watford since May 2002, being re-elected in May 2006, and in May 2010 she became the second mayor to be re-elected for a third term in the United Kingdom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MediaCityUK tram stop
Content: MediaCityUK tram stop is a stop on Greater Manchester's Metrolink light rail system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MediaCityUK tram stop
Content:  It is located in MediaCityUK, in Salford, North West England, and serves MediaCityUK, The Lowry, the Imperial War Museum North and other parts of Salford Quays.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MediaCityUK tram stop
Content:  It opened on 20 September 2010, as the terminus of a specially-constructed 360 m from the Eccles Line.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Colombo Monorail
Content: The Colombo Monorail is a proposed monorail system to be built in Colombo, Sri Lanka.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Colombo Monorail
Content:  The project is estimated to cost US$1.3 billion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Colombo Monorail
Content:  Construction was proposed to begin in 2Q 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Colombo Monorail
Content:  However a Japanese JICA transportation master plan for Colombo did not recommend a Monorail as a priority and recommended a Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) , Railway electrification an overhead light rail system instead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Colombo Monorail
Content:  A separate study by the Ministry of Megapolis and Western development (see below for more information) also revealed that a Light Rail system is much more feasible than Monorail and decided to go with a Light Rail system, electrified railway system and an Inland water transport ( a brand new boat/ferry service that utilises Colombo’s historic canal network) under the Western Region Megapolis project.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Imperial War Museum North
Content: Imperial War Museum North (sometimes referred to as IWM North) is a museum in the Metropolitan Borough of Trafford in Greater Manchester, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Imperial War Museum North
Content:  One of five branches of the Imperial War Museum, it explores the impact of modern conflicts on people and society.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Imperial War Museum North
Content:  It is the first branch of the Imperial War Museum to be located in the north of England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Imperial War Museum North
Content:  The museum occupies a site overlooking the Manchester Ship Canal in Trafford Park, an area which during the Second World War was a key industrial centre and consequently heavily bombed during the Manchester Blitz in 1940.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Imperial War Museum North
Content:  The area is now home to the Lowry cultural centre and the MediaCityUK development, which stand opposite the museum at Salford Quays.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content: DART Light Rail is a light rail system in Dallas, Texas and its suburbs owned and operated by Dallas Area Rapid Transit.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content:  The system comprises 93 mi among its four lines — the Red Line , the Blue Line , the Green Line , and the Orange Line .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content:  It is the longest light rail system in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content:  As of the fourth quarter of 2014, the system has an average weekday ridership of 101,800, making it the largest light rail system in the Southern United States by ridership, and the seventh largest in the United States by ridership.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content:  As of Fiscal Year 2015, the system average weekday riderships had fallen to 97,800.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DART Light Rail
Content:  (The system utilizes 163 light rail trains manufactured by Kinki Sharyo, with all trains being converted to "Super" LRVs which feature level boarding and higher passenger capacity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: GO-ALRT
Content: GO ALRT (Government of Ontario Advanced Light Rail Transit) was a light rail system proposed by GO Transit in 1982.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: GO-ALRT
Content:  The ARLT system would have been implemented along two new lines in the Greater Toronto Area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: GO-ALRT
Content:  It would have used a new electric train to provide interurban service along the existing and new GO corridors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: GO-ALRT
Content:  The system was based on an enlarged UTDC ICTS vehicle that was designed to offer a good compromise between passenger capacity and the level of infrastructure needed to support it.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: GO-ALRT
Content:  The project was cancelled due to budget cuts of the Tory government in 1985, although a number of the proposed lines were later implemented using conventional heavy rail systems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Los Angeles Metro Rail stations
Content: In Los Angeles County, California, the Metro Rail system, is a combined rapid transit and light rail system, operated by the Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (LACMTA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Los Angeles Metro Rail stations
Content:  The system is composed of two rapid transit lines and four light rail lines that run on 105.8 mi of track.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Los Angeles Metro Rail stations
Content:  As of March 2012, Los Angeles County Metro Rail serves an average of 323,870 passengers each weekday, 192,943 each Saturday, and 146,646 each Sunday.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Los Angeles Metro Rail stations
Content:  Metro Rail is one of the largest rapid transit and light rail systems in the United States by ridership.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Los Angeles Metro Rail stations
Content:  This is complemented by Metro's two Metro Liner bus rapid transit lines.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content: The Pittsburgh Light Rail (commonly known as The T) is a 26.2 mi light rail system in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania; it functions as a subway in Downtown Pittsburgh and largely as an at-grade light rail service in the suburbs south of the city.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content:  The system is largely linear in a north-south direction, with one terminus just north of Pittsburgh's central business district and two termini in the South Hills.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content:  The system is owned and operated by the Port Authority of Allegheny County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content:  It is the successor system to the streetcar network formerly operated by Pittsburgh Railways, the oldest portions of which date to 1903.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content:  The Pittsburgh light rail lines are vestigial from the city's streetcar days, and is one of only three light rail systems in the United States that continues to use the Pennsylvania Trolley (broad) gauge rail on its lines instead of .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pittsburgh Light Rail
Content:  Pittsburgh is one of the few North American cities that have continued to operate light rail systems in an uninterrupted evolution from the first-generation streetcar era, along with Boston, Cleveland, New Orleans, Newark, Philadelphia, San Francisco and Toronto.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content: There are three heavy rail and three light rail rapid transit systems operating in Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  The Toronto subway was the first rapid transit system in Canada when it opened a 12-station line in 1954.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  It has since grown to encompass three heavy rail lines and one intermediate rail line and has the most number of stations of any system in Canada with 69.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  Construction has begun on the Eglinton Crosstown Line and the Toronto-York Spadina Subway Extension, which will add 28 new stations and a total of 27.6 km of new track.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  Montreal introduced the Montreal Metro in 1966 and has now become the most popular rapid transit system in the country with 1,263,800 daily riders.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  The Vancouver SkyTrain, an automated guided line, was opened in January 1986 for the Expo 86 world fair and is the longest rapid transit system in Canada with a system length of 79.6 km .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  There are three light rail systems operating in Canada including systems in Calgary (the CTrain), Edmonton (the Edmonton LRT), and Ottawa (the O-train).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rapid transit in Canada
Content:  There is one light rail system under construction in Kitchener-Waterloo named Ion rapid transit.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manila Light Rail Transit System
Content: The Manila Light Rail Transit System (Filipino: "Sistema ng Magaang Riles Panlulan ng Maynila" ) popularly and informally known as the LRT is a metropolitan rail system serving the Metro Manila area in the Philippines.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manila Light Rail Transit System
Content:  Although referred to as a light rail system because it originally used light rail vehicles, it has characteristics that make it more akin to a rapid transit (metro) system, such as high passenger throughput, exclusive right-of-way and later use of full metro rolling stock.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manila Light Rail Transit System
Content:  The system is operated by the Light Rail Transit Authority (LRTA), a government-owned and controlled corporation under the authority of the Department of Transportation and Communications (DOTC).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manila Light Rail Transit System
Content:  Along with the Manila Metro Rail Transit System (MRT-3, also called the new Yellow Line), and Philippine National Railways's commuter line, the system makes up Metro Manila's rail infrastructure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: METRORail
Content: METRORail is the 23.8 mi light rail system in Houston, Texas (USA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: METRORail
Content:  As of 2015, the METRORail has an average weekday ridership of 60,600 and total annual ridership of 16,500,400.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: METRORail
Content:  After Dallas' DART Light Rail, METRORail ranks as the second most-traveled light rail system in the Southern United States and the 12th most-traveled light rail system in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: METRORail
Content:  METRORail is operated by the Metropolitan Transit Authority of Harris County (METRO).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content: The princess cut is the second most popular cut shape for diamonds, next to a round brilliant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  The face-up shape of the princess cut is square or rectangular and the profile or side-on shape is similar to that of an inverted pyramid with four beveled sides.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  The princess cut is a relatively new diamond cut, having been created in the 1960s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  It has gained in popularity in recent years as a more distinctive alternative to the more popular round brilliant cut, in which the top of diamond, called the crown, is cut with a round face-up shape and the bottom, called the pavilion, is shaped similar to a cone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  A princess cut with the same width as the diameter of a round brilliant will weigh more as it has four corners which would otherwise have been cut off and rounded to form a round brilliant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  The princess cut is sometimes referred to as a square modified brilliant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  However, while displaying a good degree of brilliance, its faceting style is unique and completely different from that of a round brilliant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  The Princess cut had its origins in the early "French" cut, having a step-modified "Double-French" or "Cross" cut crown and a series of unique, chevron-shaped facets in the pavilion which combine to give a distinct cross-shaped reflection when the stone is viewed directly through the table.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Princess cut
Content:  The Barion shaped cut has now been renamed the "Princess cut".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: International Diamond Cut Conference
Content: The 1st International Diamond Cut Conference, held in Moscow Russia, April 23–26, 2004, brought industry leaders together to discuss diamond cut, the factor where human intervention has the most influence, yet the least understood and hardest to evaluate.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Grossbard
Content: Henry Grossbard was the inventor of the Radiant Cut, a diamond cut similar to the Barrion but with different faceting, and founder of RCDC corp. marketing the original radiant cut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Grossbard
Content:  He came to the U.S. during World War II from Austria, and apprenticed with a diamantaire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Grossbard
Content:  He later became a master diamond cutter before inventing the Radiant Cut in 1976.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Grossbard
Content:  Henry Grossbard died in 2005 in a hit and run accident, while walking his dog on Passover.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Grossbard
Content:  The killer was never found.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Garry Holloway
Content: Garry Holloway FGAA, DipDT, JAA Appraiser, left a career in geology and in 1976 established Melbourne diamond design award winning jeweller, Precious Metals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Garry Holloway
Content:  In 1984 he studied the Fire Scope while undertaking the Gemological Association of Australia’s Diamond Diploma.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Garry Holloway
Content:  This led to a long and passionate interest in diamond cut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond
Content: The Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond is a 31.06 carat deep-blue diamond with internally flawless clarity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond
Content:  Laurence Graff purchased the Wittelsbach Diamond in 2008 for £16.4 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond
Content:  In 2010, Graff revealed he had had the diamond cut by three diamond cutters to remove flaws.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond
Content:  The diamond was now more than 4 carat lighter and was renamed the Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wittelsbach-Graff Diamond
Content:  There is controversy, as critics claim the recutting has so altered the diamond as to make it unrecognisable, compromising its historical integrity.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond cut
Content: In order to best use a diamond gemstone's material properties, a number of different diamond cuts have been developed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond cut
Content:  A diamond cut constitutes a more or less symmetrical arrangement of facets, which together modify the shape and appearance of a diamond crystal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond cut
Content:  Diamond cutters must consider several factors, such as the shape and size of the crystal, when choosing a cut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond cut
Content:  The practical history of diamond cuts can be traced back to the Middle Ages, while their theoretical basis was not developed until the turn of the 20th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond cut
Content:  Design creation and innovation continue to the present day: new technology—notably laser cutting and computer-aided design—has enabled the development of cuts whose complexity, optical performance, and waste reduction were hitherto unthinkable.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster
Content: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster, {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} (22 March 1767 – 17 February 1845) was the son of the 1st Earl Grosvenor, whom he succeeded in 1802 as 2nd Earl Grosvenor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster
Content:  He was created Marquess of Westminster in 1831.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster
Content:  He was an English Member of Parliament (MP) and an ancestor of the modern-day Dukes of Westminster.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster
Content:  Grosvenor continued to develop the family's London estates, he rebuilt their country house, Eaton Hall in Cheshire where he also restored the gardens, and built a new London home, Grosvenor House.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Grosvenor, 1st Marquess of Westminster
Content:  He maintained and extended the family interests in the acquisition of works of art, and in horse racing and breeding racehorses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golkonda
Content: Golkonda, also known as Golconda, Gol konda ("Round shaped hill"), or Golla konda, (Shepherds Hill) is a citadel and fort in Southern India and was the capital of the medieval sultanate of the Qutb Shahi dynasty (c.1518–1687), is situated 11 km west of Hyderabad.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golkonda
Content:  It is also a tehsil of Hyderabad district, Telangana, India.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Golkonda
Content:  The region is known for the mines that have produced some of the world's most famous gems, including the Koh-i-Noor, the Hope Diamond, Nassak Diamond and the Noor-ul-Ain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nassak Diamond
Content: The Nassak Diamond (also known as the Nassac Diamond and the Eye of the Idol) is a large, 43.38 carat diamond that originated as a larger 89 carat diamond in the 15th century in India.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nassak Diamond
Content:  Found in the Amaragiri mine located in Mahbubnagar, Telangana, India, and originally cut in India, the diamond was the adornment in the Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple, near Nashik, in the state of Maharashtra, India from at least 1500 to 1817.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nassak Diamond
Content:  The British East India Company captured the diamond through the Third Anglo-Maratha War and sold it to British jewellers Rundell and Bridge in 1818.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nassak Diamond
Content:  Rundell and Bridge recut the diamond in 1818, after which it made its way into the handle of the 1st Marquess of Westminster's dress sword.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diamond Cut Audio Restoration Tools
Content: Diamond Cut Audio Restoration Tools (also known as DC-Art) is a set of digital audio editor tools from Diamond Cut Productions used for audio restoration, record restoration, sound restoration of gramophone records and other audio containing media.

session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content: The (International) Noise Conspiracy (abbreviated T(I)NC) were a Swedish rock band formed in Sweden in the late months of 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  The line-up consists of Dennis Lyxzén (vocals), Inge Johansson (bass), Lars Strömberg (guitar), and Ludwig Dahlberg (drums).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  The band is known for its punk and garage rock musical influences, and its impassioned left-wing political stance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  Up until 2004, guitarist/organist/keyboardist Sara Almgren was also a member of the band.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  Dennis formed The (I)NC almost immediately after the breakup of his former band, Refused.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  The (I)nc takes pride in blending the roots of at least four other bands, including Totalt Jävla Mörker (Johansson), Separation (Strömberg), Saidiwas (Dahlberg and Almgren), and Doughnuts (Almgren).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The (International) Noise Conspiracy
Content:  In 2007, Inge Johansson also played in the band The Most.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Steve Perry

Content:  The members have all played in influential punk and rock bands from Sweden.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
