session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David Sandström
Content:  After Refused broke up David and the other members of Refused worked on a project entitled TEXT and released one album.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Dennis Lyxzén
Content: Dennis Lyxzén (born June 19, 1972 in Umeå, Sweden) is a musician best known as the lead vocalist for Swedish hardcore punk band Refused.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dennis Lyxzén
Content:  He is currently the vocalist in INVSN.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AC4
Content: AC4 is a hardcore band from Umeå, Sweden.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AC4
Content:  Refused members Dennis Lyxzén and David Sandström had been talking about starting a new band for a long time.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AC4
Content:  In the spring of 2008 Karl Backman had written songs for the new band and they started to rehearse.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AC4
Content:  Jens Nordén had played with Lyxzén in pre-Refused straight edge hardcore band Step Forward (1987-1991) and since 1990 with Backman in punk band The Vectors.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: AC4
Content:  The "AC" in the name is the regional code for the Västerbotten province where Umeå is located.

Title: Visa policy of Hong Kong
Content: The visa policy of Hong Kong deals with the requirements in which a foreign national wishing to enter the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region must meet to obtain an entry permit or visa, which depending on the traveller's nationality, may be required to travel to, enter, and remain in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Visa policy of Hong Kong
Content:  Visitors from over 145 countries are permitted visa-free entry for periods ranging from 7 to 180 days, to the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region for tourism or certain business-related activities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Visa policy of Hong Kong
Content:  All visitors must hold a passport valid for more than 1 month.

Title: Rabat
Content: Rabat (Berber: , Arabic: الرِّبَاط‎ ‎ , Moroccan Arabic: رّباط‎ ‎ ) is the capital city of Morocco and its second largest city with an urban population of approximately 580,000 (2014) and a metropolitan population of over 1.2 million.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rabat
Content:  It is also the capital city of the Rabat-Salé-Kénitra administrative region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinuiju Special Administrative Region
Content: Sinŭiju Special Administrative Region is a special administrative region (SAR) of North Korea proclaimed in 2002 (but has not been put into "de facto" operation as of 2014), on the border with China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinuiju Special Administrative Region
Content:  It was established in September 2002 in an area including parts of Sinŭiju and the surrounding area, in an attempt to introduce market economics, and is directly governed as in the case of "Directly Governed Cities".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sinuiju Special Administrative Region
Content:  The special administrative region was modelled after China's Special Administrative Regions (SARs), Hong Kong and Macau, and, like them, has a "Basic Law" (기본법; Kibonpŏp).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caraga
Content: Caraga, officially known as the Caraga Administrative Region or simply Caraga Region and designated as Region XIII, is an administrative region in the Philippines occupying the northeastern section of the island of Mindanao.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caraga
Content:  The Caraga Region was created through "Republic Act No. 7901" on February 23, 1995.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caraga
Content:  The region comprises five provinces: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Surigao del Norte, Surigao del Sur and Dinagat Islands; six cities: Bayugan, Bislig, Butuan, Cabadbaran, Surigao and Tandag; 67 municipalities and 1,311 barangays. Butuan is the regional administrative center.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2014 NPCSC Decision on Hong Kong
Content: The Decision of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress on Issues Relating to the Selection of the Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region by Universal Suffrage and on the Method for Forming the Legislative Council of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region in the Year 2016 (), commonly known as 31 August Decision (), is a decision made by the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress (NPCSC), the national legislative body of the People's Republic of China (PRC) on 31 August 2014 which set limits for the 2017 Chief Executive election and 2016 Legislative Council election in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ilocos Region
Content: The Ilocos Region (Ilocano: "Rehion/Deppaar ti Ilocos" ; Pangasinan: "Sagor na Baybay na Luzon" ; Filipino: "Rehiyon ng Ilocos" ) is an administrative region of the Philippines, designated as Region I, occupying the northwestern section of Luzon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ilocos Region
Content:  It is bordered by the Cordillera Administrative Region to the east, the Cagayan Valley to the northeast and southeast, and the Central Luzon to the south.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ilocos Region
Content:  To the west lies the South China Sea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content: The Wolong Special Administrative Region () is an area in Sichuan, China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content:  It is located in the southwest of Wenchuan County, Ngawa Tibetan and Qiang Autonomous Prefecture of Sichuan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content:  It was formerly known as Wolong Special Administrative Region of Wenchuan County, Sichuan Province and was founded in March 1983 with approval of the State Council.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content:  It was given its current name and placed under Sichuan provincial government with administrative supervision by the provincial department of forestry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content:  Its area supersedes Sichuan Wolong National Nature Reserve and its administrative office is the same as the Administrative Bureau of the State Forestry Administration for the reserve.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wolong Special Administrative Region
Content:  It currently has a population of 5343.

Content:  LOCPG or 中聯辦) is an organ of the Central People's Government of the People's Republic of China (PRC) in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR).

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Liaison Office (Hong Kong)
Content:  It replaced the New China News Agency (NCNA) as the representative of the PRC government in Hong Kong in 2000.

session_0.text:

Content:  Being a national law of the People's Republic of China, the Basic Law was adopted on 4 April 1990 by the Seventh National People's Congress and signed by President Yang Shangkun.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dictyosperma
Content: Dictyosperma is a monotypic genus of flowering plant in the palm family found in the Mascarene Islands in the Indian Ocean (Mauritius, Réunion and Rodrigues).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dictyosperma
Content:  The sole species, Dictyosperma album, is widely cultivated in the tropics but has been farmed to near extinction in its native habitat.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dictyosperma
Content:  It is commonly called princess palm or hurricane palm, the latter owing to its ability to withstand strong winds by easily shedding leaves.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dictyosperma
Content:  It is closely related to, and resembles, palms in the "Archontophoenix" genus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dictyosperma
Content:  The genus is named from two Greek words meaning "net" and "seed" and the epithet is Latin for "white", the common color of the crownshaft at the top of the trunk.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content: The genus Huernia (family Apocynaceae, subfamily Asclepiadoideae) consists of stem succulents from Eastern and Southern Africa, first described as a genus in 1810.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content:  The flowers are five-lobed, usually somewhat more funnel- or bell-shaped than in the closely related genus "Stapelia", and often striped vividly in contrasting colours or tones, some glossy, others matt and wrinkled depending on the species concerned.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content:  To pollinate, the flowers attract flies by emitting a scent similar to that of carrion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content:  The genus is considered close to the genera "Stapelia" and "Hoodia".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content:  The name is in honour of Justin Heurnius (1587–1652) a Dutch missionary who is reputed to have been the first collector of South African Cape plants.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Huernia
Content:  His name was actually mis-spelt by the collector.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content: Bernadette Mayer (born May 12, 1945) is an American poet, writer, and visual artist associated with both the Language poets and the New York School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  Mayer's record-keeping and use of stream-of-consciousness narrative are two trademarks of her writing, though she is also known for her work with form and mythology.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  In addition to the influence of her textual-visual art and journal-keeping, Mayer's poetry is widely acknowledged as some of the first to speak accurately and honestly about the experience of motherhood.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  Mayer edited the journal "0 TO 9" with Vito Acconci, and, until 1983, United Artists books and magazines with Lewis Warsh.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  Mayer taught at the New School for Social Research, where she earned her degree in 1967, and, during the 1970s, she led a number of workshops at the Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church in New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  From 1980 to 1984, Mayer served as director of the Poetry Project, and her influence in the contemporary avant-garde is felt widely, with writers like Kathy Acker, Charles Bernstein, John Giorno, and Anne Waldman having sat in on her workshops.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bernadette Mayer
Content:  Mayer was a 2015 Guggenheim Fellowship Recipient and received a National Book Critics Circle Nomination for her most recent book, 2016's "Works and Days".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fiction International
Content: Fiction International is a literary magazine devoted to innovative forms of fiction and non-fiction which addresses progressive political ideals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fiction International
Content:  Founded at St. Lawrence University in New York City by Joe David Bellamyin 1973, the magazine moved to San Diego State University in 1983, where it has been "edited by Harold Jaffe and Larry McCaffery until 1992, when Harold Jaffe assumed sole editorship".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Fiction International
Content:  Over the years, the magazine has published works by J.M. Coetzee, Claribel Alegría, Robert Coover, William S. Burroughs, Alberto Moravia, Malcolm X, Allen Ginsberg, Marguerite Duras, Edmund White, Kathy Acker, Eckhard Gerdes, and Alain Robbe-Grillet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Variety (1983 film)
Content: Variety is a 1983 American independent film directed by Bette Gordon, co-written by Kathy Acker, and starring Sandy McLeod, Will Patton, and Richard M. Davidson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Variety (1983 film)
Content:  The film follows a young woman who takes a job at a New York City pornographic theater and becomes increasingly obsessed with a wealthy patron who may or may not be involved with the mafia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathy Acker
Content: Kathy Acker (née Lehmann; April 18, 1944 – November 30, 1997) was an American experimental novelist, punk poet, playwright, essayist, postmodernist and sex-positive feminist writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kathy Acker
Content:  She was influenced by the Black Mountain School poets, the writer William S. Burroughs, the artist and theoretician David Antin, French critical theory, philosophy and pornography.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Narrative
Content: New Narrative is a movement and theory of experimental writing launched in San Francisco in the late 1970s by Robert Gluck and Bruce Boone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Narrative
Content:  New Narrative strove to represent subjective experience honestly without pretense that a text can be absolutely objective nor its meaning absolutely fluid.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Narrative
Content:  Authenticity is paramount in New Narrative, and is possible with a variety of devices, including fragmentation, meta-text, identity politics, explicit descriptions of sex and undisguised identification with the author's physicality, intentionality, interior emotional life and external life circumstances.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Narrative
Content:  The New Narrative movement includes many gay and lesbian authors, and the works were greatly influenced by the AIDS epidemic in the '80s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: New Narrative
Content:  In addition to founders Bruce Boone and Robert Gluck, New Narrative writers include Michael Amnasen, Dodie Bellamy, Kevin Killian, Sam D'Allesandro, Cookie Mueller, Dennis Cooper, Kathy Acker, Bo Huston, Camille Roy, Steve Abbott, Gary Indiana and filmmakers Warren Sonbert.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson Mac Low
Content: Jackson Mac Low (September 12, 1922 – December 8, 2004) was an American poet, performance artist, composer and playwright, known to most readers of poetry as a practioneer of systematic chance operations and other non-intentional compositional methods in his work, which Mac Low first experienced in the musical work of John Cage, Earle Brown, and Christian Wolff.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson Mac Low
Content:  He was married to the artist Iris Lezak from 1962 to 1978, and to the poet Anne Tardos from 1990 until his death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson Mac Low
Content:  An early affiliate of Fluxus (he co-published An Anthology of Chance Operations) and stylistic progenitor of the Language poets, Mac Low cultivated ties with an eclectic array of notable figures in the postwar American avant-garde, including Nam June Paik, Kathy Acker, Allen Ginsberg, and Arthur Russell.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson Mac Low
Content:  His work has been published in more than 90 anthologies and periodicals and read publicly, exhibited, performed, and broadcast in North and South America, Europe, Japan, Australia, and New Zealand.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jackson Mac Low
Content:  He read, performed, and lectured in New York and throughout North America, Europe, and New Zealand, San Francisco, Santa Cruz, Asnières, Paris, Bouliac (near Bordeaux), Marseilles, Buffalo, Philadelphia, and New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tim Rice
Content: Sir Timothy Miles Bindon "Tim" Rice (born 10 November 1944) is an English author and Academy Award, Golden Globe Award, Tony Award, and Grammy Award-winning lyricist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tim Rice
Content:  He is best known for his collaborations with Andrew Lloyd Webber, with whom he wrote "Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat", "Jesus Christ Superstar", and "Evita"; with Björn Ulvaeus and Benny Andersson of ABBA, with whom he wrote "Chess"; for additional songs for the 2011 West End revival of "The Wizard of Oz"; and for his work with Alan Menken on Disney's "Aladdin, Beauty and the Beast, and the musical King David").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tim Rice
Content:  He also worked with Elton John on Disney's "The Lion King", the musical "Aida", and DreamWorks Animation's "The Road to El Dorado" and Ennio Morricone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avant-Pop: Fiction for a Daydream Nation
Content: Avant-Pop: Fiction for a Daydream Nation is a Fiction Collective Two book published by Black Ice Books in 1992 edited by Larry McCaffery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avant-Pop: Fiction for a Daydream Nation
Content:  It is a collection of innovative fiction, graphic art, and various unclassifiable texts written by some of the most radical literary talents who McCaffery classifies as Avantpop.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avant-Pop: Fiction for a Daydream Nation
Content:  In his introductory chapter, McCaffery calls these writers "a new breed of pop-culture demolition artists".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Avant-Pop: Fiction for a Daydream Nation
Content:  These writers include cult figures such as Kathy Acker, Samuel R. Delany, Harold Jaffe and Derek Pell, as well as young new writers such as Euridice, Mark Leyner, and William T. Vollmann.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content: High Risk Books was founded in New York City in 1993, as an imprint of Serpent's Tail Press of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content:  Started by Ira Silverberg and Amy Scholder, who was then an editor at City Lights Books in San Francisco.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content:  High Risk Books was dedicated to publishing innovative, provocative, and progressive literature.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content:  The publishing firm was united by its concern for certain subversive impulses, and in this spirit printed authors as diverse as William S. Burroughs, Tim Dlugos, Kathy Acker, Diamanda Galas, Robert Gary Indiana, June Jordan, Cookie Mueller, Lynne Tillman, John Giorno, Pagan Kennedy, Sapphire, Jayne Cortez, and many others.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content:  As a small press, High Risk played an important role in providing a space for many emerging writers who would otherwise have found it difficult to get published in mainstream, large houses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: High Risk Books
Content:  High Risk Books ceased operation in January 1997 because of disagreements with Serpent's Tail in London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content: Blood and Guts in High School is a novel by Kathy Acker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content:  It was written in the late 1970s and copyrighted in 1978.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content:  It traveled a complex and circuitous route to publication in 1984.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content:  It remains Acker's most popular and best-selling book.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content:  The novel is also considered a metafictional text, which is aware of its status as a fictional piece.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blood and Guts in High School
Content:  The novel is interested in exploring politics, history, theories, and writing.

Title: Listed buildings in Blackpool
Content: Blackpool is a seaside town and unitary authority situated on The Fylde coast in Lancashire, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Listed buildings in Blackpool
Content:  This list includes the listed buildings in Blackpool and Bispham, a village within the borough of Blackpool.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Listed buildings in Blackpool
Content:  One is classified by English Heritage as being in Grade I and five in Grade II*.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Listed buildings in Blackpool
Content:  In the United Kingdom, the term "listed building" refers to a building or other structure officially designated as being of special architectural, historical or cultural significance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Listed buildings in Blackpool
Content:  These buildings are in three grades: Grade I consists of buildings of outstanding architectural or historical interest; Grade II* includes particularly significant buildings of more than local interest; Grade II consists of buildings of special architectural or historical interest.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  Buildings in England are listed by the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport on recommendations provided by English Heritage, which also determines the grading.

Title: Horace Brindley
Content: Horace Brindley (1 January 1885 — 1971) was an English footballer who played in the Football League for Blackpool, Lincoln City and Stoke as well as a number of Southern League clubs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blackpool Mecca
Content: The Blackpool Mecca was a large entertainment venue on Central Drive in the seaside town of Blackpool, Lancashire, in North West England, first opened in 1965.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blackpool Mecca
Content:  In the 1970s, it was particularly known for The Highland Room, which was a major Northern Soul music venue.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blackpool Mecca
Content:  The building was closed down in 1980s and was finally demolished in January 2009 to make way for new campus buildings of Blackpool and The Fylde College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Blackpool Mecca
Content:  However, following an issue with funding B&FC withdrew and as of 2013 the site is planned for residential development.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Rochdale A.F.C. seasons
Content: Rochdale Association Football Club is an English professional association football club based in the town of Rochdale in Greater Manchester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Rochdale A.F.C. seasons
Content:  The club plays in the Football League One, the third tier in the English football league system.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Rochdale A.F.C. seasons
Content:  The club's colours are black and blue and they play their home games at Spotland Stadium, which has a capacity of 10,249.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Rochdale A.F.C. seasons
Content:  Formed in 1907 and nicknamed "the Dale", they were accepted into the Football League in 1921.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Rochdale A.F.C. seasons
Content:  Since then, the club has remained in the bottom two professional divisions of English Football.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Football in England
Content: Association football is the national sport in England, where the first modern set of rules for the code were established in 1863, which were a major influence on the development of the modern Laws of the Game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Football in England
Content:  With over 40,000 association football clubs, England has more clubs involved in the code than any other country as well as the world's first club (Sheffield F.C.), the world's oldest professional association football club (Notts County F.C), the oldest national governing body (the Football Association), the first national team, the oldest national knockout competition (the FA Cup) and the oldest national league (the English Football League).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Football in England
Content:  Today England's top domestic league, the Premier League, is one of the most popular and richest sports leagues in the world, with six of the ten richest football clubs in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain in Nevada
Content: The use of the pen name of Mark Twain first occurred in Samuel Clemens's writing while in the Nevada Territory which he had journeyed to with his brother.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain in Nevada
Content:  Clemens/Twain lived in Nevada from 1861-1864, and visited the area twice after leaving.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain in Nevada
Content:  Historians such as Peter Messent see Clemens's time in Nevada as "the third major formative period of Mark Twain's career" (after his time in Hannibal and upon the Mississippi), due to his encounters with "writers and humorists who would both shape and put the finishing touches on his literary art."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain in Nevada
Content:  The "Routledge Encyclopedia of Mark Twain" states that despite the few "disagreeable experiences" he had there, Twain "thrived in Nevada."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain in Nevada
Content:  Among those things he learnt was "how far he could push a joke" a lesson learnt from some "disagreeable experiences" he brought upon himself.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isaiah Sellers
Content: Isaiah Sellers (ca. 1802–1864) was the riverboat captain from whom Samuel L. Clemens claimed to have appropriated the pen-name Mark Twain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isaiah Sellers
Content:  The story of how Clemens started to use the name is told in chapter 50 of "Life on the Mississippi" and is summarized in the main article on Mark Twain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isaiah Sellers
Content:  He allegedly wrote articles for the "New Orleans Daily Picayune".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isaiah Sellers
Content:  Since there are a few problems with the chronology of Sellers' death and Clemens' first use of the name, the story is not accepted uncritically by Twain scholars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isaiah Sellers
Content:  Captain Isaiah Sellers is buried at Bellefontaine Cemetery in St. Louis, Missouri.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Tramp Abroad
Content: A Tramp Abroad is a work of travel literature, including a mixture of autobiography and fictional events, by American author Mark Twain, published in 1880.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Tramp Abroad
Content:  The book details a journey by the author, with his friend Harris (a character created for the book, and based on his closest friend, Joseph Twichell), through central and southern Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Tramp Abroad
Content:  While the stated goal of the journey is to walk most of the way, the men find themselves using other forms of transport as they traverse the continent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Tramp Abroad
Content:  The book is the third of Mark Twain's five travel books and is often thought to be an unofficial sequel to the first one, "The Innocents Abroad".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content: Is He Dead?

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  is a play by Mark Twain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  Written by Twain in 1898, it was first published in print in 2003, after Mark Twain scholar Shelley Fisher Fishkin read the manuscript in the archives of the Mark Twain Papers at the University of California at Berkeley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  The play was long known to scholars but never attracted much attention until Fishkin arranged to have it published in book form.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  She later played a primary role in getting the play produced on Broadway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  Contemporary American playwright David Ives adapted the play for the modern stage before its inaugural performance in 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  "Is He Dead?"

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Is He Dead?
Content:  is now published and licensed for theatrical use by Playscripts, Inc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shohola Falls
Content: Shohola Falls is a 2003 novel written by Michael Pearson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shohola Falls
Content:  The novel imagines the true story of Thomas Blankenship, the young man that Mark Twain reputedly based the character of Huck Finn upon in his novel Adventures of Huckleberry Finn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shohola Falls
Content:  In "Shohola Falls", Mark Twain is set as an important character, the fictional reality aligned to the historical one.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today
Content: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today is a novel by Mark Twain and Charles Dudley Warner first published in 1873.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today
Content:  It satirizes greed and political corruption in post–Civil War America in the era now referred to as the Gilded Age.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today
Content:  Although not one of Twain's best-known works, it has appeared in more than one hundred editions since its original publication.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today
Content:  Twain and Warner originally had planned to issue the novel with illustrations by Thomas Nast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Gilded Age: A Tale of Today
Content:  The book is remarkable for two reasons–-it is the only novel Twain wrote with a collaborator, and its title very quickly became synonymous with graft, materialism, and corruption in public life.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Prince and the Pauper
Content: The Prince and the Pauper is a novel by American author Mark Twain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Prince and the Pauper
Content:  It was first published in 1881 in Canada, before its 1882 publication in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Prince and the Pauper
Content:  The novel represents Twain's first attempt at historical fiction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Prince and the Pauper
Content:  Set in 1547, it tells the story of two young boys who are identical in appearance: Tom Canty, a pauper who lives with his abusive father in Offal Court off Pudding Lane in London, and Prince Edward, son of King Henry VIII.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jap Herron
Content: Jap Herron: A Novel Written From The Ouija Board was a novel first published in 1917 claimed to be authored by Mark Twain 7 years after his death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jap Herron
Content:  The book was transcribed by Emily Grant Hutchings who claimed to have had the novel dictated to her from beyond the grave by the deceased Mark Twain through use of a Ouija board.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain: Words & Music
Content: Mark Twain: Words & Music is a double-CD produced by Grammy Award-winner Carl Jackson, a Bluegrass and Country music artist, as a benefit for the Mark Twain Boyhood Home & Museum, a non-profit foundation in Hannibal, Missouri.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain: Words & Music
Content:  The project tells the life story of Mark Twain (Samuel Clemens) in spoken word and song and features many well-known artists.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain: Words & Music
Content:  "Run Mississippi" by Rhonda Vincent reached #2 on the Bluegrass Today charts the same week that "Comet Ride" by Ricky Skaggs reached #7.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark Twain: Words & Music
Content:  The album was released on September 21, 2011 and is the most downloaded Americana album of all time on AirPlay Direct, an online music source for radio stations, with more than 7,000 downloads its first year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
Content: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn (or, in more recent editions, The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn) is a novel by Mark Twain, first published in the United Kingdom in December 1884 and in the United States in February 1885.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
Content:  Commonly named among the Great American Novels, the work is among the first in major American literature to be written throughout in vernacular English, characterized by local color regionalism.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
Content:  It is told in the first person by Huckleberry "Huck" Finn, a friend of Tom Sawyer the narrator of two other Twain novels ("Tom Sawyer Abroad" and "Tom Sawyer, Detective").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
Content:  It is a direct sequel to "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Tra-La Days Are Over
Content: The Tra-La Days Are Over is a 1973 album by American singer-songwriter Neil Sedaka.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Tra-La Days Are Over
Content:  The album was the second to be produced at Strawberry Studios in England in collaboration with Graham Gouldman, Lol Creme, Kevin Godley and Eric Stewart, who had formed the band 10cc since their first joint venture with Sedaka on "Solitaire" (1972).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bloody Tourists
Content: Bloody Tourists is the sixth studio album by the English rock band 10cc, released worldwide by Mercury Records and in North America by Polydor Records in September 1978.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bloody Tourists
Content:  Recorded at Strawberry Studios South in Dorking, the album was produced by Eric Stewart and Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bloody Tourists
Content:  The cover art was created by Hipgnosis with graphics by George Hardie.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bloody Tourists
Content:  The cover photograph was taken by Aubrey Powell of Hipgnosis.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Original Soundtrack
Content: The Original Soundtrack is the third studio album by the English art rock band 10cc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Original Soundtrack
Content:  It was released in 1975 and peaked at number four on the UK Albums Chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Original Soundtrack
Content:  "The Original Soundtrack" includes the singles "Life Is a Minestrone", and "I'm Not in Love", the band's most popular song.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreadlock Holiday
Content: "Dreadlock Holiday" is a reggae song by 10cc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreadlock Holiday
Content:  Written by Eric Stewart and Graham Gouldman, it was the lead single from the band's 1978 album, "Bloody Tourists".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dreadlock Holiday
Content:  Lead vocals were performed by Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm Not in Love
Content: "I'm Not in Love" is a song by English group 10cc, written by band members Eric Stewart and Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm Not in Love
Content:  It is notable for its innovative and distinctive backing track, composed mostly of the band's multitracked vocals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm Not in Love
Content:  Released in the UK in May 1975 as the second single from the band's third album "The Original Soundtrack", it became the second of the group's three number-one singles in the UK between 1973 and 1978, topping the UK singles chart for two weeks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I'm Not in Love
Content:  The song was also the band's breakthrough hit worldwide, reaching number one in Ireland and Canada and number two on the "Billboard" Hot 100 in the US, as well as reaching the top 10 in Australia, New Zealand and several European countries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content: Live and Let Live was 10cc's first live album, released in the Autumn of 1977.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  It was recorded at the Odeon Theatre in London between 18 and 20 June 1977 and the Apollo Theatre, Manchester between 16 and 17 July 1977.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  The album featured the new line-up of Eric Stewart, Graham Gouldman, Rick Fenn, Paul Burgess and Stuart Tosh along with Tony O'Malley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  Burgess had toured with 10cc before, acting as second drummer to Kevin Godley, and also appeared on their first post-split album "Deceptive Bends" (1977) that was issued earlier that year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  The main criticism at the time was that they only performed songs written by Stewart and Gouldman, missing out on big hits such as "Rubber Bullets", "Donna", "Life Is a Minestrone" and "The Dean and I".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  Also, the album replicated all but one of the songs from the previous album, which had only been out for six months.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  Despite this, the album was a hit, reaching No. 14 in the UK charts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Live and Let Live (10cc album)
Content:  No singles were issued in the UK; however, "The Wall Street Shuffle" was issued in America, but failed to reach the charts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Dare You! (album)
Content: How Dare You!

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Dare You! (album)
Content:  is the fourth album by British band 10cc.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Dare You! (album)
Content:  Released in 1976, it included UK hit singles "I'm Mandy Fly Me" and "Art for Art's Sake".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Dare You! (album)
Content:  It was also the last 10cc album by the original line-up of Eric Stewart, Graham Gouldman, Kevin Godley, and Lol Creme, with the latter two departing to work on their own musical projects, and eventually becoming music video pioneers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Dare You! (album)
Content:  The album was the band's third with cover artwork by the Hipgnosis creative team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Graham Gouldman Thing
Content: The Graham Gouldman Thing was the debut album by singer and songwriter Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Graham Gouldman Thing
Content:  Gouldman had already written hit singles for Herman's Hermits ("No Milk Today" and "Listen People"), the Yardbirds ("For Your Love"), the Hollies ("Bus Stop") and Wayne Fontana ("Pamela, Pamela", "The Impossible Years") and on this album Gouldman delivered his own versions of some of those songs as well as other new compositions.

session_0.speaker: System

Content: Hotlegs was a short-lived English band best known for its hit single "Neanderthal Man" in 1970.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hotlegs
Content:  The band consisted of Eric Stewart, Kevin Godley, Lol Creme and – briefly – Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hotlegs
Content:  In 1972, the band was relaunched as 10cc.

Title: And Another Thing... (album)
Content: And Another Thing... is an album by 10cc bass player Graham Gouldman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: And Another Thing... (album)
Content:  Released in 2000, the album is a mix of new songs and tracks from earlier stages of Gouldman's musical career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: And Another Thing... (album)
Content:  The title is a reference to his first solo album, released in 1968: "The Graham Gouldman Thing".

Content:  Both formats contained the two songs "Michael Jackson (The Beat Goes On)" and "I Like It Loud," which were also soon after released as singles by Dutch independent dance label Spinnin' Records.

Title: Chase Child Life Program
Content: The Chase Child Life Program addresses the social, emotional and developmental needs of children dealing with the stress of illness and hospitalization.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chase Child Life Program
Content:  child life specialists provide opportunities for children of all ages to master the healthcare experience by preparing and supporting them through medical procedures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chase Child Life Program
Content:  Therapeutic play sessions take place in Chase Place playrooms and at bedside.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chase Child Life Program
Content:  Playrooms are safe places for children to build coping skills, express creativity through play, and share emotions and concerns.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chase Child Life Program
Content:  The program also extends support and resources to families in times of bereavement.

Title: Andraé Crouch
Content: Andraé Edward Crouch (July 1, 1942 – January 8, 2015) was an American gospel singer, songwriter, arranger, record producer and pastor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andraé Crouch
Content:  Referred to as "the father of modern gospel music" by contemporary Christian and gospel music professionals, Crouch was known for his compositions "The Blood Will Never Lose Its Power", "My Tribute (To God Be the Glory)" and "Soon and Very Soon".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andraé Crouch
Content:  In secular music, he was known for his collaborative work during the 1980s and 1990s with Stevie Wonder, Elton John and Quincy Jones as well as conducting choirs that sang on the Michael Jackson hit "Man in the Mirror" and Madonna's "Like a Prayer".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andraé Crouch
Content:  Crouch was noted for his talent of incorporating contemporary secular music styles into the gospel music he grew up with.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Andraé Crouch
Content:  His efforts in this area helped pave the way for early American contemporary Christian music during the 1960s and 1970s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don't Be Messin' 'Round
Content: "Don't Be Messin' 'Round" is a song by American recording artist Michael Jackson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don't Be Messin' 'Round
Content:  The song was originally recorded in 1986 during initial recording sessions for Jackson's seventh studio album, "Bad", but was never finished and was left off the album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don't Be Messin' 'Round
Content:  Jackson reportedly revisited the track for his eighth and studio albums, but it was again abandoned from both.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don't Be Messin' 'Round
Content:  In May 2012, it was announced that Jackson's original 1986 demo for "Don't Be Messin' 'Round" was to be included on a re-release of the original first single from "Bad," "I Just Can't Stop Loving You".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Don't Be Messin' 'Round
Content:  This release marked the beginning of the promotional campaign for "Bad 25," a twenty-fifth anniversary re-release of the "Bad" album.

session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content: Dangerous is the eighth studio album by American recording artist Michael Jackson, released on November 26, 1991 by Epic Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  It was his fourth studio album released under the Epic label, and his first since 1975's "Forever, Michael" not to be produced by longtime collaborator Quincy Jones, who had agreed to split after the final recording sessions for Jackson's 1987 album, "Bad".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  "Dangerous" took over a year in production, and was produced by Jackson with additional production from his friend Bill Bottrell and Teddy Riley.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  Jackson wrote twelve of the fourteen songs on the album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  Lyrical themes expressed on the album included racism, poverty, romance, the welfare of children and the world and self-improvement, topics Jackson had covered before.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  Nine singles were released from "Dangerous" between November 1991 and December 1993, with seven singles issued in the United States, and two others released only outside the US.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  The two singles released outside the United States were successful, charting within the top ten and top forty respectively.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  "Dangerous" peaked at number one in nine countries, while charting at the top ten in four other territories.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  The only songs not released as singles were "Why You Wanna Trip on Me", "She Drives Me Wild", "Can't Let Her Get Away", "Keep the Faith" and "Dangerous"; a video and a single release for the latter was said to have been planned, but was postponed indefinitely due to the musician's tour and allegations of child sexual abuse in 1993.
