session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content: The Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration was a 2001 New York City revue show by Michael Jackson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  It took place on September 7, 2001 and September 10, 2001.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  In late November 2001, the CBS television network aired the concerts as a two-hour special in honor of Michael Jackson's thirtieth year as a solo entertainer (his first solo single, "Got to Be There", was recorded in 1971).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  The show was edited from footage of two separate concerts Michael had orchestrated in New York City's Madison Square Garden on September 7 and September 10 of 2001.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  The shows sold out in five hours.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  Ticket prices were pop's most expensive ever; the best seats cost $10,000 and included a dinner with Michael Jackson and a signed poster.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  The show was choreographed by Glenn Douglas Packard & Brian Thomas, they were nominated for a Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  The show was presented by David Gest, World Events LLC and Clear Channel Entertainment in conjunction with American Airlines, American Eagle and Amazon.com.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  Jackson reportedly earned $7.5 million for each of the two concerts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Celebration
Content:  The concert official Boxscore was $10,072,105 for both concerts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beat It
Content: "Beat It" is a song written and performed by American singer Michael Jackson single from the singer's sixth solo album, "Thriller" (1982).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beat It
Content:  The song was produced by Quincy Jones together with Jackson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beat It
Content:  Following the successful chart performances of the "Thriller" singles "The Girl Is Mine" and "Billie Jean", "Beat It" was released on February 14, 1983 as the album's third single.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Beat It
Content:  The song is also notable for its famous video, which featured Jackson bringing two gangs together through the power of music and dance.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content: "Billie Jean" is a song by American singer Michael Jackson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  It is the second single from the singer's sixth studio album, "Thriller" (1982).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  It was written and composed by Jackson and produced by Jackson and Quincy Jones.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  There are contradictory claims on the meaning of the song's lyrics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  One suggests that they are derived from a real-life experience, in which a female fan claimed that Jackson (or one of his brothers) had fathered her twins.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  However, Michael Jackson stated that "Billie Jean" was based on groupies he had encountered.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  The song is well known for its distinctive bassline played by Louis Johnson, the standard drum beat heard in the beginning, the repetition of "Billie Jean is not my lover" towards the end of the song and Michael Jackson's vocal hiccups.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Billie Jean
Content:  The song was mixed 91 times by audio engineer Bruce Swedien before it was finalized, though he reportedly went with the second mix as the final product.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thriller (Michael Jackson album)
Content: Thriller is the sixth studio album by American singer Michael Jackson, released on November 30, 1982, in the United States by Epic Records and internationally by CBS Records.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thriller (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  The follow-up to Jackson's successful previous studio album, "Off the Wall", it explores genres similar to those of its predecessor, including pop, post-disco, rock and funk.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thriller (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  Recording sessions for the album took place from April to November 1982 at Westlake Recording Studios in Los Angeles, with a production budget of $750,000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thriller (Michael Jackson album)
Content:  Quincy Jones produced the album, and Jackson wrote four of its nine songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Venice Commission
Content: The Venice Commission is an advisory body of the Council of Europe, composed of independent experts in the field of constitutional law.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Venice Commission
Content:  It was created in 1990 after the fall of the Berlin Wall, at a time of urgent need for constitutional assistance in Central and Eastern Europe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Venice Commission
Content:  The Commission's official name is the European Commission for Democracy through Law, but due to its meeting place in Venice, Italy, where sessions take part four times a year, it is usually referred to as the Venice Commission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zion Elementary School District 6
Content: Zion Elementary School District 6 is an Illinois school district serving Zion, Lake County.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zion Elementary School District 6
Content:  The school district governs seven schools.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zion Elementary School District 6
Content:  Lakeview School is responsible for prekindergarten children, housing also the district's administrative offices.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zion Elementary School District 6
Content:  Beulah Park Elementary School, East Elementary School, Elmwood Elementary School, Shiloh Park Elementary School and West Elementary School serve students between kindergarten and sixth grade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Zion Elementary School District 6
Content:  Zion Elementary School District 6's sole middle school, Zion Central Middle School, serves the seventh and eighth grades.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Village School (Great Neck, New York)
Content: Village School (also known as VS) is an American long-established public alternative school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Village School (Great Neck, New York)
Content:  It is a member of Coalition of Essential Schools and is located in the village of Great Neck, New York, serving students in grades 8 through 12.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Village School (Great Neck, New York)
Content:  Village School is one of three high schools in the Great Neck School District, which includes Great Neck North High School and Great Neck South High School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Village School (Great Neck, New York)
Content:  Village School offers its 39 students an outdoor education program, college preparatory program, and inclusion of students with disabilities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck, New York
Content: Great Neck is a region on Long Island, New York, that covers a peninsula on the North Shore of Long Island, which includes the villages of Great Neck, Great Neck Estates, Great Neck Plaza, and others, as well as an area south of the peninsula near Lake Success and the border territory of Queens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck, New York
Content:  The incorporated village of Great Neck had a population of 9,989 at the 2010 census, while the larger Great Neck area comprises a residential community of some 40,000 people in nine villages and hamlets in the town of North Hempstead, of which Great Neck is the northwestern quadrant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck, New York
Content:  Great Neck has five ZIP Codes (11020–11024), and one school district.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck School District
Content: The Great Neck School District is a comprehensive community public school district primarily serving students in Great Neck, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck School District
Content:  It also serves students in some parts of Manhasset and New Hyde Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Great Neck School District
Content:  It is Union Free School District Number 7 in the Town of North Hempstead, Nassau County, New York, United States, on Long Island.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147 is an elementary school district based in the southern suburban Chicago city of Harvey, Cook County, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  The district is composed of four schools: three are elementary schools and one is a middle school, where all schools are located in the adjacent village of Dixmoor with the exception of one elementary school located in Harvey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  District students in living in Harvey that are in prekindergarten, kindergarten, or grades one through five attend Washington Elementary School which is directed by the principal, Dr. Earnest Taylor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  Students of the same grades living in Dixmoor attend either Lincoln Elementary School or King Elementary School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  The principals of the schools are, respectively, Dr. Gregory Jackson and Sandra Wells.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  All elementary schools feed into Rosa L. Parks Middle School, which is headed by principal Abigail Phillips.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: West Harvey-Dixmoor Public School District 147
Content:  The school district's superintendent is Dr. Alex Boyd, Jr.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saddle Rock Elementary School
Content: Saddle Rock Elementary School or simply "Saddle Rock" is an elementary school, comprising grades Kindergarten through 5.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saddle Rock Elementary School
Content:  It is a public school located in Great Neck, New York, USA as part of the Great Neck School District.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Saddle Rock Elementary School
Content:  The school has been recognized by the Blue Ribbon Schools Program.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content: North Boone Community Unit School District 200 is a unified school district based in the central region of the county of its namesake, Boone County; more specifically, in the village of Poplar Grove, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  Five of the six schools in the district are located here, with the other being eastwards in the village of Capron.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  The community unit school district is composed of, as previously mentioned, six schools: there are three elementary schools, two middle schools, and one high school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  Manchester Elementary School, which serves the grades K-4 alongside Poplar Grove Elementary School, is governed by Principal Kristi Crawford; the principal of Poplar Grove Elementary School is Sharon Olds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  Capron Elementary School, the only school in the district located in a village other than Poplar Grove, serves not only the grades K-4, but also includes a prekindergarten program.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  The current principal is Matt Klett.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  All students who graduate from one of the district elementary schools will consolidate at the fifth grade in one school, called North Boone Upper Elementary School, whose principal is Mike Greenlee;all students move on to North Boone Middle School, headed by Jeremiah Auble, which educates those in grades seven and eight.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  The last leg of education this district can provide is to those from grade nine to grade twelve; the facility is called North Boone High School, whose principal is named Jacob Hubert.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  The current superintendent is Steven Baule.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: North Boone Community Unit School District 200
Content:  The mascot of the district high school is the Viking.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1 is a unified school district based in Jasper County's county seat of Newton, Illinois; it is the only school district in the county and is, consequently, the main educational body in all of Jasper County, although it serves portions of Effingham County and Cumberland County as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  This school district is composed of six schools in total; four elementary schools, one junior high school, and one high school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  There is also a prekindergarten program run at the high school of the district should parents wish to enroll their children early.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Willow Hill Elementary School, which is located in the village of its namesake, serves only kindergarteners; its proximity to the county seat and central position in the county allows its students to dawn from all parts of the county and still have easy access to the elementary school they will attend.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The principal of Willow Hill is Dave Parker.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Grove Elementary School is located in Island Grove, Illinois, the highest point of elevation in the county.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Grove Elementary School educates students from kindergarten to grade six, and it runs a prekindergarten program as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Craig Carr is the principal of this school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Ste. Marie Elementary School is located in the southern Jasper County village of Ste. Marie, and serves students in grades one through six.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The principal of Ste. Marie is David Parker, the principal of Willow Hill Elementary School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Newton Elementary School is located in the county seat of Newton, and serves most of west Jasper County's first through sixth graders under principal Travis Wyatt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The latter three elementary schools feed into Jasper County Junior High School and are taught in the facility during seventh and eighth grade while being supervised by Newton Elementary School principal Travis Wyatt before graduating into Newton Community High School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Students in grades nine through twelve spend their last leg of precollegiate education at this school; their principal is Ruth Kerner.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jasper County Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The district superintendent is Ron Alburtus, and the district's mascot is the eagle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content: Burbank School District 111 is an elementary school district located in Burbank, a Chicago suburb just south of Chicago Midway International Airport in Cook County, Illinois.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content:  There are seven elementary schools in District 111.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content:  They are all kindergarten through sixth grade and located within the city of Burbank.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content:  The elementary schools are: Luther Burbank Elementary School under direction of principal Robert Mocek, principal Marian Stockhausen's Richard Byrd Elementary School, Jacqueline Kennedy Elementary School under direction of principal Charles Roza, principal Mark Antkiewicz's Rosa Maddock Elementary School, principal Patricia Donaghue's Frances McCord Elementary School, principal Mary Anne Sheehan's Edward J. Tobin Elementary School, which was reopened in 2007 after being rebuilt, and principal Mary Rein's Harry Fry Elementary School, which closed after the 2003-2004 school year and reopened during the 2009-2010 school year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content:  Graduates from all these elementary schools move on to Liberty Junior High School, which is under direction of principal Jim Martin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Burbank School District 111
Content:  The district superintendent is Carol Kunst.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content: Charleston Community Unit School District 1 is a unified school district based in Charleston, the county seat of Coles County, Illinois; it was created through the consolidation of the charter school district it was formed from and over sixty-seven other school districts in the area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Charleston Community Unit School District 1 is a conglomerate of six schools, with all but one located in Charleston itself: four elementary schools, one middle school, and one high school form the makeup of the district.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Education begins at a prekindergarten level, and those living in the Charleston area attend Mark Twain Elementary School, which prepares early youth for schooling by taking them through prekindergarten and kindergarten under the supervision of principal Terry Diss.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Students who live in the far east reaches of Coles County may attend Ashmore Elementary School, which is based in a northeasterly village by the same name; Ashmore's distance from the other schools permits it to educate a larger range of students for the sake of efficiency.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The school educates students from kindergarten through grade four, and also runs a prekindergarten program.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Ashmore is also governed by Terry Diss.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Students who graduate from Mark Twain move onwards to Carl Sandburg Elementary School, which educates those in grades one, two, and three under principal Chad Burgett.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  At Jefferson Elementary School in Charleston, the student bodies from both Carl Sandburg Elementary School and Ashmore Elementary School are consolidated.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  Students from grades four through six are taught in the facility under the supervision of principal Deborah Poffinbarger.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  District students graduating from Jefferson are moved to Charleston Middle School, where they are taught as seventh and eighth graders; the school principal here is Sandy Wilson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The last branch of education that Charleston's school district can provide is the education of adolescents in the grades nine through twelve at Charleston High School, which is run by principal Trevor Doughty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charleston Community Unit School District 1
Content:  The superintendent of the schools in the district as of the 2007-08 school year was Dr. William Hill, although is considered as an interim;lastly, the mascots of the district and its middle and high schools are the Trojans, while the mascot of Jefferson Elementary School is the bulldog.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content: Timaeus the Sophist (Greek: Τίμαιος ὁ Σοφιστής ) was a Greek philosopher who lived sometime between the 1st and 4th centuries.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  Nothing is known about his life.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  He is the supposed author of a "Lexicon" of Platonic words which is still extant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  The "Lexicon" made use of earlier commentaries on Plato which are now lost.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  It underwent significant additions and subtractions of text during later periods leading to the inclusion of many words which have nothing to do with Plato or his philosophy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  The purpose of the "Lexicon" was to explain the usage of words and phrases which occur in Plato's works.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  The first detailed study of the manuscript and edition of the Lexicon was produced in the late 18th century by David Ruhnken (1754; 2nd ed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  1789) who also provided a detailed commentary.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Timaeus the Sophist
Content:  There was a revised version of Ruhnken's second edition by Georg Aenotheus Koch in 1828.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of English Latinates of Germanic origin
Content: Many words in the English lexicon are made up of Latinate words; that is, words which have entered the English language from a Romance language (usually Anglo-Norman), or were borrowed directly from Latin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of English Latinates of Germanic origin
Content:  Quite a few of these words can further trace their origins back to a Germanic source (usually Frankish), making them cognate with many native English words from Old English, yielding etymological twins.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of English Latinates of Germanic origin
Content:  Many of these are Franco-German words, or French words of Germanic origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Novella
Content: A novella is a text of written, fictional, narrative prose normally longer than a short story but shorter than a novel, somewhere between 7,500 and 40,000 words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content: Circumlocution (also called circumduction, circumvolution, periphrasis, or ambage) is speech that circles around an idea with many words instead of stating it directly and simply.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  It is sometimes necessary in communication (for example, to avoid lexical gaps that would cause untranslatability), but it can also be undesirable (when an uncommon or easily misunderstood figure of speech is used).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Roundabout speech is the use of many words to describe something that already has a common and concise term (for example, saying ""a tool used for cutting things such as paper and hair"" instead of "scissors").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Most dictionaries use circumlocution to define words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Circumlocution is often used by people with aphasia and people learning a new language, where simple terms can be paraphrased to aid learning or communication (for example, paraphrasing the word "grandfather" as "the father of one's father").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Circumlocution
Content:  Euphemism, innuendo, and equivocation are different forms of circumlocution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content: "How Many Words" is the official second single by the singer-songwriter Blake Lewis, from his debut album "A.D.D. (Audio Day Dream)".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  The song was expected to be followed by "Know My Name", and then "Without You".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  However, due to being dropped by Arista Records, "How Many Words" is the final single from his debut album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  He is expected to release a single from his second album at the end of 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  The single was released to mainstream radio format on March 10, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  Also, an EP featuring remixes of the song was released onto iTunes on May 13, 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: How Many Words
Content:  Lewis performed the song live on the March 6, 2008 results show of the seventh season of "American Idol".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Verbal fluency test
Content: Verbal fluency tests are a kind of psychological test in which participants have to produce as many words as possible from a category in a given time (usually 60 seconds).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Verbal fluency test
Content:  This category can be semantic, including objects such as animals or fruits, or phonemic, including words beginning with a specified letter, such as "p", for example.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content: Many words that existed in Old English did not survive into Modern English.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  There are also many words in Modern English that bear little or no resemblance in meaning to their Old English etymons.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Some linguists estimate that as much as 80 percent of the lexicon of Old English was lost by the end of the Middle English period, including a large number of words formed by compounding, e.g. "bōchūs" ('bookhouse', 'library'), yet we still retain the component parts 'book' and 'house'.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Certain categories of words seem to have been especially vulnerable.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Nearly all words relating to sexual intercourse and sexual organs were supplanted by words of Latin or Ancient Greek origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  Many, if not most, of the words in Modern English that are used in polite conversation to describe body parts and bodily functions are of Latin or Greek origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Changes to Old English vocabulary
Content:  The words which were used in Old English for these same purposes are now mostly either extinct or considered crude or vulgar, such as arse/ass.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Esperanto vocabulary
Content: The word base of Esperanto was originally defined by "Lingvo internacia", published by L. L. Zamenhof in 1887.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Esperanto vocabulary
Content:  It contained some 900 root words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Esperanto vocabulary
Content:  The rules of the language allow speakers to borrow words as needed, recommending only that they look for the most international words, and that they borrow one basic word and derive others from it, rather than borrowing many words with related meanings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Esperanto vocabulary
Content:  In 1894, Zamenhof published the first Esperanto dictionary, "Universala vortaro", which was written in five languages and supplied a larger set of root words.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of English words of Scots origin
Content: List of English words of Scots origin is a list of English language words of Scots origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of English words of Scots origin
Content:  See also "List of English words of Scottish Gaelic origin", which contains many words which were borrowed via Highland Scots.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Parasite
Content: The Parasite is an 1894 novelette by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Super Cup
Content: The 1994 European Super Cup was a football match played over two legs between Arsenal of England and Milan of Italy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Super Cup
Content:  It was the 20th staging of the European Super Cup, a fixture between the winners of the UEFA Champions League and European Cup Winners' Cup.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  The team has participated in 32 seasons of Union of European Football Associations (UEFA) club competitions, including 21 seasons in the European Cup and Champions League, 16 seasons in the UEFA Cup and Europa League, one season in the Cup Winners' Cup and one season in the Intertoto Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  Rosenborg has played six times in the UEFA Cup after qualifying via the Champions League and once via the Intertoto Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  It has played 206 UEFA games, resulting in 81 wins, 39 draws and 86 defeats.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  The club's first appearance was in the 1965–66 European Cup Winners' Cup, and it subsequently entered tournaments in six seasons until 1974–75.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  The club's next appearance was in the 1986–87 European Cup, and then in the 1989–90 European Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  Since then, Rosenborg has been involved in a UEFA tournament every season except the 2006–07 season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rosenborg BK in European football
Content:  The club's best performance is reaching the quarter-finals of the 1996–97 Champions League, while their only European trophy came when they co-won the 2008 UEFA Intertoto Cup.

session_0.text:

Content:  Since their first qualification to major European cup competition in 1966, they have participated in the Inter-Cities Fairs Cup, the European Cup Winners' Cup, the UEFA Cup, the Texaco Cup, the Anglo-Italian Cup, the Anglo-Scottish Cup, as well as winning the Tennent Caledonian Cup.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final
Content: The 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final was a football match on 4 May 1994 contested between Arsenal of England and Parma of Italy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final
Content:  It was the final match of the 1993–94 European Cup Winners' Cup and the 34th European Cup Winners' Cup Final.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final
Content:  The final was held at the Parken Stadium in Copenhagen, and Arsenal won 1–0 with the goal coming from Alan Smith.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final
Content:  It is widely considered as the peak of Arsenal's famous defence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 1994 European Cup Winners' Cup Final
Content:  Arsenal became the fourth London club to win the trophy after Tottenham Hotspur, Chelsea and West Ham United.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  The club was formed in Woolwich in 1886 as Royal Arsenal before it was renamed Woolwich Arsenal in 1893.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  They became the first southern member admitted into the Football League in 1893, having spent their first four seasons solely participating in cup tournaments and friendlies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  The club's name was shortened to Arsenal in 1914, a year after moving to Highbury.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  In spite of finishing fifth in the Second Division in 1915, Arsenal rejoined the First Division at the expense of local rivals Tottenham Hotspur when football resumed after the First World War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  Since that time, they have not fallen below the first tier of the English football league system and hold the record for the longest uninterrupted period in the top flight.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  The club remained in the Football League until 1992, when its First Division was superseded as English football's top level by the newly formed Premier League, of which they were an inaugural member.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal F.C. league record by opponent
Content:  In 2003–04, Arsenal completed a league season without a single defeat, something achieved only once before in English football, by Preston North End in 1888–89.

Title: Mary Poppins (character)
Content: Mary Poppins is a fictional character and the eponymous protagonist of P. L. Travers' "Mary Poppins" books and all of their adaptations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (character)
Content:  A magical English nanny, she blows in on the East Wind and arrives at the Banks home at Number Seventeen Cherry Tree Lane, London, where she is given charge of the Banks children and teaches them valuable lessons with a magical touch.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (character)
Content:  Travers gives Poppins the accent and vocabulary of a real London nanny: cockney base notes overlaid with a strangled gentility.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (film)
Content: Mary Poppins is a 1964 American musical-fantasy film directed by Robert Stevenson and produced by Walt Disney, with songs written and composed by the Sherman Brothers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (film)
Content:  The screenplay is by Bill Walsh and Don DaGradi, loosely based on P. L. Travers' book series "Mary Poppins".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (film)
Content:  The film, which combines live-action and animation, stars Julie Andrews in the role of Mary Poppins who visits a dysfunctional family in London and employs her unique brand of lifestyle to improve the family's dynamic.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (film)
Content:  Dick Van Dyke, David Tomlinson, and Glynis Johns are featured in supporting roles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mary Poppins (film)
Content:  The film was shot entirely at the Walt Disney Studios in Burbank, California using painted London background scenes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Monsters, Inc.
Content:  Featuring the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi, James Coburn, and Jennifer Tilly, the film was directed by Pete Docter in his directorial debut, and executive produced by John Lasseter and Andrew Stanton.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Monsters, Inc.
Content:  The film centers on two monsters employed at the titular energy-producing factory Monsters, Inc. — top scarer James P. "Sulley" Sullivan and his one-eyed partner and best friend Mike Wazowski.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Monsters, Inc.
Content:  In the film, employees at Monsters, Inc. generate their city's power by scaring children, but they themselves are afraid that the children are toxic to them, and when one child enters the factory, Sulley and Mike must return her home before it is too late.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  It was originally sung by Dick Van Dyke and Julie Andrews, and also is featured in the Cameron Mackintosh/Disney "Mary Poppins" musical.

session_0.text:

Content:  The song can be heard in the "Mary Poppins" scene of The Great Movie Ride at Disney's Hollywood Studios and during the "Mary Poppins" segment of "" at Disneyland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content: "I Love to Laugh", also called "We Love to Laugh", is a song from Walt Disney's film "Mary Poppins".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  It was composed by Richard M. Sherman and Robert B. Sherman.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  The song is sung in the film by "Uncle Albert" (Ed Wynn), and "Bert" (Dick Van Dyke) as they levitate uncontrollably toward the ceiling, eventually joined by Mary Poppins (Julie Andrews) herself.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  The premise of the scene, that laughter and happiness cause Uncle Albert (and like-minded visitors) to float into the air, can be seen as a metaphor for the way laughter can "lighten" a mood.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  (Compare Peter Pan's flight power, which is also powered by happy thoughts.)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  Conversely, thinking of something sad literally brings Albert and his visitors "down to earth" again.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: I Love to Laugh
Content:  The song states a case strongly in favor of laughter, even if Mary Poppins appears to disapprove of Uncle Albert's behavior, especially since it not only complicates the task of getting Albert down, but the infectious mood sends Bert and the Banks children into the air as well.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  It was published in 1943 by Harcourt, Brace & World, Inc and illustrated by Mary Shepard and Agnes Sims.

Content:  The film stars Emily Blunt, Lin-Manuel Miranda, Ben Whishaw, Emily Mortimer, Pixie Davies, Joel Dawson, Nathanael Saleh, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Dick Van Dyke and Meryl Streep.

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  The film is scheduled for release on December 25, 2018, giving it one of the longest gaps between film sequels in history.

Title: Step in Time
Content: "Step In Time" is a song and dance number from Walt Disney's 1964 film "Mary Poppins", and it is composed by Richard M. Sherman and Robert B. Sherman.

Title: Step in Time
Content:  The choreography for this song was provided by Marc Breaux and Dee Dee Wood.

Title: Step in Time

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Step in Time
Content:  In the first part of the song, the lines he says in the verses are "Kick your knees up", "'Round the chimney", "Flap like a birdie", "Up on the railing", "Over the rooftops" and "Link your elbows" followed by an interlude.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Step in Time
Content:  The interlude continues with Bert, Mary Poppins, Michael, Jane and all the chimney sweepers dancing around the rooftops and as Admiral Boom looks at them with the telescope, he thinks that they're Hottentots, so he orders Mr. Binnacle to make them scram with colorful fireworks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  In the second part, as all the chimney sweepers get in the house of George Banks, Mrs. Brill walks into the living room looking at them and screams, "They're at it again!"
