Title: Moses Rischin
Content: Moses Rischin (born 1925) is an American Jewish historian, author, lecturer, editor, and emeritus professor of history at San Francisco State University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  He coined the phrase new Mormon history in a 1969 article of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  Rischin is from New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  His undergraduate studies were at Brooklyn College.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  Harvard University awarded him a PhD in 1957.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  In addition to his professorship, he sits on the board for the "Journal of American Ethnic History" and on the council of the American Jewish History Society.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  There is an annual lecture given in his honor at the Western Jewish History Center, where he is director.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Moses Rischin
Content:  There is a collection of historical essays published in Rischin's honor, and a supporting character, Meyer Meyer in the 87th Precinct series, may have been partly modeled after him.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arnold Richards
Content: Arnold Richards, M.D., is a psychoanalyst and former editor of "The American Psychoanalyst" and "Journal of the American Psychoanalytic Association (JAPA)".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arnold Richards
Content:  Richards also is the Training and Supervising Analyst at the New York Psychoanalytic Institute.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arnold Richards
Content:  He is the founder and Editor-in-Chief of the International Psychoanalysis.net magazine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arnold Richards
Content:  Richards is a board member and former chair of YIVO.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Gershon Galil
Content: Gershon Galil is Professor of Biblical Studies and Ancient History and former chair of the Department of Jewish History at the University of Haifa, Mount Carmel, Haifa, Israel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeffrey S. Gurock
Content: Jeffrey S. Gurock is Libby M. Klaperman Professor of Jewish History at Yeshiva University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeffrey S. Gurock
Content:  He has written over a dozen books in the field of American Jewish history and also served as associate editor to "American Jewish History", the most important journal in that field, from 1982 to 2002.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeffrey S. Gurock
Content:  His work focuses on the American Orthodox community and the variations in Orthodox practice and ritual over the course of American Jewish history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jeffrey S. Gurock
Content:  His books include "Orthodox Jews in America" (Indiana University Press, 2009), a comprehensive social and cultural history of this group and its relations to other Jews and mainstream American society, and "Jews in Gotham" (New York University Press, 2012), which chronicles New York Jewry from 1920 to 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: YIVO
Content: YIVO (Yiddish: ייִוואָ , ] ), established in 1925 in Wilno in the Second Polish Republic (now Vilnius, Lithuania) as the Yidisher Visnshaftlekher Institut (Yiddish: ייִדישער װיסנשאַפֿטלעכער אינסטיטוט , ] , Yiddish Scientific Institute), is an organization that preserves, studies, and teaches the cultural history of Jewish life throughout Eastern Europe, Germany and Russia, as well as orthography, lexicography, and other studies related to Yiddish.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: YIVO
Content:  (The word "yidisher" means both "Yiddish" and "Jewish".)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: YIVO
Content:  The English name of the organization was changed to the Institute for Jewish Research subsequent to its relocation to New York City, although it is still primarily known by its Yiddish acronym.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: YIVO
Content:  YIVO is now a member of the Center for Jewish History.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: YIVO
Content:  It serves as the "de facto" recognized language regulator of the Yiddish language.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David N. Myers
Content: David N. Myers (born 1960) is the President & CEO of the Center for Jewish History in New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David N. Myers
Content:  He is also a professor of history at the University of California, Los Angeles, where he holds the Sady and Ludwig Kahn Chair in Jewish History.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: David N. Myers
Content:  His research focuses on modern Jewish intellectual and cultural history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content: The Center for Jewish History is a partnership of five Jewish history, scholarship, and art organizations in New York City: American Jewish Historical Society, American Sephardi Federation, Leo Baeck Institute New York, Yeshiva University Museum, and YIVO Institute for Jewish Research.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content:  Together, housed in one location, the partners have separate governing bodies and finances, but collocate resources.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content:  The partners' collections make up the biggest repository of Jewish history in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content:  The Center for Jewish History serves as a centralized place of scholarly research, events, exhibitions, and performances.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content:  Located within the Center are the Lillian Goldman Reading Room, Ackman & Ziff Family Genealogy Institute and a Collection Management & Conservation Wing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Center for Jewish History
Content:  The Center for Jewish History is also an affiliate of the Smithsonian Institution.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carole B. Balin
Content: Carole Beth Balin (born 1964) is a Reform Jewish rabbi and professor of Jewish history at Hebrew Union College-Jewish Institute of Religion in New York City.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carole B. Balin
Content:  Her research interests include Eastern European and American Jewish history, the history of Reform Judaism, and gender studies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carole B. Balin
Content:  She received laudatory reviews for her 2003 book "To Reveal Our Hearts: Jewish Women Writers in Tsarist Russia", and has co-edited two other books.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Carole B. Balin
Content:  She is a co-curator of "Bat Mitzvah Comes of Age", a traveling exhibition sponsored by the Smithsonian-affiliated National Museum of American Jewish History and the Moving Traditions Jewish non-profit.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hikmet Tanyu
Content: Prof. Dr. Hikmet Tanyu (1918 – 1992), from Turkey, was a scientist and college professor of philosophy and history of religions who specialized in Jewish religious history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hikmet Tanyu
Content:  He studied in Israel in the 1970s and wrote a book titled "Jews and Turks throughout History" which examines Jewish history and relations between Jewish and Turkish societies through history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hikmet Tanyu
Content:  This book is considered as the first serious approach to the Jewish history in Turkey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content: Israel Bartal (born October 22, 1946 in Tel Aviv, Israel), is Avraham Harman Professor of Jewish History, and the former Dean of the Faculty of Humanities at Hebrew University (2006–2010).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content:  Since 2006 he is the chair of the Historical Society of Israel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content:  He served as director of the Center for Research on the History and Culture of Polish Jewry, and the academic chairman of the Project of Jewish Studies in Russian at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content:  Professor Bartal was the co-director of the Center for Jewish Studies and Civilization at Moscow State University.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content:  Bartal received his PhD from Hebrew University in 1981.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Israel Bartal
Content:  He focuses his research on the history of the Jews in Palestine, the Jews of Eastern Europe, the Haskalah Movement, Jewish Orthodoxy and modern Jewish historiography.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content: Final Symphony II is a symphonic concert tour first held at the Beethovenhalle in Bonn, Germany on August 29, 2015 and continuing to date.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  The concert tour features arrangements of video game music selected from the "Final Fantasy" series, specifically "Final Fantasy V", "VIII", "IX", and "XIII".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  It is divided into four acts, one per game, with the newest game, "Final Fantasy XIII", first, and the oldest, "V", last; all four arrangements are single-section arrangements, with the "IX" portion as a piano concerto.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  The tour is a follow up to "Final Symphony", a similar tour of orchestral arrangement performances from "Final Fantasy VI", "VII", and "X" beginning in 2013 and continuing to date.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  The concert is produced and directed by Thomas Böcker of Merregnon Studios, with arrangements provided by Finnish composer and musician Jonne Valtonen, along with Roger Wanamo and "Final Fantasy XIII" composer Masashi Hamauzu.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  The original works were composed by Nobuo Uematsu and Hamauzu, and an introductory piece was composed by Valtonen.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Final Symphony II
Content:  The premiere concert was performed by the Beethoven Orchestra Bonn under conduction from Eckehard Stier, with guest performer Mischa Cheung joining the orchestra on piano.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thomas Erpingham
Content: Sir Thomas Erpingham KG (  1355 –1428) was an English knight who became famous as the commander of King Henry V's longbow wielding archers at the Battle of Agincourt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thomas Erpingham
Content:  He was immortalised as a character in the play "Henry V" by William Shakespeare.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thomas Erpingham
Content:  It is, however, his lengthy and loyal service to John of Gaunt, Henry IV and Henry V, which contributed significantly to the establishment of the House of Lancaster upon the English throne, that is his true legacy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amoroso (album)
Content: Amoroso (] ), released in 1976, is an album that uses an orchestral arrangement to produce the Brazilian sound of bossa nova.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amoroso (album)
Content:  The album features João Gilberto on vocals and guitar, backed by a large, but not overpowering, arrangement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriad
Content: Henriad is a common title used by scholars for Shakespeare's second historical tetralogy, comprising "Richard II"; "Henry IV, Part 1"; "Henry IV, Part 2"; and "Henry V".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriad
Content:  The plays depict the destabilising effects of the violation of political continuity with the overthrow of Richard II of England followed by the growth of Henry V of England from a wild youth to a great war leader in "Henry V".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriad
Content:  Although it was the second tetralogy to be written and performed, the subject matter comes chronologically before the first tetralogy comprising the three "Henry VI" plays and "Richard III".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henriad
Content:  The term "Henriad" derives from the Classical epics the "Iliad" and "Aeneid".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Synthesis (Evanescence album)
Content: Synthesis is the upcoming fourth studio album by American rock band Evanescence.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Synthesis (Evanescence album)
Content:  It is set to be released on November 10, 2017, by BMG Rights Management.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Synthesis (Evanescence album)
Content:  The album includes reworked versions of the band's previous material with an orchestral arrangement and electronica music elements, in addition to two new songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Synthesis (Evanescence album)
Content:  David Campbell was responsible for the arrangement, along with Amy Lee and producer William B. Hunt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Synthesis (Evanescence album)
Content:  In order to promote the new material, Evanescence will embark on the Synthesis Tour, which begins on October 14, 2017, in Las Vegas and visits cities across the US and Canada until December.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The National Anthem (Benjamin Britten)
Content: The National Anthem is a 1962 choral and orchestral arrangement of "God Save the Queen" by Benjamin Britten.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The National Anthem (Benjamin Britten)
Content:  The arrangement has been described as an "extraordinary progression from pianissimo prayer to pealing, overlapping choral fortissimo".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Suite from Henry V
Content: Suite from Henry V is a 1963 orchestral arrangement of William Walton's musical score from the 1944 film "Henry V".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Suite from Henry V
Content:  The suite, arranged by Muir Mathieson, is in five movements, although the second and fourth movements had already appeared in string arrangement form in Walton's own Two Pieces for Strings from Henry V.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V (1944 film)
Content: Henry V is a 1944 British Technicolor film adaptation of William Shakespeare's play of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V (1944 film)
Content:  The on-screen title is The Chronicle History of King Henry the Fift with His Battell Fought at Agin Court in France (the title of the 1600 quarto edition of the play).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V (1944 film)
Content:  It stars Laurence Olivier, who also directed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V (1944 film)
Content:  The play was adapted for the screen by Olivier, Dallas Bower, and Alan Dent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V (1944 film)
Content:  The score is by William Walton.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V Events
Content: Henry V is a full-service communications agency specializing in live events and experience marketing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V Events
Content:  Founded in 1978 as National Meeting Company Inc., the company – now known as Henry V – is the Northwest's oldest and largest experiential marketing agency.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V Events
Content:  Henry V is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry V Events
Content:  The privately owned company has more than 30 full-time employees with a national network of more than 100 contract event professionals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: On the Hills of Manchuria
Content: "On the hills of Manchuria" (Russian: На сопках Маньчжурии, Na sopkah Manchzhurii ) is a haunting waltz (i.e. a waltz composed of mostly minor notes and sub-4th octave arrangements) composed in 1906 by Ilya Alekseevich Shatrov.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: On the Hills of Manchuria
Content:  The original and orchestral arrangement is written in E-flat minor while the folk arrangement is in F minor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Comcast Spectacor
Content: Comcast Sports Ventures, doing business as Comcast Spectacor, is a Philadelphia-based American sports and entertainment company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Comcast Spectacor
Content:  It owns the Philadelphia Flyers of the National Hockey League, the upcoming Portland ECHL team of the ECHL, the upcoming Philadelphia team of the National Lacrosse League, and formerly owned the Philadelphia 76ers of the National Basketball Association.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Comcast Spectacor
Content:  The company owns and manages the Wells Fargo Center and formerly managed the Spectrum in South Philadelphia (before that arena was demolished), plus several community skating rinks in the Philadelphia region known as Flyers Skate Zone.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Comcast Spectacor
Content:  The Comcast SportsNet (CSN) regional sports networks were also owned by Comcast Spectacor prior to parent company Comcast's acquisition of a majority stake in NBCUniversal in January 2011; CSN is now operated through NBC Sports.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Venetian Macao
Content: The Venetian Macao () is a luxury hotel and casino resort in Macau owned by the American Las Vegas Sands company.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Venetian Macao
Content:  The Venetian is a 39-story, casino hotel on the Cotai Strip in Macau.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Venetian Macao
Content:  The 10500000 sqft Venetian Macao is modeled on its sister casino resort The Venetian Las Vegas, and is the seventh-largest building in the world by floor area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Venetian Macao
Content:  The Venetian Macao is also the largest casino in the world, and the largest single structure hotel building in Asia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: Thompson Creek Metals Company Inc. is a full cycle mining company with acquisition, exploration, development, and operation in North America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: </small> The corporate office is located in Denver, Colorado.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content:  The company primarily produces copper, gold, and molybdenum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: </small> In the past several years, the Company has evolved from being a major primary molybdenum producer to becoming a copper and gold mining company with the construction and development of the Mount Milligan (open-pit copper-gold) mine and concentrator in British Columbia, Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content:  Mount Milligan is Thompson Creek Metals principal operation and the company owns 100% of this property.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: </small> The company also owns 100% of its Thompson Creek Mine (open-pit molybdenum and concentrator) in Idaho.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content:  Thompson Creek Metals owns 75% joint venture interest in two other properties, including its Endako Mine (open-pit molybdenum, concentrator and roaster) in British Columbia, and its Langeloth Metallurgical Facility (roaster) in Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: </small> Thompson Creek Metals has additional development projects, including the Berg property in British Columbia (exploration of copper, molybdenum, and silver).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Thompson Creek Metals
Content: </small> </small>

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cotai Arena
Content: The Cotai Arena is an indoor arena, located on the premises of The Venetian Macao, on the Cotai Strip, in Macau, China.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cotai Arena
Content:  It opened in 2007 with a seating capacity of 15,000.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cotai Arena
Content:  The arena was known as Venetian Arena from 2007 to 2010, when it was renamed as "CotaiArena".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Cotai Arena
Content:  It hosts sporting events such as basketball, tennis and boxing, as well as concerts and international televised awards shows.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Istad
Content: Istad AS is a power company based in the town of Molde in Møre og Romsdal county, Norway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Istad
Content:  The company owns the power grid in the municipalities of Aukra, Eide, Fræna, Gjemnes, Midsund, and Molde.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Istad
Content:  It also operates a fiberoptic broadband in Molde.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Istad
Content:  The company owns a 25% stake in the power plants on the river Driva.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Murray Premises
Content: The Murray Premises is a National Historic Site of Canada located in downtown St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Murray Premises
Content:  The Murray Premises was renovated in 1979 and now contains office suites, restaurants, retail stores and, most recently, a boutique hotel.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Murray Premises
Content:  The buildings once served as a fishery premises, with facilities for drying and packaging fish and warehouses for fish, barrels and other items.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Murray Premises
Content:  The oldest of the buildings is the one facing on Beck’s Cove.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Murray Premises
Content:  It was built after the 1846 fire and for a time served as both shop and house.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Choice Hotels
Content: Choice Hotels International, Inc. is a hospitality holding corporation based in Rockville, Maryland, in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Choice Hotels
Content:  The company owns the hotel and motel brands Comfort Inn, Comfort Suites, Quality Inn, Sleep Inn, Clarion, Cambria Hotel & Suites, Mainstay Suites, Suburban Extended Stay, Econo Lodge, Rodeway Inn, and Ascend Hotel Collection.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Choice Hotels
Content:  The company manages 6,379 properties worldwide.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Choice Hotels
Content:  There were 505,278 rooms, with approximately $45.80 in revenue per room, totaling $758 million in revenue as of April 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DC Thomson
Content: DC Thomson is a British publishing and television production company best known for producing "The Dundee Courier", "The Evening Telegraph", "The Sunday Post", "Oor Wullie", "The Broons", "The Beano", "The Dandy", and "Commando" comics.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DC Thomson
Content:  It also owns Parragon and the Aberdeen Journals Group which publishes the "Press and Journal".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DC Thomson
Content:  It was a significant shareholder in the former ITV company Southern Television.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DC Thomson
Content:  Through its subsidiary DC Thomson Family History the company owns several websites including Friends Reunited and Findmypast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: DC Thomson
Content:  Based in Dundee, Scotland, the company also owns children books publisher Parragon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wetherspoons
Content: J D Wetherspoon plc, branded as Wetherspoon's, is a pub company in the United Kingdom and the Republic of Ireland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wetherspoons
Content:  Founded in 1979 by Tim Martin, the company owns just under 1,000 outlets, including the chain of Lloyds No.1 bars and the chain of Wetherspoon hotels.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wetherspoons
Content:  With its headquarters in Watford, Wetherspoons is known for converting unconventional premises into pubs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Wetherspoons
Content:  The company is listed on the London Stock Exchange and is a constituent of the FTSE 250 Index.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content: The Sunfeast Open was an annual WTA Tour tennis tournament that was started in Kolkata in 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content:  The event was a Tier III-tournament with a prize money of USD 175,000 and was played on indoors greenset.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content:  The fourth edition of the  WTA Sunfeast Open, slated to be held in Kolkata from October 6–12, 2008, was shifted to Mumbai as the dates clash with the Durga Puja.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content:  The 2008 tournament in Mumbai, was to be held outdoors in the premises of Cricket Club of India, unlike the previous three editions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content:  ITC, an Indian conglomerate based out of Kolkata was the primary sponsor of the event.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sunfeast Open
Content:  The company owns the Sunfeast brand of biscuits.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pulham Market
Content: Pulham Market and its sister village Pulham St Mary are situated approximately 9 miles (14.5 kilometres) north of Diss in Norfolk, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pulham Market
Content:  It covers an area of 12.08 km2 and had a population of 999 in 443 households as of the 2001 census, the population falling to 977 at the 2011 Census.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor (VC)
Content: John MacGregor VC MC & Bar DCM ED (1 February 1889 – 9 June 1952) was a Scottish-Canadian soldier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor (VC)
Content:  MacGregor was a recipient of the Victoria Cross, the highest and most prestigious award for gallantry in the face of the enemy that can be awarded to British and Commonwealth forces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor (VC)
Content:  MacGregor served in the Canadian army in both world wars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews Boat Club
Content: The University of St Andrews Boat Club, founded in 1965, is the rowing team affiliated to the University of St Andrews.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews Boat Club
Content:  Operating under the University of St Andrews Athletic Union, the University of St Andrews Boat Club (also known as UStABC) competes in head races and regattas across Scotland and England, including the Head of the River Race (London), British University Championships 'BUCS' Regatta and Henley Royal Regatta.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews Boat Club
Content:  Its national governing body is Scottish Rowing and the registration code of 'SAU'.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Prior of St Andrews
Content: The Prior of St Andrews was the head of the property and community of Augustinian canons of St Andrews Cathedral Priory, St Andrews, Fife, Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Prior of St Andrews
Content:  It was established by King David I in 1140 with canons from Nostell Priory, West Yorkshire.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Prior of St Andrews
Content:  It is possible that, initially at least, the prior of St Andrews was subordinate to the bishop as abbot, but by the 13th century the canons of St Andrews were given freedom by the bishop to elect their prior.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Prior of St Andrews
Content:  By the end of the 13th century, the abbacy of the native canons (i.e. the "Céli Dé", or Culdees) was no longer there to challenge the position of the priory, and the native canons themselves had been formed into a collegiate church.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor, Baron MacGregor of Pulham Market
Content: John Roddick Russell MacGregor, Baron MacGregor of Pulham Market, OBE PC FKC (born 14 February 1937), is a politician in the United Kingdom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor, Baron MacGregor of Pulham Market
Content:  He was educated at Merchiston Castle School, then at the University of St Andrews (MA economics and history, 1959) and at King's College London (LLB, 1962).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor, Baron MacGregor of Pulham Market
Content:  Prior to the 1979 general election he worked for Hill Samuel, a merchant bank.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Andrews Links
Content: St Andrews Links in the town of St Andrews, Fife, Scotland, is regarded as the "Home of Golf".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Andrews Links
Content:  It has one of the oldest courses in the world, where the game has been played since the 15th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Andrews Links
Content:  Today there are seven public golf courses; the Balgove, Eden, Jubilee, Strathtyrum, New, the Old Course (which is widely considered one of the finest, and certainly the most famous and traditional course in the world), and The Castle Course, sited on the cliffs a mile to the east of St Andrews and designed by the architect David McLay Kidd, which opened in June 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Andrews Links
Content:  The courses of St Andrews Links are owned by the local authorities and operated by St Andrews Links Trust, a charitable organization.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Andrews Links
Content:  St Andrews is also home to the Royal and Ancient Golf Club of St Andrews, one of the most prestigious golf clubs and until 2004 one of the two rulemaking authorities of golf (in that year, the Royal and Ancient Club passed on its rulemaking authority to an offshoot organisation, The R&A).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews
Content: The University of St Andrews (informally known as St Andrews University or simply St Andrews; abbreviated as St And, from the Latin "Sancti Andreae", in post-nominals) is a British public research university in St Andrews, Fife, Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews
Content:  It is the oldest of the four ancient universities of Scotland and the third oldest university in the English-speaking world (following Oxford and Cambridge Universities).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: University of St Andrews
Content:  St Andrews was founded between 1410 and 1413, when the Avignon Antipope Benedict XIII issued a papal bull to a small founding group of Augustinian clergy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Archdeaconry of St Andrews
Content: The Archdeaconry of St Andrews was a sub-division of the diocese of St Andrews, one of two archdeaconries within the diocese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Archdeaconry of St Andrews
Content:  The St Andrews archdeaconry was headed by the Archdeacon of St Andrews, a subordinate of the Bishop of St Andrews.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Archdeaconry of St Andrews
Content:  In the medieval period, the Archdeaconry of St Andrews contained five deaneries with a total of 124 parish churches.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Archdeaconry of St Andrews
Content:  The deaneries were Mearns (14 churches), Angus (38 churches), Gowrie (20 churches), Fife (28 churches) and Fothriff (24 churches).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John MacGregor
Content: John MacGregor, John Macgregor or John McGregor may refer to:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King's College London
Content: King's College London (informally King's or KCL) is a public research university located in London, United Kingdom, and a founding constituent college of the federal University of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King's College London
Content:  King's was established in 1829 by King George IV and the Duke of Wellington, when it received its first royal charter (as a college), and claims to be the fourth oldest university in England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King's College London
Content:  In 1836, King's became one of the two founding colleges of the University of London.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: King's College London
Content:  In the late 20th century, King's grew through a series of mergers, including with Queen Elizabeth College and Chelsea College of Science and Technology (in 1985), the Institute of Psychiatry (in 1997), the United Medical and Dental Schools of Guy's and St Thomas' Hospitals and the Florence Nightingale School of Nursing and Midwifery (in 1998).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content: Florida (Spanish for "land of flowers") is a state in the southeastern region of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content:  It is bordered to the west by the Gulf of Mexico and Alabama, to the north by Alabama and Georgia, to the east by the Atlantic Ocean, and to the south by the Straits of Florida.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content:  Florida is the 22nd-most extensive, the 3rd-most populous, and the 8th-most densely populated of the U.S. states.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content:  Jacksonville is the most populous municipality in the state, and is legally the largest city by area in the contiguous United States (due to the consolidation of Jacksonville with Duval County).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content:  The Miami metropolitan area is Florida's most populous urban area.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Florida
Content:  The city of Tallahassee is the state capital.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content: Tennessee ( ; Cherokee: ᏔᎾᏏ , "Tanasi " ) is a state located in the southeastern region of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content:  Tennessee is the 36th largest and the 16th most populous of the 50 United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content:  Tennessee is bordered by Kentucky and Virginia to the north, North Carolina to the east, Georgia, Alabama, and Mississippi to the south, and Arkansas and Missouri to the west.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content:  The Appalachian Mountains dominate the eastern part of the state, and the Mississippi River forms the state's western border.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content:  Nashville is the state's capital and largest city, with a population of 660,388.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tennessee
Content:  Tennessee's second largest city is Memphis, which has a population of 652,717.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content: The Charlotte metropolitan area (also Metrolina, Charlotte Metro, or Charlotte USA) is a metropolitan area/region of North and South Carolina within and surrounding the city of Charlotte.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content:  Located in the Piedmont of the Southeastern United States, the Charlotte metropolitan area is well known for its auto racing history (especially NASCAR).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content:  The region is headquarters to 8 Fortune 500 and 7 Fortune 1000 companies including Bank of America, Duke Energy, Sealed Air Corporation, Nucor Steel, and Lowe's Home Improvement Stores.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content:  Additional headquarters include Harris Teeter, Food Lion, Cheerwine and Sundrop.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content:  It is home to one of the world's busiest airports , Charlotte Douglas International Airport, and is also the Carolinas' largest manufacturing region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charlotte metropolitan area
Content:  The Charlotte MSA is the largest in the Carolinas, and the fourth largest metropolitan area in the Southeastern region of the United States behind, Miami, Atlanta, and Tampa.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Bentonville
Content: Downtown Bentonville is the historic business district of Bentonville, Arkansas.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Bentonville
Content:  The region is the location of Walmart Home Office; city and county government facilities; and most of Bentonville's tourist attractions for the city and contains many historically and architecturally significant properties.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Bentonville
Content:  Downtown measures approximately 1.5 sqmi and is defined as the region between Tiger Boulevard to the north, Highway 102 (AR 102) to the south, Walton Boulevard (U.S. Route 71B) to the west and J Street to the east.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Bentonville
Content:  Similar to other central business districts in the US, Downtown has recently undergone a transformation that included the construction of new condos and lofts, renovation of historic buildings, and arrival of new residents and businesses.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Downtown Bentonville
Content:  Upon opening of Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art the increased tourist traffic related to the museum has made Downtown Bentonville one of the state's most popular tourism destinations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coffeyville, Kansas
Content: Coffeyville is a city in southeastern Montgomery County, Kansas, United States, located along the Verdigris River in the state's southeastern region.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coffeyville, Kansas
Content:  As of the 2010 census, the city population was 10,295.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coffeyville, Kansas
Content:  It is the most populous city of Montgomery County and with its southeast Kansas location is located in the Tulsa, Oklahoma media market.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Coffeyville, Kansas
Content:  The town of South Coffeyville, Oklahoma is located approximately 1 mile south of the city, existing as a separate political entity immediately south of the state line.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport (IATA: ATL, ICAO: KATL, FAA LID: ATL) , also known as Atlanta Airport, Hartsfield, or Hartsfield–Jackson, is an international airport located 7 mi south of Atlanta's central business district, in the U.S. state of Georgia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content:  It has been the world's busiest airport by passenger traffic since 1998; and by number of landings and take-offs from 2005 to 2013, losing that title to Chicago-O'Hare in 2014, but regaining it a year later.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content:  Hartsfield–Jackson held its ranking as the world's busiest airport in 2012, both in passengers and number of flights, by accommodating 100 million passengers (more than 260,000 passengers daily) and 950,119 flights.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content:  Many of the nearly one million flights are domestic flights from within the United States, where the airport serves as a major hub for travel throughout the southeastern region of the country.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content:  The airport has 207 domestic and international gates.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport
Content:  ATL covers 4,700 acres (1,902 ha) of land.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Uruguay
Content: Uruguay ( ; ] ), officially the Oriental Republic of Uruguay (Spanish: "República Oriental del Uruguay" ), is a sovereign state in the southeastern region of South America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Uruguay
Content:  It borders Argentina to its west and Brazil to its north and east, with the Río de la Plata (River of Silver) to the south and the Atlantic Ocean to the southeast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Uruguay
Content:  Uruguay is home to an estimated /1e6 round 2 million people, of whom 1.8 million live in the metropolitan area of its capital and largest city, Montevideo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Uruguay
Content:  With an area of approximately 176000 km2 , Uruguay is geographically the second-smallest nation in South America, after Suriname.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate 71
Content: Interstate 71 (I-71) is a north-south (physically northeast-southwest) Interstate Highway in the Great Lakes/Midwestern and Southeastern region of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate 71
Content:  Its southern terminus is at an interchange with Interstate 64 and Interstate 65 (the Kennedy Interchange) in Louisville, Kentucky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate 71
Content:  Its northern terminus is at an interchange with Interstate 90 in Cleveland, Ohio.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate 71
Content:  I-71 runs concurrently with Interstate 75 from a point about 20 mi south of Cincinnati, Ohio into downtown Cincinnati.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Interstate 71
Content:  Almost three quarters of the route lies east of I-75, thereby putting it out of its proper place in the Interstate grid.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content: The Alabama Theatre is a movie palace in Birmingham, Alabama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  It was built in 1927 by Paramount's Publix Theatre chain as its flagship theater for the southeastern region of the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  Seating 2,500 people at the time, it was the largest in the Birmingham Theatre district.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  The district was once home to a myriad of large theaters that featured vaudeville, performing arts, nickelodeons, and large first-run movie palaces.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  The Alabama is the only district theater still operating today.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  Built to show silent films, the Alabama still features its original Wurlitzer theater organ.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Theatre
Content:  Other than the Alabama, the Lyric Theatre is the only theater still standing in the district.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arkansas
Content: Arkansas (pronounced ) is a state in the southeastern region of the United States, home to over 3 million people as of 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arkansas
Content:  Its name is of Siouan derivation from the language of the Osage denoting their related kin, the Quapaw Indians.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arkansas
Content:  The state's diverse geography ranges from the mountainous regions of the Ozark and the Ouachita Mountains, which make up the U.S. Interior Highlands, to the densely forested land in the south known as the Arkansas Timberlands, to the eastern lowlands along the Mississippi River and the Arkansas Delta.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Captain 11
Content: Captain 11 was a popular after-school children's program for over 41 years on KELO-TV, broadcast on channel 11 from Sioux Falls, South Dakota.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Captain 11
Content:  Station weatherman Dave Dedrick donned a yellow-trimmed blue pilot uniform with hat and daily (later weekly as cable TV began to cut into its ratings) became the jolly host of the show.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Captain 11
Content:  The show primarily played cartoons and other children's fare, and featured the Captain having all the kids (his "crew") introduce themselves on the air, guessing to win the toy chest, and children with birthdays working the Captain's control panel of lights and switches.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Captain 11
Content:  The show ended with the Captain playing the "freezeberg" game with the children, having them: Face the camera and wave one hand: then wave both hands: followed by wave both hands and one foot then wave both hands and both feet which usually resulted in most of the kids jumping up and down while facing the camera.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Captain 11
Content:  Finally, the Captain would tell them to "freeze", not moving a muscle as the camera panned over the crowd of kids attempting to remain still.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave Two
Content: The Search for Everything: Wave Two (also shortened as Wave Two) is an extended play (EP) by American singer-songwriter John Mayer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave Two
Content:  Released on February 24, 2017 by Columbia and Sony Music, the EP contains four tracks from Mayer's seventh studio album, "The Search for Everything", and is a follow-up to its predecessor EP, "".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave Two
Content:  It includes the lead single "Still Feel Like Your Man".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: People's Music School
Content: Founded in 1976, The People's Music School offers free group classes and private lessons to children and adults, regardless of their financial resources.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: People's Music School
Content:  People's Music School is located in Uptown, Chicago at 931 W. Eastwood Avenue.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: People's Music School
Content:  The School today offers instruction in 13 instruments (including woodwind, piano, percussion, and string instruments), as well as voice and theory classes to more than 350 students each year.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: People's Music School
Content:  In exchange for free music education, parents of the students volunteer at the school for two hours a month.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: People's Music School
Content:  The People's Music School also presents free concert performances to showcase the talents of its students and to give back to the neighborhood.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Music School
Content: The Brooklyn Music School is a community school for the performing arts in the Fort Greene neighborhood of Brooklyn, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Music School
Content:  The school was founded in 1912 as the Brooklyn Music School Settlement.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Music School
Content:  It owns and operates a four-story building located at 126 St. Felix St. that contains twenty-four classrooms, three dance studios, and a 266-seat Spanish Style theatre.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Brooklyn Music School
Content:  The Brooklyn Music School is a long-standing member of the National Guild of Community Schools of the Arts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: City of Edinburgh Music School
Content: The City of Edinburgh Music School is a state-maintained music school in Scotland in Edinburgh, Scotland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: City of Edinburgh Music School
Content:  Founded as the Lothian Specialist Music School in 1980, it changed its name in 1996 when Lothian Regional Council was dissolved into four separate unitary councils.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: City of Edinburgh Music School
Content:  It is a non-residential school, and because it is funded by the City of Edinburgh Council, it charges no fees.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: City of Edinburgh Music School
Content:  Unlike residential music schools, pupils attend comprehensive schools for their academic classes: Flora Stevenson Primary and Broughton High School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content: John Clayton Mayer ( ; born October 16, 1977) is an American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and producer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  He was born in Bridgeport, Connecticut, and raised in nearby Fairfield.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  He attended Berklee College of Music in Boston, but disenrolled and moved to Atlanta in 1997 with Clay Cook.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  Together, they formed a short-lived two-man band called Lo-Fi Masters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  After their split, Mayer continued to play local clubs—refining his skills and gaining a following.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  After his appearance at the 2001 South by Southwest Festival, he was signed to Aware Records, and then Columbia Records, which released his first EP, "Inside Wants Out".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  His following two full-length albums—"Room for Squares" (2001) and "Heavier Things" (2003)—did well commercially, achieving multi-platinum status.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Mayer
Content:  In 2003, he won the Grammy Award for Best Male Pop Vocal Performance for the single "Your Body Is a Wonderland".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greenwich House Music School
Content: Greenwich House Music School is a community arts school located at 46 Barrow Street in New York City’s Greenwich Village.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greenwich House Music School
Content:  The School is a part of Greenwich House, an organization started in 1902 as part of the settlement movement providing arts education and social service programs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greenwich House Music School
Content:  Greenwich House Music School was started in 1905 by the Greenwich House founder, Mary Kingsbury Simkhovitch, as a place for immigrant children to learn music after school, and has grown into a community music, art and dance school for both children and adults.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greenwich House Music School
Content:  Today, approximately 40 faculty members teach a range of instruments including piano, strings, guitar, harp, percussion, woodwinds, brass and Suzuki Violin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greenwich House Music School
Content:  In addition to music, the school teaches early childhood classes in music and art, as well as ballet for children 3.5–18 years of age.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Mary's Music School
Content: St Mary's Music School is a music school in Scotland in Edinburgh, for boys and girls aged 9 to 19 and is also the Choir School of St Mary's Episcopal Cathedral.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Mary's Music School
Content:  The school, which is non-denominational, provides education for children with a special talent in music, and is Scotland's only full-time independent specialist music school.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: St Mary's Music School
Content:  In 2017 the school has 80 pupils from many different backgrounds and from all parts of Scotland, the rest of the UK and abroad - 49 day pupils and 31 boarders, plus one student on the school's Part-time Pathways to Specialism scheme.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sree Rama Varma Music School
Content: Sree Rama Varma Music School (മലയാളം: ) is a music school situated in Thrissur city in Kerala, India and it is the first music school in Kerala state.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sree Rama Varma Music School
Content:  The school is also known as S.R.V. Music School.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sree Rama Varma Music School
Content:  In 2016, the institution was upgraded as a music college with the name of S.R.V. (Sreerama Varma) Govt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sree Rama Varma Music School
Content:  College Of Music And Performing Arts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave One
Content: The Search for Everything: Wave One (also shortened as Wave One) is an extended play (EP) by American singer John Mayer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave One
Content:  Released on January 20, 2017, the EP contains the first four tracks from Mayer's seventh studio album, "The Search for Everything".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Search for Everything: Wave One
Content:  It includes the lead single "Love on the Weekend" and three new tracks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor's Mansion State Historic Park
Content: Governor's Mansion State Historic Park is the location of Historic Governor's Mansion of California, the official home of the Governor of California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor's Mansion State Historic Park
Content:  It housed thirteen governors and their families from 1903 to 1967 and began housing the 39th, and current, governor in 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor's Mansion State Historic Park
Content:  The mansion is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kay Ivey
Content: Kay Ellen Ivey (born October 15, 1944) is an American politician who is the 54th and current Governor of Alabama since April 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kay Ivey
Content:  Ivey, a member of the Republican Party, served as the 38th Alabama State Treasurer from 2003 to 2011, and later became the 30th Lieutenant Governor of Alabama; she was the first Republican woman elected in this state, serving from January 2011 until April 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kay Ivey
Content:  She assumed office as governor on April 10, 2017 following the resignation of two-term governor Robert Bentley, who left office after pleading guilty to criminal charges involving campaign finance violations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kay Ivey
Content:  Bentley was also facing impeachment following a sex scandal at the time of his resignation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Kay Ivey
Content:  Ivey is Alabama's second female governor, after Lurleen Wallace, who served from 1967 until 1968, and first female Republican governor.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Abia State
Content: The office of the Governor of Abia State is an elected position.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Abia State
Content:  The governor of Abia State is the chief executive of the state and its executive branch.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Abia State
Content:  Eight different people have served as governor of Abia State since the state was created on August 27, 1991.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Abia State
Content:  The current governor is Okezie Ikpeazu of the People's Democratic Party, in office since May 29, 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Governor's Mansion
Content: The Alabama Governor's Mansion is the official residence of the Governor of Alabama and the governor's family in Montgomery, the capital city of Alabama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Governor's Mansion
Content:  The current Governor of Alabama, Kay Ivey lives at the governor's mansion.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Governor's Mansion
Content:  The original governor's mansion for Alabama was occupied from 1911 until 1950, when the current mansion was acquired.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Alabama Governor's Mansion
Content:  The current mansion was added to the National Register of Historic Places on July 3, 1972.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content: The Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is the head of the executive branch of government in the Commonwealth of Kentucky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content:  Fifty-seven men and one woman have served as Governor of Kentucky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content:  The governor's term is four years in length; since 1992, incumbents have been able to seek re-election once before becoming ineligible for four years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content:  Throughout the state's history, four men have served two non-consecutive terms as governor, and two others have served two consecutive terms.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content:  Kentucky is one of only five U.S. states that hold gubernatorial elections in odd-numbered years immediately before the United States Presidential Election.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Kentucky
Content:  The current governor is Matt Bevin, who was first elected in 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content: Arsenal Hill is a neighborhood in Columbia, South Carolina.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content:  Arsenal Hill was one of the first residential neighborhoods in the city and was the site of an arsenal during the Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content:  The South Carolina Governor's Mansion is located in Arsenal Hill.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content:  The current Governor's mansion is the only building left of The Arsenal which was the sister school of The Citadel, The Military College of South Carolina, in Charleston.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content:  The remainder of The Arsenal's buildings were burned down by General William Sherman's troops during the Civil War.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Arsenal Hill (Columbia, South Carolina)
Content:  In recent years, new residential development has been created to take advantage of the areas views of the city and its proximity to Finlay Park.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Cepello
Content: Stephen Cepello (born June 29, 1949) is an American artist and a former professional wrestler.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Cepello
Content:  As a wrestler, he was best known by his ring names, Steve Strong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Cepello
Content:  After retiring from wrestling to focus on his art career, he was selected to paint the official Governor's Mansion and Minnesota State Capitol portraits of former wrestler and Governor of Minnesota Jesse Ventura.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Governors of North Dakota
Content: The Governor of North Dakota is the chief executive of the U.S. state of North Dakota.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Governors of North Dakota
Content:  The current Governor is Doug Burgum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Governors of North Dakota
Content:  The Governor has the power to sign or veto laws, and to call the Legislative Assembly into emergency session.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Governors of North Dakota
Content:  The Governor is also chairman of the North Dakota Industrial Commission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: List of Governors of North Dakota

Title: Governor of Minnesota
Content: The Governor of Minnesota is the chief executive of the U.S. state of Minnesota, leading the state's executive branch.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Minnesota
Content:  Forty different people have been governor of Minnesota, though historically there were also three governors of Minnesota Territory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Minnesota
Content:  Alexander Ramsey, the first territorial governor, also served as state governor several years later.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Minnesota
Content:  State governors are elected to office by popular vote, but territorial governors were appointed to the office by the United States president.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Governor of Minnesota
Content:  The current governor of Minnesota is Mark Dayton of the Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party (DFL).

Title: Bryanston School
Content: Bryanston School is a co-educational independent school for both day and boarding pupils, located next to the village of Bryanston, and near the town of Blandford Forum, in Dorset in South West England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bryanston School
Content:  It was founded in 1928.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bryanston School
Content:  It occupies a palatial country house designed and built in 1889–94 by Richard Norman Shaw, the champion of a renewed academic tradition, for Viscount Portman, the owner of large tracts in the West End of London, in the early version of neo-Georgian style that Sir Edwin Lutyens called "Wrenaissance", to replace an earlier house, and is set in 400 acres .

Title: The Coade Hall
Content: The Coade Hall is a brick-built theatre and concert hall at Bryanston School, near Blandford Forum in Dorset, England.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Coade Hall
Content:  It was opened on 27 May 1966 by the Duke of Edinburgh On the opening night, there was a concert with music by Brahms, Britten, and Mozart.

Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content: The MV "Arctic Sea" is a merchant vessel cargo ship, formerly registered in Malta that was reported as missing between late July and mid August 2009 en route from Finland to Algeria.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  On July 24, the "Arctic Sea", manned by a Russian crew and carrying a cargo of what was declared to consist solely of timber, was allegedly boarded by hijackers off the coast of Sweden.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  The incident was not immediately reported, and contact with the ship was apparently lost on, or after, July 30.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  The "Arctic Sea" did not arrive at its scheduled port in Algeria, and on August 14 the ship was located near Cape Verde instead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  On August 17 it was seized by the Russian Navy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  An investigation into the incident is underway amidst speculation regarding the ship's actual cargo, and allegations of a cover-up by Russian authorities.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: MV Arctic Sea
Content:  The "Arctic Sea" was towed into harbour in the Maltese capital of Valletta on October 29, 2009.

Title: Jamie Macoun
Content: Jamie Neil Macoun (born August 17, 1961) is a Canadian former professional ice hockey defenceman who played over 1,000 games in the National Hockey League (NHL) during a 17-year career.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jamie Macoun
Content:  An undrafted player, Macoun played three seasons of college hockey with the Ohio State Buckeyes before signing with the Calgary Flames in 1983.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jamie Macoun
Content:  Macoun was named to the NHL All-Rookie Team on defence in 1984 and, after missing 17 months due to injuries suffered in an automobile accident, was a member of Calgary's 1989 Stanley Cup championship team.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jamie Macoun
Content:  He was involved in one of the largest trades in NHL history, a ten-player deal that sent him to the Toronto Maple Leafs in 1992.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jamie Macoun
Content:  He remained in Toronto until traded to the Detroit Red Wings in 1998, with whom he won his second Stanley Cup.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jamie Macoun
Content:  His surname pronounced as "mah-tsohn".

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Stephen Sondheim
Content: Stephen Joshua Sondheim ( ; born March 22, 1930) is an American composer and lyricist known for more than a half-century of contributions to musical theatre.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Sondheim
Content:  Sondheim has received an Academy Award, eight Tony Awards (more than any other composer, including a Special Tony Award for Lifetime Achievement in the Theatre), eight Grammy Awards, a Pulitzer Prize, a Laurence Olivier Award, and a 2015 Presidential Medal of Freedom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Sondheim
Content:  He has been described by Frank Rich of "The New York Times" as "now the greatest and perhaps best-known artist in the American musical theater."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Sondheim
Content:  His best-known works as composer and lyricist include "A Funny Thing Happened on the Way to the Forum", "Company", "Follies", "A Little Night Music", "Pacific Overtures", "", "Merrily We Roll Along", "Sunday in the Park with George", "Into the Woods", "Assassins", and "Passion".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stephen Sondheim
Content:  He also wrote the lyrics for "West Side Story" and "Gypsy".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Special Tony Award
Content: The Special Tony Award category includes the Lifetime Achievement Award and Special Tony Award.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Special Tony Award
Content:  These are non-competitive honorary awards, and the titles have changed over the years.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Special Tony Award
Content:  The Tony Award for Lifetime Achievement in the Theatre is to "honor an individual for the body of his or her work."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Special Tony Award
Content:  (The Tony Award for Best Special Theatrical Event was a competitive award, given from 2001 to 2009.)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Special Tony Award
Content:  Another non-competitive Tony award is the Tony Honors for Excellence in Theatre, to "recognize the achievements of individuals and organizations that do not fit into any of the competitive categories."

Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content: Herbert Brown, better known by his stage name McGruff (also known as Herb McGruff) is an American Hip hop recording artist from Harlem, New York City, New York.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  Brown started rapping in his early teens.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  He embarked on his music career in the hip hop group Bronx Most Wanted, alongside rappers Jay Q and Tee U.B. Brown later became a member of the hip hop collective Children of the Corn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  As a member of Children of the Corn, Brown worked alongside Big L, Cam'ron, Bloodshed and Mase, all of whom would go on to have successful careers in the music industry.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  After the group disbanded without releasing any material, Brown made his first appearance on Big L's debut album, "Lifestylez ov da Poor & Dangerous" (1995), on the tracks "8 Iz Enuff" and "Dangerzone".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  Eventually, Brown signed a deal with popular hip hop label, Uptown Records and began recording his debut album, "Destined to Be".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  In early 1998, the album's lead single "Before We Start" became a minor hit, charting on several "Billboard" charts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  In the Summer of 1998, "Destined to Be" was released but failed to sell many copies, only peaking at 169 on the "Billboard" 200, and Brown was released from his contract.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  Brown would make appearances with Heavy D on his album, "Waterbed Hev" and the "Woo" soundtrack.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  In 2009, Brown appeared on Mase's mixtape "I Do the Impossible".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  In 2010, he was featured heavily on The Diplomats mixtape, "The D.I.P. Agenda."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: McGruff (rapper)
Content:  In 2014, DJ Kay Slay, enlisted Brown, along with Raekwon, Fat Joe, Ghostface Killah, Sheek Louch, N.O.R.E., Lil' Fame, Prodigy and Rell, for a song titled "90s Flow".
