Title: Chiddy Bang
Content:  Prior to its breakup, Chiddy Bang was an American hip hop duo consisting of Anamege, under the stage name Chiddy, and Noah Beresin, under the stage name Xaphoon Jones.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chiddy Bang
Content:  The duo was introduced by former band member Zachary Sewall in late 2008 while the two were studying at Drexel University, in Beresin's hometown of Philadelphia.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chiddy Bang
Content:  Anamege's sound is based on the fusion of hip hop and alternative using samples from artists such as Ellie Goulding, Radiohead, Sufjan Stevens, Passion Pit, MGMT, Matt and Kim, and Yelle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chiddy Bang
Content:  In 2013, Beresin left the group to focus on producing and Anamege became a solo artist, with Beresin playing the role as frequent collaborator.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Chiddy Bang
Content:  Since departing from the group, Beresin has changed his stage name to Noah Breakfast.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Young Dolph
Content: Adolph Thornton, Jr. (born August 11, 1985), better known by his stage name Young Dolph, is an American rapper.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Young Dolph
Content:  In February 2016, Dolph released his debut studio album, "King of Memphis", which peaked at number 49 on the "Billboard" 200 chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Young Dolph
Content:  He was featured on O.T. Genasis' hit single "Cut It", which peaked at number 35 on the "Billboard" Hot 100.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content: "Shoulder Lean" is a song by American hip hop recording artist Young Dro, released July 4, 2006 as his debut single and the lead single from his debut studio album "Best Thang Smokin'".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content:  The song features Dro's fellow American rapper and Grand Hustle label-boss T.I. on the hook.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content:  The production was handled by Grand Hustle in-house producer Cordale "Lil' C" Quinn.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content:  The song was a massive hit in the United States, reaching the Top 10 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and at number one on the Hot R&B/Hip Hop Songs chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content:  This was Young Dro's first and only Top 10 single and T.I.'s fourth Top 10 single.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shoulder Lean
Content:  The song received heavy airplay and was certified 2x Platinum by RIAA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nobody Does It Better (Nate Dogg song)
Content: "Nobody Does It Better" is a song by American hip hop and R&B recording artist Nate Dogg, featuring vocals from American Hip hop recording artist Warren G.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nobody Does It Better (Nate Dogg song)
Content:  It was released in June 1998 as the second single released from the studio album "G-Funk Classics, Vols.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nobody Does It Better (Nate Dogg song)
Content:  1 & 2" (1998).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nobody Does It Better (Nate Dogg song)
Content:  The song produced by Warren G.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Nobody Does It Better (Nate Dogg song)
Content:  The song samples and contains an interpolation from “Let's Get Closer” by Atlantic Starr.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content: The discography of Method Man, an American hip hop recording artist, consists of five studio albums (including one collaborative album) and 34 singles (including 16 as a featured artist).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content:  Method Man embarked on his music career in 1992, as a member of East Coast hip hop group Wu-Tang Clan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content:  After the Wu-Tang Clan released their highly acclaimed debut album "Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers)" (1993), Method Man would be the first member to release his solo debut album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content:  In November 1994, he released "Tical", under Def Jam Recordings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content:  His debut album "Tical", features his biggest hit single to date, "I'll Be There for You/You're All I Need to Get By", which features American R&B singer Mary J. Blige and peaked at number three on the US "Billboard" Hot 100.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Method Man discography
Content:  Method Man would then go on to collaborate with fellow East Coast rapper Redman, and subsequently form a duo together.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content: Quincy Lamont Williams (born August 17, 1984), known by his stage name Peewee Longway is an American hip hop recording artist from Atlanta, Georgia who was one of the last signees of 1017 Records before founder Gucci Mane's 2013–2016 imprisonment.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content:  He is also the nephew of Brick Squad member Cold Blooded Da Don who introduced him to Gucci Mane.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content:  The artist's most celebrated releases to date are his mixtape "The Blue M&M" and his collaboration with Young Thug, "Loaded".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content:  "The Blue M&M" also featured the singles "Sneakin n Geekin" and "Servin Lean" (remix) featuring A$AP Rocky.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content:  He is also a member of a rap group with Gucci Mane and Young Dolph, collectively known as "Felix Brothers".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Peewee Longway
Content:  They released their debut project in July 2014.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content: Christopher Charles Lloyd (born April 30, 1982), better known by his stage name Lloyd Banks, is an American hip hop recording artist and member of East Coast hip hop group G-Unit, alongside childhood friends and fellow American rappers, 50 Cent and Tony Yayo.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content:  Raised in South Jamaica, Queens, he dropped out of high school in 1998.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content:  G-Unit released two albums, "Beg for Mercy" in 2003 and "T.O.S. (Terminate on Sight)" in 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content:  Banks released his first solo album "The Hunger for More" in 2004 with the top ten hit single "On Fire".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content:  He followed with "Rotten Apple" in 2006 and left Interscope Records 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lloyd Banks
Content:  In 2010 G-Unit signed with EMI to distribute Banks third studio album "The Hunger for More 2", which was released on November 22, 2010.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: O.T. Genasis
Content: Odis Oliver Flores (born June 18, 1987), better known by the stage name O.T. Genasis, is an American hip hop recording artist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collins and Maconie's Hit Parade
Content: Collins and Maconie's Hit Parade was a radio programme that aired from May 1994 to June 1997.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collins and Maconie's Hit Parade
Content:  There were 74 hour-long episodes and it was broadcast on BBC Radio 1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collins and Maconie's Hit Parade
Content:  It starred Andrew Collins and Stuart Maconie.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sons Ltd
Content: The Soul of North Staffs Recording & Publishing or Sons is an independent record label based in Stoke-on-Trent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sons Ltd
Content:  The company was formed in 2004 by Neil Graham, Seb Clarke, Al Padmore, James Wakefield and Tangwyn Davies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sons Ltd
Content:  The label consists solely of bands native to the North Staffordshire area and has become a noted figure both locally and nationally, having recently received attention from Steve Lamacq and Huw Stephens (6music, BBC Radio 1) as well as Mark Radcliffe and Stuart Maconie (BBC Radio 2) SONS has earned comparisons to Factory Records for its DIY ethic and its unique 'SONS sound'.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Hill
Content: David John "Dave" Hill (born 4 April 1946) is an English musician, who is the lead guitarist and backing vocalist in the English glam rock group, Slade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Hill
Content:  Hill is known for his flamboyant stage clothes and hairstyle.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Hill
Content:  The music journalist Stuart Maconie commented "he usually wore a jumpsuit made of the foil that you baste your turkeys in and platforms of oil-rig-derrick height.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Hill
Content:  All of this though paled in comparison with his coiffure, a sort of demented tonsure with a great scooping fringe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Dave Hill
Content:  He even had one outfit around 1973 famously called his 'Metal Nun' suit but later styles were much toned down."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Laid (album)
Content: Laid is the fifth studio album by British alternative rock band James.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Laid (album)
Content:  It was released on 27 September 1993.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Laid (album)
Content:  It was the first of several collaborations between the group and Brian Eno, who produced all but one of the album's tracks – in Stuart Maconie's authorised biography of the group, "Folklore", they admitted that Eno didn't like the song "One of the Three" so they recorded it when he took a day off.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Laid (album)
Content:  The sessions also resulted in the experimental "Wah Wah" album.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stuart Maconie
Content: Stuart Maconie (born 13 August 1960) is an English radio DJ and television presenter, writer, journalist, and critic working in the field of pop music and popular culture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stuart Maconie
Content:  He is currently a presenter on BBC Radio 6 Music, where he hosts an afternoon show five times a week (Monday–Friday, 1pm–4pm), alongside Mark Radcliffe, called "Radcliffe & Maconie", which broadcasts from the BBC's MediaCityUK in Salford, Greater Manchester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Stuart Maconie
Content:  The pair had previously presented an evening show on BBC Radio 2.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit Parade 2
Content: Hit Parade 2 is a compilation album by The Wedding Present released in January 1993.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit Parade 2
Content:  Having decided to release a limited edition single every month for all of 1992 (each featuring an original track on the A side and a cover version on the B side) the group subsequently compiled the songs as two LPs called Hit Parade 1 and Hit Parade 2.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit Parade 2
Content:  In 2003, a double CD was issued called simply "The Hit Parade".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Somebody's Gotta Go
Content: "Somebody's Gotta Go" is a 1945 song by Cootie Williams and His Orchestra.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Somebody's Gotta Go
Content:  With vocals performed by Eddie Vinson, the single was Cootie Williams' most successful entry on the Harlem Hit Parade, hitting number one on the Harlem Hit Parade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Somebody's Gotta Go
Content:  "Somebody's Gotta Go" was the final number one on The Harlem Hit Parade chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe & Maconie
Content: Radcliffe & Maconie is a weekday radio programme that is broadcast on BBC Radio 6 Music in the United Kingdom.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe & Maconie
Content:  It runs from 1 pm–4 pm on Monday to Friday, and is presented by Mark Radcliffe and Stuart Maconie.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe & Maconie
Content:  The show originally ran on BBC Radio 2 from 16 April 2007 until 23 March 2011, where it was known as "The Radcliffe and Maconie Show".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe & Maconie
Content:  The current 6 Music show broadcasts from the BBC's MediaCityUK in Salford, Greater Manchester.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Radcliffe & Maconie
Content:  The duo celebrated the 10th anniversary of the show and broadcasting together on 17 April 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit parade
Content: A hit parade is a ranked list of the most popular recordings at a given point in time, usually determined by sales and/or airplay.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit parade
Content:  The term originated in the 1930s; "Billboard" magazine published its first music hit parade on January 4, 1936.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit parade
Content:  It has also been used by broadcast programs which featured hit (sheet music and record) tunes such as "Your Hit Parade", which aired on radio and television in the United States from 1935 through the 1950s.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit Parade 1
Content: In 1992, The Wedding Present decided to release a limited edition single every month, each featuring an original track on the A side and a cover on the B side.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hit Parade 1
Content:  The tracks were compiled as two LPs called Hit Parade 1 and "Hit Parade 2" and re-released as a double CD in 2003 called "The Hit Parade".

Title: Justin Gimelstob
Content:  Gimelstob has been a resident of Morristown, New Jersey, and as of 2009 lived in Santa Monica, California.

Title: Angelique Kerber
Content: Angelique Kerber (] ; born 18 January 1988) is a German professional tennis player and former world No. 1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelique Kerber
Content:  Having made her professional debut in 2003, Kerber rose to prominence upon reaching the semifinals of the 2011 US Open as the no. 92 ranked player in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Angelique Kerber
Content:  She ascended to the top of the rankings on 12 September 2016, thus becoming the twenty-second and oldest player to achieve the number one ranking for the first time and the first new number one player since Victoria Azarenka in 2012.

Content: The 2001 Canada Masters – Doubles was the men's doubles event of the one hundred and twelfth edition of the Canada Masters; a WTA Tier I tournament and the most prestigious men's tennis tournament held in Canada.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2001 Canada Masters – Doubles
Content:  Sébastien Lareau and Daniel Nestor were the defending champions but they competed with different partners that year, Lareau with Justin Gimelstob and Nestor with Sandon Stolle.

session_0.speaker: System

Content:  Gimelstob and Lareau lost in the first round to Mark Knowles and Brian MacPhie, as did Nestor and Stolle to Jan-Michael Gambill and Simon Larose.

Content:  Jiří Novák and David Rikl won in the final 6–4, 3–6, 6–3 against Donald Johnson and Jared Palmer.

Justin Gimelstob and Sébastien Lareau won in the final over David Adams and John-Laffnie de Jager, 7–5, 7–6.

session_0.text:
Title: Iron Man Experience
Content: Iron Man Experience (Traditional Chinese: 鐵甲奇俠飛行之旅) is a 3-D motion simulator attraction in Tomorrowland at Hong Kong Disneyland, which opened on 11 January 2017.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iron Man Experience
Content:  The attraction is based on the Marvel Comics character Iron Man, becoming the first Disney attraction to be based on a Marvel property.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Iron Man Experience
Content:  Set at the fictional Stark Expo, the attraction features Tony Stark recruiting guests to fend off extraterrestrial beings that are attacking Hong Kong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rocket Jets
Content: Rocket Jets was an attraction in Disneyland at the Disneyland Resort in Anaheim, California.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rocket Jets
Content:  This attraction opened in 1967 with the new Tomorrowland and closed in 1997 for the 1998 New Tomorrowland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rocket Jets
Content:  It was the third spinning rocket attraction in Tomorrowland and stood three stories above the ground.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Rocket Jets
Content:  When Tomorrowland was redone for 1998, the Rocket Jets were replaced by a new attraction based on Orbitron at the entrance to Discoveryland in Disneyland Park Paris.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content: Mystic Point () is a section of Hong Kong Disneyland that officially opened to the public on 17 May 2013.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  Prior to that it had a soft opening for a selected audience.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  It is set in a dense, uncharted rain forest surrounded by mysterious forces and supernatural events.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  The site features Mystic Manor, a Haunted Mansion style attraction with the same trackless ride system developed at Pooh's Hunny Hunt in Tokyo Disneyland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  Mystic Point, along with Grizzly Gulch and Toy Story Land has been exclusive to Hong Kong Disneyland among all Disney theme parks for 5 years from opening.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  Composer Danny Elfman scored the music for the Mystic Manor dark ride attraction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mystic Point
Content:  It is the final land to open in Hong Kong Disneyland's three land expansion plan approved in July 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Autopia
Content: Autopia is a Disneyland attraction, in which patrons steer specially designed cars through an enclosed track.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Autopia
Content:  Versions of Autopia exist at Anaheim, California and Disneyland Paris in Marne-la-Vallée, France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Autopia
Content:  There was also an Autopia at Hong Kong Disneyland on Lantau Island, Hong Kong before it closed on June 11, 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Autopia
Content:  Other versions of the attraction can be found at the Magic Kingdom as the Tomorrowland Speedway and formerly at Tokyo Disneyland as the Grand Circuit Raceway.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Snow White Grotto
Content: Snow White Grotto is an attraction at Disneyland in Anaheim, California which originally opened on April 9, 1961, Tokyo Disneyland at the Tokyo Disney Resort in Japan in 1983, and at Hong Kong Disneyland in Hong Kong in 2005.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Snow White Grotto
Content:  It is a wishing well located at the east of Sleeping Beauty Castle for Disneyland and west for Hong Kong Disneyland and Cinderella Castle for Tokyo Disneyland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Snow White Grotto
Content:  Guests can throw a coin and make a wish in front of the grotto.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Gurr
Content: Robert Henry "Bob" Gurr (born October 25, 1931 in Los Angeles, California) is an American amusement ride designer and Imagineer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Gurr
Content:  His most famous work was for Walt Disney's Disneyland Park, and its subsequent sister parks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Gurr
Content:  Gurr is said to have designed most, if not all, of the ride vehicles of the Disneyland attractions, including Autopia, Haunted Mansion, the Disneyland Monorail, the Submarine Voyage, and the Matterhorn Bobsleds.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Gurr
Content:  He was named a Disney Legend in 2004.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bob Gurr
Content:  He also worked on the King Kong Encounter animatronic for Universal Studios Hollywood.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jungle Cruise
Content: The Jungle Cruise is an attraction located in Adventureland at many Disney Parks, including Disneyland, Magic Kingdom, and Tokyo Disneyland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jungle Cruise
Content:  At Hong Kong Disneyland, the attraction is named Jungle River Cruise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jungle Cruise
Content:  Disneyland Paris and Shanghai Disneyland are the only Magic Kingdom-style Disney parks that do not have the Jungle Cruise in their attraction rosters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content: Hong Kong Disneyland () is a theme park located on reclaimed land in Penny's Bay, Lantau Island.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content:  It is located inside the Hong Kong Disneyland Resort and it is owned and managed by Hong Kong International Theme Parks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content:  It is, together with Ocean Park Hong Kong, one of the two large theme parks in Hong Kong.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content:  Hong Kong Disneyland opened to visitors on Monday, 12 September 2005 at 13:00 HKT.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content:  Disney attempted to avoid problems of cultural backlash by incorporating Chinese culture, customs, and traditions when designing and building the resort, including adherence to the rules of feng shui.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hong Kong Disneyland
Content:  For instance, a bend was put in a walkway near the Hong Kong Disneyland Resort entrance so good qi energy wouldn't flow into the South China Sea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Skyway (Disney)
Content: The Skyway was a gondola lift attraction at Disneyland, at the Magic Kingdom, and at Tokyo Disneyland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Skyway (Disney)
Content:  Since all versions of this attraction took riders back and forth between Fantasyland and Tomorrowland, the route from Tomorrowland was called Skyway to Fantasyland, and the route from Fantasyland was called Skyway to Tomorrowland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Playhouse Disney Hong Kong
Content: Playhouse Disney Hong Kong is a Playhouse Disney-branded pay cable television channel for viewers in Hong Kong based in Kowloon Peninsula and is available in 3-national languages: English, Cantonese and Chinese.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: Poor Jake's Demise

session_0.text:

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Poor Jake's Demise

session_0.speaker: System

session_0.text:
Title: Descriptivist theory of names
Content: The descriptivist theory of proper names is that the meaning or semantic content of a proper name is identical to the descriptions associated with it by speakers, while their referents are determined to be the objects that satisfy these descriptions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Descriptivist theory of names
Content:  Bertrand Russell and Gottlob Frege have both been associated with the descriptivist theory, which is sometimes called the Frege–Russell theory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content: In the philosophy of language a proper name, for example the names of persons or places, is a name which is ordinarily taken to uniquely identify its referent in the world.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content:  As such it presents particular challenges for theories of meaning and it has become a central problem in analytical philosophy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content:  The common sense view was originally formulated by John Stuart Mill in "A System of Logic" where he defines it as "a word that answers the purpose of showing what thing it is that we are talking about but not of telling anything about it".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content:  This view was criticized when philosophers applied principles of formal logic to linguistic propositions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content:  Gottlob Frege pointed out that proper names may apply to imaginary and inexistent entities without becoming meaningless, and he showed that sometimes more than one proper name may identify the same entity without having the same "sense", so that the phrase "Homer believed the morning star was the evening star" could be meaningful and not tautological in spite of the fact that the morning star and the evening star identifies the same referent.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name (philosophy)
Content:  This example became known as Frege's Puzzle and is a central issue in the theory of proper names.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Farshid
Content: Farshid (or Farsheed) is one of the most ancient Persian proper names whose philological background could be traced back even in Old Persian scriptures.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Farshid
Content:  It consists from two morphemes far ( = splendour, shine) + shîd (= sun, sun beams).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Farshid
Content:  In this manner we could translate the Old Persian proper name Farshid/Farsheed as Sunshine or the splendor or the pomp of the sun.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lollia Paulina
Content: Lollia Paulina, also known as Lollia Paullina (15-49) was a Roman Empress for six months in 38 as the third wife and consort of the Roman emperor Caligula.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Lollia Paulina
Content:  Outside of her term as a Roman Empress, she was a noble Roman woman who lived in the Roman Empire of the 1st century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free City of Greyhawk
Content: The Free City of Greyhawk, also known as Greyhawk City and the "Gem of the Flanaess," is a fictional city-state in the "World of Greyhawk" campaign setting for the "Dungeons & Dragons" fantasy roleplaying game.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free City of Greyhawk
Content:  Since the setting was originally published in a folio and then again in a boxed set that were both titled "World of Greyhawk", the word "Greyhawk" is often used to describe the entire campaign world setting, although the proper name for that part of the world is the Flanaess.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Free City of Greyhawk
Content:  Sometimes the lands controlled by the Free City are also referred to as Greyhawk, though the proper term for the region is the Domain of Greyhawk.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name mark
Content: In Chinese writing, a proper name mark (Simplified Chinese: 专名号, zhuānmínghào; Traditional Chinese: 專名號) is an underline used to mark proper names, such as the names of people, places, dynasties, organizations.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Proper name mark
Content:  The related book name mark (Simplified Chinese: 书名号, shūmínghào; Traditional Chinese: 書名號)， indicated by a wavy underline (﹏﹏) is used to mark the titles of publications or texts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caligula
Content: Caligula ( ), properly Gaius Julius Caesar Augustus Germanicus (31 August AD 12 – 24 January AD 41) was Roman emperor from AD 37–41.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caligula
Content:  Born Gaius Julius Caesar Germanicus (not to be confused with Julius Caesar), Caligula was a member of the house of rulers conventionally known as the Julio-Claudian dynasty.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caligula
Content:  Caligula's biological father was Germanicus, and he was the great-nephew and adopted son of Emperor Tiberius.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Caligula
Content:  The young Gaius earned the nickname "Caligula" (meaning "little soldier's boot", the diminutive form of "caliga", hob-nailed military boot) from his father's soldiers while accompanying him during his campaigns in Germania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Antonomasia
Content: In rhetoric, antonomasia is a kind of metonymy in which an epithet or phrase takes the place of a proper name, such as "the little corporal" for Napoleon I.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Antonomasia
Content:  Conversely, antonomasia can also be using a proper name as an archetypal name, to express a generic idea.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collis (planetary nomenclature)
Content: Collis (plural: colles, from the Latin word for "hill") is a small hill or knob on a celestial body.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collis (planetary nomenclature)
Content:  The term is used in planetary nomenclature: it is a part of international proper names of such features.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collis (planetary nomenclature)
Content:  Like other generic terms, it is capitalized and stands after the proper name (for example, "Bilbo Colles"; the only exception is "Colles Nili" on Mars).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Collis (planetary nomenclature)
Content:  As of 2015, only groups of the hills have names, and the term is used only in plural.

Title: Masarjawaih
Content: Māsarjawaih (Arabic: ماسرجويه‎ ‎ ) was one of the earliest Jewish physicians of Persian origin, and the earliest translator from the Syriac; he lived in Basra about 683 (Anno Hegirae 64).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  His name, distorted, has been transmitted in European sources; it has not yet been satisfactorily explained.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Masarjawaih

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Masarjawaih

session_0.text:
Title: Masarjawaih
Content:  132) compares the name ""Masarjawaih"" with the Hebrew proper name ""Mesharsheya""; but the ending ""-waih"" points to a Persian origin.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  The form ""Masarjis"" has been compared with the Christian proper name ""Mar Serjis""; but it is not known that Masarjis embraced either Christianity or Islam.

Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)
Content: Robert Ramsey (February 15, 1780 – December 12, 1849) was born in Warminster Township, Pennsylvania on February 15, 1780.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)
Content:  He attended school in Hartsville, Pennsylvania.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  He served in the Pennsylvania General Assembly from 1825 to 1831 and served in the 23rd United States Congress as a Jacksonian from Pennsylvania's sixth district, March 4, 1833 to March 3, 1835.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)
Content:  He didn't run for a second term to the 24th Congress, but did win reelection later in 1840 to the 27th Congress, still representing the sixth district, but this time as a Whig.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Content:  He once again did not run for reelection and instead left congress to engaged in agricultural pursuits.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)
Content:  Ramsey died in Warwick, Pennsylvania on December 12, 1849.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Ramsey (congressman)
Content:  He was interred at Neshaminy Cemetery in Hartsville.

Title: The Telltale Head
Content: "The Telltale Head" is the eighth episode of "The Simpsons"<nowiki>'</nowiki> first season.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on February 25, 1990.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  It was written by Al Jean, Mike Reiss, Sam Simon and Matt Groening, and directed by Rich Moore.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  In the episode, Bart cuts the head off the statue of Jebediah Springfield in the center of town to impress Jimbo, Kearney and Dolph, three older kids he admires.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  The town's residents, including the three boys, are horrified and Bart regrets his actions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  After telling his family, Homer and Bart head to the center of town, where they are met by an angry mob.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  After Bart tells the mob he has made a mistake, the townspeople forgive Bart and he places the head back on the statue.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Telltale Head
Content:  The episode's title is a reference to the short story "The Tell-Tale Heart" by Edgar Allan Poe.
