Title: Pegu Club (cocktail)
Content: The Pegu Club or the Pegu is a gin-based cocktail that was the signature drink of Burma's Pegu Club.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pegu Club (cocktail)
Content:  The club was located just outside Rangoon, and its members were those Britons who were senior government and military officials and prominent businessmen.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pegu Club (cocktail)
Content:  The club was named after the Pegu, a Burmese river.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pegu Club (cocktail)
Content:  The recipe appears in the "Savoy Cocktail Book of 1930" by Harry Craddock as "The Pegu Club Cocktail," and the 1930 edition of "Cocktails by "Jimmy" late of Ciro's London" as "Pegu Club."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pegu Club (cocktail)
Content:  However, it appears to be first listed in "Barflies and Cocktails" by Harry McElhone of the famous Harry's New York Bar in Paris.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seafood cocktail
Content: Seafood cocktails are appetizer dishes including shrimp cocktail, squid cocktail, and mixed seafood cocktails.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seafood cocktail
Content:  Various preparations use ingredients such as fish and octopus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seafood cocktail
Content:  Seafood cocktails often include lime juice, a tomato based sauce and are sometimes served with lemon.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Seafood cocktail
Content:  Seafood cocktails are eaten in the U.S. and Latin America.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Corpse Reviver
Content: The Corpse Reviver family of cocktails are intended as 'hair of the dog' hangover cures, hence the name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Corpse Reviver
Content:  Most of the corpse reviver cocktails have been lost to time, but the cognac- and gin-based Corpse Reviver and Corpse Reviver #2 cocktails that were first listed in the Savoy Cocktail Handbook by Harry Craddock in 1930 have survived to this day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Clover Club Cocktail
Content: The Clover Club Cocktail is a cocktail consisting of Gin, Lemon Juice, Raspberry Syrup, and an egg white.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Clover Club Cocktail
Content:  The egg white is not added for the purpose of giving the drink flavor, but rather acts as an emulsifier.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Clover Club Cocktail
Content:  Thus when the drink is shaken a characteristic foamy head is formed.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jack and Coke
Content: Jack and Coke (also referred to as JD and Coke, Jack Coke, or a Lemmy) is a cocktail made with Jack Daniel's whiskey and Coca-Cola.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jack and Coke
Content:  The drink is usually served with ice in an old-fashioned glass or a Collins glass.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Hess (cocktail expert)
Content: Robert B. Hess is a cocktail expert, a co-founder of the Museum of the American Cocktail, and a technology evangelist for Microsoft.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Hess (cocktail expert)
Content:  Hess is the author of "The Essential Bartender's Guide: How to Make Truly Great Cocktails" published by Mud Puddle Books in 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Hess (cocktail expert)
Content:  He is also the host of "The Cocktail Spirit," an online video series published by the Small Screen Network.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Hess (cocktail expert)
Content:  In late 2008, Hess, along with several other well-known cocktail personalities, founded The Chanticleer Society, a "Worldwide Organization of Cocktail Enthusiasts."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Robert Hess (cocktail expert)
Content:  Hess also writes a column entitled "Classic Cocktails" for the bi-monthly fine beverage publication, Mutineer Magazine.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content: Heublein was originally a restaurant in Hartford, Connecticut founded in 1862 by Andrew Heublein, a German American entrepreneur.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  He was soon joined in business by his two sons Gilbert F. and Louis Heublein.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  In 1875 they took an order to prepare a quantity of pre-mixed martini and manhattan cocktails for the annual picnic of the Governor's Foot Guard.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  The event had to be cancelled due to rain.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  A few days later, a restaurant employee was instructed to dispose of the stored cocktails.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  But his curiosity led to the discovery and declaration that the alcoholic drinks were "still good".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  It had been duly noted by the two brothers, who started selling pre-mixed cocktails in the restaurant.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  These ready-made cocktails were so popular that a distillery was built just to satisfy the increasing demand.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  The business became Gilbert F. Heublein and Bro.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  upon its transfer to Andrew's sons Gilbert and Louis Heublein in 1890, when the focus was turning towards their lucrative line of "ready-made" alcoholic cocktail drinks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  In 1906 the business gained the rights to distribute (and later produce) A1 Steak Sauce for the US market, under license from Brand & Co. Ltd. of Vauxhall, London, UK.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  Heublein started sales in the US under the name "Brand's A.1.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heublein
Content:  Sauce" .

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manhattan (cocktail)
Content: A Manhattan is a cocktail made with whiskey, sweet vermouth, and bitters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manhattan (cocktail)
Content:  Commonly used whiskeys include rye (the traditional choice), Canadian whisky, bourbon, blended whiskey, and Tennessee whiskey.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manhattan (cocktail)
Content:  The cocktail is often stirred and strained into a cocktail glass, where it is garnished with a Maraschino cherry with a stem.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manhattan (cocktail)
Content:  A Manhattan can also be served on the rocks in a lowball glass.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Manhattan (cocktail)
Content:  The whiskey-based Manhattan is one of five cocktails named for one of New York City's five boroughs, but is perhaps most closely related to the Brooklyn cocktail, a mix utilizing dry vermouth and Maraschino liqueur in place of the Manhattan's sweet vermouth, as well as Amer Picon in place of the Manhattan's traditional bitters.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content: The specialty cocktail drink known as the Hand Grenade is sold frozen or on-the-rocks exclusively by 721 Bourbon, Inc. through five licensed nightclub bars in the New Orleans French Quarter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  Pam Fortner and Earl Bernhardt, owners of the Tropical Isle bar founded during the 1984 Louisiana World Exposition, created the melon-flavored Hand Grenade as their signature cocktail.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  Since 1987, the federally registered trademark "Hand Grenade" has been used to uniquely identify the cocktail and its packaging.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  Since January 1992, the Hand Grenade has been served in a green, translucent, plastic yard glass container with a bulbous, textured base shaped like an oversized hand grenade.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  The grenade has anthropomorphic features, including black oval eyes and an upturned smile.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  Printed in bold, black lettering, the legend extends vertically down the neck, with the cocktail name and a reference to its high alcohol content: "Hand Grenade® New Orleans Most Powerful Drink".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  Consumers purchasing Hand Grenade cocktails in these containers receive discounts on refills at the licensed establishments.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Hand Grenade (cocktail)
Content:  In January 2012, a lower-calorie "Skinny Hand Grenade" was announced.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bronx (cocktail)
Content: The Bronx Cocktail is essentially a Perfect Martini with orange juice added.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bronx (cocktail)
Content:  It was ranked number three in "The World's 10 Most Famous Cocktails in 1934", making it a very popular rival to the Martini (#1) and the Manhattan (#2).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bronx (cocktail)
Content:  Today, it remains a popular choice in some markets, and was formerly designated as an Official Cocktail by the International Bartender Association.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Bronx (cocktail)
Content:  Like the Manhattan, the Bronx is one of five cocktails named for one of New York City's five boroughs, but is perhaps most closely related to the Queens, which substitutes pineapple for the Bronx's orange.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content: The concept of the soft path was first used for energy resource management and was developed by Amory Lovins shortly after the shock of the 1973 energy crisis in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  This concept has now been refined and applied to water, most notably by water expert Peter Gleick and David Brooks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  The soft path is often framed as a more integrated and effective alternative to supply-side water resource management.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  Supply-side water management focuses on meeting demands for water through centralized, large-scale physical infrastructure, and centralized water management systems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  In the 20th century, this approach focused on constructing bigger dams and drilling deeper wells to access more water to meet projected demands of consumers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  More recently, a focus on demand-side management has emerged in regions where water supply is increasingly constrained (see, for example, Peak water), and it focuses on managing demand and making current practices more efficient.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  The soft path integrates both supply and demand concepts but in a broader context by recognizing that water is a means to satisfy demands for goods and services and asking how much water, of what qualities, is actually required to satisfy those demands efficiently and sustainably.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  Soft path water planning also requires broader institutional approaches to water management including the application of smart economics, the potential for distributed rather than centralized water systems, and more democratic participation in water policy decisions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content:  Others have described the soft path as "unleashing the full potential of demand-side management."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft water path
Content: ,

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jack Goldman
Content: Jacob "Jack" Goldman (Brooklyn, New York, July 18, 1921 – Westport, Connecticut, December 20, 2011) was an American physicist and former chief scientist of Xerox Corporation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jack Goldman
Content:  He was also a faculty member at Carnegie Tech and directed the Ford Scientific Laboratory.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Jack Goldman
Content:  He is especially notable for hiring physicist Dr. George Pake to create the Xerox Palo Alto Research Center, which produced many seminal ideas in modern computing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content: Dr. Greg Zacharias is the current Chief Scientist of the United States Air Force.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  The position of the Chief Scientist was created over 60 years ago to provide independent scientific advice to the Secretary of the Air Force and the Chief of Staff of the Air Force, as well as to its senior leadership.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  In this role, he works with the top scientists and engineers within the Air Force as well as in academia, industry, and the other armed services to ensure that the Air Force's research and development efforts remain relevant and effective.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  Additionally, as the Chief Scientist he responds to any tasking from the Secretary of the Air Force and the Air Force Chief of Staff on issues or opportunities of a scientific and technical nature that may arise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  He also interacts with other services and the Office of the Secretary of Defense on issues affecting the Air Force in-house technical enterprise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  He serves on the Executive Committee of the Air Force Scientific Advisory Board.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Greg Zacharias
Content:  He is the principal science and technology representative of the Air Force to the civilian scientific and engineering community and to the public at large.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mica Endsley
Content: Dr. Mica Endsley is an engineer and a former Chief Scientist of the United States Air Force.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mica Endsley
Content:  The position of the Chief Scientist was created over 60 years ago to provide independent scientific advice to the Secretary of the Air Force and the Chief of Staff of the Air Force, as well as to its senior leadership.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mica Endsley
Content:  In this role, she worked with the top scientists and engineers within the Air Force as well as in academia, industry, and the other armed services to ensure that the Air Force's research and development efforts remain relevant and effective.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mica Endsley
Content:  Additionally, as the Chief Scientist she responded to any tasking from the Secretary of the Air Force and the Air Force Chief of Staff on issues or opportunities of a scientific and technical nature that may arise.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mica Endsley
Content:  Dr. Endsley was the first human factors engineer and the first female to serve as Chief Scientist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amory Lovins
Content: Amory Bloch Lovins (born November 13, 1947) is an American physicist, environmental scientist, writer, and Chairman/Chief Scientist of the Rocky Mountain Institute.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amory Lovins
Content:  He has worked in the field of energy policy and related areas for four decades.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Amory Lovins
Content:  He was named by "Time" magazine one of the World's 100 most influential people in 2009.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content: John Mace Grunsfeld (born October 10, 1958) is an American physicist and a former NASA astronaut.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content:  He is a veteran of five Space Shuttle flights and has served as NASA Chief Scientist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content:  His academic background includes research in high energy astrophysics, cosmic ray physics and the emerging field of exoplanet studies with specific interest in future astronomical instrumentation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content:  After retiring from NASA in 2009, he served as the Deputy Director of the Space Telescope Science Institute in Baltimore, Maryland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content:  In January 2012, he returned to NASA and served as associate administrator of NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John M. Grunsfeld
Content:  Grunsfeld announced his retirement from NASA in April 2016.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Negawatt power
Content: Negawatt power is a theoretical unit of power representing an amount of electrical power (measured in watts) saved.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Negawatt power
Content:  The energy saved is a direct result of energy conservation or increased energy efficiency.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Negawatt power
Content:  The term was coined by the chief scientist of the Rocky Mountain Institute and environmentalist Amory Lovins in 1985, within the article, "Saving Gigabucks with Negawatts," where he argued that utility customers don’t want kilowatt-hours of electricity; they want energy services such as hot showers, cold beer, lit rooms, and spinning shafts, which can come more cheaply if electricity is used more efficiently.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Negawatt power
Content:  Lovins felt an international behavioral change was necessary in order to decrease countries' dependence on excessive amounts of energy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Negawatt power
Content:  The concept of a negawatt could influence a behavioral change in consumers by encouraging them to think about the energy that they spend.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft energy path
Content: In 1976 energy policy analyst Amory Lovins coined the term soft energy path to describe an alternative future where energy efficiency and appropriate renewable energy sources steadily replace a centralized energy system based on fossil and nuclear fuels.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark J. Lewis
Content: Dr. Mark J. Lewis was the Chief Scientist of the U.S. Air Force, Washington, D.C. from 2004 to 2008 and was the longest-serving Chief Scientist in Air Force history.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark J. Lewis
Content:  He served as chief scientific adviser to the Chief of Staff and Secretary of the Air Force, and provided assessments on a wide range of scientific and technical issues affecting the Air Force mission.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark J. Lewis
Content:  In this role he identified and analyzed technical issues and brought them to attention of Air Force leaders, and interacted with other Air Staff principals, operational commanders, combatant commands, acquisition, and science & technology communities to address cross-organizational technical issues and solutions.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mark J. Lewis
Content:  His primary areas of focus included energy, sustainment, long-range strike technologies, advanced propulsion systems, and workforce development.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft energy technology
Content: Soft energy technologies may be seen as "appropriate" renewable technologies.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Soft energy technology
Content:  Soft energy technologies are not simply renewable energy technologies, as there are many renewable energy technologies which are not regarded as "soft".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Tempest (1979 film)
Content: The Tempest is a 1979 film adaptation of William Shakespeare's play of the same name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Tempest (1979 film)
Content:  Directed by Derek Jarman, with Heathcote Williams as Prospero, it also stars Toyah Willcox, Jack Birkett and Helen Wellington-Lloyd from Jarman's previous feature, "Jubilee" (1977), as well as his long-time cohort Karl Johnson.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Shakespeare
Content: William Shakespeare ( ; 26 April 1564 (baptised) – 23 April 1616) was an English poet, playwright, and actor, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language and the world's pre-eminent dramatist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Shakespeare
Content:  He is often called England's national poet, and the "Bard of Avon".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Shakespeare
Content:  His extant works, including collaborations, consist of approximately 38 plays, 154 sonnets, two long narrative poems, and a few other verses, some of uncertain authorship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: William Shakespeare
Content:  His plays have been translated into every major living language and are performed more often than those of any other playwright.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content: Henry Carey (c. 26 August 1687 – 5 October 1743) was an English poet, dramatist and song-writer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  He is remembered as an anti-Walpolean satirist and also as a patriot.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  Several of his melodies continue to be sung today, and he was widely praised in the generation after his death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  Because he worked in anonymity, selling his own compositions to others to pass off as their own, contemporary scholarship can only be certain of some of his poetry, and a great deal of the music he composed was written for theatrical incidental music.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  However, under his own name and hand, he was a prolific song writer and balladeer, and he wrote the lyrics for almost all of these songs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  Further, he wrote numerous operas and plays.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  His life is illustrative of the professional author in the early 18th century.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  Without inheritance or title or governmental position, he wrote for all of the remunerative venues, and yet he also kept his own political point of view and was able to score significant points against the ministry of the day.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Henry Carey (writer)
Content:  Further, he was one of the leading lights of the new "Patriotic" movement in drama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holy Sonnets
Content: The Holy Sonnets—also known as the Divine Meditations or Divine Sonnets—are a series of nineteen poems by the English poet John Donne (1572–1631).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holy Sonnets
Content:  The sonnets were first published in 1633—two years after Donne's death.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holy Sonnets
Content:  The poems are sonnets and are predominantly in the style and form prescribed by Renaissance Italian poet Petrarch (or Francesco Petrarca) (1304–1374) in which the sonnet consisted of two quatrains (four-line stanzas) and a sestet (a six-line stanza).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Holy Sonnets
Content:  However, several rhythmic and structural patterns as well as the inclusion of couplets are elements influenced by the sonnet form developed by English poet and playwright William Shakespeare (1564–1616).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
Content: Samuel Taylor Coleridge ( ; 21 October 177225 July 1834) was an English poet, literary critic, philosopher and theologian who, with his friend William Wordsworth, was a founder of the Romantic Movement in England and a member of the Lake Poets.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
Content:  He wrote the poems "The Rime of the Ancient Mariner" and "Kubla Khan", as well as the major prose work "Biographia Literaria".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
Content:  His critical work, especially on William Shakespeare, was highly influential, and he helped introduce German idealist philosophy to English-speaking culture.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
Content:  Coleridge coined many familiar words and phrases, including suspension of disbelief.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
Content:  He was a major influence on Ralph Waldo Emerson and American transcendentalism.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isabella Cervoni
Content: Isabella Cervoni (Colle Val d'Elsa, 1575–1600) was an Italian poet of the Counter-Reformation period, active between 1590 and 1600.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isabella Cervoni
Content:  She wrote encomiastic and polemical poems addressed to numerous secular and religious dignitaries of the Italian Renaissance, including Pope Clement VIII, Maria de' Medici, Christina of Lorraine and Henry IV of France.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Isabella Cervoni
Content:  She was praised for her talent and ambition by Cristoforo Bronzini in his 1625 dialogue "Della dignità delle donne, dialogo…settimana prima e giornata quarta" as having "given the world many beautiful and spiritual compositions" despite her "most tender age."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shakespeare bibliography
Content: William Shakespeare (1564–1616) was an English poet and playwright.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Shakespeare bibliography
Content:  He wrote approximately 38 plays and 154 sonnets, as well as a variety of other poems.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Goodall (poet)
Content: Charles Goodall (1671—May 11, 1689) is a minor English poet.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Goodall (poet)
Content:  A student of Eton College and then Merton College, Oxford, he wrote a number of romantic and erotic poems referring to male students at said colleges.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Goodall (poet)
Content:  In 1689, the year of his death, he put together a collection entitled "Poems and Translations" which contains 33 poems with male-male subject matter, eleven regarding women, and 13 to a mistress named 'Idera' (considered probably imaginary).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Charles Goodall (poet)
Content:  A number of the homoerotic poems have been rewritten to remove the same-sex subject matter.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content: As the last in the famed collection of sonnets written by English poet and playwright William Shakespeare from 1592 to 1598, Sonnet 154 is most often thought of in a pair with the previous sonnet, number 153.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content:  As A. L. Rowse states in "Shakespeare's Sonnets: The Problems Solved", Sonnets 153 and 154 "are not unsuitably placed as a kind of coda to the Dark Lady Sonnets, to which they relate."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content:  Rowse calls attention to the fact that Sonnets 153 and 154 "serve quite well to round off the affair Shakespeare had with Emilia, the woman characterized as the Dark Lady, and the section of the Dark Lady sonnets".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content:  Shakespeare used Greek mythology to address love and despair in relationships.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content:  The material in Sonnets 153 and 154 has been shown to relate to the six-line epigram by the Byzantine poet known as Marianus Scholasticus, who published a collection of 3,500 poems called "The Greek Anthology".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Sonnet 154
Content:  When translated, the epigram resembles Sonnets 153 and 154, addressing love and the story of Cupid, the torch, and the Nymph's attempt to extinguish the torch.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heathcote Williams
Content: John Henley Heathcote-Williams (15 November 1941 – 1 July 2017), known as Heathcote Williams, was an English poet, actor, political activist and dramatist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heathcote Williams
Content:  He wrote a number of book-length polemical poems including "Autogeddon", "Falling for a Dolphin" and "Whale Nation", which in 1988 became, according to Philip Hoare, "the most powerful argument for the newly instigated worldwide ban on whaling."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heathcote Williams
Content:  Williams invented his idiosyncratic "documentary/investigative poetry" style which he put to good purpose bringing a diverse range of environmental and political matters to public attention.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heathcote Williams
Content:  His last published work, "American Porn" was a critique of the American political establishment and the election of President Donald Trump: Publication date was the date of Trump's inauguration (20 January 2017).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Heathcote Williams
Content:  In June 2015, he published a book-length investigative poem about the "Muslim Gandhi", Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, "Badshah Khan".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Additur
Content: An additur (Latin: "it is added to") is a legal term referring to the practice of a trial judge adding damages additional to the original amount awarded by the jury.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Additur
Content:  It is not allowed in U.S. federal courts, as held by "Dimick vs. Schiedt", 293 U.S. 474 (1935).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Additur
Content:  However, "Dimick" was decided before "Erie Railroad Co. v. Tompkins" (1938), which given the rarity of additur makes it unclear whether federal courts are bound by this rule when applying state law in diversity cases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One, Inc. v. Olesen
Content: One, Inc. v. Olesen 355 U.S. 371 (January 13, 1958) is a landmark United States Supreme Court decision for LGBT rights in the United States.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: One, Inc. v. Olesen
Content:  It was the first U.S. Supreme Court ruling to deal with homosexuality and the first to address free speech rights with respect to homosexuality.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad
Content: The Erie Railroad (reporting mark ERIE) was a railroad that operated in the northeastern United States, originally connecting New York City — more specifically Jersey City, New Jersey, where Erie's former terminal, long demolished, used to stand — with Lake Erie.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad
Content:  It expanded west to Chicago with its 1941 merger with the former Atlantic and Great Western Railroad, also known as the New York, Pennsylvania and Ohio Railroad (NYPANO RR).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad
Content:  Its mainline route proved influential in the development and economic growth of the Southern Tier, including cities such as Binghamton, Elmira, and Hornell.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad
Content:  The Erie Railroad repair shops were located in Hornell, and were Hornell's largest employer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad
Content:  Hornell was also where Erie's main line split into two routes, one north to Buffalo and the other west to Cleveland.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ragan v. Merchants Transfer & Warehouse Co.
Content: Ragan v. Merchants Transfer & Warehouse Co., 337 U.S. 530 (1949), is a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court held that federal courts sitting in diversity should begin the running of the statute of limitations for a claim according to state law instead of according to the federal rules of civil procedure.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Ragan v. Merchants Transfer & Warehouse Co.
Content:  The court reasoned that a claim could not be given longer life in federal court than it would have had in a state court while being consistent with the holding in "Erie Railroad v. Tompkins".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad Co. v. Tompkins
Content: Erie Railroad Co. v. Tompkins, 304 U.S. 64 (1938) , is a landmark decision by the Supreme Court of the United States in which the Court held that federal courts did not have the judicial power to create general federal common law when hearing state law claims under diversity jurisdiction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Erie Railroad Co. v. Tompkins
Content:  In reaching this holding, the Court overturned almost a century of federal civil procedure case law, and established the foundation of what remains the modern law of diversity jurisdiction as it applies to United States federal courts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content: On April 28, 2015, the Supreme Court of the United States heard oral arguments for "Obergefell v. Hodges" (Ohio), which was consolidated with three other same-sex marriage cases from the other states in the Sixth Circuit: "Tanco v. Haslam" (Tennessee), "DeBoer v. Snyder" (Michigan), "Bourke v. Beshear" (Kentucky).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  On June 26, 2015 the Supreme Court reversed the Sixth Circuit's decision, paving the way for same-sex marriage to become legal in those states, and setting a precedent for the entire nation.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  All four states complied with the ruling the same day it was issued before the mandate was actually issued.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  Every state in the circuit had a district court ruling against their states' ban, but they were eventually stayed pending appeal.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  The Sixth Circuit consists of Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and Tennessee.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  On August 6, 2014, the Sixth Circuit heard oral arguments for same-sex marriage cases from each state within the circuit.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  On November 6, 2014, the Sixth Circuit in a split 2-1 decision, upheld the states' same-sex marriage bans, reversing the district courts' rulings that struck them down.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Same-sex marriage in the Sixth Circuit
Content:  The Sixth Circuit was the first and only circuit court since the landmark ruling "United States v. Windsor" to uphold the constitutionality of states' same-sex marriage bans which caused a circuit split.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Criminal law in the Waite Court
Content: During the tenure of Morrison Waite as Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States (March 4, 1874 through March 23, 1888), the Supreme Court heard an unprecedented volume and frequency of criminal cases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Criminal law in the Waite Court
Content:  In just fourteen years, the Court heard 106 criminal cases, almost as many cases as the Supreme Court had heard in the period from its creation to the appointment of Waite as Chief Justice.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Criminal law in the Waite Court
Content:  Notable cases include "United States v. Cruikshank" (1875), "United States v. Reese" (1875), "Reynolds v. United States" (1878), "Wilkerson v. Utah" (1879), the "Trade-Mark Cases" (1879), "Strauder v. West Virginia" (1880), "Pace v. Alabama" (1883), "United States v. Harris" (1883), "Ex parte Crow Dog" (1883), "Hurtado v. California" (1884), "Clawson v. United States" (1885), "Yick Wo v. Hopkins" (1886), "United States v. Kagama" (1886), "Ker v. Illinois" (1886), and "Mugler v. Kansas" (1887).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klaxon Co. v. Stentor Electric Manufacturing Co.
Content: Klaxon Company v. Stentor Electric Manufacturing Company, 313 U.S. 487 (1941) , was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court applied the choice-of-law principles of "Erie Railroad v. Tompkins" to conflicts between laws of different states for cases sitting in federal court on diversity jurisdiction.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Klaxon Co. v. Stentor Electric Manufacturing Co.
Content:  The court held that a federal court sitting in diversity must apply the choice-of-law doctrine of the forum state to choose between the forum state's law and the other state's law (as distinguished from the federal choice-of-law doctrines which had been used before "Erie").

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content: V.L. v. E.L., 577 U.S. ___ (2016) , is a case decided by the Supreme Court of the United States concerning the adoption rights of same-sex couples.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  In 2007, a Georgia Superior Court granted adoption rights to V.L., the partner of E.L., the woman who gave birth to their three children.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  However, after moving back to Alabama, the couple split up.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  E.L. tried to block V.L. from seeing the children, but V.L. filed a lawsuit seeking visitation and other parental rights.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  On September 18, 2015, the Supreme Court of Alabama ruled that the state did not have to recognize the adoption judgment, saying that the Georgia court misapplied its own state law.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  The court voided the recognition of the adoption judgment in Alabama.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  V.L. petitioned the United States Supreme Court to stay the ruling during her appeal and allow her to see her children.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: V.L. v. E.L.
Content:  On December 14, 2015, the Supreme Court stayed the ruling pending their action on a petition for a writ of certiorari filed by V.L. On March 7, 2016, the Supreme Court of the United States reversed the decision of the Alabama Supreme Court by per curiam summary disposition.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guaranty Trust Co. v. York
Content: Guaranty Trust Co. v. York, 326 U.S. 99 (1945), was a United States Supreme Court case that described how federal courts were to follow state law.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Guaranty Trust Co. v. York
Content:  Justice Frankfurter delivered the majority opinion further refining the doctrine set forth in "Erie Railroad Co. v. Tompkins".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content: Mental Overdrive is the primary solo moniker of Per Martinsen (born 31 July 1966), one of Norway's most prolific and influential techno musicians.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  His tracks have ranged from hardcore rave techno to vibrant space-disco, and he's always maintained a healthy balance of humor and braininess.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Active since 1990, he began his career releasing several 12" EPs of aggressive, rave-ready hardcore techno on revered Belgian label R&S, including 12000 AD (1990), The Second Coming (1991), Move!

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  (as Confusion Club, 1991), and The Love EP (1992).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  In 1994, Martinsen began releasing atmospheric techno singles as part of Illumination, his duo with Nicholas Sillitoe.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  The next year, Mental Overdrive released the single "Disto Disco," which featured a B-side ("Faith") co-written by R&S artist Outlander, best known for the 1991 classic "Vamp."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  The A-side appeared on Mental Overdrive's full-length debut Plugged, released on Martinsen's own Love OD Communications.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  The album showed a notable progression in his music, maintaining its rough, distorted hardcore techno sound while adding more cerebral elements, placing it closer to Warp's Artificial Intelligence series.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Martinsen displayed his sense of humor with 1996's Unplugged, a limited conceptual release containing silent "versions" of the tracks on Plugged.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  083 In 1997, Mental Overdrive signed to Virgin and released About Jazz, a significantly more house/disco-influenced EP than his previous work.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  This was followed by full-length Ad Absurdum, which continued in a more light-hearted and funky direction than his previous releases.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  He took a few years off from releasing Mental Overdrive recordings, instead devoting time to Frost (his more pop-focused electronic duo with his wife Aggie Peterson) and Illumination, which released two albums on RCA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Following the 2003 release of Mental Overdrive's Me EP on Love OD, he signed to Norwegian label Smalltown Supersound and released full-length 083, which featured the single "Diskodans."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  In 2005, the label compiled his early R&S material on CD as The Phuture That Never Happened.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Two years later, Mental Overdrive's single "Spooks" appeared on Prins Thomas' Full Pupp label.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  The song appeared on his next Smalltown full-length, You Are Being Manipulated, which was released in 2008.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  The album was perfectly at home with the label's other left-field dance artists like Bjørn Torske and Kim Hiorthøy, while maintaining the unique Mental Overdrive sound.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Martinsen continued releasing Mental Overdrive singles on Full Pupp and Love OD, and contributed to Rune Lindbæk's Meanderthals project.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  In 2012, he released Man with a Movie Camera, an EP featuring music he'd composed for a 1996 screening of the Russian silent film of the same name, which also featured pieces by Biosphere which would later appear on the 2001 remaster of his classic album Substrata.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  Mental Overdrive returned to his Love OD label for 2013 full-length Cycls, as well as 2014's Everything Is Connected, which compiled a few previously released EPs.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  In 2016, Full Pupp sublabel Rett I Fletta released a new version of Plugged consisting of alternate takes sourced from the original DAT tapes.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Mental Overdrive
Content:  (Paul Simpson for allmusic.com)

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content: "Catch Me (I'm Falling)" is a song released by American group Pretty Poison in 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content:  It was included on the soundtrack to the film "Hiding Out", which starred Jon Cryer and came out the same year; the song later appeared on Pretty Poison's debut album, "Catch Me I'm Falling" (1988).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content:  It was the group's biggest hit single to date, peaking at number one on the "Billboard" Hot Dance Club Play chart in late September 1987.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content:  Later that same year, the song charted inside the top ten of the "Billboard" Hot 100 chart, peaking at number eight and remaining in the top 40 for 14 weeks.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content:  The single was certified gold by the RIAA on March 9, 1989.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Catch Me (I'm Falling)
Content:  In the UK the song entered the Top 100 for two weeks at the end of January 1988 and peaked at number 85.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Long Walk (song)
Content: "A Long Walk" is a song released in 2001 by American recording artist Jill Scott, from her debut studio album, "Who Is Jill Scott?

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Long Walk (song)
Content:  Words and Sounds Vol.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Long Walk (song)
Content:  1".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Long Walk (song)
Content:  The song peaked at No. 9 on "Billboard's" R&B Singles chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: A Long Walk (song)
Content:  It was sampled on the track "Stimulation" from Disclosure's 2013 album Settle.

session_0.text:
Title: Five Finger Death Punch discography
Content: The discography of Five Finger Death Punch (5FDP), an American heavy metal band, consists of six studio albums, one live album, one extended play (EP), 23 singles and 16 music videos. Formed in Las Vegas, Nevada in 2005, the group features vocalist Ivan Moody, lead guitarist Jason Hook, rhythm guitarist Zoltan Bathory, bassist Chris Kael and drummer Jeremy Spencer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Five Finger Death Punch discography
Content:  In 2007, the band released its debut album "The Way of the Fist", which reached number 107 on the "Billboard" 200 and was certified gold by the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Five Finger Death Punch discography
Content:  All three singles from the album reached the top 20 of the "Billboard" Mainstream Rock Songs chart.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Five Finger Death Punch discography
Content:  After Hook replaced previous guitarist Darrell Roberts, 5FDP released "War Is the Answer" in 2009 which reached the top ten of the "Billboard" 200 and was certified platinum by the RIAA.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Five Finger Death Punch discography
Content:  Four singles from the album reached the Mainstream Rock top ten, while the band's cover of "Bad Company" was certified platinum.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Safety Dance
Content: "The Safety Dance" is a song by Canadian new wave/synth-pop band Men Without Hats, released in Canada in 1983 as the second single from "Rhythm of Youth".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: The Safety Dance
Content:  The song was written by lead singer Ivan Doroschuk after he had been kicked out of a club for pogoing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content: "Toxic" is a song recorded by American singer Britney Spears for her fourth studio album "In the Zone" (2003).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  It was written and produced by Christian Karlsson and Pontus Winnberg (known collectively as Bloodshy & Avant), with additional writing from Cathy Dennis and Henrik Jonback.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  The song released on January 13, 2004, by Jive Records, as the second single from "In the Zone".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  The song was originally offered to Kylie Minogue for her ninth studio album "Body Language" (2003), but she rejected it.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  After trying to choose between "(I Got That) Boom Boom" and "Outrageous" to be the second single from "In the Zone", Spears selected "Toxic" instead.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  A dance song with elements of bhangra music, "Toxic" features varied instrumentation, such as drums, synthesizers and surf guitar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  It is accompanied by high-pitched Bollywood strings, sampled from Lata Mangeshkar and S. P. Balasubrahmanyam's "Tere Mere Beech Mein" (1981), and breathy vocals.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  Its lyrics draw an extended metaphor of a lover as a dangerous yet addictive drug.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  The song has received acclaim from music critics, deeming it the strongest track of "In the Zone", and praised its hook and chorus.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Toxic (song)
Content:  "Toxic" won Spears her first and only Grammy Award at the 2005 ceremony in the category of Best Dance Recording.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Safety Dunce
Content: Safety Dunce is an instrumental metal and hard rock solo album released by guitarist Jason Hook in 2007.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Safety Dunce
Content:  The album title is an obvious play on words of the song "The Safety Dance" by Men Without Hats.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Safety Dunce
Content:  Safety Dunce won a 2007 L.A. Music Award for Best Instrumental Record.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pyromania (song)
Content: "Pyromania" is a song performed by German Eurodance group Cascada, released as the first single from their fourth studio album, "Original Me".

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pyromania (song)
Content:  It was written by Yann Peifer, Allan Eshuijs, and Manuel Reuter, and it was produced by Reuter and Peifer.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:

Title: Debbie Reynolds
Content: Mary Frances "Debbie" Reynolds (April 1, 1932 – December 28, 2016) was an American actress, singer, businesswoman, film historian, humanitarian, and mother of the actress and writer Carrie Fisher.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Debbie Reynolds
Content:  She was nominated for the Golden Globe Award for Most Promising Newcomer for her portrayal of Helen Kane in the 1950 film "Three Little Words", and her breakout role was her first leading role, as Kathy Selden in "Singin' in the Rain" (1952).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Debbie Reynolds
Content:  Other successes include "The Affairs of Dobie Gillis" (1953), "Susan Slept Here" (1954), "Bundle of Joy" (1956 Golden Globe nomination), "The Catered Affair" (1956 National Board of Review Best Supporting Actress Winner), and "Tammy and the Bachelor" (1957), in which her performance of the song "Tammy" become the first song by a female solo artist to reach number one on the "Billboard" music charts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Debbie Reynolds
Content:  In 1959, she released her first pop music album, titled "Debbie".

session_0.text:

Title: 2009 Australian Production Car Championship
Content: The 2009 Australian Production Car Championship was a CAMS sanctioned motor racing title for drivers of Group 3E Series Production Cars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 Australian Production Car Championship
Content: <ref name="B09/033">CAMS Bulletin B09/033 – 2009 Australian Manufacturers Championship & Australian Production Car Championship Sporting Regulations As archived at www.webcitation.org on 3 December 2009</ref> It was the 16th Australian Production Car Championship title to be awarded by CAMS.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 Australian Production Car Championship
Content:  As well as claiming the Class A2 title, Garry Holt won the overall championship in his BMW 335i.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 Australian Production Car Championship
Content:  Holt held off Class A1 champion Rod Salmon by just two points in the overall standings.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2009 Australian Production Car Championship
Content:  Other class champions were Jake Camilleri in his Mazda 3 MPS (Class B) and Stuart Jones' Toyota Celica (Class C).

session_0.speaker: System

Title: 2016 Australian Production Car Series
Content:  It was the first Australian Production Car Series to be contested following the cancellation of the Australian Production Car Championship at the end of 2015.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2011 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content: The 2011 Australian Manufacturers' Championship was a CAMS sanctioned national motor racing championship for car manufacturers.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2011 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  It was the 26th manufacturers title to be awarded by CAMS and the 17th to be contested under the Australian Manufacturers' Championship name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2011 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  The championship, which was open to modified production touring cars, also incorporated three drivers titles, the 2011 Australian Production Car Championship, the 2011 Australian Production Car Endurance Championship and the 2011 Australian Endurance Championship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content: The 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship was an Australian motor racing series for modified production touring cars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  It comprised two CAMS sanctioned national championship titles, the Australian Manufacturers’ Championship (for automobile manufacturers) and the Australian Production Car Championship (for drivers).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  The 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship was the 28th manufacturers title to be awarded by CAMS and the 19th to be contested under the Australian Manufacturers' Championship name.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  The 2013 Australian Production Car Championship was the 20th Australian Production Car Championship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2013 Australian Manufacturers' Championship
Content:  The Manufacturers title was awarded to Mitsubishi and the Australian Production Car Championship to Garry Holt.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Australian GT Production Car Championship
Content: PROCAR had first promoted a national series for production based cars in 1994.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Australian GT Production Car Championship
Content:  This “Australian Super Production Car Series” accommodated numerous models (including high performance GT type cars) which were not eligible for the official Australian Production Car Championship, which at the time had tightened regulations in the interests of cost-control and was limited to vehicles with an engine capacity of less than 2.5 litres.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Australian GT Production Car Championship
Content:  The PROCAR series was renamed the “Australian GT Production Car Series” for 1995.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Australian GT Production Car Championship
Content:  For 1996 the series was given full CAMS national title status to become the Australian GT Production Car Championship while the Australian Production Car Championship itself was discontinued after the 1995 title.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007 Australian Production Car Championship
Content: The 2007 Australian Production Car Championship was a CAMS sanctioned Australiann motor racing title open to Group 3E Series Production Cars.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007 Australian Production Car Championship
Content:  The championship, which was administered by the Production Car Association of Australia, was promoted as the Shannons Australian Production Car Championship.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: 2007 Australian Production Car Championship
Content:  It was the 14th Australian Production Car Championship.

Title: John Kemeny (film producer)
Content: John Kemeny (April 17, 1925 – November 23, 2012) was a Hungarian-born Canadian film producer whom the "Toronto Star" dubbed "the forgotten giant of Canadian film history."

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Kemeny (film producer)
Content:  His production credits included the well-known 1974 film, "The Apprenticeship of Duddy Kravitz", which starred Richard Dreyfuss, directed by Ted Kotcheff, based on a novel by Mordecai Richler.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: John Kemeny (film producer)
Content:  Kemeny also produced the 1980 romantic comedy, "Atlantic City", starring Burt Lancaster and Susan Sarandon.

Title: Diane Ladd
Content: Diane Ladd (born November 29, 1932) is an American actress, film director, producer and author.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diane Ladd
Content:  She has appeared in over 120 film and television roles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diane Ladd
Content:  For the 1974 film "Alice Doesn't Live Here Anymore", she won the BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role and was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diane Ladd
Content:  She went on to win the Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress on Television for "Alice" (1980–81), and to receive Academy Award nominations for "Wild at Heart" (1990) and "Rambling Rose" (1991).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diane Ladd
Content:  Her other film appearances include "Chinatown" (1974), "Ghosts of Mississippi" (1996), "Primary Colors" (1998), "28 Days" (2000), and "American Cowslip" (2008).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Diane Ladd
Content:  Ladd is the mother of actress Laura Dern, with her ex-husband, actor Bruce Dern.

Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content: Texas Chainsaw (promoted as Texas Chainsaw 3D) is a 2013 American slasher film directed by John Luessenhop, with a screenplay by Adam Marcus, Debra Sullivan and Kirsten Elms and a story by Stephen Susco, Marcus and Sullivan.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  It is the seventh installment in "The Texas Chainsaw Massacre" franchise and was presented in 3-D.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  The film serves as a direct sequel to the 1974 film "The Texas Chain Saw Massacre" (ignoring the events of the second, and films, not including the remake films).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  The film stars Alexandra Daddario, Dan Yeager, Trey Songz, Tania Raymonde, Scott Eastwood, Thom Barry, Paul Rae and Bill Moseley, with Gunnar Hansen and Marilyn Burns, who had appeared in the original 1974 film.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  The story centers on Heather, who discovers that she was adopted after learning of an inheritance from a long-lost grandmother.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  She subsequently takes a road trip with her friends to collect the inheritance, unaware that it includes her cousin, Leatherface, as well.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
Content:  Filming began in the summer of July 2011, and it was released January 4, 2013.

Title: Marcel Benoist Prize
Content: The Marcel Benoist Prize, offered by the Marcel Benoist Foundation, is a monetary prize that has been offered annually since 1920 to a scientist of Swiss nationality or residency who has made the most useful scientific discovery.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Marcel Benoist Prize
Content:  Emphasis is placed on those discoveries affecting human life.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Marcel Benoist Prize

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content: Pieter van Musschenbroek (14 March 1692 – 19 September 1761) was a Dutch scientist.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content:  He was a professor in Duisburg, Utrecht, and Leiden, where he held positions in mathematics, philosophy, medicine, and astronomy.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content:  He is credited with the invention of the first capacitor in 1746: the Leyden jar.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content:  He performed pioneering work on the buckling of compressed struts.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content:  Musschenbroek was also one of the first scientists (1729) to provide detailed descriptions of testing machines for tension, compression, and flexure testing.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Pieter van Musschenbroek
Content:  An early example of a problem in dynamic plasticity was described in the 1739 paper (in the form of the penetration of butter by a wooden stick subjected to impact by a wooden sphere).

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: International Prize for Biology
Content: The International Prize for Biology (国際生物学賞 , Kokusai Seibutsugaku-shō ) is an annual award for outstanding contribution to the advancement of research in fundamental biology.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: International Prize for Biology
Content:  The prize, although it is not always awarded to a biologist, is one of the most prestigious honours a natural scientist can receive.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: International Prize for Biology
Content:  There are no restrictions on the nationality of the recipient.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: International Prize for Biology
Content:  Past laureates include John B. Gurdon, Motoo Kimura, Edward O. Wilson, Ernst Mayr, Thomas Cavalier-Smith and many other great biologists.

session_0.speaker: System

Title: Tribometer
Content: A tribometer is an instrument that measures tribological quantities, such as coefficient of friction, friction force, and wear volume, between two surfaces in contact.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Tribometer
Content:  It was invented by the 18th century Dutch scientist Musschenbroek

session_0.text:
Title: Goralenvolk
Content: The Goralenvolk was a geopolitical term invented by the German Nazis in World War II in reference to the population of Podhale region in the south of Poland near the Slovak border.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goralenvolk
Content:  The Germans postulated a separate nationality for people of that region in an effort to extract them from the Polish citizenry during their occupation of Poland's highlands.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goralenvolk
Content:  The term "Goralenvolk" was a neologism derived from the Polish word "Górale" (the Highlanders) commonly referring to the people living in the mountains.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goralenvolk
Content:  In order to make Gorals collaborate with the SS, the Nazis proclaimed that this group were part of the Greater Germanic Race and worthy of separate treatment from the Poles.

session_0.speaker: System
session_0.text:
Title: Goralenvolk
Content: <ref name="podhale24.pl/"> </ref>
